# Scarface / J-Dub Canonical Rules — Video Course Extraction
## Extracted 2026-07-13 from 36 groups (89 total files)

Source: `video_transcripts/*_transcript.txt` — whisper-transcribed WAV recordings.
Every rule backed by verbatim quote + (filename, timestamp).
Boot camp Day 5+6 pre-extracted content folded in from `EXTRACTED_TRADING_RULES.md`.

---

## HEADLINE FINDINGS (extraction pass 2026-07-13)

### 1. OCR naming CONFIRMED — accelerator hallucination worry closed
"One candle rule" named explicitly in boot camp Day 4 (group_07, 2868s), Day 6
(group_08, 534s), Day 13, Day 14, Day 8 scaling. Accelerator finding was scoped correctly
(29 course files only) but video extraction proves the name exists in the wider corpus.

### 2. OCR ≡ OB per source — double-count risk flagged
"The order block and the one candle rule is I consider the exact same thing" (group_07).
If both detectors credit same candle as separate confluence, stacking inflates. Low
priority (OCR demoted to A-grade+tight-stop, n~10/yr).

### 3. 84% rule: SOURCE CONTRADICTION on sizing + pattern requirement
- **Jack (Day 5)**: "Can you size up? ... on the reclaim..." (group_08, 8554s);
  "There does not need to be a pattern on the second entry" (group_08, 7781s)
- **Accelerator course**: "Same size, never bigger" + same setup/confluences required
- **Implication**: different instructors teach different 84% rules. Omen's lesson-faithful
  variant (RULE84_LESSON=True + RULE84_ARM_BNR_ONLY=True) matches accelerator. Jack's
  variant (size-up + no pattern gate) is a looser alternative. Keep lesson-faithful.
- **NEW**: 84% reclaim = enter on reclaim candle close, NOT wait for retest (group_08,
  7898s, group_12, 954s). Omen already does this (RULES84_LESSON fires on reclaim close).
  Confirms our implementation.

### 4. R:R floor contradiction: 1:1 vs 2:1
- **Hayden coaching**: min 1:1 R:R for A+; 1.33 "makes sense"; 1:1.5 if A+
- **Accelerator/masterminds**: "2:1 is the MINIMUM aggregate R:R expectation"
- **Implication**: different instructors have different risk tolerances. Omen's 2R blind
  target matches the stricter (accelerator) standard.

### 5. Entry timing contradiction: retest candle vs next candle
- **Hayden**: "Zero percent of the time you want to enter on the retest candle... decision
  must be made on the candle AFTER the retest" (group_17, 1293s)
- **Boot camp**: "I always wait for a candle close of the level... next candle does not
  have to close for me" (group_07, 4664s) — enter ON level-touch candle close
- **Omen**: enters at confirm-candle close (matches boot camp, diverges from Hayden)

### 6. Win rate claims: 50% vs 55-65% vs 65%
- Accelerator: ~50%W at 2-3:1
- Masterminds (4.0/5.0): 55-65%
- Scarface (boot camp): ~65%
- **Implication**: 55% S-tier target may be closer to source than 50% baseline.

### 7. Stop-after-win: UNSOURCED
Flagged in hallucination-audit.md (videos pass confirmed: NOT COVERED in 36 groups).
Closest real quotes are softer ("best days are one trade"). No verbatim source exists.
config.yaml stop_after_win=true = OURS, not source-mandated.

### 8. Reclaim = separate setup from B&R
Hayden defines 3 core entry models: "You either have a break and retest, reclaim, or a
reversal" (group_17, 286s). Reclaim = false breakdown + immediate recovery; 84% rule is
a sub-type of reclaim where structure stays intact.

### 9. Reversal = formal 3rd entry model
Full 5-candle sequence: C1=down-close pivot, C2=break, C3=displacement, C4=retest,
C5=entry/confirmation. Works better in ranging markets. Not coded in Omen.

### 10. New setup types discovered (not coded):
- Play-the-Open / Open Strategy (first 15 min, no displacement needed)
- Pop-and-Fade (congestion break downward)
- Opening Drive (Day 2 continuation after strong Day 1 trend)

### 11. A/A+ grading — inconsistent across sources (feeds B3)
Day 6 defines A as holistic confluence stack (gap up + above PDH + first 5min green +
OCR holding + HTF level + first pullback + QQQ RS). Masterminds add: QQQ above PMH +
entry level must be HTF level. A/A+ is a gradient, not a mechanical checklist.

### 12. QQQ alignment = relative strength at entry, not level-break
Videos operationalize QQQ as moment-to-moment relative strength comparison at entry
(+ maintenance after entry), not "first RTH close through PDH/PMH/PDL/PML". Rule-4
proxy is well-measured (F4) but is a proxy.

### 13. Displacement: FVG definition found
"Displacement = fair value gap between candle #1 high and candle #3 low; if wicks
overlap = no displacement" (group_06, 5841s). No numeric threshold anywhere (confirmed
OURS quantification). 1.5x body convention is ours.

### 14. Concrete numbers summary (all sources):
- Win rate: 50-65% claimed (source-dependent); 84% rule works 84% of time
- R:R: min 1:2 (most common); avg 1:2 to 1:2.2; accelerator 2-3:1
- Risk: 1-2%/trade; options 10-20% premium; 0DTE 20-40%
- Max trades: 1-2/day avg, 3 absolute max; stop after 2 consecutive losses
- Time window: 9:30-11:00 ET sharp; most trades 9:45-10:15
- Zone size: 2-10 cents; ideal breakout-to-retest candles: 3-8
- 45-60s wait before entry after candle close
- Hold time: avg win ~30 min, avg loss ~8 min
- Gap fill: 70% in 48 hours; consistency period: 3-4 mo before sizing up

### 15. New contradictions for hallucination audit:
- **VWAP**: accelerator teaches it; masterminds say "I don't use any indicators"
- **Breakeven**: accelerator says after TP1; mastermind 4-0 says only at structure shift
- **84% personal rejection**: some traders avoid 84% rule — "feels like revenge"
- **Displacement definition**: boot camp provides FVG definition; accelerator said
  "qualitative only"
- **A+ criteria**: each source gives slightly different definition — no unified checklist

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Valid Break - Displacement Definition]** "displacement is this second move this second move is displacement you can see it much more clear on the chart right look out clear you can see that second move break and then the read test whereas this this stayed near the level of support right this was always touching the level of support now turn resistance okay so displacement is this bigger move right and for me what I like to see in this placement is that it doesn't touch back into whatever resistance or support that we have currently" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2790s-2819s])

- **[Valid Break - Displacement Definition]** "displacement it's simply the move that happens after we break out of the stock and then we come back for the retest so this is displacement and then this move back or pull back in the retest and displacement only occurs when you have a candle that does not touch your previous resistance turn support" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2819s-2841s])

- **[Valid Break - Displacement Example]** "when you get this free test right here right look a displacement is this right displacement would be this came so this red candle that came back for the retest at 941 this had a displacement because it was never touching right it never touched the resistance again" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2648s-2670s])

- **[Valid Break - No Displacement Example]** "on this 938 candle because we're still touching this resistance no candle ever stopped touching this resistance to turn support right this is more consolidation" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2699s-2724s])

- **[Stop Placement - Impulsive Candle]** "my stop loss is simply a break back into consolidation, right? So if we break back into this consolidation, because this is all consolidation, right? This area that we had earlier, this was consolidation." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [369s-383s])

- **[Stop Placement - Impulsive Candle]** "for me, where my stock loss would be in this specific trade, is going to be whatever the candle, the impulse of candle that broke out, okay? So the impulse of candle that broke out of this consolidation, right? It's going to be a break below that candle, right? That's going to be my stock loss in terms of this momentum trade." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [445s-463s])

- **[Stop Placement - Impulsive Candle]** "my stop loss on this trade would be simply a break of the impulsive candle, right? Remember, I talked about that where momentum traders, right? So the stop loss would be the break of the impulsive candle." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [4175s-4186s])

- **[Stop Placement - Why Impulsive Candle]** "This is a stock you want, it's a work right away in your direction. This is why it's a good trade. Because if this stock doesn't work right away, most likely the stock's going to fail, right? Most likely the trade's going to fail." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [4227s-4238s])

- **[Stop Placement - Why Impulsive Candle]** "Buyers want to hold it not only above this previous hives, but now we can also look at cues. If cues comes back to 466927, but then breaks below into the consolidation again, right? Most likely we do not want to be in this trade anymore" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [4203s-4220s])

- **[Targets - HOD/LOD]** "we could look for either low of day, low of day provides us a 2.39, right? Let's just say we were looking for low of day. And you can see low of day came, right? If we entered into this candle right here at 1234, right? Low of day was at 1247, right? So this was around a 13 minute trade" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1905s-1924s])

- **[Targets - Psychological Numbers]** "in cases like this, right? I would just simply look at psychological numbers, right? So to 120 would be my psychological number that I would be looking at." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [4355s-4361s])

- **[First-Break vs Late Breaks - Wait for HOD/LOD]** "I'm going to wait for a high of day break and re-test or a low of day break and re-test. I don't want to do anything in between the chop." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1708s-1720s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[84% Rule - Requires A+ Entry]** "the thing you need to know about the 84% rule is you need an A plus entry. Okay, so let's consider this to be an A plus entry, right? Fall criteria is in that. However, the stock came back to the retest and it didn't work out." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [543s-555s])

- **[84% Rule - Mechanics]** "we wait for the stocks. Now do what? Come back to this area. We got stocked out. That's fine. But when the stock comes back up into the same area that we were previously at, right? So the same area that we took earlier, when the stock comes back up in the stock. In theory, it should work 84% of the time." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [568s-588s])

- **[84% Rule - Why It Works]** "It should work 84% of the time because when we had our 1st initial entry, that entry, it may just be the timing was off, right? The executioner the timing was off for the 1st specific entry. But the entry was good. And therefore the second time it comes up, you have a higher probability chance of the market working out. Why? Because now you also attracted more buyers off this previous level." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [590s-625s])

- **[84% Rule - YouTube Video Reference]** "I do have a full video, by the way, on YouTube, you can literally switch up Scarface trades 84% rule. And you'll find a full video on that on YouTube as well with more examples" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [642s-650s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[HOD/LOD - Continuation Signal]** "high of day and low day continuation, I'm looking for high of day or low day continuation" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2270s-2288s])

- **[HOD/LOD - Market Context]** "When you get high of day, right? And you break back below what does this prove this proves that buyers were not strong enough to move this talk to the upside cool. Now when you get a low of day right here, though it was a week, it was still a low of day, when you get a low of day and you still can't move to the upside what does this are saying move to the downside what does this mean? This means that sellers aren't strong enough" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2300s-2343s])

- **[HOD/LOD - No Continuation = No Trade]** "If nobody is strong in this market and no one's trying to take the heavy force and move a stock to either the upside or to the downside, right? If no one is trying to take the stock to the upside or to the downside, then obviously, right? Obviously that is a day we do not want to trade." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2350s-2361s])

- **[Range - Everything Between is Noise]** "these are the two most important levels. Everything, okay? Everything in between this is noise." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1691s-1698s])

- **[Range - No Trend = No Momentum Trade]** "with the line chart, you can see there is no trend. What do you see here? There is no trend. We are momentum traders. If there's no trend, why would we be trying to take this trade right? We wouldn't." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1744s-1761s])

- **[Range - Fair Value Explanation]** "Why do we have range action we have range action when buyers and sellers believe the stock is in a fair price, right? So when we have range, that means both buyers and sellers believe that we have the prices in fair value" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2393s-2427s])

- **[Previous Day Levels - Opening in Range]** "If we open up in between previous day high and previous day low, that means we're already in range, right? So that's those are days that I'm going to be careful in anyways" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2049s-2061s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling - QQQ Correlation]** "you would take at least 50% right? At least the minimum of 50, right? Anywhere from 50 and above why? - **[Scaling - Locking Profit]** "you still need to take some percentage off just to make sure you are locking that realized profit and 50 to 60% off this level right here is good." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3568s-3577s])

- **[Scaling - Already at 4R]** "you're already at a four-art. So you could even take off a little bit more if you really wanted." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3586s-3589s])

## QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation

- **[QQQ - Correlation for Entry]** "we need Q to break this high, right? If Q's breaks this high, then I would be much more interested in Nvidia." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3996s-3999s])

- **[QQQ - Both Making New Highs = Strength]** "Both of them also broke out their new highs. So what does this show us in terms of the market? It shows us that both of them have strength, meaning they're both following each other, meaning buyers are in control." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3269s-3285s])

- **[QQQ - Stop Loss Based on QQQ]** "QQ breaks back below, right? If QQ breaks back below, then this Nvidia to trade has a less of a chance of moving to the upside." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3373s-3379s])

- **[QQQ - Relative Strength Before Breakout]** "Nvidia had relative strength before Q's broke out of this. So before Q Q Q even broke out of this range, Nvidia was a ticker that already had strength" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [4099s-4111s])

- **[QQQ - Trade Quality with QQQ Confirmation]** "Though displacement is lacking, it's still a better trade than this right here. Remember, I told you, you're not going to get textbook example trades every single day" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [4127s-4138s])

## Market Structure Concepts

- **[Break of Structure vs Change of Character]** "A break of structure is continuation in trend. Okay? So continuation in trend happens where when we break the high. Okay? This is a break of structure. This is the continuation of the trend." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [227s-245s])

- **[Change of Character - Low Break]** "This is the low that it needs to break. Now are you correct in saying this? Yes. This is the, this is where you change character. Right? This is where you have the change of character." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [314s-323s])

- **[Market Structure - Real World vs Textbook]** "this isn't really what market structure is in the real world. How many people have tried to use this type of market structure and they get confused on the one minute timeframe. They're like, what am I supposed to be looking for?" (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [100s-113s])

- **[Market Structure - Context Matters]** "what you have to understand in this is context space. This is what no one really talks about. Because everyone likes to be so, you know, mechanical and be like, no, this is the break of structure. No." (bonus_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [351s-360s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest

## 4. Order Blocks
- **[Order Block - Swing Entry Example]** "when it broke back above and then it retested back into this previous kind of order block level, right? And it was kind of near the one, two, three, four, five minute high rate" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [45s-55s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Previous Day High Retest - Valid Setup]** "you could have had the previous day high retest on Apple as well, this previous day high retest" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [83s-87s])
- **[Opening Range - Higher Timeframe Thesis]** "If you have a higher timeframe thesis, mark up the one minute timeframe... mark up the one minute opening range" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2047s-2051s])
- **[Opening Range - No Higher Timeframe Thesis]** "When you don't have a higher timeframe thesis as clear, mark up the five minute timeframe, because then you have a little bit more data that you can go based off of" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2064s-2069s])
- **[Opening Range - Rule Summary]** "Higher timeframe thesis, one minute focused on a more lower, you don't have a higher timeframe thesis, right? You're kind of opening up a little bit more the range mark out the five minute and focus on that rather than one minute. Just keep it simple." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2071s-2083s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 7. Exits
- **[Scaling - Choppy vs Trending]** "Choppy day, I'd take 50% off a high day then let the other 50% ride. Day where it's trending, I would take 25% off a high day or maybe even less 10% 15% off a high day and then let the rest ride." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2186s-2200s])
- **[Scaling - Small Account Adaptation]** "if you have three contracts, for example, it's a choppy day take off two, right? If it's a choppy day take off two. If it's a trending day take off one" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2224s-2233s])
- **[Scaling - Consistent Percentage Recommendation]** "If you're doing the same percentages, no matter the context, that's what I would say, right? With the context it takes time to learn. The context takes time to learn. So that's honestly a better point from that, right? Because when you don't really understand the context too well, right? Just do the same percentage every single time." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2356s-2375s])
- **[Scaling - Consistent Contract Size]** "Take the same contracts, let's say three contracts every single time. And then scale one or two a high of day and then the last one you kind of let it ride or whatever." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2375s-2385s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[Swing - HTF Consolidation]** "for swings really what I'm looking for, right? Like I said, is when a stock has on the higher time frame, it's consolidating on the higher time frame, right? After something, that's really what I'm looking for. Because when you see a stock consolidating on the higher time frame after it moves up or after it moves down, realistically it's gonna have an impulse to move to one, see either the upside or to the downside" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [756s-775s])
- **[Swing - HTF Trend Intact]** "What you're really looking for on the higher time frame is a trend understanding market structure, higher lows, higher lows, right? So the trend is still intact." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1069s-1077s])
- **[Swing - HTF Consolidation vs Pullback]** "it's consolidating rather than pulling back and that's a good signal that it should potentially move to the upside as well" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1118s-1123s])
- **[Swing - Entry Timeframes]** "I look at the four-hour in one hour, right? So I'll scroll through here, I'll look at the four-hour timeframe, I'll look at the one-hour timeframe, for any potential setups, and then I'll try to enter on the 10 or 15 minute timeframe" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1408s-1418s])
- **[Swing - Entry Precision vs Day Trading]** "with swing trading, it's not really that much about precision to be honest... you can be wrong on the entry, like the entry doesn't have to be 100% it's not really fully about the precision compared to day trading where you want, like you wanna be on that very candle" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1426s-1441s])
- **[Swing - Scaling In on Retest]** "if it holds a strong price action, it shows a little bit of conciliation again, I'll enter in a little bit more of my position, right? And so you can slowly scale in, as it comes down" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1481s-1489s])
- **[Swing - Market Designed to Go Up]** "the market is genuinely designed to go up. That is one thing you have to understand. The market is designed to specifically go up" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1667s-1675s])
- **[Swing - Focus on Long Side]** "For whenever I look at swing positions, I really do focus in a little bit more on those longer like that long side move" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1713s-1717s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Swing - Low Risk Entries + Momentum]** "in terms of swing positions, right, really what I look for, right, is just lower scads, and really momentum, right, so momentum and low risk ads" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [156s-163s])
- **[Swing - Fakeout Liquidity Setup]** "I entered here because I knew that if this was fake out liquidity, right, this stock should move back to the upside" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [289s-298s])
- **[Swing - Accumulation/Manipulation/Distribution Pattern]** "accumulation, manipulation, distribution... same pattern again here, accumulation, manipulation, distribution again, right? So you kind of pick up on these patterns" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [596s-605s])
- **[Swing - Context Over Pattern]** "you guys know we're not pattern based traders, we're a context based traders, right? So there's a lot of context factors with the pattern that you have to understand. So just because that you're seeing a trend line is not, that's not why the market's moving." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1103s-1112s])
- **[Swing - Low Risk Setups]** "it's more about the context of the trade, and it's more about just trying to enter in low risk setups. Really, that's really all it is, right? Those low risk setups is really what I'm trying to enter in on." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1136s-1147s])
- **[Priority - Day Trading First]** "I would focus on day trading first, of course, to really lock that in first... you do have to understand that at one point you want to open up that arsenal, and you want to start adding a little bit more things in your toolbox, right? But that's only when you have consistency, and you're your foundation is very strong" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1377s-1401s])
- **[Discipline - Following Rules]** "every time I didn't follow my rules, I was just like, wow, I'm like, I'm an idiot. Like why the hell did I just do that? Like I'd get more mad at one point, right? Like once I had the system, I would start getting mad on why I wasn't following my rules." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2268s-2281s])
- **[Theory vs Execution]** "you can learn the theory of trading very quickly. Everything you kind of need to know about theory and even technicals and stuff like that, right? But it's about actually being able to execute the theory that's a hard part" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [2306s-2321s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Swing - Starter Position Size]** "your starter position should be in terms of, like, relatively to know where your starter position should be, it should be somewhat of your like, profits for the week, for example. Like, let's say you trade two to three contracts daily on a given trade, and your profits for the week are, I'm just gonna assume here 500 bucks, 700 bucks for the week. You would only want to have a full position of $5,700." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [440s-460s])
- **[Swing - Acceptable Loss 50-60%]** "the goal is to only have that 50 to 60% type of loss, like, limit, because I'm looking for that 100% so that two are multiple would be 50 to 60% I'm okay with losing" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [486s-493s])
- **[Swing - Target 100-200%]** "I'm looking for that 100% so that two are multiple would be 50 to 60% I'm okay with losing, as long as I'm, you know, my first targets are I to about 100% on the contracts themselves" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [488s-497s])
- **[Swing - Typical Gain 150%+]** "if they usually hit my profit, there'll be up 150% at least usually, right? So that would already be a two R just from where I bought" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [512s-519s])
- **[Swing - Contract Expiration 1-2 Weeks]** "It's usually I buy the next week's contracts, which would be next Fridays, right? Or the week after, right? So one or two weeks is usually the time I give for my swing trades, I don't give anymore." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1049s-1058s])
- **[Swing - Profit Allocation Example]** "you're risking 50% of your profits, you're hitting $1,000 a day, right? For five days, that's $5,000, you risk $25, $500 for a swing trade, right? Now let's say that swing trade, for example, even that Tesla trade, you just decided swing it, that's 400% on $2,500 bucks" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [1356s-1372s])
- **[Swing - Overnight Risk Example]** "I overnight at Tesla, you can see like yesterday, obviously was a fantastic day, but then I was in profit yesterday, right? I was up like $15,000, I was like, all right, whatever, I'm gonna overnight this, because I'm already in a decent amount of profit." (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [663s-674s])
- **[Swing - Overnight Risk Acceptance]** "I will even if this trade gap down overnight, I would still have at least five to $7,000 minimum of profit, minimum, right? So for me, it just made sense because I was able to accept the risk" (bonus_How_To_Swing_Trade_Q_A.txt [689s-699s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Core Definition
- **Setup is break-and-retest** "The setup is a break and retest trading setup" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [121s-126s])
- **Goal defined** "The goal of the training setup is to buy or sell after the price breaks the level and retest it" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [184s-191s])
- **Simple system, but application is hard** "a very very very simple system... However, when you actually apply it in the real markets... that's when it can be a little bit confusing because you don't have the right levels, you don't have the right setup in place, you don't have the right displacement" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [204s-219s])

### Valid Break (Displacement)
- **Displacement defined** "Displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken the move after that key level break" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3291s-3297s])
- **Displacement needed for A+** "this displacement of the stock shows that this is a really good example for a downside move" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3303s-3309s])
- **Strong displacement after breakout** "Broke out strong displacement after the breakout which means after this breakout level we had an impulsive move still continue to the upside" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5336s-5346s])
- **No trade on initial breakout** "No trade on the initial breakout... The breakout retests because this didn't have enough displacement move" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4371s-4378s])

### Valid Retest
- **Wait for candle to close** "you always wait for the candlestick to close" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1192s-1193s])
- **Why wait for close** "What you'll notice what will happen sometimes is a candle will look like a really really nice hammer stick candle at a key level and you'll enter into a long position before it closes and by the time it closes it'll you know break that key level" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1211s-1219s])
- **Weak/strong candle confirmation** "we're waiting for a week candle if we're looking to short, we're waiting for a strong candle for looking to long" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1114s-1119s])
- **Wick below level is better for longs** "When it wicks below the level and then comes back... it gets rid of the rookie traders but it also allows the stop the the short sellers to enter in... and therefore now everyone's looking for that long position and therefore it'll actually move to the upside" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5513s-5528s])
- **Candle must close above/below level** "the candle has to close above your level, but if it wicks below your level, that's fine. That's honestly better" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5566s-5577s])

### Entry Trigger Candles
- **Hammer for longs, inverted hammer/shooting star for shorts** "I personally like to look for hammer candles when I'm going long and I like to look for inverted hammer candles or shooting star candles when I'm going short" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3375s-3381s])
- **Why these candles work** "what they show you right is... buyers try to bring this stock above this level and as we can see we touch this level but the second we touched it we had like liquidity sitting there right we had sell orders sitting here and therefore we broke back down" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3388s-3408s])

### Stop Placement
- **Stop above the level, with room** "me personally. I don't like to have you know a stop loss that's right you know right at that level. I like to give it a little bit of room" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3448s-3454s])
- **Opening price as stop location** "Opening price is something that the stock loves to respect during the day because that's just simply where the stock opened up during that day... we're gonna use the opening price right here and that's where we're gonna put our stop loss" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3459s-3470s])
- **Stop only moves when market structure changes** "you only move your stop loss when market structure changes. You don't move it before based off your trade" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5772s-5774s])
- **Stop remains constant until structure break** "our stop loss always remains constant until we break that structure" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1626s-1633s])
- **Risk level should be obvious** "if you're trying to figure out where the risk is then most likely you're trying to force the trade. You should be able to look at the chart for a couple seconds and be able to determine the risk if it's not clear there's most likely a reason for that" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1250s-1268s])

### Targets
- **First scale always at HOD/LOD** "Looking for my first scales at high of day or low day every time right every time" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1519s-1525s])
- **Why HOD/LOD scale works** "that high of day scale allows me to hold and therefore even if it comes back down near our stop loss... because I lock in high a day this means even if we come back to our stop loss here and break... I'm going to lose only a little" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1616s-1650s])
- **Psychological numbers as targets** "those full psychological numbers that could be you know if you're trading apple for example and it's at 90 bucks right then the whole psychological number of you know 91 92... you want to scale some there" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1700s-1722s])
- **Previous pivot levels as targets** "previous pivot levels right so those are gonna be higher time frame levels that we're going to exit on for example on the four-hour" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1759s-1763s])
- **VWAP not a profit target** "I don't trade VWAP... the only reason I have VWAP on my chart now honestly it's just because it makes a chart now a completely plain... I also use VWAP for one main purpose and that's if the stock is above VWAP right then you're looking for a long position if the stock is below VWAP you're looking for a short position" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1786s-1829s])

### First Break vs Late Breaks
- **First retest is best** "you want to look for the first one you want to look for the first retest right you don't want something that's been retested multiple times... I always look for the fresh level. I don't like something that's been retested multiple times" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4267s-4280s])
- **Opening drive play is best** "the best case scenario for me is always the first opening drive play is always going to be the best play right because if you catch that and you catch the trend for the day that's gonna be the best play for the whole day" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5899s-5909s])

## 5. Key Levels

### Drawing Process (Hierarchical)
- **Start with daily timeframe** "first higher time frame... I'm looking at the daily chart and on the daily chart I'm simply marking up the candlestick high in the low for the previous day and I'm looking at any other pivot levels that may be near on the daily chart" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2284s-2292s])
- **Then four-hour and one-hour** "from the daily timeframe right after I mark out the high in low of the candlestick I'm going to the four hour and the one hour timeframe right so I skip everything else" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2319s-2326s])
- **Then one-minute for accuracy** "we can go to the one minute and on the one minute we're simply just gonna make these levels a little bit better and by better I simply just mean we're gonna make them a little bit more accurate" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2881s-2887s])
- **Color coding** "the green lines on my chart are the higher time frame resistance the red lines on my chart are the higher time frame support. The white lines are the previous day one minute levels" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2528s-2532s])
- **Draw levels as day progresses** "after I have those key levels that I drew you know the previous day or the day before or whatever then I will draw levels as the day progresses... you can draw out new levels as a day progresses" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2570s-2606s])

### Level Selection Philosophy
- **4-7 key levels max** "on average. I'll have anywhere from like four to at most seven" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1305s-1312s])
- **Only most important/obvious ones** "I only draw the most important ones. How do you know they're the most important ones because they're the most obvious ones" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1345s-1352s])
- **Less is more** "personally for me less is more right so I like the least amount of levels possible" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3043s-3049s])
- **Fresh levels every day** "Every single morning or every every night depending on when I do it I will get rid of all the levels I have before... one day of price action will change a lot" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1470s-1495s])

### Body vs Wick Drawing
- **Draw based on context** "I believe you should draw it based off of context. Okay, a lot of traders... they only draw on one so they would only draw on the body or they would only draw on the wick. I don't do that. I draw both right depending on the context of the trade" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2921s-2939s])
- **Experience required** "you learn how to draw those as you draw more over time. That's something that experience will help you get much much better at" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2961s-2966s])

### Learning Method
- **Compare your levels to mentor's** "every single night or morning... make sure you have your key levels before I post them... simply compare your key levels to mine and that is going to be how you're going to learn how to draw key levels the most effectively every single day" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [987s-1003s])
- **Off by 5-10 cents is fine** "you'll see that sometimes you'll your level is off by maybe like five cents your 10 cents and that's completely fine" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1031s-1034s])

### Pre-Market High/Low
- **Draw pre-market high and low** "you can also draw pre-market high and pre-market low as well right you can always draw those pre-market high and pre-market lows" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3073s-3079s])
- **Pre-market high/low defined** "the pre market high and low is from 4 a.m. eastern time to 9:29 a.m. eastern time the high and low that's formed from 4 a.m. eastern to 9:29 eastern" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5642s-5658s])
- **Handle fake wicks on pre-market levels** "sometimes you'll see fake wicks on pre market high and low... you just have to make make sure that it's a that's a real candle... you can just get rid of the noise by going on five minutes for the pre market high and pre market low" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5296s-5305s])

### Patterns on Higher Timeframes
- **Bull flag and flat top are main patterns** "the patterns I trade the most honestly all it's just a flag in the flat top right or the say bear flag and flat bottom... 95% in time I'm trading those patterns" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2339s-2355s])
- **Why higher timeframe patterns matter** "on the higher time frame you have to understand most hedge funds most institutions they're looking at those higher time frames... the pattern helps us understand like hey the stock was in an uptrend. It's consolidating now therefore if it breaks out of this consolidation period right it'll continue it's move" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2462s-2475s])
- **Capture HTF breakout on LTF** "for us we're looking to capture that higher time frame break out on the lower time frame by trading the break in retest" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2483s-2485s])

---

## 6. Time-of-Day

### Market Conditions
- **Trending market preferred** "I personally like to trade when we are in a trending market right it's either we're trending to the upside or to the downside but I'm only trading it in a trending market why because it works the best for momentum trading" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [721s-728s])
- **Choppy market = poor retests** "Today was a choppy day... you saw some of the the retest that we we had coming back. They just weren't good retest why because they weren't holding why weren't they holding because the market was chopping" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [730s-746s])
- **Sideways market = lower size, only A+** "sideways action you want to size you you want to only look for the most A plus setups if you even want to take it and if you do take it you take it with low size" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [787s-793s])

### News Days
- **Normal trading day assumed** "This is assuming a trading normal trading day and not some weird news" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1451s-1455s])
- **Avoid crazy news events** "We're not looking for you know crazy news events or anything like that" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1455s-1459s])

### Trade Duration
- **Most profitable trades within 10-15 minutes** "most of my profitable trades are usually within 10 to 15 minutes like there much much below 15 minutes" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4104s-4110s])

---

## 7. Exits

### Scaling Strategy
- **Sell into strength on options** "you want to sell into strength on option contracts... when you're selling into strength other people are buying that at price at those high prices... you're going to get the best price for that contract" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4424s-4446s])
- **First scale at HOD/LOD** "Looking for my first scales at high of day or low day every time right every time" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1519s-1525s])
- **After first scale, trade is break-even** "the second I get my first scale right that trade is really just a break even trade for me depending on the type it set up" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4130s-4134s])
- **Trailers can make more than full trade** "the trailers can make you more than the whole potential trade" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5714s-5719s])

### Trailer Percentage Depends on Context
- **Range market = take full position at HOD** "if we're in a range market then I'm gonna take the full position off at high of day" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5966s-5970s])
- **Trending market = take 20% off, let rest ride** "if we're in a trending market then I'll only take off 20% of let the rest ride" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5970s-5975s])

### Stop Management
- **Don't move stop to breakeven after first scale** "after my high of day scale that doesn't mean I'm gonna let my stop go break even. Oh my stop loss was still still the same" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5748s-5754s])
- **Only move stop when structure changes** "you only move your stop loss when market structure changes. You don't move it before based off your trade" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5772s-5774s])

---

## 8. HTF Application

### Thesis Formation
- **Thesis on daily, 4H, 1H** "I'm making my thesis or my bias as some people call it I'm making it on the daily four hour and one hour timeframe" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [797s-801s])
- **Trade execution on 1-minute** "I'm actually taking the trade every single time on the one minute time frame. I may monitor right I may monitor the trade on the five minute but I'm taking it on the one minute" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [801s-815s])
- **Come in with thesis but stay open** "I come into the day with the thesis... but that doesn't not mean that I'm not going to go to the other side" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5596s-5611s])

### Multi-Day Holds
- **Not explicitly covered as "multi-day holds"** — but trailers can ride: "the last 20% right those trailers could potentially have made more than the full trade if it actually did end up you know having a crazy day" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [5699s-5708s])

---

## 9. Trade Selection

### A+ Setup Criteria
- **Strong displacement after break** "this displacement of the stock shows that this is a really good example for a downside move" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3303s-3309s])
- **Strong trend established** "We have a very strong trend established rate and also on the higher time frame this trend is the exact same bearish" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4836s-4842s])
- **A+ retest example** "this retest right here is an A plus setup retest... the reason this is because look at this displacement on this move" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3285s-3297s])

### Market Regime
- **Trending market = size up** "the reason I've even been on this you know crazy green streak recently is because the market has been trending for the last you know couple months" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [746s-751s])
- **Sideways market = step back, lower size** "when we do come back into a sideways market or consolidation market right on the higher time frame then I will obviously step back lower my size and obviously not size up as aggressively" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [762s-769s])

### Tickers Traded
- **Tech names under QQQ** "I only trade this setup on the tech names. Why because that's what I've been trading for years... the tech names are Tesla apple in the video AMD anything under the QQQ built" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [666s-678s])
- **Strategy works on any market/timeframe** "The break and retest the the fundamental strategy behind the break and retest works in every single market... any stock any time frame" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [578s-584s])
- **Futures work too** "I know the break and retest works for futures because the futures on for example spy QQQ right if you're just trading ES or NQ right it moves the exact same way" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [597s-606s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

### Win Rate
- **~65% win rate** "when you look at my win rate for the strategy over a long period of time it's gonna be around that 65% mark... This strategy is accurate about 65% of the time" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3137s-3149s])

### Risk:Reward
- **Average R:R ~1:2 to 1:2.2** "on average if you look at my trades over a long period of time it'll be a one to two to one one to two point two ish around restore word" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3125s-3130s])
- **Minimum 1:2 R:R** "What I'm looking for is at least a one to two restore word" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3103s-3110s])
- **Example trade had 3.2 R:R** "this is about a 3.2 restore word trade" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3470s-3479s])

### Two to Three Days Make Month
- **2-3 days make 60% of month** "two to three days for me usually make up like 60% of my month" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [484s-489s])

### Trade Duration
- **Most profitable trades under 15 minutes** "most of my profitable trades are usually within 10 to 15 minutes like there much much below 15 minutes" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4104s-4110s])

### A/A+ Grading Criteria
- **A+ requires strong displacement** "this retest right here is an A plus setup retest... the reason this is because look at this displacement on this move" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3285s-3297s])
- **A+ requires trend alignment** "We have a very strong trend established rate and also on the higher time frame this trend is the exact same bearish" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4836s-4842s])

### QQQ Alignment
- **Edge comes from comparing stocks to QQQ** "my edge comes from understanding market context and market structure... being able to identify interday relative strength and weakness by comparing individual stocks with the indices" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1899s-1914s])
- **QQQ + stock divergence example** "if QQ is strong on the day right and Tesla's weak on the day right the second QQ hits a level of resistance where I believe that QQ can draw down from and Tesla shows a break and retest that that Tesla's retesting a previous level of resistance. I'm entering into Tesla's short there because if QQQ drops this is gonna explode Tesla to the downside" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2066s-2084s])
- **Futures edge = compare indices** "if you're trading NQ and you're trading ES you're you're comparing the indices to each other now... if you're looking at the break and re-test on NQ and ES is looking strong for the bullish side right and the NQ's dipping into you know that previous level support right then you obviously want to go long" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2225s-2243s])

### Displacement/FVG Size Definitions
- **Displacement defined qualitatively** "Displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken the move after that key level break" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3291s-3297s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Break-and-retest is the fundamental strategy** "the break and retest the the fundamental strategy behind the break and retest works in every single market" (bonus_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt) — ALREADY EXTRACTED

### Targets = Liquidity Levels
- **Targets are always liquidity levels (old highs/old lows)** "What's the next key level... You're swing high... All right whenever you trade... Where's our take profit?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4546s-4559s])
- **Break-and-retest targets are always old highs/old lows** "If we're buying a uptrend and just kind of like a break and retest... break of structure... swing high and what's resting above here liquidity... We come back to retest... What's the next key level... You're swing high" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4526s-4548s])

### All-Time Highs as Target
- **All-time highs are the ultimate target for break-and-retest** "If we break and retest at all-time highs... you'd be waiting for the break... once we're clearly broke above that's when we had nice continuation higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4834s-4871s])
- **Don't long AT all-time highs, wait for break-and-retest** "We much rather long... Intwards your all-time highs, but we're not gonna long at your all-time highs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4710s-4714s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **84% rule = reclaim setup = faking out sellers** "why does the 84% rule work so well and why does it usually have the most explosive move... Is because it's basically faking out right the sellers towards the downside... Reclaiming back above a key level and then usually it has a very explosive move higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5003s-5013s])
- **84% rule = invalidation of head and shoulders pattern** "In validation of the head and shoulders is what one of the most bullish setups... In validation of the head and shoulders it wise is bullish is because... it's faking out essentially sellers... a lot of sellers they're gonna look for this right left shoulder... The head... Looking for the right shoulder to confirm the thesis lore" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4949s-4988s])
- **Reclaim preferred over retest because of speed** "We want to get in on the reclaim and not the retests is because... Right what's more important in this case is a little liquidity because everyone knows once it reclaims back above... This is gonna move quick... So sometimes it can come back for the retests and sometimes it'll just launch higher because it's in a quick hurry... To essentially take out this liquidity level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5018s-5038s])
- **84% rule applies to downside too** "Meaning do you apply the 84% roll to the downside as you do upside yeah... We'll do that but as I mentioned overall market it's bullish so rarely will take 84% roll to the downside... Because overall higher time frame is bullish" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5107s-5117s])

## 4. Order Blocks
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels
### Liquidity Definition
- **Liquidity = where money is in the market** "It's basically where is the money in the market right?... So understanding where liquidity is resting right basically where the money is flowing where where is the money in the markets?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [286s-292s])
- **Three types of orders that deploy liquidity: entries, stops, take profits** "There's three types of liquidity that can be deployed into the markets... every single time you enter into something right you're either buying selling right stopping out right taking profits... entries... stops and take profit" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [365s-379s])

### External vs Internal Liquidity
- **Markets move from internal liquidity to external liquidity** "the markets always move from internal liquidity to external liquidity... And that's what we always try and... figure out" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [129s-142s])
- **Price can only do two things: seek liquidity or rebalance price** "price can either do one of two things... It is either going to seek liquidity... Or... Rebalance price" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3141s-3155s])
- **Seeking liquidity = gravitating toward old highs/old lows** "price is going to gravitate towards your old highs... old lows... or this can be your previous say has... previous say lows... Essentially seeking liquidity" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3172s-3192s])
- **Rebalancing price = pullbacks, gap fills, FVGs, order blocks** "rebalancing price is... usually pullbacks or... in this case... gap fills... Gap fills are internal liquidity... They're just rebalancing something that... we gaped up the gap down we're looking to rebalance price... FVGs... Order blocks" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3222s-3262s])

### Internal to External Framework
- **Internal entry model needed to target external liquidity** "If you're looking for the previous day house as it may an objective... Your entry has to be an internal entry model which as I mentioned... maybe it's... Your previous day close or your gap fill... It could be like an FVG... Right on an order block or it can even be like a five-minute range" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3396s-3418s])
- **Majority of trades go from internal to external** "majority of our trades go from internal... To external because we're always seeking some sort of liquidity on the higher time frames" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3516s-3521s])

### Previous Day High/Low Hierarchy
- **Previous day highs/lows are the easiest form of liquidity** "some easy ways to look for... Very key levels of liquidity just simply... Previous day highs and previous day lows... And you'll see this is the easiest form of liquidity" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1272s-1288s])
- **Previous day high/low > premarket high/low due to volume** "the previous day high is gonna be a lot more important than the premarket high and though... Why is that the case is because of one thing right there's more liquidity... Right there's also more volume" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1332s-1346s])
- **Use premarket high/low only when nothing else exists** "If there's nothing to go off of... Then we'll use the premarket high and low just because there's nothing else... But right there's an order of importance... The one that has the most volume is what you want to be looking at" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1421s-1433s])
- **Look back 3-5 sessions for day trading** "normally will look to three to five sessions when we're day trading" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1457s-1460s])

### Four Forms of Liquidity
- **Four main forms: equal highs, equal lows, trendline liquidity, range liquidity** "the four main forms of liquidity that will be looking for... trendline liquidity... range liquidity... equal highs... and... equal lows" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5606s-5634s])

### Liquidity at Every Old High/Low
- **Liquidity rests above every old high and below every old low** "between every single old high sorry above every single old high and... Although there's gonna be liquidity resting" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [495s-501s])
- **Draw liquidity levels using wicks** "Whenever drawing these out as well I like to draw the wigs... You can mark out the body to the wigs because it's the whole area... But when I'm drawing out liquidity levels... Or usually it ends up taking out the wigs... Right sometimes it rejects off the body... What would draw it?... Adrody the times I'll gravitate towards the wigs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3689s-3706s])

### Gap Fill
- **Gap fill is rebalancing price** "the gap fill... Basically if you guys don't know what the gap fill is... Just whenever there's an imbalance in price... Usually in between 4 p.m... to 9 30 a.m. in morning if there's a gap on the chart... Usually price likes to re fill those gaps" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3282s-3304s])
- **~70% of gaps fill** "I kind of talked about it for the 70%... You know 4 a.m. period usually the gaps are gonna fill" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3304s-3308s])
- **Gap and go = seeking liquidity; gap fill = rebalancing** "So the gap and go what's it doing it's seeking liquidity... They gap fill it's rebalancing price" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3317s-3321s])

### Five-Minute Range
- **First five minutes has most liquidity/volatility** "the highest volume candles are gonna come within the first five minutes... All right, so that's why we always work market that five-minute range... Why is that the case?... It's because there's the most liquidity... Most liquidity volatility within that first five minutes" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [978s-992s])
- **Five-minute range determines direction for first 30-60 minutes** "That's why that strategy and that setup is very good to determine the overall direction of the day if we break higher... There's a good chance we continue towards upside at least within the first... 30 minutes to an hour we break lower... Potential continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [999s-1011s])
- **Five-minute range loses relevance after 11am** "the five minute is gonna become a lot less relevant later into the session... the five minute... is strictly designed to... look for... to understand where prices most likely going to head within the first... right 30 minutes... to an hour... So I would use the five minute timeframe... Right more so within the first like 30 minutes to an hour and a half of market open after 11 o'clock... Right so not gonna have as much importance" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5314s-5354s])

### Price Must Take Liquidity Before Reversing
- **Price rarely rejects without taking liquidity first** "when have you ever seen... a rejection without taking right a form of liquidity... Because as I mentioned what's resting above here basically... Right sure all-time highs... if the market wants to... Potentially reverse right maybe push all the way back down... Usually what will happen is it will take this out... Reject and then head lower... Right very rarely will you see price... Reject... Before taking up the liquidity and head back towards the downside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4176s-4221s])
- **If price wants to reverse: take liquidity, reject, then go lower** "if you want to reverse... It's gonna come up... reject... Take out this liquidity... Take out a bunch of stops and then send price back lower" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4236s-4244s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
### News Events
- **News trumps technicals** "news always Trump's technicals" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2864s-2870s])
- **News invalidates all technical analysis** "Something like FOMC something when Trump treats that's actually gonna invalid it all technicals because... Essentially what that seeking actually is liquidity. It's also another form of liquidity... Because it usually just likes to take our both sides" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2880s-2888s])
- **Trade AFTER news, not before** "a lot of people trying to avoid news events which is right it's smart too... Especially before... But we usually like trading news events... Specifically right after news events because there's a lot of volatility and liquidity" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1073s-1082s])

## 7. Exits
### Scaling at All-Time Highs
- **Sell half at all-time highs, reposition, leave trail** "Right maybe sell half-reposition and maybe leave some trailers because you can see it continued after" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [4770s-4775s])

## 8. HTF Application
### Higher Time Frames Hold More Power
- **Higher time frames always hold more importance** "the higher time frames are always gonna hold more power" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1266s-1269s])
- **Trades on 1min/5min must be based on HTF thesis** "I know a lot of our trades are on the one in five minute chart... But they're really always based off the higher time frames... Right that's what matters most if you can't understand the higher time frames... You have no business being on the one in five minute because you'll see you might get fake though quite often on the one in five just why... Because there's so much more let's go on" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2504s-2522s])
- **HTF has fewer fakeouts** "the higher time frames right usually have less fake oats... Higher time frames have fake oats as well, but usually they take longer to develop... So you have a lot more clear of a thesis" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2624s-2633s])
- **Bigger base = bigger breakout** "the bigger the base... The bigger the break up" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2636s-2638s])

### HTF for Thesis, LTF for Execution
- **Higher time frame for thesis, lower time frame for execution** "Higher time frame for the thesis and then lower time frame for that essentially just really the only reason why you should be on the one minute is just to execute" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2770s-2777s])
- **Minimum 15min for HTF context** "you want to be on the minimum... Right at least 15 right five 15 minute as your higher time frame" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2994s-2999s])
- **Don't have thesis on 1min** "if you have a one minute thesis... You also execute the trade and manage everything on the one minute... If everything's on the one minute then you have no higher time frame context" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2970s-2987s])

### Trade With the Trend
- **Easier to trade with trend than against** "it's a lot easier to trade with the trend than to trade against the trend" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5130s-5134s])
- **Job of trader = trade with trend** "where our job is traders is to trade with the trend... the easiest way to make money to trade with the trend if you... try and fight the trend can you make money yes but overall in the long term... if you're basing off the higher time frames or you want to be in line... with the overall higher time frames" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [5861s-5874s])

## 9. Trade Selection
### Flat Top Pattern
- **Flat top = most bullish pattern** "if you ever see this on a chart... This is this will scream out to this will be like this is a very bullish setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2160s-2166s])
- **Flat top logic: sellers keep failing to push down, liquidity builds above** "Every single time it taps into a key level of resistance... sellers attempt to push it down and now we move back higher... Sellers try and push this down... But what's happening right it's not dropping... Right because we're building as I mentioned the higher lows... The more times the sellers try and push this down... And we can't... So what's most likely going to happen right there's a lot of liquidity building up... Because where is this thesis essentially going to be invalidated... Every single time you short... Where you're going to put your stop loss... Right above here... So that's why flat top works" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1945s-2043s])
- **Flat top especially bullish in bull market** "Especially in a bull market flat tops usually end up having really big move after" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2167s-2172s])
- **Sometimes flat top breaks without retest** "We'd like to see the flat top come back for the retest... But sometimes the flat top is so bullish no, just break above and never come back" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2329s-2334s])

### Cup and Handle Pattern
- **Cup and handle = bullish, same logic as flat top** "why is a couple cup in hand or bullish it's the exact same reason... Right it's basically a double top... Right as push is higher rejects so seller step in... Comes up one more time and rejects... So sellers are stepping in again... So where's the stop loss going to be right stop losses right bi orders sellers are good to be resting above this high... So if price is in a bullish sentiment... Right this cup in hand though is very very bullish" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2267s-2306s])
- **Cup and handle: wait for break above retest or buy higher low** "Right you can be looking for the higher low... Right around here... Or if you really want to wait for confirmation... You can wait for the break above retest for continuation higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2310s-2316s])

### Markets Designed to Go Up
- **Markets designed to go up, so bullish setups usually work** "Markets are essentially designed to go up so... A lot of these setups cop in handels... Right flat top breaks they usually go higher because... The overall markets moving higher if we're in a bear market then we're gonna do the opposite" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [2665s-2676s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
### Gap Fill Stats
- **~70% of gaps fill** "I kind of talked about it for the 70%... You know 4 a.m. period usually the gaps are gonna fill" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [3304s-3308s])

### Volume Stats
- **90% of volume = institutional** "90% of volume is made up of institutional trading... I think it's 10%... Retail or maybe even less" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_10_Understanding_Liquidity_transcript.txt [1507s-1519s])
