## PSYCHOLOGY / MENTAL GAME (only topic present)

### Measuring Progress Beyond P&L
- **Create execution metrics** "Create metrics, create a way of grading yourself from an execution, a quality standpoint, and make that somewhat quantitative, right? Grade your performance. That's how you can make progress beyond P&L." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [341s-355s])
- **Define quality execution** "You can define for yourself what quality execution looks like. And you can define for yourself what really crappy shitty execution looks like. And maybe average level of execution." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1073s-1082s])
- **Measure proficiency with routines** "You can measure your proficiency with your routines. The quality of your thinking, the quality of your decision making, right? What factors are you ignoring? What is high quality decision making look like?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1082s-1094s])
- **Attach confidence to execution, not P&L** "You have to attach more of your confidence on your execution and less on your PNL." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1102s-1120s])
- **Consistency is function of feedback quality** "Consistency is a function of how good the feedback is that you get on a daily basis and you're the one who needs to be able to provide that." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2386s-2397s])

### Overconfidence
- **Overconfidence = believing a lie** "Overconfidence is best described as believing a lie. You believe something about yourself, something about your system, something about the market, something about the future that you can't know because you're not a freaking psychic." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [442s-458s])
- **Define the lie to correct it** "Define exactly what it is that you're believing that is a lie, and then you know to correct it yourself back on the more solid ground." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [458s-466s])
- **Overconfidence causes impatience** "You have overconfidence. Overconfidence is what's causing the impatience." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [428s-436s])
- **Predictive vs probabilistic thinking** "Predictive thinking versus probabilistic thinking, risk management versus certainty — I mean these are very clear lines that get crossed when you're overconfident." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1909s-1927s])
- **Feeling less good about winning vs feeling accurate** "I want you to feel accurate about winning. And when you're winning and you're believing a lie which is that you have some idea of some predictive future." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1892s-1909s])
- **Know your weaknesses to prevent overconfidence** "You don't want to get overconfident, ensure that you always know what your weaknesses are." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2791s-2796s])
- **If you don't know your weaknesses, you ARE the weakness** "If you don't know what your weaknesses are, then you are the weakness. Your perspective is the weakness." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2812s-2826s])

### Low Confidence / Fear of Losing
- **Low confidence = believing a negative lie** "Low confidence ironically is very similar. You're kind of believing in a negative lie." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1154s-1166s])
- **Confidence is about perception** "Confidence is all about perception. Your perspective on your skills, the value of your wisdom, experience, knowledge, system, the viability of your system or strategy." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1166s-1178s])
- **Understand causality, don't just manage** "When you have lingering hesitation, your job is not to try to like, just rebuild the confidence. Your job is to understand why your confidence is getting altered in the first place. Understand the causality." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2566s-2583s])
- **Real solutions demand seeking causality** "Real solutions demand a perspective that seeks real solutions, not just a management of it." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2604s-2624s])
- **Loss aversion after blowing account is protective** "Your loss aversion is just protecting you from making the exact same mistake you made before. Right? So you're smart to be a loss aversion after you blow account. Because you don't know why the hell it happened. Right? Understand the causality." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3492s-3514s])

### Discipline
- **Discipline is a muscle of the mind** "Think of discipline as being the muscle of the mind." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [709s-713s])
- **Discipline requires pushing through difficulty** "The main reason you all have your routines fall apart and where discipline becomes problematic is because you give in at times when discipline gets hard to do. Thinking that the signal of it being difficult is a signal to give up." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [756s-774s])
- **Gains come from pushing through** "If you're in the gym trying to get stronger and you keep giving up any time that it begins to get hard, you don't gain any freaking muscle. Right, the gains come from pushing yourself through those last several reps." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [774s-790s])
- **Measure against reasonable, day-in-day-out standards** "You have to measure yourself against what is reasonable on a day in and day out basis. So sometimes the discipline is best built by being accurate about where you are, not delusional about it." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [833s-847s])

### FOMO / Impatience
- **Be precise about what FOMO means** "FOMO can mean hitting delus, it can mean you have a fear of falling behind. It can mean that you're losing confidence. Those are all things that can create the experience of FOMO. It can be the hatred of losing. Be precise about exactly what FOMO means for you. Otherwise, you're not going to actually be able to solve it." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [388s-420s])

### Counterintuitive Nature of Trading
- **Retrain mind on waiting for confirmation** "In those moments when you see price rising and you know that you have to wait for more confirmation, remind yourself that this is what this system requires. This is sound profitable trading. This is what patience means. This is not I'm making a mistake." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [536s-554s])
- **Waiting creates more conviction** "You're actually creating more conviction in the actual trade itself, which should actually cause you to have more confidence, not less." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [566s-572s])

### Tunnel Vision
- **Set alarms to zoom out** "You might need to have an alarm go off every ten minutes, maybe even more regularly than that, that just kind of wakes you up and says, take a step back and make sure you're continuing to zoom out." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [604s-619s])
- **If tunnel vision is from intense emotion, address emotion directly** "If the tunnel vision is being generated by intense emotion, right, anger, fear being the primary ones, then you actually have to address the fear and anger directly. The tunnel vision will not get corrected based on the tactic I described." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [633s-647s])

### Repetitive Mistakes / Emotional Escalation
- **Emotions shut down ability to access new knowledge** "Why you keep making mistakes over and over again is because you're more emotional than you realize. When emotions rise too high, they shut down your ability to control those emotions and access new knowledge. So you just default back into standard operating." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2654s-2680s])
- **Map your pattern of escalation** "You've got to be able to have precision in understanding the escalation of emotions, so you can take action early. When you still retain enough mental functioning to be able to use your mind to control and correct your emotions in real time." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3295s-3313s])
- **If you don't see emotions until too big, it's too late** "You don't see your emotions until they get to be too big. Well, then it's too late." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3313s-3325s])

### Desperation / Blowing Accounts
- **Desperation = extreme weakness in confidence** "High degree of certainty here that you have a problem that I would describe as desperation. It's an extreme example of a weakness in confidence." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1934s-1948s])
- **Closest to gambling problem** "It really is the closest you'll get to a gambling problem. Some might argue that you already have proof of that." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1968s-1982s])
- **Old emotion gets locked away and triggered** "What happens is old emotion gets kind of locked away in your mind. And as soon as maybe you take one loss or you're winning or the markets turn on and you're just oversizing, that emotion gets triggered and overwhelms your ability to apply anything more recently." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2040s-2054s])
- **Most severe issue** "This is the most severe issue that I deal with." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2076s-2082s])

### Self-Sabotage
- **Self-sabotage does not exist** "Self-sabotage does not exist. I understand what you're describing is seemingly you kind of tripping on yourself. That obviously happens, but self-sabotage implies a level of control that you do not have." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3394s-3412s])
- **Nobody willfully acts against own interests** "Nobody is willfully acting against their own interests. There's a self-preservation mechanism that is there for a reason. You're protecting yourself for a reason." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3454s-3468s])

### Pre-Trade Mental Preparation
- **Connect to process goals, not just P&L** "Number one is being connected to your goals for what you're trying to accomplish today. And I say that not just about from a PNL standpoint. You need to be able to be clear about what from a process learning standpoint you are trying to get better at." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1666s-1698s])
- **Weaponize pattern recognition on yourself** "Weaponizing your pattern recognition abilities that you're using within the markets on yourself. Turning that to say, ah, when I am feeling X, Y, and Z, I'm more likely to do Y or A. And more likely to make this mistake." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [1725s-1742s])

### Best Traders / Success Traits
- **Best trader definition** "The best trader is the person with the biggest edge that they will to apply it regularly." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2089s-2107s])
- **Traits of successful newer traders** "They're inquisitive, they're highly passionate. They're self-aware, so they can be incredibly intelligent but they're aware of their own limitations where they can be really dumb and aware of their own limitations." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2119s-2134s])
- **Minimum intelligence required is not very high** "There's a minimum standard of intelligence that is required but it's not very high. I mean you can have average intelligence to be a great trader." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [2134s-2150s])

### Psychology vs System
- **Psychology is not 100% of trading** "Psychological components are a big component to trading. But I think he's misconstruing the nature of decision-making. Decision making is the golf swing of traders. There's a lot of technique associated with that that has nothing to do with your psychology." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3688s-3715s])
- **Knowledge required beyond psychology** "There is a shit ton of knowledge that is required to do a skill that has nothing to do with psychology." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3740s-3750s])

### Recommended Books
- **Trading in the Zone by Mark Douglas** "Trading in the Zone is an essential one. Just from a probabilistic thinking. Many aspects of just the realities of trading." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3518s-3532s])
- **On the Edge by Nate Silver** "On the Edge by Nate Silver, if you have any interest in poker, it's mostly a poker book, but it really does kind of get into a lot of the realities of trading." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3532s-3545s])
- **Fooled by Randomness by Nassim Taleb** "Fooled by Randomness as well. You want more about variance." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_11_Psychology_Q_A.txt [3545s-3550s])

# EXTRACTION FROM: boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk_transcript.txt

### Break-and-Retest
- **Works across asset classes** "the break and retes can work in a multitude of different asset classes, right? Features, right, options, stocks, right, crypto." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [297s-303s])
- **Works on gold trending markets** "the break and retests has been working super solid on gold, just because right, as long as we're in a trending market, right, that's what we really want to be looking for, anything that's trending." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1080s-1086s])
- **Works best in trending markets** "the best break-and-retest setups happen when we're in a trendy market." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [6124s-6130s])
- **Works on futures same as options** "if you're trading the five minute range break and retest for Spine Qs, as I mentioned, it's the exact same. The way price moves on yes is going to be the same as the Spine. The NQ, the same thing as the QQ. So if it works on the Qs, then it's also going to work on the futures as well." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1314s-1333s])
- **Break and retest works best on tech names due to trends** "if you're trading the tech name, there's always some sort of name trending. And right we like trading trends because that's when the break-and-retest works the best. If we're trading in ranges right, it's going to be a lot harder to trade the break-and-retest because we're looking for continuation." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [6103s-6119s])
- **Spy/QQQ more mean reversion than continuation** "Markets are efficient. A lot more reversals. Mean reversions. Bounces, rejections off key levels. Right the markets are efficient. Markets don't try and as much specifically talk with the spine cues. So a lot of your setups are going to be reversal setups. Mean reversions is mentioned. Bounces are support. Redrucks and software systems. Right. You can still trade them. I'm hoping a little bit more difficult." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [6075s-6103s])

### Risk Management - Futures vs Options
- **Futures risk is FIXED** "When it comes to determining risk when you're trading futures, it's fixed. So what does this mean when it's fixed? It means it doesn't, right, it doesn't aggregate with price." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [2872s-2885s])
- **Futures: what you pay is what you get** "what you pay is what you're getting. If you buy it and it goes up, you're making money. If you buy it and it goes down, you're losing one." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [2907s-2913s])
- **Futures: time is not a factor** "No matter what happens, it doesn't matter about time. Right? You're going to make 750 bucks because as I mentioned, it's fixed." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [3099s-3105s])
- **Futures: time does not affect P&L** "The time is not an issue here. Right? Time does not play an effector. Whereas when you're trading options, right? Time is a big factor." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [3135s-3142s])
- **Options: only way to know exact risk is size for zero** "The only way you know exactly how much you're losing when you're trading options is if you size for zero. How much you paid for the contract? Because we can't lose more than a hundred percent of the contract." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [3541s-3558s])
- **Options: 10-20% stop loss rule for day trades** "we only need to risk 10 to 20% on day trades. When you're talking about swing positions, of course, it's going to be a little bit different." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [4046s-4057s])
- **Options: 10-20% is on the premium** "the 10 to 20% is the option contract, the premium." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5144s-5148s])
- **Options: 10-20% rule is the rule** "This is the rule. If I might always go by 10 to 20%, 10 to 20% on the option contract is all what you need." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5229s-5240s])
- **Options: if risking more than 20%, rethink entry** "If you're risking more, maybe rethink about your entry and be a little bit more cautious." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5240s-5245s])
- **Options: target 1:2 risk-reward minimum** "we're looking for a one to two essentially. Negative 20, you want to be looking for 40." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [4431s-4436s])
- **Options: zero-day contracts need wider stops (20-40%)** "If you're trading zero day contracts, those move a lot more volatile. So instead of risking your normal 10 to 20%, maybe you're risking around 20 to 40." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5189s-5201s])
- **Options: swing trades need wider stops (50-100%)** "If you're switching it, maybe you're risking 50 to 100% in the contract. She's because you have to give it a lot more room." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5201s-5208s])
- **Options: use Delta to calculate risk (0.5 rule)** "if you're trading one out the money contract, the normal Delta is going to be 0.5. So essentially, you can just do quick math." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5272s-5282s])
- **Options: Delta 0.5 means $1 underlying move = $0.50 option move** "if the Delta is 0.5, which is on average for one out of the money calls weekly expiration. So let's say the Delta's point five. That means for every dollar move, test the pushes higher or lower. It's going to move 50 cents on the option contract." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5329s-5345s])
- **Options: best way to calculate risk is using Delta** "this in my opinion is the best way to calculate options price is by using Delta, where you don't have to look at the option chart." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5644s-5656s])
- **Options: one out the money weekly = 0.5 Delta rule of thumb** "If it's one of the money contracts, weekly, is which we always trade. It's usually write 50 cents on the underlying. So if we lose a dollar, you risk 50 cents. That's the in my opinion, the best way to do it." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5662s-5674s])
- **Options: Delta changes near expiration** "when you get closer to expiration, the option contracts are going to be more volatile. So these numbers are going to be exaggerated." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5699s-5712s])
- **Options: choose one out the money, week of expiration** "A good rule of thumb, as I mentioned, is just choosing one of the money contracts and choosing week of the expiration." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5804s-5815s])

### Futures vs Options - Trading Differences
- **Futures: speed/momentum don't matter** "futures doesn't care about speed and momentum. Because you're not getting paid for. Right? It can as I mentioned, it can move to your target in two minutes. You're going to get the as if it moved to your target in two hours." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5883s-5899s])
- **Options: speed and momentum matter (gamma)** "if options, if it moves to your target in two minutes, right? It's something called gamma. You're going to get paid more than something that would take longer because options is about speed and momentum." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5899s-5909s])
- **Futures: can take any setup** "When you're trading futures, technically you can take any setup you want because setups that don't care about time, you can trade momentum. You can trade reversals, you can trade bounces, rejections. You can do a lot of thing with features because where you don't have to worry about time." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5916s-5932s])
- **Futures: can hold positions all day** "you can sit in a position all day if you want. Right? Let's say you're trying to short. Right? You can sit in a position technically all day and not really worry about it because we understand how much you're making. You understand how much you're risking." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5937s-5948s])
- **Options: cannot hold short positions all day** "If you're trying to hold a short old day trading options, this is really untradable because it's very hard to hold your options, especially if you have shorter expiration. Just because you're going to get affected quite a bit by these Greeks." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5948s-5961s])
- **Options: focus on momentum** "if you hear us talk about no trade days, there would be a little bit of opportunity, but ultimately there's nothing that is momentum. And that's strictly what we focus on and that's what you should focus on when you're trading options is right speed and momentum." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5966s-5976s])

### Futures Basics
- **Futures vs ETFs: identical price action** "the ES and spy are identical. They're the exact same, at least in price movements. The only difference is they're different asset classes." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [682s-689s])
- **Futures: 24-hour trading** "futures, it's 24 hours a day. The only time it closes is actually between market. It's basically from 5 to 6." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1793s-1804s])
- **Futures: opens Sunday at 6pm EST** "It actually opens up on Sunday at 6 p.m. So you get a little bit of an extra day when you're trading features." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [2028s-2038s])
- **Futures: three major sessions** "the three major sessions, you have the New York. I had said the New York, the London and the Asia Session." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1898s-1904s])
- **Futures: mini vs micro (1/10th)** "the mini is our regular and then our micro is a tenth of a mini." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1511s-1516s])
- **Futures: ES = $50 per point, NQ = $20 per point** "One point of yes is fifty dollars. We're talking about the ES mini contract. One point of the IQ is twenty dollars." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1662s-1671s])
- **Futures: NQ more volatile than ES** "IQ, a lot more volatile, why just because it's worth more. So if you're trading ES, first the IQ, you have to be careful because one contract, if an IQ, is going to move a lot more than ES." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1676s-1684s])
- **Futures: only 4 expirations per year (quarterly)** "we really only have four major features, expirations. And they happen once a quarter." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [2343s-2349s])
- **Futures: expiration codes** "If it ends with each, it expires in March, but it ends with M, expires in June. And if it ends with you, expires in September." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [2482s-2492s])
- **Futures: use continuous contract to avoid gaps** "I like to keep this on, so there's no gap in the chart." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [2694s-2697s])
- **Futures: bracket orders common** "you'll see a lot of people when you trade futures, you have bracket orders. You can already place our take profits and stops because you already know what you're looking for. Usually it's like a one to two." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [3260s-3273s])

### Trade Selection
- **Tech names preferred over spy/QQQ for break and retest** "That's why we trade primarily the tech names because there's always something trending." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [6130s-6135s])
- **QQQ relative strength/weakness is main confluence for tech names** "When we trade the tech names right, one of our main conferences is looking at the QQ relative strength and weakness. So that's one of our main things." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [6240s-6245s])
- **Futures: want more confluences than options** "We're afraid of trading the features. I'd probably want a couple more other conferences as well." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [6245s-6249s])

### Concrete Numbers
- **Options: risk 10-20% of premium on day trades** "10 to 20% on the option contract is all what you need." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5229s-5240s])
- **Options: target 1:2 risk-reward (risk 20%, target 40%)** "we're looking for a one to two essentially. Negative 20, you want to be looking for 40." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [4431s-4436s])
- **Options: zero-day contracts risk 20-40%** "maybe you're risking around 20 to 40." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5193s-5201s])
- **Options: swing trades risk 50-100%** "maybe you're risking 50 to 100% in the contract." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5201s-5208s])
- **Futures: ES = $50/point, NQ = $20/point** "One point of yes is fifty dollars. One point of the IQ is twenty dollars." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [1662s-1671s])
- **Futures: typical 1:2 risk-reward** "Usually it's like a one to two." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [3267s-3273s])
- **Options: Delta 0.5 for one out the money weekly** "if you're trading one out the money contract, the normal Delta is going to be 0.5." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_12_Futures_Managing_Risk.txt [5272s-5282s])

--- Chunk 3 ---
# EXTRACTION FROM NEW TRANSCRIPTS

## Risk Management (Types of Risk)

- **Four types of market risk: market risk, credit risk, liquidity risk, news risk** "There's four specific types of risk in the market. Number one, the first type of risk in the market is market risk. The next risk we have is credit risk. This one is called liquidity risk. The last risk we have is news risk." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [166s-1759s])

- **Market risk: losses from market conditions (interest rates, exchange rates, commodity prices)** "Market risk refers to the potential of losses because of market conditions. This can be interest rates, exchange rates, or commodity prices." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [221s-230s])

- **Credit risk: counterparty defaulting on margin obligations** "Credit risk relates to the possibility of a counterparty to default on a trade or fail to meet their obligation... when you have a margin account and you don't pay back the margin, that is you being able to default on your credit risk." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1009s-1100s])

- **Liquidity risk: insufficient market liquidity to execute at desired prices** "Liquidity risk is the risk of the road and there is insufficient market liquidity to execute trades at desired prices." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1298s-1305s])

- **News risk: FOMC, earnings, news trumps technicals** "News risk is FOMC, earnings... News trumps technicals and is unpredictable." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1753s-1776s])

- **Margin trading: broker can liquidate before stop loss hits** "When you're on margin... the broker will first give you a warning, giving you a margin call... they will liquidate your position before it hits your stop loss." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1208s-1242s])

- **Avoid margin trading, especially with small accounts** "I typically avoid margin trading and I typically tell a lot of traders to avoid margin trading because it's a lot more dangerous in my opinion, especially if you have a smaller account." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [868s-877s])

## Position Sizing

- **Beginners must use constant position size, NOT market-sentiment-based sizing** "A lot of you guys in the early stages need to be typing number two. Number two means constant position size... because you guys have not earned the right to size up based off market sentiment." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1954s-1974s])

- **Market sentiment determines position sizing only for experienced traders** "For me, the market sentiment will determine position sizing... I have, Jack has, Nello has, Hayden has, but you have not yet." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1913s-1992s])

- **Constant position size balances out over time** "Stay with the constant position if the market's doing really well or doing really bad. And what will happen is, overall, over a long period of time, at least it will balance out." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2028s-2037s])

- **Adapt to each market: size down when market shows weakness** "When the market is showing a little bit more weakness, my account, right, I will size down, meaning that I have less risk on the table." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2058s-2069s])

## Stop Loss Discipline

- **Respect stop loss at all times - changing stop loss consistently leads to consistent losing** "If you're consistently changing your stop loss, you will consistently be losing. Over a long period of time, you will lose if you consistently change your stop loss." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2083s-2092s])

- **Use price action (PA) stop losses, not dollar amounts** "I typically do PA stop losses because dollar amounts stop losses will stop you out before the market actually ends up doing anything." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3218s-3222s])

- **PA stop loss definition: wait for candle to close below key level** "PA is price action, it's just you're waiting for the stop, you're waiting for the candle to close below your key level." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3518s-3522s])

## 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **84% reclaim allows re-entry after first stop-out** "The next 84% reclaim I entered in and then I was able to catch this entire trade." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2142s-2149s])

- **84% rule does NOT require a retest** "I thought the 84% rule doesn't have to retest. It doesn't." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3234s-3237s])

- **System designed so missing first trade doesn't prevent capturing the move** "With my system, the way I make my system, there's no chance that I shouldn't be able to enter high probability trades even if I mess up on the first trade." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2208s-2213s])

## Higher Time Frame Application

- **Build thesis on daily, 4-hour, and 1-hour timeframes** "The key things I'm looking for are the daily and four hour specifically for me. It's going to be the daily four hour and one hour where I build my thesis." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2960s-2963s])

- **Higher timeframe thesis: higher highs + higher lows = bullish bias** "The thesis was we have a low here. We have then a higher low, higher low... If we break the low, then Tesla on the higher time frame now becomes a lot more neutral or bearish." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2407s-2424s])

- **Neutral with bullish price action = not opposed to going long** "It's neutral with more bullish price action, right? Because we have a high, high, and we made a brand new high... I would not be opposed to going long on Tesla." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2429s-2439s])

## QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation

- **QQQ relative strength/weakness is primary confluence for tech names** "When we trade the tech names right, one of our main confluences is looking at the QQQ relative strength and weakness." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2509s-2515s])

- **Trade probability lower when QQQ is dropping** "The overall context is lower because once again, QQQ is dropping." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2541s-2547s])

- **Higher probability entry = 84% reclaim + QQQ at key support** "This for me was a higher probability entry... because not only is this the 84% rule meaning that Tesla's reclaiming, but QQQ is now at a key level of support holding above their support." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2587s-2634s])

- **Scale 80% at high of day when QQQ at risk of breaking support** "I scale 80% out of high of day, because I know that though I took the risk, QQQ if it breaks below this level, the momentum on Tesla may die." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2684s-2689s])

- **Don't need both QQQ and SPY - too much information** "QQQ and SPY gives me the same thing, NQ and ES would... I don't want to add an extra step when I just don't need to." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3146s-3160s])

## Trade Selection

- **Trade same tickers daily, select based on relative strength at open** "I trade the same ticker every day. I just select them based off relative strength when the market actually opens." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3004s-3008s])

- **Tech names (Tesla, NVDA, AMD, Apple) have higher correlation with QQQ** "I trade tech stocks in general because they have higher correlation with QQQ. And therefore I don't have to look at much information overall in the markets." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2926s-2942s])

- **Pick one strategy, backtest it, find which works in different markets** "You pick one and then you back test that one. And then out of the three that you have, you can then look at which one has the highest probability win rate in different markets. And then you trade that in that market." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3101s-3120s])

## Exits / Scaling

- **Scale 80% at high of day when market context is uncertain** "I scaled 80% today because the probability with QQQ when it dropped below that 631 level, it became a lot lower. So I wanted to make sure that I'm coming out with profit." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3273s-3284s])

- **Scaling percentage adapts to market cycle** "This whole week, I've been scaling anywhere from 50 to 80% at high of day. However, if you go back to my trade reviews from July and June... I was only scaling out 25 or 30% at high of day, sometimes even less." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3293s-3310s])

- **Different market cycles require different scaling approaches** "It's the different market cycles that you need to understand." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3310s-3314s])

## Concrete Numbers

- **Typical position size: 100-150 contracts, max 200** "I'll usually enter in like 50 and then I'll enter in 50 again. So that's 100. And then if I really like the setup, I'll enter in another maybe 50 to 50 maximum contract so far as 200. But typically, I'll do like 100 to 150." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2975s-2995s])

- **Win rate is usually higher than 50%** "My win rate has never been 50%, it's usually almost always higher than 50%." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3260s-3267s])

- **Risk $2-3k on lower probability trades, more on higher probability** "You're risking around $2 to $3,000 here, maybe potentially even three to $4,000 for that move to the upside, which is worth the risk." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2654s-2665s])

## Options Specifics

- **Trade weekly options (Friday expiry)** "In terms of expiry day, I typically do weekly, right? So weekly expires. Meaning the expiry on Friday." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1708s-1717s])

- **Require at least 1,000 volume on option contracts** "For option contracts on top of this, I like to have at least a thousand volume." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1614s-1622s])

- **If can't afford one OTM, look for closest whole psychological number with 1,000+ volume** "If you can afford it, then I would be looking for something that has over a thousand volume and most likely the next closest whole psychological number." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [1652s-1666s])

- **One OTM = first contract out of the money** "Out of the money is simply just the one, the first contract out of the money. So let's say a stock price is 400, out of the money would be 401." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3082s-3090s])

- **R:R calculation on options is difficult in real-time - use general rule of 2:1** "It's almost impossible for you to be doing the calculations of where 2:1 is on your option contract... It's the general rule that we want 2:1. So when you look at your option contracts, let's say it's a dollar, you want the option contract to obviously go up to $1.50, $2." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3440s-3461s])

- **To roll positions: close current profitable position, open new one** "The best way is to honestly just close the current position if you're in profit and then just open the next one. That's the easiest way for a lot of new traders." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3333s-3342s])

## Break and Retest

- **Immediate retest with no displacement: depends on higher timeframe strength** "If it's an immediate retest, you want to look for more specifically is the higher time frame showing you relative strength or weakness. If the higher time frame is really good, you see relative strength, the market's moving up... sometimes you have to take an immediate retest." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3352s-3371s])

- **When market not showing strength, want more displacement and A+ opportunities** "If the market's not showing as much strength and it's just slowly kind of moving, then yeah, then you want to take more displacement trades and you want to make it as much A+ opportunity as possible." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3371s-3382s])

## Time of Day

- **One-hour candle before open: don't need to wait for close, look for bullish/bearish bias** "You don't have to wait for that one hour candle to close for that. You're just looking for if the one hour coming into the day is bullish or not, you don't have to wait for the one hour close on the 9 a.m. candle." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3054s-3070s])

- **For more confluence, look at 8 a.m. candle too** "If you want more confluence, look at the 8 a.m. candle. If the 8 a.m. candle is also bullish and the last two candles are bullish, then you're obviously going to be looking for more of a bullish scenario coming into the day." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3070s-3077s])

## Swing Trading

- **Swing trade on 4-hour and daily timeframe, not monthly** "I usually typically swing trade the four hour and the daily time frame, daily time frame max. I don't swing trade the monthly, it's too much." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3386s-3396s])

- **Good entry on 4-hour/daily will ride monthly anyway** "I look at the four hour and if I can get a good entry in the four hour slash daily, I'll be able to ride the monthly time frame anyways." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3396s-3400s])

## Psychology / Trading Philosophy

- **Lazy traders become good because they simplify** "One of my mentors used to say is, lazy people will find the best way to... usually become very good traders because they try to simplify everything." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [3166s-3178s])

- **Real trades are not picture-perfect like YouTube examples** "On my YouTube videos, a lot of trades that I show are picture perfect. But in reality, the trades that you guys will be taking on a consistent basis are trades like these." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_13_Trameframe_Psychology.txt [2792s-2806s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **Small account targeting** "usually the best setups, and the ideal situation for the trades is going to be, like, some form of displacement, and then, like, a pullback... these are ideal for small accounts, because, like, at least you have a high day target with decent risk reward" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1754s-1778s])

- **Clear risk/target structure** "you have a clear risk and you have a clear target. Usually, you'll have decent risk reward" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1801s-1808s])

- **Trade works or doesn't** "most of the time, like honestly, you'll see most of the time the trade either works out right away, or it doesn't at all" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2840s-2849s])

- **No adding contracts** "there's really no reason to add contracts ever because if it bounces right in you buy in... it's going to either work it right away or it's not at all... there's never really a point to add more contracts" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3927s-3963s])

- **Invalidation point** "if it closes below that, there's no, that's the invalidation point, right? You have the invalidation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3947s-3953s])

- **Scaling out vs scaling in** "scaling out is different because you can hold onto your winners longer, you can get those exponential gains" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3973s-3988s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **Named as "one candle rule"** "I think the one candle rule is really good for when you're like, you know, you can't find entry just have that first candle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5524s-5528s])

## 4. Order Blocks

**NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

## 5. Key Levels

- **Pre-market setup** "I kind of set up the pre-market where like I won't trade in this area, I'll trade above here below here" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5641s-5645s])

- **PDH/PVL good breaks** "previous day high and previous day low are good breaks" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5621s-5627s])

- **Levels for ORB setups** "it's good for your ORB setups and you have to draw it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5646s-5650s])

- **Too many levels = noise** "having the extra lines kind of just adds a little bit of noise" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5641s-5645s])

- **A+ trade checklist** "the A plus trade checklist is my favorite part for sure... that's one of the best ones" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5650s-5655s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **Best trading window** "trades from 930 to 11, that's usually, that's the better I would say time to trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3489s-3494s])

- **Later session clarity** "Recently you see more like clarity and direction later into the session" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3494s-3498s])

- **Not ideal after 11** "generally speaking though, it's not ideal to trade after this" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3498s-3503s])

- **Opening drive plays** "your opening range breakouts... these won't necessarily work later into the session, because they're like opening drive plays" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3538s-3544s])

- **Need data for later trades** "if you go in back test and you work on key levels and you see trades happening later in the day, then be my guest, right? Just make sure you have the data to prove that you can trade after this" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3550s-3564s])

- **Friday/end of week** "end of the week, if it's Friday, you don't have to trade zeros, or you can buy next week, they're just going to be a little bit pricier, get a move a little bit slower" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4042s-4051s])

- **0DTE on trending days** "if there's a really super big trending day, like the 0DTE trades, man, like why not? Like you can get cheap contracts, you can scale more" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4056s-4067s])

- **Avoid 0DTE on choppy days** "if it's choppy day and like QQQ's in a range and SPY's in a range and nothing's really breaking out, then like maybe you don't want to take zeros" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4074s-4078s])

## 7. Exits

- **Small account: close whole position** "anywhere from one to two, even three contracts, you probably don't want to scale anything. You probably just can't close those to the whole position" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1623s-1631s])

- **Target 2R for small accounts** "when you're trading on a small account, you do have to go for these like 2R setups" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1435s-1441s])

- **Below 1.5R = breakeven** "if your risk reward is anywhere below this, on average, below 1.5R... you're probably going to be breakeven" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1454s-1462s])

- **Sell into strength for options** "with call options you sell into green candles that's for the ideal you're going to get the most juice out of them. We always say that always sell into strength" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4362s-4373s])

- **Trailing stops for stocks/futures** "stocks futures for sure trailing stop is decent it's really good" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4403s-4410s])

- **Trailing stops for options after scaling** "trailing stops are good for if you already taken off like a position... you already scaled out and then yeah like maybe if it breaks down below like if you break structure maybe you have like a higher here or something" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4373s-4392s])

- **P&L watching causes early exits** "I do genuinely think if you just see green and you're like let me just take profit like that is the main thing... 95% of it is probably looking at your P&L, seeing it's in the green closing it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4135s-4162s])

- **Turn off P&L** "if the trade is working out, then like do not look at your P&L" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4119s-4127s])

- **Think long term** "think about a long term. Like even your intraday trades to be green on the weeks and months like not just today" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4176s-4187s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **Morning parameter setup** "ideally in the morning, you're going to be like, okay, if AMD reclaims this, I'm going to jump to the lower time frames. I'm going to look for trades" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3090s-3095s])

- **Set parameters, avoid noise** "you want to set like parameters for yourself. So you don't really need higher time frames charted by your side" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3121s-3125s])

- **Trade above/below levels** "I'm going to trade X above Y level. I'm going to trade AMD above 258. And I'm not going to trade it's in here" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3125s-3131s])

- **Multi-timeframe structure** "if you make higher highs or higher lows you know then it makes sense... if we break these lows and close below these lows or start making higher lows... the time frame should kind of mesh" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4940s-4962s])

- **Daily trend context** "you look at your daily, daily looks solid, super super bullish" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4979s-4984s])

- **Personal preference: higher timeframe trend** "I like just overall higher time frame trend, I like trading with the trend more, I like all the time frames aligning like breakouts on stocks" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4689s-4706s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **Max 3 trades per day** "three trades per day, think of a max" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2989s-2994s])

- **1-2 trades ideal** "if I want it to risk more, is it why say one or two trades per day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2994s-2998s])

- **2-3 good setups per day** "I would say like, two good setups a day, like anywhere from, like, two to three, maybe" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3601s-3606s])

- **Sometimes zero setups** "I would say anywhere from like one to three days, sometimes there's zero, right? Sometimes there's none" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3623s-3628s])

- **Focus on one name** "you should be focusing on like one name, one to go for the most part" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3641s-3647s])

- **3 trades is max max** "three trades is like, Max Max, right? It's like Max" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3674s-3680s])

- **5+ trades = technical issue** "if you see more than, like, really good setups, then like, I'd say five, then like, it could probably something wrong with technicals" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3690s-3700s])

- **Beginners: 1 trade/day or 3/week** "honestly, beginners need to trade once a day instead of maybe one of three trades per week. Yeah, I agree" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2578s-2583s])

- **A+ setups rare** "the A plus setups, like, I would say anywhere from like one to three days, sometimes there's zero" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [3623s-3628s])

- **A+ trade checklist** "the A plus trade checklist is my favorite part for sure... that's one of the best ones" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5650s-5655s])

- **Entry model flexibility** "there's good scenarios for every single one... I don't really just use one and I don't think it's good... you have to kind of adapt and use like a strategy" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5451s-5476s])

- **Criteria for entries** "you can build like some sort of candle formation maybe you want to see three up close candles maybe on the retest candle you you want to see no closures right below this" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [5533s-5545s])

- **System definition** "It is essentially a built up, like, group of, you know, risk management, your, your playbook, right? The trades that you're going to take your entry models, it is your psychology, it's, it's everything combined. That is, that is your system" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [318s-333s])

- **No edge if deviating** "you have no edge if you, if you deviate from your plan" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [361s-362s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Risk per trade: 1-2% of account** "no more than one to two percent of your trading account" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [829s-836s])

- **$10,000 account: $10-20 risk** "a $10,000 account, you should be risking around $10 to $20 per trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [853s-859s])

- **Options stop loss: 10-20% of contract cost** "it's around 10 to 20% stop loss on the contracts you buy. So, if your contract costs at $1, then ideally your stop loss would be like 10 cents to 20 cents on the contract" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1959s-1972s])

- **Target 2:1 risk reward** "ideally, you probably want to be in this area anywhere around 2 to 1. So, you're risking about $100, so make about $200" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1926s-1939s])

- **50-60% win rate at 2:1 is ideal** "ideally, I believe anyways, is, like, likely, you know, 50 to 60 percent win rate at like a two to one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1863s-1870s])

- **40-50% win rate still profitable at 2:1** "you can even be in 40 to 50 percent though, and still be in profit" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [1871s-1877s])

- **Daily max drawdown: 3-5%** "on the average trading day... it should be around to 3%, I think that is ideal 3 to 5, 3 to 5%" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2302s-2320s])

- **Weekly max drawdown: 10%** "maybe for weekly, you have like 10" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2327s-2332s])

- **10% daily is too high** "10% is a little bit high to be honest" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [2320s-2326s])

- **Percentage recovery math** "if you take a trade or if you take any trade and you lose 10% of your account size, you need to make 11% to make that money back to get to break even. If you lose 20% you have to now win 25% back to get back to break even" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [494s-509s])

- **80% drawdown = 400% recovery** "a minus 80% drawdown in your account. You need to now make 400% on that account back to just get back to break even" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [520s-531s])

- **$1,000 to $2,000 at 2:1 = 50 trades** "to take $1,000 to $2,000, using a two-to-one risk reward model, it would take you 50 trades" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [890s-897s])

- **1-2% risk per trade for average setups** "for average trades I want to say like one to two percent is good" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4263s-4268s])

- **3% risk very rare** "very rarely that you should risk three percent per trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [4290s-4296s])

- **Under $1,000: paper trade/prop firm** "if you are under $1,000, likely paper trading, prop firms, back testing is going to be the go to" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [713s-721s])

- **$1,000-$10,000: can trade** "anywhere between a thousand and 10,000, you can trade stocks, you can trade options, you can trade futures" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_14_How_To_Grow_A_Small_Account.txt [744s-756s])

# EXTRACTION: boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **B&R as entry model, HTF breakouts as directional bias** "I trade the break in retest every entry that I have is within the break in retest... based off the higher time frames we're usually looking for some sort of continuation setup so whenever we have a higher time frame breakout that usually the mark that means the market's trending so then based off that information then we can go into the lower time frames to look for the break in retest entry model" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3501s-3531s])

- **B&R vs reversal trades: B&R wins because momentum** "the reason the break and retest overpowers the reversal trades is because you're trading momentum so you're done the day within you know 30 minutes to an hour whereas sometimes the reversal trade may work but it may consolidate for 40 minutes before making its way back up into high of day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3820s-3835s])

- **Displacement needed depends on HTF context** "if on the higher time frame if there is a lot of consolidation then I would look for more displacement however if higher time frame the is very very strong or bullish in the overall markets bullish then I'm not going to look for as much displacement because the confluence there would be based off the higher time frame" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [4751s-4775s])

- **B&R works less in consolidation/range days** "if the higher time frames is consolidating then you do usually see right usually the break in retest doesn't work as well just because the market's consolidating" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3542s-3546s])

- **Range day definition: wait for first 5 minutes, then break of range** "a range day you typically um want to wait for the first five minutes and then after the first five minutes right you want it to either break to the upside or to the downside that would be breaking out of the range that's typically where I would want to trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3558s-3572s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **One-minute first candle rule used when market is very aggressive** "if the market's very aggressive I'll use a one candle first candle I'll use the first minute first candle one candle rule but that's if the market's very aggressive right typically I'm gonna wait for the five minutes especially in the last like month or two like we're gonna have to wait for the five minutes but if the market's really aggressive then yeah one minute it does work right" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2530s-2547s])

- **Five-minute opening range model: entry timing depends on day type** "if it if the day's more choppy so if the day's a little bit more choppy then I will wait for a little bit more confirmation if the day's has momentum then I won't wait for as much confirmation... typically if the if it's a very strong day I'll only wait for the one minute candle compared to if it's a little bit more of a week or day or we're consolidating where I would have to wait for the five" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2986s-3025s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **84% rule covered in depth on Day 5** "if you go back into the boot camp recordings... day number five, every setup... I talk about the 84% roll in depth at the very end... I explain all the scenarios right how to manage 84% roll is it on the reclaim, is it on the breakout, right? Are you going to sell some at high-day after the reclaim? So there's a lot of different rules that you can go that you can use for the 84% roll" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1391s-1436s])

- **84% rule currently working better than other setups** "right now in this market, 84% rule is working a lot better" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [529s-533s])

- **Backtest one setup at a time to find what works in different conditions** "when you're back testing, you want to back test one set up at a time... go one set up at a time, see which one works in different market conditions because there are different setups that work better in different market conditions" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [491s-529s])

## 4. Order Blocks

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels

- **Thesis for the day: previous day high/low + pre-market reactions** "some of the key levels I will look for coming into the day is just your previous day high and previous day low... we're just going to be looking for the reactions at least in the pre-market... the reactions that we have, number one, all for our higher timeframe key levels, which will be our previous day highs in previous day lows" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [908s-929s])

- **Strong break above PDH = look for continuation** "if we have, right, a strong break above your previous day highs, then we'll usually be looking for continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [929s-934s])

- **Multiple confluences = higher probability** "if you have a pre-vest day high retest along with a five minute opening range retest that usually just makes it higher probability because now there's two conflances instead of one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3302s-3311s])

- **Bonus class on drawing key levels coming** "in the bonus section... 40 minutes of me going through drawing key levels going through you know that the the A plus set ups right like 40 minutes of literally just me talking about A plus set ups and how to create A plus set ups and showing you examples of it while drawing out key levels as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2412s-2427s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **Red folder news: trade first 30 min to 1 hour, then stop** "when there is red folder news for me, typically what I'll do is unless there's a really good setup that'll set up within the first 30 minutes to an hour. I just won't trade... if within the first 30 minutes to an hour, if something doesn't set up, I'm out" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1470s-1487s])

- **Red folder days: lower probability until 2pm volume** "I know it's going to be a little bit lower probability until volume kicks in at around 2pm" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1496s-1499s])

- **FOMC: trade first 30 min to 1 hour, then avoid** "For example, let's say it's FOMC. Right? I simply will trade for the first 30 minutes to an hour. And then I'll come off. And if there's no setup, right? I will be much more picky trading that 30 minutes to an hour than a normal day as well. Or I'll typically just avoid it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1499s-1509s])

- **Before trading live for others: would genuinely not trade red folder days** "before I have to trade live in front of thousands of you guys in the accelerator and stuff, right? I would genuinely just not trade that day. I wouldn't even come to the laptop or computer that day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1514s-1522s])

- **Christmas rally: typically November or December, most profitable quarter** "there's gonna be a Christmas rally... that usually happens either in November or before Christmas and that's the like for winter quarter four that's when I typically make the most money for my quarter is when that Christmas rally happens" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2555s-2571s])

- **Christmas rally timing unpredictable** "you can never predict when it's going to start typically it will start after the FOMC event in November that we have that we're going to have but once again there's no way to predict it sometimes it you'll rally up before sometimes you'll rally after" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2920s-2937s])

## 7. Exits

- **Do NOT exit early - let it hit target or stop** "don't intervene there's no reason for you to exit early there is absolutely no reason for you to exit early when you enter into a trade you let it hit your profit target or you let it hit your stop loss" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2605s-2615s])

- **Move stop to breakeven only when market structure says to** "I move stop loss to break even only if the market structure tells me to move at two break even" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [4450s-4454s])

- **With 2 contracts: scale 1 at HOD, 1 for HTF key levels** "if you're trading wanted to contracts get one is hard but let's just say two get out a high of day for one and then your second one go for higher time frame key levels don't scale anything until higher time frame key levels don't scale on pops just go look for the higher time frame key levels" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2871s-2883s])

- **Scaling to HTF levels drops win rate but increases R:R** "this will make your risk to reward much higher right but it'll drop your winery as well right so your risk to reward will go up to like four are right in general but your winery may go from sixty seventy percent to fifty percent or maybe even forty eight somewhere in that forty five to fifty percent range" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2883s-2901s])

- **With 1 contract: take fewer trades, aim for at least 2R** "for one contract in consistency... you want to take trades that are a little bit so for example something like this right take trades that were high-of-day actually does give you a solid risk to reward... I would want at least a 2R 2 high-of-day which means you're gonna take less trades selectively obviously right you're going to take less trades overall" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3426s-3474s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **HTF patterns: flat top breaks, cup and handles, trend continuations** "the higher timeframes patterns that I look for... flat top breaks, right, cup and handles, right, trend continuations. Those are some of the best higher timeframes patterns that we'll look for" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [841s-850s])

- **Trending HTF = hold for HTF objectives; ranging HTF = scalp** "if the market is trending on the overall markets, like spine cues, then we can hold for those higher timeframes objectives. If we're just ranging on let's say the 15 or one hour chart, then we're probably looking for a scalp and we can sell more of our position and not leave as much trailers" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [855s-876s])

- **HTF tells you direction, then look for low-risk entry on LTF** "if the higher time frame is showing a really strong move to the upside and then a flat top and then you know pre-market let's say we're breaking about that flat top that's a really good example of okay we're gonna go long today we just have to look for low you know low risk trade right" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3930s-3947s])

- **HTF understanding key to bigger moves** "understanding the higher timeframes which was really key because I realized all the bigger moves always happen on the higher timeframes so if you can focus in and understand what the higher timeframes is doing then it's going to be a lot easier to trade on the lower timeframes" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3241s-3256s])

- **HTF exercise: identify bullish/bearish in 20 seconds** "go exercise on any time frame if you look at it right can you identify if it's bullish and bearish like within the first like five seconds right... within the first 20 seconds right without really overthinking it... can you identify if that stock is bullish or bearish and where the where the trend should continue" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3956s-3992s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **80% mechanical, 20% discretion = QQQ correlation** "80% of our strategy is very mechanical, right, where technically a robot could technically come in and trade the way we trade. However, the 20% discretion we have is a correlation between KQ and that's what we need to get better at" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [710s-720s])

- **QQQ correlation: if QQQ bearish but stock shows relative strength at support, that's a good setup** "if KQ is bearish, for example, but Tesla is showing a lot of relative strength, if KQs, I know is coming down into a level of support, as long as KQs can hold a level of support and buyers, I see stepping in and Tesla's retesting, that's a good setup for me to take, right? Because that even if KQs bounces a little bit, if Tesla has so much relative strength, it'll help Tesla" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [728s-748s])

- **Backtest QQQ confirmation separately** "if you're back testing the 5 and high and low on Tesla, right, then you back test that and then you add in KQ confirmation for the second back test. So that would be too back test in a row" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [684s-695s])

- **A+ setups bonus class coming** "we have more examples of A plus setups that will be coming out for bonus for you guys as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [439s-445s])

- **Pick tickers based on HTF + news catalysts** "I'm looking at higher timeframe and if there was a news event. So for example, if Apple had news or Apple had earnings the other day and I was looking for that dip in rip... that was the name that I was looking at based off of the news event and more so the catalyst behind it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1266s-1284s])

- **Stick to stocks you know their personality** "I like trading those because I have the most personality like I understand their personality I understand how they move so I have even more of a deeper edge with them" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [4196s-4204s])

- **Only trade other names if they show extreme momentum** "I'll trade them once in a while but I'll only trade them if they're showing so much momentum or it's like all right this one just makes sense to trade based off the momentum but if they're not doing anything on the higher time frame then I'm not going to be trading them" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [4208s-4216s])

- **Start with SPY/QQQ or momentum tech names - personality dependent** "it depends on your personality right so that one's a personality question... some people like to come in every day and just trade the one ticker they trade right they just like the personality of spy they like the personality of cues... but for some people that's not something they do right some people like for example me I like to trade momentum and because if I'm trading momentum some days spy and cue cues will chop around all day and Tesla will have a really nice move right and on those days obviously I want to trade Tesla" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [1896s-1927s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Tony's daily P&L range: $20k-$80k** "anywhere from 20 to 80 thousand dollars a day... is how much my contract position sizes" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [3481s-3495s])

- **Tony's position size: $15k-$25k per trade on Tesla** "for me, right? 15 to like 25 k position size... for Tesla, let's say the contracts are about a thousand dollars per contract, then I can get around anywhere between right 15 to 25" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [792s-808s])

- **With 2 contracts scaling to HTF: win rate drops to 45-50%, R:R goes to 4:1** "your risk to reward will go up to like four are right in general but your winery may go from sixty seventy percent to fifty percent or maybe even forty eight somewhere in that forty five to fifty percent range" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [2888s-2901s])

- **Profit distribution: 80-90% to long-term account, 10-20% to pay self** "all the money that I make from day trading I typically try to take 80 to 90% of it and put into a long term account... I would literally just transfer from my I'd be care day trading account to my long term account or my swing trading account and it'll just move over and then I'll take the other 10% or 20% profit depending on how much I took out and I will just pay myself with that" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_15_Conclusion_Final_Q_A.txt [4397s-4438s])

# EXTRACTION FROM boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Entry execution (bid/ask)** "I buy the ask and I sell the bid this way I get in and out of trades as fast as possible" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [541s-547s])
- **Why buy ask/sell bid** "I need to be very quick with my trades... I need to enter in as I see the break and retest forming" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [490s-496s])
- **Limit order risk on break-and-retest** "you had a limit order... the price actually does touch a hundred dollars but it quickly moves back up into your profit target and you had an order... but you didn't get filled" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [835s-861s])
- **Reason for missed fills** "the reason this is happening to you is because you're trying to enter in at the best price possible" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [882s-888s])
- **Mindset on entry price** "I'm not trying to enter in on the best price possible. I'm just trying to enter in because I know if I can enter into this name... I'll still make enough profit where it makes sense for me to enter in quicker" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [889s-908s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Daily watchlist names** "I mark out spy, QQQ, Apple, Tesla, and Nvidia and AMD... those are the names that I mark out every single day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4812s-4819s])
- **Fallback names** "if none of those names have an opportunity then I'll look for something else based off the higher time frame that I can potentially trade like Palantir or any other name that's really showing that higher time frame opportunity" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4824s-4835s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Days to avoid - Quadwitching** "Quadwitching is when all four financial contracts are expiring... occurs quarterly... typically on a Friday... I typically tend to avoid quadwitching days unless I see a very very high probability setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4562s-4605s])
- **Days to avoid - J-Pow/FOMC days** "Jerome Powell days... the market typically waits for his speeches... you will tend to see chop or range before two pm... you don't want to trade before then" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4607s-4630s])
- **FOMC day behavior** "the market doing this until two pm or it's just kind of in range and range and range and then I'll do a big move to one side and then it'll correct the move to the other side" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4634s-4646s])
- **FOMC timing** "two pm hit you'll see this kind of big move from two pm to around three 30 pm and then the market will cool down again for the next day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4646s-4657s])
- **Days to avoid - Earnings days** "days that the stock you trade reports earnings... the reason you want to avoid that is because the market's typically just waiting for the earnings to come out" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4668s-4689s])
- **Earnings example** "Tesla's going to report earnings this week... going into tomorrow and Wednesday most likely not going to trade Tesla right it's going to be hands off for me until the earnings happened" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4480s-4492s])
- **Where to find event schedule** "every single week we have the weekly outlook and we talk about if there's earnings for specific names, if there's FOMC we show you guys the full schedule. So just go look at the weekly outlook that we post in the accelerator" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4715s-4728s])

## 7. Exits
- **Limit order for exits at key levels** "if I see a key level here on Tesla... I bought at $95 and there's a key level at 100... you can put a limit order here... when Tesla does end up hitting this name... you'll get filled at the highest price when it does touch that level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1418s-1436s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **HTF for fallback names** "if none of those names have an opportunity then I'll look for something else based off the higher time frame that I can potentially trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4824s-4829s])

## 9. Trade Selection

### QQQ Alignment
- **QQQ holds tech** "QQQ holds the top 100 technology companies in the US" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1790s-1801s])
- **QQQ importance in system** "the QQQ plays a very big part in my system" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1817s-1822s])
- **Why QQQ matters - relative strength/weakness** "if the spy is moving to the upside and QQQ is moving to the upside and let's say I want to trade apple... but apple is moving to the downside... it means apples weak right it means the apple has relative weakness" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1866s-1908s])
- **Higher probability with alignment** "if apples continuously showing relative weakness and let's just say QQQ starts showing a little bit of consolidation or it breaks market structure and apple is retesting something... that means that this apple entry is going to be a much higher probability entry" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1912s-1927s])
