## ADDITIONAL TOPICS COVERED (not in standard extraction list but present)

### Order Types (Market vs Limit)
- **Market order definition** "a market order... is an instantious way to buy a trade... you will get filled instantly" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1177s-1198s])
- **Market order fills on ask/bid** "the market order will do the work for you and it will fill you on the ask price when you're looking to buy the trade and it will sell on the bid price when you're looking to sell" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1206s-1219s])
- **Market order for new traders** "for a newer trader I think market orders are just so simple... I used to recommend limit orders but I kind of made it complicated for people and then I realize like most people would just be better off doing market orders" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1231s-1247s])
- **Market order tradeoff** "when you do a market order you're not going to get the best prices... however you will be getting filled" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1265s-1277s])
- **Limit order definition** "a limit order... is going to give you the best buy and sell price on a stock... when you're looking to buy a limit... you're going to get filled on the bid" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1294s-1314s])
- **Limit order risk** "the problem with the limit order is that sometimes when a stock is moving too quickly it won't fill you therefore you may miss an entire move" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1346s-1355s])
- **Limit order benefit** "if the stock does come back down and then move back up... your limit order will get you the best entry for that move up so you'll have technically the best risk to reward" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1364s-1370s])
- **Market order for small accounts concern** "for some people that have smaller accounts... market order may not be the best way to enter in... if you're trading stuff like Apple or names that have less spread then market order is definitely the way to go" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1513s-1535s])

### Bid/Ask Mechanics
- **Bid definition** "the bid price is the amount where buyers are willing to purchase a trade... the bid is showing the strength and demand in a stock" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [420s-435s])
- **Ask definition** "the ask price... is the amount where sellers are willing to sell a trade and this will show more weakness and supply" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [435s-440s])
- **Bid/Ask spread** "the bid and ask spread is the difference between the bid and ask price" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [676s-682s])
- **Stock price as midpoint** "the stock price represents the midpoint of the ask and the bid" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [693s-701s])
- **Smaller spread is key** "having the smallest bid and ask spread is key because the smaller the bid and ask spread is the less likelihood of you losing more potential money on that trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1082s-1092s])

### Options Basics
- **Option definition** "options are contracts that give the buyer the right but not the obligation to buy or sell the underlying asset at a pre-determined price on a specified date" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2595s-2607s])
- **Calls vs Puts** "the call is essentially looking for the price to move to the upside... with the puts this gives the buyer the right to sell the underlying" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2753s-2788s])
- **One contract = 100 shares** "every one option contract controls 100 shares of the underlying stock" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2245s-2255s])
- **Leverage via options** "we use leverage for options themselves... every one option contract controls 100 shares... technically we are using leverage but we're using leverage with the actual financial instrument that we're trading and not typically with our actual capital" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2216s-2268s])
- **Options risk** "you can blow your entire account... it is a lot harder to blow an account with shares... whereas if I bought Tesla a hundred thousand dollar option position expiring on Friday and Tesla doesn't make the move that I needed to make... I just lost the full hundred thousand because the option contract will expire at zero" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3528s-3626s])
- **Options purpose - hedging** "the main purpose of options is to hedge against potential losses... it was invented as a way for insurance or to hedge positions" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3345s-3372s])
- **How we use options** "what we're doing is we're speculating on the direction of the underlying asset" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3474s-3481s])

### Options Greeks
- **Only two Greeks needed** "the way I trade specifically there's only two Greeks that I need to know... Delta and Theta" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3699s-3711s])
- **Delta definition** "Delta is what measures the price movement in options with the one dollar change in stock price" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3738s-3750s])
- **Delta example** "if you have a Delta of 0.5... for every one dollar move in the underlying price... the option contract will move 50 cents" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3760s-3788s])
- **Theta definition** "theta is the measurement in change in price of options as the time passes... theta is always going to kill your option contract... it will always kill it and it doesn't matter if you're trading calls or puts" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4145s-4239s])
- **Gamma definition** "gamma is what measures the rate of change in Delta itself" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3910s-3928s])
- **Vega definition** "Vega is the measurement of the option price sensitivity to implied volatility or IV" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4044s-4052s])
- **Greeks not used** "I've never used them in my life I've never even looked at them and I don't even have them on my chart" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [3719s-3726s])

### Implied Volatility (IV)
- **IV definition** "implied volatility is the measure of expected volatility of the underlying stock over the life of the option contract" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4265s-4275s])
- **IV spikes = avoid buying** "every time IV spikes we do not want to buy the contracts... when the IV spikes the contract price is going to spike as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4348s-4359s])
- **IV crush explained** "if this drops... the IV is going to get crushed... if the IV drops... the option contract where it may have been nine dollars here... may only be worth a dollar 50... that's because the IV is crushing your profits" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4438s-4469s])
- **IV causes of spikes** "the spikes in IV are essentially just either news events... typically it's going to be news events earnings FOMC" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4325s-4343s])

### Slippage
- **Slippage definition** "slippage is essentially where the execution price of trade slips from the expected price of the trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1971s-1978s])
- **Slippage example** "you click sell on a hundred and ten dollars... and you only see seven dollars in your account... your market order slipped below the bid into 107s" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [1998s-2113s])
- **Slippage in illiquid names** "mid cap names typically will show you a little bit more slippage" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2160s-2164s])

### TradingView Setup
- **Layout** "I typically have QQQ on the right hand side here... and then I have the main ticker that I'm trading... on my top screen I have another two of these... I'll typically have another one right so two of these and then I'll have two more up here and that'll be like Nvidia and Apple or Nvidia and Tesla" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4778s-4807s])
- **Data bundle** "I have the New York Stock Exchange the Nasdaq the NYSE ARCA Nasdaq kids OTC and the US stock markets bundle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [4841s-4852s])

### Market Makers
- **Market maker role** "a market makers responsibility is for a stock to not just barcode all day... they will utilize their own money or their institution or their firms money and they will buy the stock aggressively to either break it above a specific key level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2372s-2395s])
- **Market maker not bad** "a market maker is not necessarily bad... the reason some people think a market maker can typically be bad is because you'll see these fake out moves... in reality that's just where the liquidity was sitting so their job was literally just to get you to the next liquidity pool" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2420s-2442s])

### Leverage Warning
- **No leverage recommendation** "I personally do not recommend traders to trade with leverage just because especially in the beginning stage... if you lose you're gonna lose more money than you already have in your account" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2200s-2210s])
- **Slippery slope** "when you start using leverage on top of leveraging options it's just a very slippery slope that you don't want to go down" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_2_Introduction_To_Trading.txt [2269s-2273s])

### GROUP 6
Files: boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis_transcript.txt

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Bodies vs Wicks, Displacement)
- **Body close for break of structure**: "I personally like looking for the body. So if the body closes underneath, then that's essentially going to validate it. If it wicks underneath, or I always go by, right wicks through the damage, bodies to the store" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2831s-2844s])
- **Displacement defined via fair value gap**: "If you create a gap, what's called a fair value gap, in between, candle number one, the high of candle number one and the low of candle three, this is what's called a former displacement. If the wicks are overlapping, then there's no displacement" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5846s-5861s])
- **Displacement as indication, not determining factor**: "displacement is not the overall determining factor of a breaking retest is going to work or not, but there's an indication. Of course, the the more displacement, the better a breaking retest is going to occur" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5861s-5877s])

### Valid Retest (Touch/Zone, Max Wait)
- **Drawing levels from wick to body**: "I personally like drawing them from the wick to the body" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [1519s-1524s])
- **Areas vs exact lines**: "I'll talk about that. That's more based off of areas of retest instead of levels, because order blocks, privacy, has in privacy, loads, very similar. It's just right, they're the exact same levels, either the areas in price or levels in price. I just like drawing it from the wig to the body" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [4705s-4733s])

### Entry Trigger (Hammer/Shooting Star)
- **Three entry models**: "So our first one is essentially our limit order slash no confirmation. We don't use this one as much... The second one, strong price action slash buyers stepping in. This is something we look for every single day. And then the third one here as well is also one of my favorites, strong price action slash buyers stepping in plus the confirmation candle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [3129s-3158s])
- **Entry model #2 - buyers stepping in**: "all that means is right once it taps into the key level, you can see buyers are stepping in based off of red this candlestick formation... if price taps into it and you have a lower wick, right? It doesn't matter what candle color... The color of the candle does not matter as much as right the price action around the key level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [3454s-3484s])
- **Entry model #3 - confirmation candle**: "this confirmation candle essentially confirms that price wants to gravitate back towards the upside... basically when this candle right here closes above its previous candle. So as you can see right here, this candle closes above its previous candle close. So this candle close right here, it closes above its previous candle close. This is what's called confirmation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [3587s-3619s])
- **Three bar pattern**: "first candle here is our lead candle. Our lead candle is what pushes towards the upside. Second candle, our reaction candle. This is very important as well, right? Because this is technically the first two candles is our second entry model. Our third candle is what confirms our third entry model, which is what's called the confirmation candle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [4446s-4467s])
- **Confirmation candle close levels**: "it's bullish is if it closes above its second candle, but it's even more bullish, if it can close above that first candle high, which is going to be that lead candle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5031s-5043s])
- **Smaller lead candle is better**: "if you have a small leading candle, that's going to be the best. So if you have a huge lead candle, then it's going to be a little bit more difficult" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5543s-5553s])

### Stop Placement
- **Stop for entry #3**: "stop a break underneath. And you'll be looking for high day plus continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [4876s-4880s])
- **Stop options for bearish**: "If you want to have a little bit more of a protective stop, you can make it your second candle, which is going to be your reaction candle. If you want to have a little bit of a tighter stop, you can make it that third candle, which is going to be that confirmation candle. Usually I just like to put it at your second candle, just to have a little bit more of a protective stop" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5321s-5334s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **84% rule mentioned as reclaim setup**: "This is technically that reclaimed back above this left shoulder. So this now is a very key pivot point, right? So this was our original break and retest. This would have been the best for continuation higher. Ultimately, right, we broke underneath maybe for whatever reason. It was an as bullish. But if we can reclaim back above, this is very bullish and it signifies right continuation back towards the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2740s-2763s])
- **84% rule = head and shoulders fail**: "This is a very similar setup to what happened on AMD last week... this is what's called our invalidation of a head and shoulders or a head and shoulders fail pattern, which is very very bullish because it's just ultimately a reclaim" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2763s-2778s])
- **84% rule psychology**: "if we don't, it's sellers don't step in here. That usually means that price is going to gravitate back up towards high of day and it's going to happen very fast because right sellers were looking to step in here. Ultimately, that failed. They usually either have a stop at high of day. And that's exactly where price is going to gravitate towards to take out essentially those stops" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2789s-2812s])
- **Full 84% rule details deferred**: "I'll actually have a in-depth one about the 84% rule on Thursday, Ken. - **Body vs wick for reclaim**: "you can choose the body or the wig. I personally like choosing the wig, but you can also choose the body as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [6530s-6541s])

- **Higher timeframes = 15min and 1hr**: "I consider higher timeframes right the 15 minute, one hour and you can also use the daily and weekly, but these trades don't occur as often. - **Manage trades on 1min or 5min**: "I personally like to manage trades on the one minute time because I like tighter risk. If I have a hard time frame thesis, then I'm going to manage it on the five" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5421s-5429s])

## 11. Higher Timeframe Chart Patterns

### Flat Top Break
- **Definition**: "what a flat top break looks like... basically what happens is we have higher lows being built and we have a clear level of resistance... we have a nice push towards upside. Now we're creating this higher low. We push up again, a little bit of rejection here. We create another higher low. Come up, rejection once again, another higher low and then we do we keep doing that. And what happens right here is we usually have a big push towards upside. This is the most bullish pattern on the higher timeframes" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [1969s-2015s])
- **Two to three touches for validity**: "preferably what I consider is two to three touches. Right. That's always a good rule of thumb. Well, creates a pattern in my opinion, or trend line, or a flat top break, or a ranging market is two to three touches. Right. If you have two to three touches, then it's valid" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2060s-2076s])
- **Logic**: "we're building up liquidity. Every single time we touch a level, right, sellers are stepping in here. So if you can break above this, there's a very good chance we have continuation towards the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2092s-2103s])

### Cup and Handle
- **Definition**: "a cup in handle is fairly similar. You have this kind of nice push towards upside... Price pushes higher, we do something like this, and then it pulls back. And we have a clear level of resistance... what matters here, it's a logic in thesis, right, higher lows are being built. We have multiple touches. Right, what we're looking for is to capture this leg in price" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2198s-2257s])

### Range Break
- **Definition**: "what we have is a clear range of the markets... So the last thing you want to do is try and anticipate the break. So that's why you want to wait for the break and then you want to be looking for the retest for continuation higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2320s-2342s])
- **Don't anticipate**: "If you try and anticipate a range break, a lot of times what will happen is you'll end up getting freaked out" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2328s-2336s])

### Head and Shoulders Fail/Invalidation
- **Definition**: "if we're in a clear uptrend, then this is very bullish. If you can invalidate this right shoulder... if it invalidates this right shoulder, this is a good sign that price is going to continue higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2626s-2669s])
- **Invalidation condition**: "What invalidates it if it is if it breaks underneath. If it wicks underneath and still holding, I consider this still bullish, preferably even if it has higher lows, it's even more bullish. But as long as it can maintain above this higher low pivot point, I consider this still bullish" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2696s-2713s])
- **Same as 84% rule**: "this is technically that reclaimed back above this left shoulder" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [2740s-2746s])

### Five Main HTF Patterns
- **List**: "A flat top pattern. We have a cup and handle. We have a range break, regular break and retests and another one was called a head and shoulders fail or a head and shoulders invalidation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [1774s-1795s])

---

## 12. Bearish vs Bullish Entry Differences

- **Stair step up, elevator down**: "stair step up elevator down. Right? So if you ever see price move in an up, upwards line, right? When you're trading towards upside, usually can wait for those candles to close, right? That's what I like to do. But if you're trading the downside, sometimes you want to be a little bit more aggressive and you want to take entry number two instead of entry number three, because sometimes if you wait for the confirmation candle on the downside, it probably will already be a low of day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5081s-5105s])
- **Downside is more aggressive**: "price usually likes to stay heavy, right? The downside, it's usually a lot more aggressive. It likes to stay heavy. So usually sometimes it might tap in once and then never come back and just stay heavy towards the downside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [5111s-5125s])

---

## 13. Market Structure / Trend Definition

- **Uptrend**: "when price moves in higher, when price moves higher over time, forming higher highs and higher lows" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [823s-835s])
- **Downtrend**: "when price moves lower over time, forming lower lows and lower highs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [1040s-1046s])
- **Trend intact until key pivot broken**: "the way to determine if price is still in a trending market is when we have that impulsive leg... So this swing point towards the upside or what I call an impulsive move is going to create our higher low swing point. So until we invalidate this swing point right here, I would consider us still in a trend towards the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [921s-942s])
- **Impulsive move creates new pivot**: "the impulsive leg towards the upside is now going to create this new low and any single time it pulls back. That's going to be our higher low pivot point for continuation higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [994s-1005s])

---

## 14. Candlestick Basics

- **Color doesn't matter for small bodies**: "the color of the candle does not matter as much. Right, this can be green, this can be red, at least when it's neutral... the color of the candle state does not matter as much. Of course, the color of the candlestick matters when we have a big body, but when we have a small body, it does a matter as much because it's more neutral" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [489s-505s])
- **Large wick = strong buying/selling**: "if we have a large wick towards the upside... we open here. We have a big push lower, we create a brand new low, and then price pushes all the way back towards the upside... So this is a strong form of buyers stepping into the market" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [562s-610s])
- **Context matters**: "not one single candle without context is going to matter... one candle is not going to matter as much. It's going to matter a lot more in the right context" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_3_Technical_Analysis.txt [728s-754s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Bodies vs Wicks, Displacement)
- **Breakout vs retest distinction:** "I don't trade breakouts... the reason that I don't trade breakouts myself, okay, is because if we have a breakout trade... just because we break out of the resistance does not mean that we have confirmed a move to the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [68s-197s])
- **Confirmation comes from retest:** "For me, the confirmation comes when we create what what is what happens when we come back into this area... this is now the higher low, right, the higher low or support. So this resistance, this was previously resistance back here, obviously. This has now turned into support up here" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [217s-245s])
- **Breakout fails more often:** "The breakout trade... there's no confirmation. Therefore, there's a higher likelihood chance that this one fails" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [397s-403s])
- **Break and retest = higher probability:** "With the break in retest, what we're doing is we're waiting for the confirmation to the upside... this means that buyers or demand is over supply. And then when we come back into this area, and we actually see strong price action, that means the demand is over supply even on the pullback as well, which was mean this is a higher probability setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [407s-422s])

### Valid Retest (Touch/Zone, Max Wait)
- **Retest as support/resistance flip:** "This resistance once it turns into support and buyers do step in, right, we need to understand price action. When buyers do step in, this is where we want to enter because this is technically the lowest risk at the highest reward trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [245s-261s])
- **Levels are zones, not exact ticks:** "One thing you have to understand is that we don't need a perfect touch of each level. Right? Like, it's, it's, the break and retests is not going to happen every time. Or it's like a perfect touch in the name breaks back about these levels are essentially small zone. So when you look at these levels, right? Consider them like small areas where like if you break below it a little bit, that's not a big deal as long as you see that buyers are stepping up still" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [3361s-3386s])

### Entry Trigger (Hammer/Shooting Star)
- **Wait for candle close at level:** "I always wait for a candle close of the level right Jacob. So the key part here is you have to wait for the candle to close at the level that you're trading. So at the level that I'm trading the candle needs to close once it closes at that level. Next candle does not have to close for me. The next candle I just need to see weakness or strength. And I can enter that because we already waited for the candle to touch the key level that we're entering on" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4664s-4688s])
- **Weak price action defined (for shorts):** "This is week price action because if you can see here, what we see is buyer stepping up. And then when you see this big upper weight right here, right big upper weight with week price, sorry, big upper weight and then the candle closes red. This shows that buyers tried to at one point, this was a full green candle. But then sellers stepped in and they closed it back at lows. Therefore, this is week price action" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4771s-4793s])

### Stop Placement
- **Manual stop, not auto:** "I have a manual stop. So I don't have an auto stop. I have a manual stop. I have to stop myself out" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2882s-2888s])
- **Wait for close below stop:** "Yes, I wait for the close below my stop up loss. Typically though, if it's moving really quickly, then I'll stop myself out myself. However, typically I'll wait for a stop, a close below my stop" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4845s-4854s])
- **Stop below the break level:** "Your stop can be one of two things. If you're looking for a higher time frame move, right? Your stop can essentially be just a break below the candle body that we're breaking above... In this case scenario for myself, typically my stop would just simply be a little bit of a break below this previous day high" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2779s-2803s])
- **Stop for short trade:** "Our stop loss in this example would simply be a break back above the candle that we're entering on" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4452s-4455s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **First target = high of day:** "If targets my first targets, at least being high of day, and then 450s as my second targets and then 450 ones as my final kind of targets" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2803s-2806s])
- **Short target = low of day:** "Our profit targets would simply be low of day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4455s-4457s])
- **Pre-market high as profit target:** "Our first target's at least would be this pre-market high. And then we would be able to target up into 450's essentially" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [3580s-3586s])

### First-Break vs Late Breaks
- **First retest is the entry:** "The first break in retest is the first pullback. So I would have entered in here regardless" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2957s-2959s])
- **Second setup = reclaim:** "The second setup is good. This would be more so of a reclaim where you most likely would have actually entered in at 10.08 compared to actually entering in down here" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2961s-2968s])

### Options-Specific Reason for Retest
- **Premium cools on retest:** "If we trade options, there is another reason why we don't want to trade breakouts if we're trading options... when we have a big move like this, we're getting an increase in our delta, right, both delta and gamma are increasing at the same time... having an entry into this trade here, right, we're buying when the premiums are at the peak. So therefore, if the stock does decide to pull back... we're going to be risking a lot more money on this pull back than if we just waited for the retest because when we wait for the retest, the premium will also cool down" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [269s-370s])

### Instrument-Specific Behavior
- **Breakout works better in crypto:** "The breakout trade works way better in crypto than it does in options. This is true... breakout trades work better. There's a higher likelihood chance when a stock breaks, or sorry, when a crypto coin breaks out, it will work. There's a higher probability chance of it working than an option trade on Tesla, for example. To on Tesla, the breakout retest will work a little bit better" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [550s-581s])

---

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

### Definition
- **One candle rule defined:** "The one candle rule essentially means that for every down close can, so when we're in an uptrend, the down close candle will hold as support. And when we're in a downtrend, the up close candle will hold as resistance" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2868s-2878s])
- **Wicks represent buyer/seller fight:** "The wick represents, so when you see a wick, it represents sellers and buyers fighting at this area. Right. So when you see a wick, it represents the interaction between buyers and sellers. So if a stock wicks below and then the body closes above, that simply means that buyers won that kind of trade off, right? Buyers won here. So every time we came back here, buyers won" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2917s-2940s])
- **One candle rule as confluence:** "We know that if we come back now, right, the one candle rule is there for a move back to the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2712s-2722s])
- **Context overrides one candle rule:** "Yes, you could enter in on the one candle rule there. However, the context behind that trade was the previous day low. That was the thesis that we created all day. And therefore, if sellers did want to step up, they were going to step up near the previous day low, not near the one candle rule" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4741s-4757s])
- **Jack will cover setups tomorrow:** "The one candle rule is a setup that Jack will be talking about tomorrow. So any setup that you guys don't understand that I may briefly mention. Don't worry. We're going to talk about it much more in depth within the next 14 days tomorrow. I believe Jack has a full setup session. So the first candle strategy, the one candle rule, dip and rip, rip and dip... Jack will talk about it tomorrow in terms of the setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2982s-3012s])

## 5. Key Levels

### PDH/PDL (Previous Day High/Low)
- **PDH/PDL are most important:** "The reason the daily time frame is important is because we know there is a lot of liquidity sitting at the previous day high, right? And the previous day low. Now when we know that there's a lot of liquidity sitting at previous day high and previous day low, we know those are levels that we need to have on our charts" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1684s-1698s])
- **Don't trade between daily high and low:** "I know for a fact is I don't want to trade in between this range. So anywhere in between the daily high and the daily low, I don't want to take a trade there. Okay. The only way I want to take a trade is if we are above the daily high or we're below the daily low" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [3139s-3150s])

### Higher Timeframe Levels (Daily, 4H, 1H)
- **Daily first, then 4H and 1H:** "I'm going to be looking at the daily time frame... And then after that, I am now looking at the four hour and the one hour... I'm looking at the four hour for more so for the context of the day. Okay? So context of the day and I'm looking at it for the overall thesis that I can come in with" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1676s-1742s])
- **4H/1H for thesis creation:** "On the 4 hour and the 1 hour timeframe, this is where I'm creating my thesis. This is very important to understand. On the 4 hour and 1 hour timeframe, this is where the thesis comes in" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2349s-2355s])

### Color Coding System
- **Green = higher timeframe resistance:** "My levels are the green equals higher timeframe resistance. Red equals higher timeframe support. White equals one minute levels. And then obviously boxes equal one candle rule" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2125s-2143s])
- **Green/red = daily timeframe:** "For both of these, these are typically going to be on the daily timeframe. So whenever you see green or red lines, those are typically going to be on the higher timeframe for me" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2157s-2162s])
- **White = 1-minute intraday levels:** "The white lines that you see are going to be one minute intraday levels" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2165s-2169s])

### Drawing Levels as Day Progresses
- **All-time highs = no levels to look left:** "When we're making all-time highs, we don't have to look left because there's no levels. So for those days, that that's when we're looking forward that one can't rule, right? The one can't rule. That's when we're looking at, you know, psychological numbers" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1896s-1908s])
- **1-minute pivot levels from previous day:** "On the 1 minute timeframe, we're simply going to come over to the previous day from 930 to 4pm and this is where you guys will get the white lines that I draw right? Those white lines are those 1 minute pivot levels. I draw mine based off of where there is either the most touches or where there's like a V shape recovery from previous day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2421s-2438s])
- **Levels should be obvious:** "These white lines should stick out to you. These should be very, very, very clear. Okay, these white lines should be very clear on what you're looking for" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2441s-2447s])
- **Levels within previous day range = white lines:** "Any levels on the higher timeframe, that are within the range of the previous day candle, that's where I use the white one minute candles" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2249s-2253s])

### Pre-Market High/Low Usage
- **Context-dependent:** "Pre-market high and low levels are typically used when you're either gapping up or gapping below, or if you have no key levels, you can also use pre-market high and pre-market low. It's more or so context" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [5014s-5023s])
- **Don't draw when context doesn't fit:** "I don't need to draw a pre-market high in pre-market low right now because the context doesn't make sense. Why would I draw a pre-market high in low when I just said the only way to trade Tesla is above the previous day high, right? Because of the context on the four hour and the one hour" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2521s-2544s])
- **Pre-market high as profit target:** "You can draw a pre-market high because this is going to be a key level that you can potentially look for... this could be a level where you can look for profit targets" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [3451s-3466s])

### 5-Minute High/Low for Neutral Days
- **Neutral start = use 5-minute levels:** "The only reason I'm looking for the five minute setup here is because it's a very neutral start to the day. We don't see buyer stepping in. We don't see seller stepping in. This is just neutral. Okay. Does that make sense? And that's the only reason that we can draw out the five minute high and the five minute low" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4317s-4331s])

### Levels vs Zones
- **Levels are small zones:** "Consider them like small areas where like if you break below it a little bit, that's not a big deal as long as you see that buyers are stepping up still. Right? So there's small zones essentially" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [3376s-3386s])

- **After 11 AM = switch to 5-minute:** "If you're trading after 11 AM for any of you guys that do trade after 11 AM, rather than trading from 9, so I trade from 9, 30 to 11. If you're trading after 11 AM, what you need to do is go on the five minutes setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [5119s-5126s])

- **1-minute entry for higher timeframe patterns:** "I'm going to be entering this break and retest on the one minute time frame in today. ## 9. Trade Selection

### A/A+/B/C Grading Criteria
- **Not all break and retests are equal:** "Not every single break and retest is going to be a high probability break and retest. - **Context determines probability:** "You need to understand that not all break and retests are equal and that's where understanding market structure and context comes into play" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1204s-1210s])

### Confluence
- **Multiple confluences = high probability:** "We have not only the previous day high, we have relative strength, we held market structure, higher time frame is bullish, and we have the one candle rule. Do you guys see how much of a high probability setup this is already?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [2726s-2736s])

## Edge Definition
- **Edge = statistical advantage:** "Your edge is your statistical advantage over the market over a long period of time. Now, it's very important to understand if you want to be a profitable trader, this is the name of the game" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1027s-1036s])
- **Edge comes from market context:** "My edge comes from understanding the market context, compared to the individual stock. So I'm comparing essentially QQ and spy, compare to the overall ticker that I'm trading" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1423s-1434s])
- **Edge not shared publicly:** "My edge is something that I don't talk about on YouTube. You'll never see me or hear me talk about my edge on YouTube or any social media platform for that sake, right? And that's because that's what I've developed over a long period of time" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1092s-1103s])
- **Break and retest alone works, edge makes it profitable:** "Publicly all I talk about is the break and retests, because the break and retests itself works. If you can trade the break and retests, you know, consistently every single day, it works. However, combining the break and retests with your edge is what's going to make you a profitable trader over a long period of time" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1113s-1126s])

---

## Personality in Trading
- **Personalize the system:** "I can give you the full roadmap, and I will give you the full roadmap on exactly wherever you are right now... However in the system, you need to add checkpoints for yourself, right? Because there's some things that, there's some things that, you know, I may do that may not be for your own personality" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [615s-637s])
- **Jack vs Tony entry style:** "Jack will enter in a trade, usually, right? As it's kind of hitting that support, it'll show a little bit of buyer stepping in and then Jack will enter here... Whereas this exact same trade for me... I may wait for just a little bit more confirmation, meaning that I need to see more buyer stepping up. So where Jack entered in, he technically gets the lowest risk, but the highest reward on this trade. Whereas where I enter in, there's a higher probability that it hits high of day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [733s-772s])
- **Figure out personality through experience:** "You figure that out through experience. You figure that out through experience" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [713s-715s])

---

## QQQ Alignment
- **QQQ context determines trade validity:** "If QQs immediately starts failing and... immediately starts retesting and coming back to the downside, right? This Apple trade is a bad trade, 100% I'm off of it, right? I don't even want to look at the Apple trade. However, if Qs decides to break above this previous support turn resistance and it breaks above and it starts reclaiming above, and Apple is showing strength, then I will look to enter into Apple looking for high-end continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1336s-1358s])
- **Full session on relative strength later:** "For the actual relative strength and weakness compared to QQ and the indices, we're going to have a full session on this" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1217s-1220s])
- **Tony uses QQQ, not NQ:** "Any difference between Q versus NQ, futures per conference, I just use QQ myself. It's a little bit more easier for me to just read. And it moves exactly the same as the individual tickers. You can use NQ as well. I just use QQ" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4833s-4840s])

---

## Learning Progression
- **Break and retest pattern recognition in 3-5 months:** "The patterns and pattern recognition, that takes you maybe a month to really figure out two months, you know, three months at most, right? And you're going to be the best break and retest trader. You'll be able to see the break and retest pretty clearly in about three to five months, right? It really shouldn't take you more than that" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [1041s-1053s])
- **Compare levels to Tony's pre-market charts:** "I send in my pre-market levels probably around 5 to 10 minutes before the market opens... you guys should already have your pre-market levels set up, right? And therefore when I actually send mine, you can compare your pre-market levels to mine. And when you're comparing your pre-market levels, you'll see where you may have seen a potential, you know, pivot structure where I didn't. And therefore the more you do that, once again, you guys are live trading with us for a year. Right? That's it more than enough time for you guys to actually get your key levels dialed down" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_4_Bread_Butter_Strategy.txt [4562s-4589s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Bodies vs Wicks, Displacement)
- **Opening plays involve break and retest on higher time frames:** "When we're looking for an opening drive it's usually sometimes right at breaking retest on the higher time frames" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [2308s-2314s])
- **Displacement makes opening plays better:** "The best opening plays also have displacement, so more displacement is better" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7277s-7284s])
- **Pop and drop is essentially a break and retest:** "So essentially just a break and retest" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4805s-4807s])
- **Pop and drop example showing break-retest-continuation pattern:** "Break retest... Break retest... Continuation so a lot of these ones are fairly similar" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5022s-5035s])

### Valid Retest (Touch/Zone, Max Wait)
- **Immediate retest of key level is entry model #1:** "The first one is our immediate retest off of our previous day highs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [743s-749s])
- **Three entry models for gap and go:** "immediate retest of a key level... entry number two would be potentially like a reclaim or an order block... entry number three which is our opening range break in retest" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [783s-812s])
- **For gap fill inside range, entries include breakdown, retest, and order block:** "The first one is gonna be the breakdown... We have the retest and we have an order block" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3094s-3102s])

### Entry Trigger (Hammer/Shooting Star)
- **Three-bar pattern confirms reversal:** "Third candle confirms it towards the downside so right it just looks something like this pop... break underneath retest and this three-bar confirmation can be used on the one-minute, five minute, right 15. I usually like looking at this on the five and see this basically confirms that reversal" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5136s-5157s])

### Stop Placement
- **For order block entry, stop below the order block:** "Stop at the below the order block and we're looking for pushes back higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6145s-6148s])
- **For order block with entry confirmation, tighter stop possible:** "Maybe waiting for this candle maybe you can have a tighter stop loss" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6154s-6158s])
- **For pop and drop aggressive entry:** "I usually for the downside a good rule if I might go by is something like this stare stuff up... elevator down so on the downside I'm always good to be a little bit more aggressive compared to the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4884s-4899s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **For gap fill, target is the gap:** "Our targets in this case was just our gaff" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3227s-3228s])
- **For dip and rip, target is HOD plus continuation:** "Target's back at high today plus continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4647s-4649s])
- **For 84% rule, hold for original target past HOD:** "Now I'm primarily gonna be holding for my original target which may it might be like a previous day high or some sort of higher time frame objective... So instead instead of selling at highday, now I'm gonna be looking for my main target" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8317s-8325s])

### First-Break vs Late Breaks
- **On reversals, prefer entries 2 and 3 (later):** "I like choosing number two and three, which will be either an opening range break and retest later into the session or retest some sort of order block... I like waiting for confirmations on reversals" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3597s-3614s])
- **Best entries on reversals are later entries capturing the explosive last leg:** "The best for personal trades are gonna come at the variant... That's when it's gonna have the most explosive move when we're talking about reversals in itself... Usually price doesn't have just a v-shape come sometimes it does that but majority of the times what will happen is it'll slowly kind of break structure, move and then have a crazy explosive move... You want to be capturing this is what's called the last leg in price... My opinion this is the best time to trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3618s-3656s])
- **For gap and go, first two entries preferred if bullish:** "Preferably if we're fairly bullish you want to be looking for number one and two... But if it's really bullish we can always look for number three for this trend continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [823s-830s])
- **Outside the range, be more aggressive with entries 1 and 2:** "If we're outside the range I like to be a little bit more aggressive so look for number one... If we're outside the range then you can be a little bit more aggressive because this is a key level here as well... So you can be looking for number one and number two... Number three actually usually ends up failing sometimes just because we already had a nice push higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4552s-4577s])
- **Inside the range, wait for entries 2 or 3:** "Inside the range you probably want to wait for two or three" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4583s-4585s])

---

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** — The speaker does not use the term "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as a named setup in this transcript. The opening range is discussed but not as a "one candle rule."

---

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

### Definition
- **Core definition:** "If price stops you out on a trade and the same trade presents itself again, you can take the same original trade a second time using the original stop and targets and it'll work out 84% of the time" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7751s-7764s])
- **No pattern needed on second entry:** "There does not need to be a pattern on the second entry so this is key... A lot of people will be like you need to wait for a retest. No, it's a reclaim entry... And it says a reclaim of a key level. There does not need to be a pattern" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7781s-7801s])
- **Strong confirmation is candle close above:** "Our signal really the only thing is a strong confirmation if it closes above we can be looking for the reclaim for a continuation higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7801s-7807s])
- **First entry must have been valid:** "A lot of people think they're taking 84% rules, but if you take... a proper break and retest... take the second setup thinking it's invalid and 84% rule you're gonna lose again because essentially you're taking an improper initial setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8475s-8485s])

### Sizing
- **Can size up on the second entry:** "Can you size up on the second one? Since it's the 84% rule and it's a real one... Let's say if you're not fully convinced on the first setup... So let's say on the first one you take... 10 contracts... Right, so you stop out over 10 contracts... on this reclaim... Let's say you take 15 contracts... Which is not your normal size you're actually sizing up on this one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8554s-8715s])
- **Risk on sized-up 84% entry is slightly more but probability favors it:** "Because the risk is fairly similar if you lose... Right you're gonna lose basically fairly similar to what you lost earlier... Now you might lose like one point five R... Instead of risking your normal one R... Now you have 15 contracts, but it works 84% of the time... So if you get your actual move which now the odds are in your favor... You can easily make... on the 84% rules" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8736s-8764s])
- **Keep size consistent generally, base on personal statistics:** "That does just mean go around right sizing up randomly you want to try and keep your size consistent... And as I mentioned you want to base it up of your own personal statistics" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8847s-8851s])

### Target Management on 84%
- **Hold for original target past HOD:** "Now I'm primarily gonna be holding for my original target which may it might be like a previous day high or some sort of higher time frame objective... So instead instead of selling at highday, now I'm gonna be looking for my main target" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8317s-8325s])
- **Sell into strength, not before HOD:** "I'm gonna sell into strength so at least want to see highday plus continuation... I might sell a little bit of buff highday, but I don't want to sell some before highday" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8388s-8396s])
- **If you didn't take first trade, can scale at HOD:** "If you take the 84% rule without taking the first trade then you can scale some at highday" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8406s-8410s])
- **Scaling example with 10 contracts:** "Maybe you sell kind of like five as it pushes up into our highday... you can sell like five and then like two two one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8618s-8654s])

### Psychology
- **84% rule addresses the frustration of being right but stopped out:** "One of the most frustrating parts about trading is... you get stopped out before the trade works... you know you're right but somehow you're not making money" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7438s-7512s])
- **If 84% fails, market is likely choppy:** "If 84% rule fails, it's a very good chance that it's choppy and you would be very cautious" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8068s-8072s])

---

## 4. Order Blocks

### Definition
- **Order block is one candle:** "Order block is essentially one candle... Right it can be on the one five 15 minute, but all it is is right up close candles and down close candles on a chart" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5430s-5442s])
- **Directional rule:** "Up close candles in a downturn... Down close candles in an uptrend to support price" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5445s-5448s])
- **Order blocks are areas of significant orders:** "An order block is is an area where large market participants have placed significant buy and sell orders... Order blocks are key level such areas where there's lots of liquidity on a chart" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5468s-5478s])

### Drawing (Wick-to-Body)
- **Highest probability: wick to body:** "We're gonna mark out from the wick to the body highest probability here. So if price breaks above... respects the wick to the body... even closes above it... That's your highest probability order block" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5587s-5601s])
- **Second highest: top of wick to bottom of body:** "Your second highest probability you're gonna draw from essentially wicks and respects the top of the wick to the bottom of the body" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5609s-5617s])
- **Lowest probability: full candle:** "Your third order block or technically I want to consider this your lowest probability because it's all based off context, but you're gonna draw at the full candle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5617s-5624s])
- **Full candle gives more room:** "Usually I just like to draw at the full thing just to give it a little bit extra room" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5739s-5742s])

### Validity
- **Order block is valid when price closes through it:** "When is an order block valid? An order block is valid when price closes underneath... the down close candle... So this right here it validates the order block. If we have no close underneath it then it's not valid" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5918s-5927s])
- **For downtrend, valid when close above:** "When is this candle valid now? Is when we close underneath it so we close underneath it here now this should support price lower" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5930s-5936s])

### Probability Hierarchy
- **Wick-to-body respect is most bullish/bearish:** "The most bearish price action is gonna respect this... the wick to the body is the most important part for the most bearish price action" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5833s-5840s])
- **Big candles are lower probability due to wide stop:** "If you have such a big up close candle like this... it makes it my pain a little bit more difficult just because you're gonna have a pretty wide stop... That's why I really don't like these big candles... And that's why I also have it at the lowest probability" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6314s-6350s])

### Confluence
- **Best when aligned with higher timeframe break and retest:** "Usually lines up the best order blocks with higher timeframe break and retests points as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5962s-5967s])
- **More confluence is better:** "Confluences really the more confluences the better" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [5456s-5460s])
- **Best trades on key levels:** "Best trades will occur on key levels previous day highs previous day lows... More confidence is the better" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6413s-6419s])

### Entry/Stop/Target
- **Entry at order block touch, stop below:** "When it taps into the order block the easiest is right you have an entry here... Stop underneath and now you're looking for high today" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6117s-6134s])
- **Can wait for entry confirmation for tighter stop:** "Maybe for waiting for entry confirmations you can have a few different things here... Maybe tighten up your stop loss" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6138s-6140s])
- **For downside order blocks, stop at top:** "Stop would be actually be all the way back up here... So in this case it probably better to wait for entry confirmation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6231s-6241s])

### Market Regime
- **Order blocks work best in trending markets:** "These work best in a trending market... if the markets are ranging then your order blocks are ultimately gonna fail" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6392s-6403s])

---

## 5. Key Levels

### PDH/PDL
- **PDH/PDL are primary key levels:** "Key levels that we have marked out as a mention we have the immediate retest potentially off your previous day highs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1157s-1164s])
- **Gap above PDH/PDL for gap and go:** "Gap above your key levels... In this case primarily our previous day highs and previous day lows" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [501s-506s])
- **For gap fill, key levels are PDH/PDL:** "The key levels could be just in today levels. For inside the range key levels are going to be your previous day highs, previous day lows, old highs and old lows" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [2635s-2640s])

### HOD/LOD
- **HOD is a target for dip and rip:** "Target's back at high today plus continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4647s-4649s])
- **For 84% rule, hold past HOD:** "I'm gonna sell into strength so at least want to see highday plus continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8388s-8390s])

### Gap Fill
- **Gaps fill 70% of time in 48 hours:** "Gaps actually fill 70% of the time in a 48 hour period" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [440s-449s])
- **Gap fill considered filled when wick touches:** "Gaps that consider filled when the wicks... It just basically has to fill the imbalance... The wick and then once it touches it I consider that the full gap fill" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1060s-1078s])
- **Gap can be partially filled:** "Does a gap have to be completely filled or it can be partially... it can be partially filled... If there's a big gap... It doesn't have to be filled the whole way... Half the gap right that's a key level especially if it's a very big one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1209s-1233s])
- **Draw gaps on 1 and 5 minute timeframes:** "I personally like drawing like the gaps on the one and five minutes time frame" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1092s-1099s])

### Hierarchy
- **When levels are close, draw area not precise level:** "If they're close by one another just draw out the area... There's a reason why I draw out the areas the levels are sometimes way too precise... Precision's really good, but if you're too precise then usually you're looking every single one minute candle... You're not really focusing on what really matters and which is the higher time frames" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7007s-7036s])

### Zones vs Ticks
- **Draw areas, not precise levels:** "Just mark out this whole range... Once it broke above retest continuation... If they're close by one another just draw out the area" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6997s-7007s])

---

## 6. Time-of-Day

### Trade Window
- **Opening plays within first 15-30 minutes:** "For opening plays I consider opening plays within the first really 15 to 30 minutes" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [86s-93s])
- **If no opening play, wait first 15-30 minutes:** "If there's no opening plays then we usually want to be patient so sometimes you'll hear us say right let's wait for the first 15 to 30 minutes to play out" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [125s-132s])
- **Gap and go entries primarily within first 15-30 minutes:** "You primarily want to see it happen within the first 15 to 30 minutes and then the third entry model is actually an intraday setup as well so this can happen much later into the session" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [842s-853s])
- **Gap fill has no time limit (48 hours):** "There's not a time on the gap fills... It could fill it within the first 30 minutes. I could fill it within 24 hours. It could fill it within 48 hours" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [2415s-2424s])
- **Don't trade first 15-30 minutes when starting out:** "What I recommend for you guys to trade within the first 15 to 30 minutes when you're starting out? No because you can easily get fake out and you'll probably lose a lot a lot more money" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [2372s-2382s])

### Best Days
- **Day 2 moves after strong trending day:** "Day number one specifically refers to a strong trending day any day that has news or just as a nice trend higher... And then day number two is one we can be looking for an opening drive" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1982s-2011s])
- **Opening plays are typically day 2 moves:** "Majority of these ones are day 2 movers so whenever you have an opening play you want a little bit more context preferably... It's gonna be a day 2 move" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4830s-4842s])

### News Days
- **News catalyst brings volatility and opportunity:** "If there's a news catalyst then that's a reason why the market can move... News catalyst just brings in volatility and liquidity... there's just more opportunity" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4306s-4333s])
- **Opening drive plays often happen after large news:** "A lot of times the open drive plays happen after a large news... So if you have a news and then maybe day two day three... This usually happens on a day two move or day three move after strong news catalyst because you can expect continuation" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1905s-1916s])

### Chart Timeframe Rules
- **Intraday setups on lower timeframes:** "Today specifically we're gonna be going over intraday setups, which is primarily on the lower time frames" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [60s-66s])
- **Opening range: use 1, 5, or 15 minute:** "Mark with the first candle in New York Session Open... One five or 15 minute candle" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [6653s-6658s])

---

## 7. Exits

### Scaling %
- **Scale at HOD partially:** "For this one I'll shave my executions... I scaled a little bit at high day and that was just partially and along the way just because we were at all time highs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [4475s-4486s])
- **On 84% rule, sell some at HOD, hold rest:** "Maybe you sell kind of like five as it pushes up into our highday... you can sell like five and then like two two one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [8618s-8654s])
- **Tony's approach: sell 80-90%, hold 10%:** "Tony... sell majority... Right 80 90% of his position... His remaining 10% of his position actually made majority of his money and that's the power of options" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3891s-3906s])

### Breakeven (When)
- **Scale into strength, cut rest at breakeven:** "I took the second entry... I contract around five scaled up into key level and then cut the rest essentially break even" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3685s-3690s])
- **With multiple contracts, sell some, hold some for breakeven stop:** "If you have like two or you have three right sell some right you can hold some for break even stop" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3879s-3884s])

### Trailing
- **Trailers capture powerful moves:** "If I had some trailers you can have seen how powerful the trailers these contracts... Went from five all the way. I think close to like 25 or 30 dollars" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3945s-3955s])

### Position Management
- **One contract is difficult to manage:** "If you maybe have one contract... very difficult because you can't scale... If you buy one contract here... pushes higher... You have like you kind of two factors should you sell one here because it's at a key level or should you potentially hold for a higher time frame thesis? My opinion it's way too difficult because you only have one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [3850s-3878s])

---

## 8. HTF Application

### Weekly/Daily Levels
- **Higher timeframe thesis determines direction:** "What matters more is understanding the higher time frame thesis because ultimately you can have a perfect entry and if you don't have a good higher time frame thesis, it can fail. You can have a horrible entry, but if you have a good higher time frame thesis... that's ultimately going to work" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7048s-7058s])
- **Daily chart shows trend for gap and go:** "If you go into the daily chart just to give us a little bit more context... We're fairly bullish... We're in a clear upturn" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1302s-1330s])
- **Higher timeframe break and retest for gap and go:** "I consider this a higher time frame break and retest if you go into the daily chart" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [1302s-1309s])

### Multi-Day Holds
- **Swing trades difficult in ranging markets:** "During this period if you ever put on a swing trade during this time... swings are very difficult because you have to time it perfectly... timing it in a ranging market is very difficult... timing in a trending market super easy" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [2807s-2823s])
- **Ranging markets = day trades, trending markets = swings:** "During these periods we're gonna be primarily looking for day trades... Whenever we have more volatility what's good for day trading... What's bad for swings long term" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [2834s-2853s])

### HTF Overriding Intraday
- **HTF thesis is primary, entry is secondary:** "If you can ultimately nail the higher time frame thesis then you actually really don't even need to have the perfect entry... Entries matter a lot but what matters more is understanding the higher time frame thesis" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7036s-7050s])

---

### R:R

- **Reclaim = candle close above key level:** "Our signal really the only thing is a strong confirmation if it closes above we can be looking for the reclaim for a continuation higher" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7801s-7807s])
- **90% of trades are reclaim entries:** "90% of my trades it's gonna be on the reclaim" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7926s-7929s])

- **Wait for candle close on reclaim:** "Majority times we're gonna wait for the candle close... Wait for those candles to close because you'll see the body" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_5_Every_Setup.txt [7977s-8020s])

## 9. Trade Selection
### First Pullback Rule
- **First pullback rule defined:** "It's if we see strong relative strength in the morning or relative weakness within the first let's just say 5 10 15 minutes of the day... the first pullback in the upside usually gets bought up for that continuation move to the upside or the first pullback to the downside usually gets bought up for a move to the downside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [275s-304s])
- **First pullback has higher probability:** "There's a higher probability chance at the first pullback that's bought up" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [315s-320s])
- **First pullback on QQQ provides context:** "This is technically the first pullback on Q so in terms of context... the best absolute best context would be if Q's was above this level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1120s-1138s])

### A/A+ Grading
- **A setup vs A+ setup distinguished by QQQ context:** "The reason this is an A setup is because we can see Q's above or sorry Q's still holding first pullback buyer stepping up however the reclaim of the pre-market high is going to give us the highest probability entry model" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1149s-1165s])
- **A setup criteria - multiple confluences:** "Number one we have a very strong gap up on the date... We're above previous day high already showing relative strength within the first five minutes... We show a lot of relative strength as well... We also have the one candle rule... previous higher time frame highs being there... this was the first pullback of the day on top of strong price action" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [513s-581s])
- **Grade is NOT based on whether trade works:** "Just because some trades work doesn't make it A plus just because some trades don't work doesn't not make it A plus" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [495s-504s])
- **A setup - would take 10 out of 10 times:** "I would consider this an A setup... I would take this 10 out of 10 times every single time it sets up" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [581s-591s])
- **A+ setup requires QQQ reclaim of pre-market high:** "The reclaim of the pre-market high is going to give us the highest probability entry model" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1160s-1165s])

### Do Not Add to Losing Positions
- **When candle shows lower probability, do not add:** "This candle now shows you the contract you've already entered in stay with them... do not enter in for a new position" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1698s-1713s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **$10,000 loss on day, still up $40,000 on week:** "I actually took a $10,000 loss today, which is tough. But still up 40,000 on the week" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [4019s-4030s])

## SPECIAL AUDIT QUESTIONS

### A/A+ Grading
- **A setup = multiple confluences (gap up, above PDH, relative strength, first 5 min all green, one candle rule, HTF highs, first pullback):** "Number one we have a very strong gap up on the date... We're above previous day high already showing relative strength within the first five minutes... We show a lot of relative strength as well right one two three four five the first five minute candles every single candle was green... We also have the one candle rule right here... previous higher time frame highs being there... this was the first pullback of the day on top of strong price action" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [513s-581s])
- **A+ setup = A setup + QQQ reclaim of pre-market high:** "The reason this is an A setup is because we can see Q's above or sorry Q's still holding first pullback buyer stepping up however the reclaim of the pre-market high is going to give us the highest probability entry model" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1149s-1165s])
- **Grade independent of outcome:** "Just because some trades work doesn't make it A plus just because some trades don't work doesn't not make it A plus" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [495s-504s])

### QQQ Alignment
- **QQQ used for entry confirmation, especially at weakest point of ticker:** "When we are entering in on this pull back or this weakness that Nvidia has this is where we need to see the strength in QQQ" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1350s-1359s])
- **QQQ matters most at entry point:** "QQ for us or the indices really matter only specifically the most when we're entering" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1359s-1365s])
- **Need QQQ in same direction as trade:** "We need QQQ to overall be in the same direction of the market that we're looking to enter in with our individual ticker" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3893s-3903s])
- **After entry, relative strength is on the individual name, not QQQ:** "After we enter in the relative strength of Tesla or Nvidia or whatever trade we're taking... that relative strength is now only on that name. So it could now start moving way more aggressively than QQQ" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3903s-3915s])
- **When ticker shows weakness but QQQ shows strength = good for entry:** "When the stock that we are trading is showing weakness, but QQQ is showing strength at that very same moment... it is good for us because this is where we need QQQ to show us the strength" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [2155s-2199s])
- **When QQQ makes new high of day but ticker shows no buyers = invalidate trade:** "I cut my full in video trade there because Q's made a brand new high of day and in video didn't show any buyer stepping in and this 100% invalidates the trade for me with momentum" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1755s-1771s])
- **QQQ preferred over SPY for tech names:** "Because I trade tech names, I prefer QQQ" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3351s-3359s])
- **Watch both SPY and QQQ - SPY for overall market context:** "I'm going to watch QQQ more carefully. But I need to watch spy to know the context of the overall market" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3578s-3582s])
- **Not every tech name follows QQQ 100%:** "Does every tech name follow QQQ price action 100% of the time? No" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3879s-3886s])

### Displacement/FVG
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

### Stop-After-Win / Max Trades/Day
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

### Reclaim Setup
- **Reclaim entry confirmed:** "The reclaim entry... for that continuation move to the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [2673s-2684s])
- **Reclaim of pre-market high = highest probability entry:** "The reclaim of the pre-market high is going to give us the highest probability entry model" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1160s-1165s])

### Stock Personalities
- **Tesla and Nvidia show most strength/weakness:** "Tesla really nice personality. If Tesla shows relative strength, it will run. Nvidia same thing... Apple is something that's a little bit slower... My favorite names are going to be Tesla and Nvidia, specifically, that show a lot more strength and weakness whenever they move compared to other names" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3668s-3693s])

### Candle Size Matters for Relative Strength
- **Bigger candle = more strength:** "The size and range of candles do matter. The bigger the range of a candle, the more relative strength they would have" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [3833s-3857s])

### Loss of Momentum
- **Loss of momentum = buyers tried but couldn't reclaim key area:** "Loss of momentum some simply states is you can see that buyers where they needed to step up... they tried to however now they're not able to reclaim back above essentially this key area" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_6_Market_Structure_Indices.txt [1607s-1620s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Personal setup preference]** "I'm only taking trades on two names, and it's only one setup. One strategy, one setup." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5654s-5664s])
- **[Personal setup definition]** "I call it the break retest moong... basically a break on retest of a support turning to resistance area. On SPY and QQQ, but it actually works on most of the assets." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5991s-6007s])
- **[Entry confirmation requirement]** "I'm going to wait for the break and retest signal... worked with a break of a key level, enough displacement, a good retest, and a very reaction on that key level. So buyers is stepping in or sellers is stepping in, but something that confirms the move in my direction." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [3393s-3427s])
- **[Personal bread and butter setup]** "Break and retest of the previous day high... that was the set up that I really like with the market alignment. So if SPY could you was where optringing, I went look for the one name with the relative strength and I like the break and retest of the previous day high." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4397s-4422s])
- **[Personal setup refinement]** "I'm focusing only on one strategy, one setup, with only two tickers, that's my bread and butter." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5638s-5647s])
