## 5. Key Levels
- **[Personal preparation]** "I use... having relevant key levels on the 5th minute chart. So I can identify my potential entries on the one minute chart." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [742s-751s])
- **[Personal preparation]** "Confirming support and resistance, key levels for the indices on the 5th time in a chat." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [672s-679s])
- **[Personal preferred setup levels]** "Break and retest of the previous day high and the break and retest of the pre-market low." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4383s-4396s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Personal rule - no first 15 minutes]** "I'm not trading on the first 15 minutes of the market. Why? Because I need... to settle down a little bit, I need... to cool off, to fake everything on the opening... Then after 15 minutes, we may have a few hints of where the market wants to go." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1620s-1645s])
- **[Personal timer rule]** "I set 15 minute timer for when the market opens, to when I start entering into position. So before my 15 minute timer rings, I barely take trades." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1734s-1748s])
- **[Personal stats on rule]** "Less than 10% of my trades are before the 15 minute mark." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1756s-1761s])
- **[Personal rule rationale]** "I just skip any opportunity before the 15 minute mark. Why? Because that's what worked for me." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1873s-1879s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Personal scaling change from journal data]** "Instead of cutting one quarter of my position with 10, 15% on the green, I changed my strategy to dream one third of my position but at high of the day." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4535s-4543s])
- **[Impact of exit change]** "I used to do $10,000 dollar trades... with this change, I was able three months later, to reduce my size to $7,000, and guys I was making more money with $7,000 trades than with my initial size." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4557s-4584s])
- **[Exit philosophy from coach]** "Jack was pushing me to wait... you should not be cutting your trades with 10, 15%, you should be waiting for high of the day for your first stream." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4461s-4484s])
- **[In-trade reassessment timer]** "When I enter into a trade, I have a five minute timer, and that timer works for me to reassess every five minutes if I'm staying in a trade... because the chart is doing, it's telling me to stay, or... if it's my ego or if it's my grinder... this timer helps me to stay longer on my winning trades, but also helps me to cut faster my losing trades." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1798s-1832s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[Trading with trend]** "When you are trading with the trend of the markets, of the indices, that's the way I'm cooking you, everything is easier." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1656s-1663s])
- **[Index alignment]** "If you are trading... and you are going with the indices, everything feels easier, everything feels as modern, everything feels like it's gonna work." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1672s-1683s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Personal focus]** "I'm only taking trades on two names, and it's only one setup. One strategy, one setup." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5654s-5664s])
- **[Personal strategy refinement from journaling]** "I'm focusing only on one strategy, one setup, with only two tickers, that's my bread and butter." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5638s-5647s])
- **[Personal trade count]** "I usually take one trade a day, or less if I want to trade that day." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5537s-5547s])
- **[Personal risk management reason for sticking with strategy]** "Managing my risk... with my personality, it was super hard to recognize my mistakes... when I started using the break on retest with this car face approach, it made way easier to manage my risk. That's why I stick to this strategy." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [6030s-6079s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Personal win rate change from exit adjustment]** "I used to do $10,000 dollar trades... with this change, I was able three months later, to reduce my size to $7,000, and guys I was making more money with $7,000 trades than with my initial size." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4557s-4584s])
- **[Personal best trade]** "A few days ago, I have 680 something percent return on a contract." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [2428s-2433s])
- **[Personal risk-reward target]** "I try to have my 1 to 2 or my 1 to 2.5 risk reward rate, most of the time." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [3137s-3143s])
- **[Personal stats on time rule]** "Less than 10% of my trades are before the 15 minute mark." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1756s-1761s])

## Performance Coaching / Psychology (Methodology)
- **[Three phases of trading session]** "We walk through the three different faces of a trading session... before the trading session... during the trading session... after your trading session." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [534s-575s])
- **[Technical preparation]** "With your technical preparation, you set up your charts, you review your strategy, you check your news, you kind of build your bias... you prepare for today." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [650s-667s])
- **[Mind preparation - visualization]** "I used to visualize nailing my trades... I tried to see myself calm and confident... my mind preparation is being prepared for the good stuff, but also for the opposite stuff." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [756s-798s])
- **[Emotional check before trading]** "Before your first trade, pause and ask, am I calm, focus, center, ready?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1508s-1515s])
- **[Waiting with intention]** "Waiting with intention is not just chilling in front of your computer. It's analyzing what's in front of you. It's looking to the candles. It's looking for patterns. It's looking for your setup to come to you." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1708s-1723s])
- **[In-trade emotional check]** "Mid-trade ask, is this emotion driving this mid-decisions? If yes, pause... whatever you're doing and reassess." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [1773s-1783s])
- **[4F Framework for journaling]** "The four F stands for facts, feelings, findings and fix." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [2603s-2611s])
- **[Facts definition]** "Facts is the raw data... you log your trade details, your entry, your exit, your size, your market conditions, how much time you stay on the trade, percentage of return." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [2717s-2734s])
- **[Feelings definition]** "How did you feel? Anxious, calm, rush, hesitant, overconfident." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [2742s-2747s])
- **[Findings definition]** "What did you learn? You were rush, you miss a signal." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [2755s-2767s])
- **[Fix definition]** "What's your next step?... your proposal of improvement for the next time." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [2767s-2773s], [3220s-3241s])
- **[Fix for good trades]** "If the trade was done properly and according to your plan... the fix can be a reinforcement... something that you did good and you felt like that was the thing that actually worked well... you just recognize that and you keep doing that." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [3247s-3277s])
- **[Journaling timing]** "I start taking notes during my trade, so when I'm journaling, I don't forget anything." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5482s-5491s])
- **[Journaling as mind dump]** "My journaling time, it's my mind dump exercise... I take all of the emotions, I take all of the rush... and I completely forget... I don't take the emotions to my lunch with my go-to, or to my time with my kids." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5568s-5602s])
- **[Virtuous spiral concept]** "Better preparation leads to better execution and better execution leads to better journaling and better learning. And this is going to be your virtuous spiral." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4822s-4844s])
- **[Key insight on learning source]** "I learned more from my journaling than from any other coach that I have on my training life. I learn more from my experience than from any other lesson or session I have with the coaches." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [4979s-4999s])
- **[Double down on strengths]** "Double-down on your strengths instead of general like fixing all of your weaknesses. It's better. So I double-down on what I was doing better." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_7_Peak_Performance_Trading_Coaching.txt [5694s-5709s])

# EXTRACTION FROM NEW TRANSCRIPTS

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Scaling out (not in) for break-and-retest]** "There's very rare times where you will want to scale into a position especially with the break and retests and how the break and retests works... you're only scaling out of positions" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [213s-285s])
- **[Why not scale into break-and-retest]** "Usually our trades work out right away or they'll typically go in there will hit your stop... usually they're pretty quick move so that is why you wouldn't want to scale into them" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [291s-301s])
- **[Entry identification process]** "Before you trade obviously you're going to want to identify your entry right when you have a break right and you have price pull back and you're looking to enter the trade you're going to need to identify the entry point right likely when you see a bounce or a buyer step in" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1001s-1016s])
- **[Stop loss identification]** "The first thing you're going to do when you enter that trade is identified the entry and then your stop loss next... you're going to just identify where price breaks" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1021s-1043s])
- **[Trade works right away or not at all]** "They will work out right away or they won't at all" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [4057s-4060s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **[One candle rule entry mentioned]** "It was yeah like a one candle rule like entry" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1131s-1136s])
- **[One candle rule as scale point]** "The one-kand rule you can use fibonacci retracement tool as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2379s-2385s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 4. Order Blocks

- **[Order blocks as scale points]** "New highs of day or lows of day you could do it at order blocks too we'd talk about order blocks" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2379s-2385s])
- **[Order block as break-even cut level]** "If you're treating zero days right you're probably going to cut break even as soon as you break below this level right here because this is technically like your next your last retest yeah if you're kind of order block right here and you're your like last pivot high right here technically so it's like that's a good level to cut it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2627s-2648s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[High of day / low of day as primary scale targets]** "High of day or low of day is risk reward wise relative to your stop and entry" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1049s-1058s])
- **[Key psychological numbers as scale points]** "Key psychological numbers... those are always good skill points as well always good skill points" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2397s-2403s])
- **[Higher timeframe key levels for runners]** "Your higher time frame key levels is gonna be kind of your main objective if you are holding onto some of the trailers" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2338s-2345s])
- **[New highs/lows as scale points]** "New highs of day or low of day... price pulled back made a new high this could be a scale point" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2345s-2379s])
- **[Price gravitates to next level of liquidity]** "If you have highs and lows usually the next point if we're if you're buying a retest right here this is the next level of liquidity so it will naturally gravitate towards that level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1699s-1710s])
- **[Previous session pivots for all-time highs]** "For all-time highs you can use fib tool for scaling maybe because like you just I would just use old pivots if there is like all-time highs" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3451s-3457s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Zero days - 11 o'clock rule]** "If you're holding options zero days like on the next set that most of the time it's gonna be Friday... if by 11 o'clock your trade hasn't played out for the most part you're just gonna want to close it out no matter what... rule of thumb for zero days just 11 o'clock is like the rule just like don't don't hold any after that" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [4291s-4323s])
- **[Zero days require trending/volatile conditions]** "Typically I will trade zero days I just think that there has to be a very clear trending day and there has to be some volatility" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3498s-3508s])
- **[QQQ for zero day decision]** "You could base this maybe off QQQ if you want to trade zeroes or not" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3498s-3504s])

## 7. Exits (Scaling)

- **[Definition of scaling out]** "Taking partial profits at key levels and then using the rest as a trailer position" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [327s-331s])
- **[Purpose of scaling]** "To avoid the all or nothing trap... to secure daily profits to stay consistent and keep runners... for potential exponential options gains... help to reduce stress increase confidence protect capital" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [346s-366s])
- **[General rule: 75% day trade, 25% runners]** "You should just be having 75% of your contracts as a day trade your initial scale just rule of thumb 75%... most of your position should be gone at that trade... and then about 25% of the position is going to be letting it ride" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1272s-1302s])
- **[First scale at 1.5R minimum]** "Rule of thumb for me when high of day or low of day right is 1.5 risker word or a lower base on the entry and based on your stop loss... minimum 1.5 is usually a very good level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1376s-1501s])
- **[Scale percentages by R multiple]**
  - "When it is around 1.5 to 1.75 restore reward... I usually 60% is a very good scale" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1530s-1538s])
  - "When high day or low days around 1.75 plus to 2R... the rule thumb is like around 70%" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1607s-1621s])
  - "When high day or low day is too r or more... you're going to go 80%" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1635s-1643s])
- **[Scale into strength for calls]** "With options you need to be fast and you need to scale call options into green candles right you're gonna get the most juice of the premiums that way... with put options obviously you are doing opposite you are selling them into red candles" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2283s-2304s])
- **[Break-even after first scale]** "The only time you should probably go break even is like after you take your first scale" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1759s-1769s])
- **[Break-even timing rule]** "If I've already hit the first TP and the trade goes against me and it's been like over 10 minutes I'm just gonna stop over it even if it's been like under five minutes or so I'll usually use the actual stop loss" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3285s-3297s])
- **[Cut runners when structure breaks]** "For anything past 10 minutes I would say you want to use your like actual structure so like your higher highs higher lows... if it loses structure right then you can go you can just cut it period you don't even have to wait for break even you can just cut it period" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3303s-3340s])
- **[2R as default target when no HOD/LOD]** "If you take the immediate retest and there isn't a high of day point to actually you know go off of usually 2R is a good rule of thumb" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1164s-1178s])
- **[Use position tool for R calculation]** "We use the position tool as a as a good reference right as a good reference" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1193s-1201s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[HTF key levels for runners]** "Your higher time frame key levels is gonna be kind of your main objective if you are holding onto some of the trailers" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2338s-2345s])
- **[Holding overnight with HTF context]** "Google was at all time highs it broke out and it held that level i'm okay with my entry and everything like holding a few right but it's only like 10% of my position i already scaled that up like a majority of it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2725s-2744s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Small position size - don't scale]** "When you should not scale it up trades when you have very small position size so it's not worth splitting short term scalp trades... if you're trading around one to two contracts the reason why scaling won't work as well" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [764s-819s])
- **[One to two contracts - close entire position]** "With one to two contracts you want to close out your whole position" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [879s-885s])
- **[Three contracts - 2:1 split]** "With three contracts right this is probably the next level you can do a two to one... two at high bid and then one would be like either next higher timeframe level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2997s-3020s])
- **[Four contracts - 3:1 split]** "It's gonna be three to one three and one split major at the time" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3091s-3097s])
- **[Five contracts - 3:1:1 split]** "Five contracts... you can do a three one one split" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3162s-3174s])
- **[Six contracts - 4:1:1 split]** "For six I would say the exact same you're just gonna do like four one one" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3242s-3248s])
- **[Don't trade further OTM for more contracts]** "You shouldn't trade further out of the money to get more contracts... just stick with like around the money stick with end of week" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [3676s-3695s])
- **[Pre-plan exits before entry]** "You can pre-plan your eggsics before entering the trade it's always good to do" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [561s-566s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Personal monthly profit]** "The last like seven months I want to say has been like around 20,000 a month trading options in today" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [174s-184s])
- **[Win rate needed for scaling at 1.5R]** "If you have a decent win rate like about 50% right 40% you should still be technically in the green if you're taking me setups" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [1901s-1907s])
- **[One to two contracts - 60-70% win rate break-even]** "If you are trading with one to two contracts right and you take this trade 10 times if you have a like 60% of when right or even a little behind maybe 70% you're going to be pretty close to break even just because of the nature of options" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2900s-2916s])
- **[Example: 10 contracts, sell 6 at HOD, 2 at next level, 1 at next, 1 at final]** "10 contracts... I would have sold six of them at this high day... when price pulled back and made a new high... maybe sell another two... maybe sell one contract here and maybe one at the next key level" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [2455s-2499s])
- **[Example: Tony sold all but 10, was up 7k, those 10 became 28k]** "Tony sold all contracts except 10 and was up around 7k at the end of the day with those same 10 contracts he was up 28k" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_8_Scaling_With_Options.txt [4105s-4115s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **[84% rule defined as re-entry mechanism after stop-out]** "We have the 84% rule. This is a rule that I use on a consistent basis. The reason I developed that rule, I put that into my own playbook" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1070s-1082s])
- **[84% rule prevents blowing account]** "That 84% rule saves me from blowing my account time and time again" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1089s-1096s])
- **[84% rule avoids maximum loss]** "The reason the 84% rule is so good is because it avoids the maximum loss" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1114s-1120s])
- **[84% rule eliminates "losing big" outcome]** "If you eliminate the losing big part... go back to your journal... get rid of your biggest two losing days every single month. I can almost assure you... if you got rid of your two biggest losing days per month for this entire year... you would either be a very small break even trader or you would actually be profitable consistently" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1159s-1193s])
- **[84% rule mechanics - lose small twice, still profitable]** "If the first trade I take, I'll lose small. And if it does come back and the trade looks like it's going to go again, I'll enter, re-back it, right? I'll re-enter again. And if let's say the 84% rule does fail on me. Well, now, if the 84% rule fails, well, I lost $100 the first time and I lost $100 a second time. So in total, I lost $200 on that trade. The trade risk to reward was 677. Right? So the risk to reward is still over a 2R even though I lost two times on this trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1204s-1240s])
- **[84% rule vs holding through stop - math comparison]** "Would you rather enter into a trade two times and lose $200, but have two chances of high probability setups? Or would you take one setup?... you lose $1,000 on a setup that ends up being a little bit more of a mid setup" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1246s-1265s])
- **[Reclaim entry requires weak price action first]** "Would we enter in on this reclaim entry? No, because the price action wasn't weak enough. Right. We needed to see a bigger impulsive red candle to the downside. We didn't see that yet" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [880s-895s])
- **[Maximum loss should be original stop only]** "The maximum you should have lost if you were risking a hundred dollars was the one hundred dollars. Right. That's literally the maximum you should have lost. If you lost anything over that on this trade, that was an invalid setup for you" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [900s-915s])

## 4. Order Blocks
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Pre-market low as thesis level]** "Our thesis for this day is going to be the pre-market low... a pre-market low-retest for continuation to the upside" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1334s-1350s])
- **[Pre-market low reclaim setup]** "I need the reclaimed above pre-market low for me to enter into this trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1431s-1436s])
- **[Whole psychological numbers as support/resistance]** "The whole psychological numbers were buyers should step up... the 441 levels a whole psychological number. So technically that's where the most buying and selling action is happening" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1564s-1573s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[No trades after 11 AM]** "I don't trade after 11 am. So no trades after 11 am" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2060s-2071s])
- **[Three trades max per day]** "I can only take three trades a day, three trades max" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2073s-2083s])
- **[Avoid range price action]** "Avoid range PA... I do try to avoid range price action. That is a rule I have" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2119s-2133s])
- **[Avoid low probability days - FOMC, news]** "Avoid low probability days, right? And that would be something like FOMC, right? News, etc." (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2133s-2142s])
- **[After FOMC volume similar to open]** "After FOMC is very similar to trading the open, right? Because you have volume that's pouring in" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3390s-3400s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Scale at high of day / low of day]** "I like to scale a high of day, slash low of day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2083s-2102s])
- **[Lock in profits at HOD/LOD, then trail]** "Lock in profits, small at high of the day, low of the day, and then you can truly your trade" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4098s-4112s])
- **[Stop loss hits = stop out, profit target hits = exit, everything else is noise]** "The only thing that you need to do every single time that you enter into a trade is when your stop loss hits, you stop out. When you're profit target hits, you exit. Anything in between these levels... is noise to you. It is, it means nothing" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1627s-1653s])
- **[Never move stop loss - stick to original plan]** "You should not enter into trades and trade or switch your plan just because you're trying to make money" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1615s-1621s])
- **[Rarely cut before stop loss for new traders]** "Very, very, very, very rarely as a new trader, I would never recommend that, just wait till the stop loss" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4035s-4043s])
- **[Don't look at P&L during trade]** "Making sure that you stick to the trade the entire time, the best way to do it is not looking at your P and L, I'm just looking at the chart itself" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4167s-4181s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[HTF thesis must align for trade]** "This is based off the higher time frame, right? The higher time frame is aligning" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [242s-245s])
- **[HTF retest vs intraday retest distinction]** "This is a higher time frame retest. I want to make that clear. So this is retesting, because this is a higher time frame retest" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [372s-381s])
- **[HTF context can override - but don't move stops based on it]** "I know based off the higher time frame this is weak... I also know that look, it's rejecting again. So what I'm going to do is instead of having this hundred dollar stop loss... let's move our stop loss up here now" (EXAMPLE OF WHAT NOT TO DO - leads to blowing account) (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [550s-577s])
- **[Wrong HTF thesis leads to blowing account]** "The higher time frame was actually bullish on the name. Right. It wasn't even bearish. Right. The higher time frame was never even bearish. You just decided the higher time frame was bearish" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [780s-790s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Thesis is an if-then statement, not a bias]** "When you come into a thesis, it's an if then statement... So if the thing that I want to happen doesn't happen, then yes, I'll change my thesis because the if then statement didn't happen. It's not a bias that I'm coming into the day with, right? It's not a bias. It's a thesis" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4128s-4145s])
- **[One-and-done after first winning trade]** "If I have a good winning day, if my first trade is a winning trade, I typically will tend to avoid taking other trades. If I take one and done, I'm one and done" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3983s-3997s])
- **[Why stop after first win - protect mentality]** "If the market is rewarding you for your first trade, why would you go in and risk taking a loss and ruining your mentality for tomorrow?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4007s-4018s])
- **[Trade only your system]** "I can only trade my system. I can't follow and, you know, leave anything else" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2155s-2159s])
- **[Step 1: 2-3 contracts only]** "In your step one, all you should be doing is having around two to three contracts. Right? That's literally all you should have. Right? All you should have is two to three contracts per trade. Every single trade have two to three contracts" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3262s-3271s])
- **[Don't ask about sizing until Step 2]** "When people ask me... how much what's your sizing? It's like, why does that care? Like you shouldn't care. Right? Because like if I sizing with 500 contracts or if I sizing with 50 contracts, right? That's me looking at my risk management. You guys haven't even got to that point yet" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3289s-3303s])
- **[Upsize only when journaling confirms profitability over 3 months]** "To grow your account, right, you really want to look at the last three months of data and look at are you profitable over a long period of time in this three... And are you profitable by following your rules? If both of those answers are yes, then you start up sizing" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3948s-3966s])
- **[Backtest minimum 6 months per ticker]** "When you're back testing, typically, right, I would back test every ticker, a minimum of six months" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4054s-4061s])
- **[Backtest one setup at a time]** "When you're back testing, you stick to one and you look at the actual setup. So that would be a setup within the system... when you're back testing, you do a setup per time" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [4074s-4088s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Example: $100 risk for $677 reward = 6.77R]** "We are risking essentially a hundred dollars... this is a six seven trade, which means we will be making... six hundred and seventy seven dollars" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [490s-511s])
- **[Example: $100 risk for $215 reward = 2.15R]** "We have a 2.15 risk to a war trade. So we're risking again $100 down here $100 of risk for $250 profit" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1484s-1507s])
- **[Personal monthly profit range example]** "For this month, I'm probably only up, like $100, $250,000 this month" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3608s-3615s])
- **[Personal best month example]** "When Tesla was ripping in the last couple months... with my long term account, with my swing account, and my day-trading account, I made over seven figures in a month" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3588s-3603s])

## SPECIAL AUDIT: Discipline System (NEW material - not in previous extractions)

### Three Levels of Discipline
- **[Preventive Discipline - Level 1]** "Preventive discipline... this is where we need to establish expectations in guidelines" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2038s-2048s])
- **[Warning day for first rule break]** "We need to give ourselves a warning, right? So warning day. So on this day, we will be giving ourselves a warning... hey, I didn't follow my rules today. I did not follow my rules. And therefore I need to be careful for tomorrow" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2229s-2247s])
- **[Supportive Discipline - Level 2 - last warning]** "Supportive discipline... this is essentially our last warning, right? So the first ones are first warning. This ones are last warning" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2446s-2459s])
- **[Supportive discipline = no trades next day]** "What this warning means for us in terms of supportive discipline is we need to on the next day not take a trade again" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2471s-2489s])
- **[Corrective Discipline - Level 3 - punishment]** "Corrective discipline... if you fail to actually change what you've done in the last couple of days, this is where you need to essentially punish yourself or do something that you don't want to do" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2619s-2631s])
- **[Corrective discipline = healthy activity you hate]** "Do something that you don't want to do, but it's beneficial for you. So you have to go into your own life and think about something that is beneficial, but you hate to do. Right? Something that you know is good for you, but you specifically hate doing it. Now for myself, what that was was a five kilometer run" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2648s-2669s])
- **[Corrective discipline must be done same day]** "If Friday, I have to... if it's raining outside, or it's snowing. It doesn't matter. On the same day, I have to go and run" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2687s-2700s])
- **[Worst thing = getting rewarded for bad trades (positive reinforcement trap)]** "The worst thing that can happen to you is if you don't follow your rules, but on top of that, you get rewarded for not following your rules" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2757s-2764s])
- **[Positive reinforcement problem in trading]** "Every time we take a good trade... we make money that's positive reinforcement for us. However, the problem with trading is every time we take a bad trade potentially, right? But we make money that's also positive reinforcement" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2833s-2851s])

### Three Steps to a Winning System
- **[Step 1: Profitable proven system with edge]** "Step one of a winning system is having a profitable but proven system... it needs to have edge" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [2998s-3011s])
- **[Step 2: Proper risk management + discipline]** "Step two here for the system would be proper risk management... and we need discipline to follow our system" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3078s-3117s])
- **[Step 3: Psychology + journaling + consistency]** "Psychology, journaling... consistency. Right? Showing up every day" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3152s-3200s])
- **[90% of traders are in Step 1]** "90% of you guys are still in step one... You haven't even seen step two or step three yet" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3219s-3226s])
- **[Step 1 takes 3-12 months - no timeline]** "Step 1 for some people may take three months for some people who may take six months for some people who may take a year. Right. Don't give yourself a timeline in terms of when you need Step 1 done by just get Step 1 done" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3906s-3918s])
- **[Step 2: Lower contract size to improve scaling]** "If this means in step two that you actually have to lower your contract size from three contracts to two contracts to get even better at scaling, so be it" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3420s-3431s])
- **[Step 2: Switch to shares to practice scaling]** "Maybe in step two... they go from actually taking contracts to taking shares, right? Because you don't lose as much money in shares, and so they end up taking five to ten shares... the point of step two is not to be really making money at this point. It's to be getting better at the scaling part of your trading" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3446s-3473s])
- **[Step 3: Review 6-8 months of journaling for market cycles]** "Look back at the last six to eight months... have you gotten better as a trader? Have you seen more consistency as a trader? And have you been able to trade different market cycles?" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [3505s-3525s])

### PTSD of Trading (Psychology Concept)
- **[PTSD of trading defined]** "The PTSD of trading... is you've seen where... they stop you out right here... you lost a hundred dollars, so you respected your plan... And then from this... it has a 90 degree move to the downside... So if you stayed in this trade for like five to 10 more minutes... you start navigating in your head like damn if I stayed in this trade for five more minutes, not only would I have not lost a hundred dollars, but I would have made $677" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [989s-1034s])
- **[PTSD leads to marrying thesis and blowing account]** "When you actually do get into a trade and you hit your stop loss... you start thinking again, like, okay, I've seen the trade reverse exactly from my stop loss before. What's the chances that it does it again this time? So let me just stick to this trade, right? And you end up marrying the thesis and you end up losing way more" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1046s-1064s])

### Four Outcomes of Any Trade
- **[Four possible trade outcomes]** "There's genuinely three things that should happen when you enter a trade... You win big, you win small, you lose big or you lose small" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1120s-1147s])
- **[Goal: eliminate losing big]** "We never want to lose big. As a trader, the one thing that we always want to avoid is the losing big part" (boot-camp-recordings_Day_9_How_To_Create_A_Winning_System.txt [1277s-1285s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Scaling applies to break-and-retest]** "Typically when you're trading the break and retests system, you are looking for a break, maybe there's a range, maybe could be your previous days highs, previous days lows, could be some sort of key level. At this point what I'm looking at is this level of resistance right here. Right? So this previous resistance, once this price breaks out is now turned into a support zone." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [246s-268s])
- **[Entry timing]** "you are looking for a break, right, and you have price pull back and you're looking to enter the trade, you're going to need to identify the entry point, right, likely when you see a bounce or a buyer step in." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1006s-1019s])
- **[Stop placement]** "your stop loss next. So you're not going to put it to price sorry yet, you're just going to put wherever you're going to close that position out... In this case, if you look at this drawing right here, it's just a close or a price pulls back above that retest level. In this case, it's a short position taking puts this would be your stop loss" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1020s-1054s])
- **[Never scale into break-and-retest]** "you never obviously re-add sides unless there's a plus setup. I would just ignore this. Just never, never add in size to these trades for the break and retest. Doesn't make sense." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [734s-744s])
- **[Why not scale into]** "Usually our trades work out right away or they'll typically go in and they will hit your software to away. Usually you won't see a ton of ranging around there. Usually they're pretty quick moves. That is why you wouldn't want to scale into them." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [286s-301s])
- **[First scale target - HOD/LOD]** "identify wherever high of day or low of day is risk reward wise, relative to your stop and entry, right, relative to your stop loss and your entry point, and that's very key." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1054s-1065s])
- **[First scale target - 2R default]** "if I take the immediate retest, and there isn't a high of day point to actually go off of. Usually 2R is a good rule of thumb, right?" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1163s-1176s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
- **[Mentioned but not defined]** "It was, yeah, like a one candle rule, like entry, but I will, I'm going to talk about the setup. I'm just going to talk about scaling and how to actually do this first." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1130s-1139s])
- **NOTE:** Speaker mentions "one candle rule" entry but does not define it in this session. No further specification provided.

## 4. Order Blocks
- **[Mentioned as scale point]** "You could do it at order blocks too. We talk about order blocks the one-kinned rule." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2375s-2387s])
- **NOTE:** No definition or specification of order blocks provided in this session.

## 5. Key Levels
- **[HOD/LOD as primary scale target]** "identify wherever high of day or low of day is risk reward wise, relative to your stop and entry" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1054s-1065s])
- **[Higher timeframe key levels for runners]** "your higher time frame key levels is going to be kind of your main objective if you are holding onto some of the trailers." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2337s-2344s])
- **[New HOD/LOD as scale points]** "New highs of day or low of day. So in this case scenario... price pulled back, made a new high. This could be a scale point." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2344s-2375s])
- **[Key psychological numbers]** "key psychological numbers. I know we talk about that too a lot. So Jack, Tony talk about key psychological numbers. Those are always good scale points as well." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2398s-2405s])
- **[Previous session levels for all-time highs]** "for all time highs you can use fib tool for scaling... I would just use old pivots if there is like all time highs... for new highs of day you use other levels from like previous like previous sessions" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3443s-3462s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Zero DTE cutoff]** "if you're holding options zero days like on the next set like most of the time it's gonna be Friday... if by 11 o'clock your trade hasn't played out for the most part you're just gonna want to close down... rule of thumb for zero days just 11 o'clock is like the rule just like don't don't hold any after that" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [4289s-4323s])
- **[Zero DTE conditions]** "you could base this maybe off QQQ if you want to trade zeros or not like typically I will trade zero days I just think that there has to be a very clear trending day and there has to be some volatility" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3496s-3512s])
- **[QQQ for zero DTE]** "when you look at the like QQ and spy at market open if you see higher highs higher lows being made or if you see lower lows lower highs like all the time big momentum it's a little bit volatile then trading zero d to e is pretty it's like it's useful right" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3546s-3556s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Scaling definition]** "taking partial profits at key levels and then using the rest as a trailer position." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [321s-333s])
- **[General rule - 75/25 split]** "you should just be having 75% of your contracts as a day trade, your initial scale. Just rule thumb, 75%. Usually it's never a bad thing. Most of your position is off." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1264s-1290s])
- **[Scale % when HOD/LOD is 1.5-1.75R]** "when it is around 1.5 to 1.75, first reward, relative to your entry and your stop, I usually 60% is a very good scale." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1528s-1545s])
- **[Scale % when HOD/LOD is 1.75-2R]** "when high-day or low days around 1.75 plus to two are relative to where your entry and your stop is. The rule of thumb is like around 70%." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1614s-1627s])
- **[Scale % when HOD/LOD is 2R+]** "when high-day or low-day is two are or more relative to your entry and stop, you're going to go 80%." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1638s-1650s])
- **[Minimum 1.5R to scale]** "minimum 1.5 is usually a very good level." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1499s-1503s])
- **[When NOT to scale - small position size]** "When you have very small position size. So it's not worth splitting." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [766s-775s])
- **[1-2 contracts - no scaling]** "with one to two contracts, you want to close out your whole position" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [873s-886s])
- **[1-2 contracts - target]** "with one to two contracts you're scaling out the 100% of your position at like either like 2r your high of day key level whatever may be probably at least good to have 1.5 minimum no matter what." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2861s-2879s])
- **[3 contracts - 2:1 split]** "you can do a two to one split so two at high of day and then one would be at either next higher timeframe level" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2999s-3017s])
- **[4 contracts - 3:1 split]** "it's gonna be three to one three and one split major at the time" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3094s-3101s])
- **[5 contracts - 3:1:1 split]** "you can do a three one one split... your first scale would be three contracts here maybe the next new high of day scale one maybe there's like a higher time frame level up here right price continues maybe scale another one" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3162s-3197s])
- **[6 contracts - 4:1:1 split]** "for six I would say the exact same you're just going to do like four one one" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3242s-3251s])
- **[Sell calls into green candles]** "you need to scale call options into green candles, right? You're going to get the most juice of the premiums that way, with put options obviously you are doing an opposite. You are selling them into red candles, right? With momentum." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2284s-2303s])
- **[Breakeven after first scale - 10 minute rule]** "usually after I take my first scale... I will usually go back and I will cut up break even... if after 10 minutes the price falls back below your like at your first scale. Like usually the momentum isn't exactly there and you want to go break even." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2519s-2549s])
- **[Breakeven vs stop loss timing]** "if it's been five minutes and the trade if it hit TP one or like high of day... and price immediately rejects after. I'll probably use my actual stop loss because I haven't gave it that much time. Whereas if the trade has been like 10 minutes and it's just kind of doing this right I'm probably going to go break even." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2775s-2813s])
- **[Cut runners on structure break]** "if you make it higher low or lower high and lower low, that could be a way to do it." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2662s-2668s])
- **[Hold runners only if momentum intact]** "Let runners ride only if momentum is still intact." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [730s-734s])
- **[Overnight holds possible with small remainder]** "you don't even have to sell all of those the same day right because like even let's just say you take four off and that next contract like prices still continue higher like you could always hold that one overnight right it's a little bit riskier but the profits that you took from your initial four are likely this last contract is not going to lose enough value to to even be close" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [3203s-3231s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[HTF levels for runners]** "your higher time frame key levels is going to be kind of your main objective if you are holding onto some of the trailers." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2337s-2344s])
- **[HTF context for holding]** "Google was at all time highs. Price the contracts. I was down maybe for about like a minute right here. Just because of the context in Google at all time highs, I still have three contracts left. Price really never broke my soft loss or really like closed below my entry. Like the level is still being held, especially like even on the higher timeframes. Right? I'm okay with holding a few into next week." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2596s-2621s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Scaling not for short-term scalp trades]** "Short term scalp trades. You don't really want to, if you're not very confident in your setup, right? You're always going to scale a little bit more or you're going to close out the whole position." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [776s-787s])
- **[Consistency through system]** "you do want to have some sort of rulebook, something to refer back to, once you do start scaling out of your trades" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1834s-1848s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Win rate needed for early scaling]** "you're going to have to have a really high win rate, which is, okay, if you have a really, really, really high win rate, 80% plus, then maybe it makes sense to scale at like 1-to-1" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1402s-1414s])
- **[Minimum 1.5R for scaling]** "minimum 1.5 is usually a very good level." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [1499s-1503s])
- **[1-2 contracts need 1.5R minimum]** "with one to two contracts... probably at least good to have 1.5 minimum no matter what." (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2868s-2879s])
- **[1-2 contracts break-even trap]** "if you are trading with 1 to 2 contracts right and you take this trade 10 times if you have a like 60% win rate or even a little bit higher maybe 70% you're going to be pretty close to break even just because of the nature of options just because of how they work because of fills" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2903s-2925s])
- **[10 contracts vs 1-2 contracts math]** "if you have 10 contracts and you have the exact same win rate a lot of the times you're going to enter here and price is going to go way higher so these contracts that you sell up here are going to make up for a lot of these like probably three of those losses" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [2925s-2943s])
- **[Personal monthly numbers]** "the last like seven months I want to say has been like around 20,000 a month trading options intraday" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [178s-185s])
- **[Zero DTE cutoff time]** "rule of thumb for zero days just 11 o'clock is like the rule just like don't don't hold any after that" (boot-camp-recordings_day-8-scaling-with-options [4315s-4323s])

# EXTRACTION: building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-retest
- **[Dip and Rip as break-and-retest across days]** "this is what's called the dip and rip and what we like to see is price dip into a key level... this is basically the exact same thing as a break and retest, but it would just happen the next day" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [244s-249s])
- **[Third entry model = previous day high retest]** "your third entry model in this case is gonna be that previous day high retest... which is the retest that we look much later into the day which would be the final retest as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [499s-508s])
- **[Third entry doesn't always occur]** "the third one doesn't always happen but if it does happen usually it's an immediate retest and it happens fairly quickly within usually the same candle as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [933s-941s])
- **[First two entries guaranteed]** "the first two... these two ones are guaranteed to happen every single time no matter what right the third one doesn't always happen" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [926s-933s])
- **[Flat top break = most bullish pattern]** "when you're looking at this at least on the higher time frames it's what's called the flat top break and this is one of the most bullish patterns that we always look for" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [991s-995s])
- **[Flat top = building liquidity]** "whenever we have a flat top forming it's just building up liquidity... we'll have a nice push towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [969s-977s])

## 2. One-candle-rule / opening-candle-retest
- **[First dip candle as entry]** "the first potential entry point that we have is gonna be that first dip so that first dip you like to see get bought up... we like to see usually form something like this which is our hammer stick candle... a lower-wick put in body candles close strong at the highs" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [393s-420s])
- **[First dip entry is aggressive]** "this is primarily an a really aggressive entry so number one fairly aggressive entry" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [423s-427s])
- **[First candle retests require strong HTF thesis]** "these first candle retests you have to be very aggressive. I would not recommend anyone taking these first candle minute retest unless you had a really solid higher time frame thesis" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [1785s-1792s])
- **[Wait for five-minute candle to develop]** "you want to wait for the first at least five in a candle to develop" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [1793s-1797s])

## 3. 84% rule / re-entries
- **[Reclaim entry for dip and rip]** "the other entries that we look for are the ones that we usually talk about more which is the reclaim... it's kind of like the 84% where price gravitates back towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [450s-466s])
- **[Reclaim of opening range high as entry]** "the reclaim of either your opening range high your reclaim above your pre-market highs or your previous day high" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [450s-457s])

## 4. Order blocks
- NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key levels
- **[Previous day highs as key level for dip and rip]** "what we like to see is price dip into a key level... this can be your previous day highs right some sort of old higher old low" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [249s-279s])
- **[Previous day highs as support after break]** "as long as it maintains your previous day highs... you can expect this to continue back towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [1546s-1551s])
- **[Monday/Tuesday highs as key levels]** "key levels for major indices are still gonna be Monday and Tuesday's highs we have created" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3812s-3817s])
- **[Inside day = no clear opening play]** "coming to today all we had at least right off the bat was just more consolidation... we had that inside bar day that we talked about" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3970s-3987s])

## 6. Time-of-day
- **[Opening plays within first 30 minutes]** "the opening plays always happen within the first 30 minutes... you like to see them happen within the first 30 minutes of market open" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [546s-557s])
- **[Game plan for first 30 minutes to 1.5 hours]** "the game plan for the first 30 minutes to an hour and a half basically up until 11" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3889s-3895s])
- **[Wait for red folder news events]** "what I expect to happen today is the market is consolidation for the first 30 minutes until red folder news event" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3805s-3809s])
- **[Friday + inside day + news = wait]** "we had news at 10 o'clock so you're gonna most likely wait for the first 30 minutes anyways" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3987s-3993s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Scaling with 1 contract is difficult]** "if you're gonna trade with one contract... you only can scale once and the options work the best because when it's like exponential... the value in itself is not linear" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [4098s-4335s])
- **[Trailers can outperform initial scale]** "if you sell 90% of high of day but you leave 10% for trailers to like let's say an external level your 10% potentially as an opportunity to make more than your 90%" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [4351s-4358s])
- **[Options advantage for scaling]** "that wouldn't occur if you're trading anything else like stocks or futures it only occurs when you're trading options" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [4358s-4364s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[HTF thesis required for aggressive entries]** "the only reason why this is a good entry is because of the higher time frame thesis with along with the news as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [1162s-1169s])
- **[Day two continuation after news event]** "the first the second day it usually offers you at least your pre-market highs or your high day... the first dip usually gets bought up on day number two especially after day number one if there's a bullish news event" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2012s-2027s])
- **[Day three = retest of day one high]** "day number three what will happen is will come back down for the retest of your day number one high... this would be just a retest of your day number one high for this continuation towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2954s-2964s])
- **[Day three/four less high probability]** "day three and four they're less high probability right and it's harder to kind of calculate" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2913s-2917s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[A+ setup criteria - news + HTF]** "bigger picture higher time frames or we have any sort of news that's in the market these two if you have these two checked off that means you have a potential for what's called the like an A plus setup" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2154s-2166s])
- **[Setup hierarchy: news/HTF > technicals > order flow]** "the first thing that we're gonna be looking for that's more important than anything else is number one... bigger picture higher time frames or we have any sort of news... the next two will be your technical analysis your actual setup in itself and then your type of order flow slash volume" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2148s-2176s])
- **[Pure scalping = no news + no HTF]** "if we're purely scalping that usually means we do not have this... we do not have this and all we're doing is just looking for this in this right this I would consider primarily scalping" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2181s-2194s])
- **[Dip and rip requires bullish context]** "the conditions have to be right so we're not looking for the dip and rip in this market... if you try and trade the dip and rip in this condition right now it's gonna be very difficult just because of the context" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [1190s-1253s])
- **[No news = wait for price action around key levels]** "whenever there isn't news you want to be a little bit more cautious and you just want to wait for the price action around the key levels" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [2777s-2783s])
- **[Inside day = no A+ setup]** "I really didn't want to take anything I kind of like test a bit I was on like super light size where I was like okay potentially could let had lower, but I wasn't an A plus setup" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3785s-3792s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[1 contract needs 2R potential]** "instead of selling at high of day... you look for trades with a two R potential... let's say you're win rate decreases to 40% this is more profitable than... your one R with 50% because one R with 50% is break even" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [4285s-4306s])
- **[50% win rate at 1:1 = break even]** "a 50% win rate and let's say you have a one to one then you're basically break even" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [4252s-4259s])
- **[40% win rate at 2R = profitable]** "if you have two R with 40% that means you're a profitable trader" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [4301s-4306s])
- **[8 trades: 6 wins, 2 losses]** "this week I took actually eight trades two-failed six win" (building-your-profitable-system_Dip_Rip.txt [3108s-3111s])
