# Extracted Trading Rules

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Opening range break as entry model** "your first entry is gonna be a breakdown... it's the same thing as a reclaim but it's towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [635s-638s])
- **Retest preferred over breakout** "we'll always look for the retest instead of the actual opening range break in itself" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [665s-668s])
- **Opening range break and retest as second entry** "this is the second opportunity that you could be looking for which is the retest now of this gap fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1330s-1336s])
- **Opening range break as aggressive entry** "this is your first entry model, which is your opening range break... as soon as it closes underneath right you can look to enter into a position with their stop just a break above" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1295s-1314s])
- **Breakdown entry for gap fill** "your first entry is gonna be a breakdown... price pushes back towards the downside... we break back underneath this low and that's gonna be a super aggressive entrance" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [635s-660s])
- **Conservative entry is retest of opening range** "your second entry model which is the retest... a retest of either your five minute opening range your one minute opening range" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [882s-890s])
- **Third entry model is order block** "your third entry model which is an order block... this one's a little bit more difficult to understand... doesn't always set up" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [896s-908s])
- **Order block as later entry** "your third entry model will be order blocks... it's a much later entry so if you missed the first two you could be looking for the third order block" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1408s-1422s])
- **Order block less high probability** "just know this one depending on the overall context and that might not be as high probability as the first two" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1422s-1427s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** - The term "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as a named setup is not mentioned in this transcript.

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **84% reclaim concept mentioned** "let's say an 84% real reclaim what happens is we break underneath your previous level support and then price kind of reclaims is key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [639s-647s])
- **84% reclaim similar to downside** "similar to the downside will happen" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [648s-650s])

## 4. Order Blocks
- **Order block as third entry model** "your third entry model which is an order block... this one's a little bit more difficult to understand... doesn't always set up" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [896s-908s])
- **Order block can occur at various locations** "the order block can occur in the middle of the gap or it can occur at the very bottom of the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [924s-929s])
- **Order block for gap fill continuation** "your third entry model in all... that's like an order block... you could just mark out your down close candles" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1401s-1413s])
- **Order block as later, lower probability entry** "it's a much later entry so if you missed the first two you could be looking for the third order block... just know this one depending on the overall context and that might not be as high probability as the first two" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1417s-1427s])
- **Prefer order block before gap fill target** "preferably you would like to see it before it hits the gap fill... you'd much rather look to trade an order block let's say this up close candle before it hits your gap fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1438s-1445s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Previous day high/low as key levels for gap fill** "we have a gap in towards like a previous day close right previous day high previous day low like some sort of old high and old low" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [366s-373s])
- **Gap into key level = caution for reversal** "if we gap in towards a key area you want to be very cautious because there's a potential for that reversal and the reversal is a gap fill trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [373s-385s])
- **Opening range high/low as key levels** "we always mark out the five and the one minute... your first five minute candle high and five minute candle low drawn is also coincided with the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1245s-1259s])
- **Opening range coincides with gap** "if you can break outside of the five minute opening range that also means we're breaking inside the gap as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1259s-1264s])
- **Previous day close as gap fill target** "your previous day close was right here and what's gonna create is what's called the gap in the market" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [475s-481s])
- **FOMC lows as key level** "your FOMC lows, which is really key... also coincides with your pre-market lows, which also coincides with the higher time frame key levels" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1988s-1993s])
- **Half gap as target for large gaps** "if the gap is huge... I'm gonna mark out 50% in the gap... if the gap is a fairly small range... what you want to see is just this full gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1776s-1789s])
- **Half gap lines up with other key levels** "you can draw basically half the gap... which also lines up with this key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1919s-1928s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Best gap fills happen within first 30 minutes** "the best gap fills will happen right off the bat... within the first 30 minutes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [559s-571s])
- **Gap fill within first 30 minutes = fast fill** "if you have a gap and then it originally pushes within the direction of the gap... it'll probably fill within the first 30 minutes of market open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1828s-1839s])
- **Later gap fills take longer** "if price kind of consolidates and then eventually heads back in towards the gap like after the first 30 minutes maybe later into the day... it's gonna take a little bit longer to fill the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1845s-1856s])
- **Gap fill can happen next day** "it can happen right off the bat it can happen later into the day it can happen the next day as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [355s-361s])
- **CPI/PPI news days** "CPI PPI their big news events and a lot of the times what will happen is we'll end up whatever way the market goes we usually go the opposite direction" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1667s-1677s])
- **News candle behavior** "if a news candle at least a red folder news... we'll take out the news high and they'll come back down and take out the news low... majority of the time" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1684s-1697s])
- **News in line with expectations = chop** "if the news is not clear... it's just gonna take out the high take out the low and just chop everyone around" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1748s-1754s])
- **Unexpected news = continuation** "if the news is unexpected and we have a crazy beat... that's when you can have that continuation" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1754s-1763s])

## 7. Exits
- **Gap fill as main target** "your gap fills always going to be a key target that you're going to be looking to take profits at" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1100s-1105s])
- **Scale 50% at gap fill** "let's say you sold 50% at this gap fill or maybe more... let's say you sold 75%" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2076s-2084s])
- **Trail after gap fill** "you're looking for continuation held trailers in this case this would have been one of those scenarios where your trailers would have made more money" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2084s-2089s])
- **Half gap as partial target** "you could look to take profits or you could just wait for that full gap fill which majority of the times that full gap fill will hit" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1804s-1810s])
- **After gap fill = different thesis** "once it fills the main gap objective after that then it's gonna be... you're gonna be looking for other confluence... the gap fill trade in itself is just down towards this gap fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1470s-1484s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **Gap fill outside range needs HTF thesis** "the only time you want to look for a gap fill outside the range is if there's some sort of higher time frame thesis of both" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2185s-2191s])
- **Gap down into HTF key level = buy dip** "the only way you can look to buy the dip is if there's some sort of higher time frame thesis" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2349s-2358s])
- **HTF trend determines gap fill vs gap and go** "if we're in a trending market... those gaps a lot of times will be left unfilled because they're called breakaway gaps" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [786s-797s])
- **Ranging markets favor gap fills** "whenever we have gaps and markets are ranging... what's more likely to happen that price is gonna reverse back inside the gap fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [302s-312s])
- **15-minute thesis for key levels** "if I'm a thesis on the 15 minute like let's have a key level... majority of the time... you want to wait for a five minute candle closure and then you can go into the one minute to look for an entry" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3771s-3789s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **Gap fill is a reversal trade** "the gap fill in this case is more of a reversal type trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [104s-110s])
- **Gap fill is intraday setup** "my personal favorite setups a gap fill for me is what's called an intraday setup... there's no really higher time frames involved" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3273s-3279s])
- **Lighter size on reversal trades** "if I do take these gap fill trades they're usually on much lighter size because it's a reversal trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3297s-3301s])
- **Continuation trades get bigger size** "my best trades come when I have continuations" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3301s-3306s])
- **Reversal trades harder to time** "when you're trying to time a reversal it's a lot more difficult because... you also kind of have to get the timing right as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3329s-3338s])
- **Continuation preferred for options** "I look for continuation because if I have a continuation then most likely as long as it holds my key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3346s-3353s])
- **Gap fill criteria: ranging market** "one of the criteria that we like to look for when looking at a gap fill is ranging markets" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [317s-319s])
- **Gap fill criteria: gap into key level** "we have a gap into a key level... if we gap in towards a key area you want to be very cautious" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [366s-379s])
- **Gap fill criteria: sweep of liquidity, break of structure** "a sweep of liquidity break a structure and trade inside the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [395s-399s])
- **No news = gap fill** "if there's no news... majority times we're gonna look to fill that gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [761s-766s])
- **News = potential gap and go** "if there's some sort of external factor in this case which would be a strong news event then that's a catalyst that can push the market away from the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [766s-774s])
- **Spy vs QQQ gap differences** "you can sometimes look at the gaps in the differences between spy and the Q's because they're not always the same" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1109s-1115s])
- **QQQ closes 15 min later than SPY** "the Q's closes at 4:15 compared to spy that close at 4pm... sometimes at the very end of the day in the last 15 minutes after market closes you'll have some sort of action where it'll mess up the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1153s-1173s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Gaps fill 70% of time in 48-hour period** "gaps fill 70% of the time in a 48 hour period" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [320s-326s])
- **70% in 40-hour period** "70% of the time in a 40 hour period" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [344s-352s])
- **Gap fill within first 30 minutes = best** "the best gap fills that will happen will occur within the first 30 minutes of market open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1825s-1833s])
- **Gap fill within first hour to hour and a half** "if we have a push right off the bat then most likely we can fill the gap within the first hour to an hour and a half" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2427s-2432s])

## 11. Gap Fill Specifics
- **Definition: gap between yesterday's close and today's open** "we need a gap in between yesterday's close in today's open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [217s-225s])
- **New York session gap** "the market closed which is at 4pm Eastern standard time... and then it opens at 9:30am EST... this was what's called a New York session gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [233s-242s])
- **Gap = imbalance/inefficiency** "whenever we have gaps in the markets... these gaps or what's called imbalances so it's an inefficiency in the market" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [328s-333s])
- **Draw gaps on 1 and 5 minute chart** "the best time to draw the gaps are on the 1 and 5 minute chart" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1055s-1061s])
- **Regular trading hours to see gaps** "if you click on the regular trading hours, you're gonna see a lot of these imbalances in the markets" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1006s-1018s])
- **Two types: gap fill inside range and outside range** "there's two different types of gap fills. There's a gap fill where usually gaps above its previous day highs or gaps below its previous day lows... and then there's another one where we gap and we just have a gap inside of the range" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [409s-419s])
- **Gap fill inside range = clearer setup** "inside the range it's pretty clear because it's just a gap inside the range" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2180s-2185s])
- **Gap fill outside range = trickier** "this is a gap fill that is kind of a little bit different and in my opinion a little bit more trickier to have because now you have to have a higher time frame thesis" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2170s-2180s])
- **Interday gaps vs daily gaps** "we're just strictly talking about interday gaps that we can see within the past like three to five sessions... whereas there's some gaps as well we'll see on the daily time frame which are called their daily gaps" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [2957s-2969s])
- **Daily gaps as targets** "these are gaps that we're gonna look to target... you can see how well price is respecting these gap levels" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3005s-3023s])

## 12. Market Regime / Context
- **Ranging markets favor gap fills** "whenever we have gaps and markets are ranging... what's more likely to happen that price is gonna reverse back inside the gap fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [302s-312s])
- **Trending markets favor gap and go** "if we're in a trending market... those gaps a lot of times will be left unfilled because they're called breakaway gaps" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [786s-797s])
- **SPY/QQQ efficient, gaps usually fill** "spy cues... it's gonna come back if there's a gap it's gonna come back and fill it because it's as I mentioned 70% of time in a 40 hour period" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3614s-3619s])
- **Individual stocks more volatile** "something like Tesla... everyone's trying to find the fair value of Tesla... that's why it's so volatile in both ways" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3688s-3701s])
- **Conflicting theses = harder trading** "whenever you have a bunch of like opposing theses that's why it makes it a little bit harder to trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [4016s-4021s])
- **Clear trend = easy trading** "if we have a clear trend it's super easy everything's aligning... that's when it's very easy to trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [4075s-4084s])

## 13. Position Management / Sizing
- **Lighter size on reversal gap fills** "if I do take these gap fill trades they're usually on much lighter size because it's a reversal trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3297s-3301s])
- **Bigger size on continuation trades** "my best trades come when I have continuations" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3301s-3306s])
- **Size based on HTF confluence + news** "whenever I size up I personally look for higher time frames plus right any sort of news factors" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3286s-3291s])

## 14. Gap Fill vs Gap and Go Differentiation
- **News is main differentiator** "how can we differentiate gap and go from gap fills should we look at news? Yes... news is the main driving factor" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1584s-1597s])
- **Gap above resistance = gap and go** "if we gap above your previous day highs then we could be looking for continuation right off the bat" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1622s-1628s])
- **Gap into resistance = gap fill** "if we're gapping in towards your previous day highs and there's a better chance of price pushing back down towards a gap fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1628s-1636s])
- **Momentum away from gap = gap and go** "the chart starts showing you momentum of the opening range away from the gap and look for gap and go" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [1607s-1612s])

## 15. Trade Review / Judgment
- **Today's gap fill was slow, not ideal for options** "this is like this is very difficult to trade in options just because you're trying to time it... if you're trading especially zero days it's gonna be much trickier holding through all this chopping consolidation" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [4159s-4190s])
- **Yesterday's gap fill had speed because inside range + key level bounce** "yesterday this moved with speed because it was inside the range is bouncing off a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3318s-3322s])
- **Today's gap fill slow despite support bounce** "today you can see bounce off support but you can see how slow it ended up moving" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3322s-3324s])
- **Quadwitching Friday added volatility** "it's a Friday where you had quadwitching... I knew that there was gonna be a lot of volatility" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3539s-3554s])
- **Aggressive entry on ES with 5 point risk** "I put on five points of risk here on the ES I thought if we get some continuation it would be worth it" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3544s-3547s])
- **Trade didn't work out, but followed the book** "technically I follow the book pretty well the trade just didn't work out like it is what it is it happens" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Fill.txt [3570s-3574s])

# EXTRACTION FROM: building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **Gap and go IS a form of break and retest** "this is just a break and retest that happens right off the bat because if we have a gap, right, it's basically a gap and go. But it's another form of a break and retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [531s-537s])
- **Gap above key level = previous resistance becomes support** "since we're above it, this level your previous day highs, you'd like to turn into support for continuation back towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [511s-516s])
- **Immediate retest of previous day high is most aggressive entry** "your first entry in this case would be the immediate retest of the previous day high or some sort of key level. This one is one of the most aggressive entry models" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [593s-604s])
- **Aggressive entry works best with bullish news** "this aggressive entry model is really good if you have some sort of news that you're very much bullish and you want to see continuation towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [604s-611s])
- **Opening range break retest is the last entry model** "the opening range break retest is the last entry model. And this is the entry model we like to look for. But sometimes when prices very much bullish it doesn't always come back for the retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [631s-640s])
- **Three entry models for gap and go: immediate retest, reclaim, opening range break retest** "the few entry models that we have is your immediate retest. And then we have your reclaim, which would be your reclaim of your opening range, your reclaim of your pre-market highs... And then the last one is going to be the opening range break" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1443s-1465s])
- **First two entries always set up, third doesn't always** "Number one and two always set up no matter what. Your third one, the opening range break and retest doesn't always set up because it doesn't always come back for a retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1465s-1477s])
- **Want to trade as close to key levels as possible** "you always want to be trading as close to your key levels as possible" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3367s-3372s])
- **Pullback toward gap is perfect for entry** "if it pulls back in towards a gap that's perfect because it's pulling back in towards a key level of support" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3372s-3378s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **First candle dictates market direction** "usually that first candle is going to dictate what the market's going to want to do" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [767s-770s])
- **Opening candle high and low are critical** "that candle high and that candle low. That's why we always have that candle marked out is because this is going to indicate if price wants to continue towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [771s-780s])
- **Break below opening candle = potential gap fill** "if we break underneath that open candle, what's going to happen? That technically means we're going to break underneath its previous day highs once again and we could be looking for the gap fill back towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [784s-792s])
- **Hold above previous day highs + break up = continuation** "if we hold your previous day highs and then we break back towards the upside, this is going to indicate that potentially we can continue higher on the overall market" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [792s-801s])
- **Opening plays + opening candles + logic = clearer picture** "if we combine these opening plays with the opening candles as well, plus we have logic built in. It's going to give us a lot better of a clear picture of what the market wants to do" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [801s-812s])
- **930 candle is the opening candle to mark** "the 930 candle is your opening candle and that's the one that you can mark up for at least your opening range" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3764s-3769s])
- **First five-minute candle also important** "for the five minute you want to mark up that first five minute candle" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3770s-3774s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **84% rule used for reclaim confirmation** "we like to see the body close back above a key level to be indicated. And that's why we use the 84% roll, the 84% roll, right. We like to see the body can't close back above a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1045s-1054s])
- **84% rule applies to downside reclaims too** "a body can't close underneath your previous day lows for that to set back up once again" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1054s-1059s])
- **Wick above level is NOT a reclaim** "if the candle cannot close above this high, that would just indicate that sellers are stepping back in and you couldn't reclaim that level because of this upper wick" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1030s-1039s])
- **Re-enter after stop out using 84% rule** "I have that 84% rule in my toolbox so I would rather cut my losses short and always look to re-enter" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4249s-4254s])
- **Re-entry can be at better opportunity with better context** "I can always look to re-enter at an even nicer opportunity or when things or when the confidence line up a lot better" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4265s-4270s])
- **84% rule lesson available in technical analysis section** "84% rule this is a really good lesson underneath technical analysis" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4385s-4390s])
- **Reclaim requires body closure above level** "the body has to get back above your key level. So it could wick, but as I mentioned, the bodies is what matters, how it closes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1012s-1022s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **Down close candles as order blocks on gap and go** "every single down close candle, if you marked out right, Marko down close candles, you can just see it end up using a nice every single down close candle. We're suspected" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1382s-1392s])
- **Order blocks used for entry on retest** "once it retested down close candles, right order blocks. And also at your five minute opening range for this nice push towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1360s-1364s])
- **One minute order block as entry** "we ended up getting it on this one minute order block" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4547s-4553s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **Gap above previous day high = key level** "we like to see a gap above some sort of key level, this can be your previous day high, previous day low. Some sort of old higher low in the market. Basically, just gapping above a key resistance level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [307s-321s])
- **Always mark previous day highs and lows** "we always mark out your previous day highs, previous day lows. You really want to see the reaction that we have off this level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [394s-399s])
- **Pre-market highs as key level** "your pre-market highs, which is our 260. Our 260 is going to be key" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1181s-1186s])
- **Previous week's high as key level** "this is your previous week's high. And for gapping above your previous week's high, what do we like to see? Really, we like to see this previous week's high hold for a continuation towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1157s-1171s])
- **Multiple touches build liquidity at level** "there's a lot of liquidity being built up around this one 50. So if we can get above this level, what do we like to see? It's what's called this gap and go towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1734s-1738s])
- **Reclaim defined** "reclaim is just a reclaim of a key level... a reclaim is when price just gets back above a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [898s-915s])
- **Reclaim can be previous day highs or opening range** "it can be a previous day highs and it can be your one minute, five minute opening range, whatever a reclaim is it just gets back above your level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [919s-929s])
- **Breakdown = opposite of reclaim for downside** "for the downside, I just call it a break back down... it's the exact same thing once it breaks back below a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [945s-959s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **Opening plays happen within first 30 minutes** "with all these opening plays, they usually like to see them happen within the first 30 minutes of market open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [668s-676s])
- **Gap and go and opening drive are more aggressive, happen right off the bat** "if you have a gap and go or you have a break away gap, you have an opening drive play. These ones are more aggressive. And you like to see these ones happen right off the bat" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [676s-685s])
- **Most traders lose money in first 30 minutes** "if you look at your statistics and you go and back test it and you see that let's say you lose majority of the time within the first 30 minutes... then just don't trade the first 30 minutes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4073s-4088s])
- **Speaker was profitable after 10am, losing before 10am** "I made money like after 10 o'clock I was very profitable after 10 o'clock but before 10 o'clock I was actually losing money" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4098s-4108s])
- **Wait for first 30 minutes unless clear opening play** "just wait for the first 30 minutes right wait for the first 30 minutes the play out unless you have a super strong clear opening play" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4148s-4158s])
- **After 10am setups are clearer, risk easier to manage** "you can manage risk a lot easier after 10 o'clock the setups are a lot more clear as well because it's a five minute setup" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4158s-4162s])
- **Opening plays are more difficult, happen quickly** "the opening plays they are more difficult as well because they happen so quick a lot people usually end up getting a bad entry" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4404s-4413s])
- **Stay away from open unless opening play** "when you're trading close to the open unless you have an opening play you want to use the stay away from the open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4069s-4073s])

## 7. Exits

- **Scale majority at pre-market highs target** "I end up hitting your pre-market highs. And that's where I scaled majority of my position" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2972s-2976s])
- **Sell as it approaches pre-market highs target** "I was looking to sell as it went up into words is pre-market highs... I was only looking to sell as it approaches my pre-market highs because it's a very possible target that everyone's looking to have" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3875s-3901s])
- **Tight stop because can always re-enter** "I have a very tight stop because I can always look to enter right in" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4219s-4224s])
- **Cut loss short, re-enter later** "I would rather much cut my position instead of holding through all this and then looking to re-entering the exact same spot" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4231s-4240s])
- **If it "has to drop" and doesn't within 1-2 candles, exit** "I want to see Tesla drop within the next one or two candles if it doesn't drop I do not want to see a push back towards upside whatsoever" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3962s-3971s])
- **Bad scaling on AMD trade** "I ended up doing a very bad job of scaling on this trade... I scaled up in towards that 156 contracts and then moving like a hundred percent as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2370s-2389s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **Gap and goes work best in trending markets** "the best time to trade the gap and goes is when we have some sort of trending market" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [345s-350s])
- **Bull market = gap and goes, bear market = gap fills and pop and fades** "when the markets in an uptrend, basically there was a plenty of examples from like 2024... every single higher time frame on the daily charts on a video we're just looking for the gap and goes because price was very much bullish" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [845s-865s])
- **All timeframes aligned = gap and go** "every single time frame was aligned where that's exactly when you want to see the gap and goes happen" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [870s-878s])
- **Currently in scalpers market, not bull market** "right now currently we're not in a bull market, we're basically in a scalpers market. So this playbook will come in handy once the market flips" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1585s-1595s])
- **Current playbook is pop and fades, gap fills** "right now the playbook is basically pop and fades, right gap fills" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1595s-1599s])
- **Gap and go on individual stocks with news, not indices** "on the indices themselves I usually don't look for breakaway gaps... whereas on the tech side I can play breakaway gaps" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3617s-3627s])
- **Day one after earnings = best for continuation** "day one earnings play nice push towards upside... day one almost always has continuation day two" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3644s-3676s])
- **Three gap ups in a row = caution for mean reversion** "if you have a couple gap ups in a row then you can be looking for like the fourth day usually a good signal that you want to be very cautious" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3691s-3704s])
- **Don't short the trend, just play continuation** "if you are just capturing the trend originally you could have made a lot more money than trying to short day two or short day three at this all-time highs" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3719s-3728s])
- **Gap and go towards downside less common, more cautious** "I do not like playing these gap downs and continuation lower just because a lot of times when you're having a gap and go set up we're gapping above a key level of resistance... on gap downs usually we're kind of gapping down into some sort of higher time for your level support" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4683s-4701s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **A+ setup on AMD** "this was an A plus out of its ideal conditions" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1904s-1909s])
- **A+ setup on Nvidia** "this was like a textbook A plus setup after earnings play with a gap and go" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2976s-2981s])
- **Everything checked off = aggressive sizing** "we had everything checked off. We had I think news this day as well. So even had news. So there's a lot of factors that played into a fact why price was this bullish and why I was looking to be this aggressive coming into market open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2421s-2436s])
- **Gap and go requires: trending market + news + key level break** "there's really only strict criteria for it because you need higher time frames to usually like to see some sort of news event as well. If it's going to gap above a key area of basically a key area of resistance" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3087s-3097s])
- **Gap and go is discretionary, harder to backtest** "it's a little bit trickier to back test these ones because they are a bit more discretionary because you have to have higher time frames. You have to have news. You have to have a trending market" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3139s-3148s])
- **Gap and go = personal favorite setup** "the gap and goes are my personal favorite setups" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2474s-2478s])
- **60 contracts on AMD, $10,000 position** "what I had here was 60 contracts. This was a $10,000 position size" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1763s-1770s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Gaps fill 70% of time in 48 hour period** "gaps fill 70% of the time in a 48 hour period. So you'll see 70% of the time it fills in a 40 hour period" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [250s-257s])
- **30% of gaps left unfilled** "30% of the time the gaps are left unfilled in a 40 hour period" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [267s-272s])
- **Gap and go happens 30% of the time** "these ones only happen 30% of the time" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3108s-3113s])
- **200% move on Amazon gap and go** "this was like 200% move in about... seven minutes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4753s-5049s])
- **IV crush: contracts lost 50% value then moved 200%** "the contracts how the moved on this... from here they lost like 50% of its value and then basically within however long it moved like 200% within seven minutes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [5038s-5049s])

## 11. Gap and Go Definition and Criteria

- **Gap between yesterday's close and today's open** "the criteria for a gap and go. So basically what we need in this case is a gap in between yesterday's close and today's open" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [212s-222s])
- **Gap occurs from 4pm EST to 9:30am EST** "that gap occurs from 4 PM Eastern Standard Time to 930 AM Eastern Standard Time. And so it's called our New York" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [228s-234s])
- **Gap and go = trending market, gap fill = ranging market** "what we had previously for a gap fill is what's called a ranging market. So whenever market ranges, we'd like to see gaps fill" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [239s-250s])
- **Key criteria: gap above key level + hold above + continue away from gap** "what we like to see is a hold above the key level and continue away from the gap. So that's where that breakaway gap comes from" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [321s-330s])
- **Two scenarios at open: drop below PDH for gap fill, or hold PDH for gap and go** "there's really only two scenarios. So the two scenarios, number one, potential drop, get underneath your previous day highs. And we can be looking for the gap fill towards the downside... another scenario is if we could hold your previous day highs, we can get what's called the breakaway gap or the gap and go continuation towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [404s-423s])
- **Always open to playing both sides** "we're always going to mark up and we're going to be open to playing both sides" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [428s-432s])
- **Gap and go = breakaway gap** "this is what's called the gap and go or what's called the breakaway gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [262s-267s])
- **Remove extended hours to see gap clearly** "if you want to see the gaps more clear, you can get rid of the extended trading hours and you can just have the regular trading hours on" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [459s-469s])
- **Gap and go works best with elevated ATR** "a gap and go occurs the best times that the gap goes, like the breakaway gaps is when the ATR is elevated" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1956s-1961s])

## 12. Reclaim Setup

- **Reclaim = getting back above a key level** "reclaim is just a reclaim of a key level... a reclaim is when price just gets back above a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [898s-915s])
- **Body must close above for valid reclaim** "the body has to get back above your key level. So it could wick, but as I mentioned, the bodies is what matters, how it closes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1012s-1022s])
- **Wick above then rejection = NOT reclaim** "if the candle cannot close above this high, that would just indicate that sellers are stepping back in and you couldn't reclaim that level because of this upper wick" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1030s-1039s])
- **84% rule confirms reclaim** "we like to see the body close back above a key level to be indicated. And that's why we use the 84% roll" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1045s-1054s])
- **Reclaim can be on any timeframe** "it's whatever time frame that you're on is the reclaim" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1909s-1914s])
- **Five minute timeframe preferred for stronger reclaim** "a five minute, it depends on your key level... obviously, the higher time frames are going to hold more power. So I prefer a five minute time frame" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1074s-1094s])

## 13. IV Flush / IV Crush

- **IV flush happens on first pullback after earnings** "whenever the IV happens you want some sort of pullback in the overall markets and in the first two minutes you had these pretty big upper wicks and the IV cooled off" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3440s-3450s])
- **One candle pullback is enough for IV to cool** "all you need is just one candle because then I'll pull off the IV" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3493s-3500s])
- **If first candle launches and keeps going, no IV flush opportunity** "if it moves higher within the first candle then you probably don't want to touch it" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3471s-3476s])
- **IV flush = contracts lose value then recover** "the contracts from this pullback right here it absolutely destroyed the contracts and we got it for really cheap" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4957s-4962s])
- **IV crush example: 50% loss then 200% gain** "from here they lost like 50% of its value and then basically within however long it moved like 200% within seven minutes" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [5038s-5049s])
- **IV elevated during earnings season** "IV's elevated usually there's something kind of going on like Fridays is usually elevated during earnings seasons" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3791s-3798s])
- **Day before earnings = avoid due to IV** "the day before earnings you do not want to touch that day because they're the expected move there's going to be a big move" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3806s-3812s])

## 14. Gap Fill vs Gap and Go Differentiation

- **News is main differentiator for breakaway gaps** "the reason why we always like to trade breakaway gaps on names like the individual tickers is because there's some sort of news" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3552s-3558s])
- **Indices fill gaps 70% of time, individual stocks can breakaway** "when I talk about the gaps filling 70% of the time that happens on the major indices because the major indices are really efficient" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3584s-3590s])
- **Individual stocks have earnings/news catalysts for breakaway** "in video they can have earnings you can have any sort of a news event that's going to push the market" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3612s-3617s])
- **Indices = reversals/mean reversion, tech = breakaway gaps** "on the indices themselves I usually don't look for breakaway gaps... majority of the times if I do trade the indices are going to be reversals mean reversions whereas on the tech side I can play breakaway gaps" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3617s-3627s])
- **Gap above resistance = gap and go, gap into resistance = gap fill** (confirms previous extraction) "if we gap above a key level of resistance... what you don't want to see is it stay at the underneath your level of resistance" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2580s-2588s])

## 15. Trade Review / Judgment

- **AMD trade was A+ but bad scaling** "this was an A plus out of its ideal conditions... I ended up doing a very bad job of scaling on this trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [1904s-2376s])
- **Nvidia earnings trade was textbook A+** "this was like a textbook A plus setup after earnings play with a gap and go" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [2976s-2981s])
- **Tesla trade: had to drop immediately, didn't, cut loss** "I want to see Tesla drop within the next one or two candles if it doesn't drop I do not want to see a push back towards upside whatsoever" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [3962s-3971s])
- **Tesla trade: bigger loss than expected due to slippage** "I took a bigger loss than I expected even though I had a super solid entry just because of the kind of slippage that I had" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4029s-4038s])
- **Friday trading adds volatility** "especially on Fridays it would have been a bigger loss" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4034s-4038s])
- **Context matters for re-entry** "the context now is a lot weaker on the cues now in the second time because now we're underneath your what your pre-market lows" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4286s-4296s])
- **Same setup, different time and context = different outcome** "exact same setup exact same stop exact same targets but what's the difference the difference is the time and now also the context as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Gap_Go.txt [4350s-4362s])

# Extraction from building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **Valid break definition** "If we're underneath your previous day lows, majority of the times where we want to be looking for is around to short around your previous day lows. If we're above your previous day highs, right, we want to be looking for the retest of your previous day highs for continuation." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [735s-747s])

- **Retest entry - wick vs body** "what you'd like to see is the best retest happen when it wicks inside your zone and then shows some strength. Right? Because that would indicate if it broke above, right? If it wicks inside your zone, usually it would indicate some sort of hammer candle. Right? So this would be the most bullish case." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [2684s-2698s])

- **Retest - consolidation vs confirmation** "If it comes back into your zone, right? Kind of consolidate here doesn't really show too much strong price action. Then usually we would have to wait for what's called like a confirmation candle to show some strength outside of the zone." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [2702s-2714s])

- **Gap above key level - only two scenarios** "Whenever you have a gap like this and it gaps above a key area, which is your previous day high, there are only two scenarios that you'll be looking for. Number one, gap and go. Right. Break above right gaps above a key level, which is your previous day high. Now what you like to see is a retest of your previous day high for a continue push back towards upside. This is scenario number one. Right. scenario number two is a gap fell. Right. Break underneath, retest continuation towards the downside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3294s-3323s])

- **Previous day high break and retest - the setup** "This is one of the setups that we always look for right, a previous day high break and retests or your previous day lows. Right. And the how you play this is if it breaks above, we're proves to high. Let's say in the pre-market, right, we're going to be looking for the retests of your previous day high for continuation. If it's below your previous day low in the pre-market, we're going to be looking for the retests of your previous day low for continuation." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3464s-3481s])

- **Drawing level from wick to body for precision** "if you really want precision, right, you can just draw from your work to your body. Kind of drag this over. Price, as you can see right here, comes back down taps into your level perfectly." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3525s-3536s])

- **Reclaim of opening candle low as confirmation** "the key level to reclaim was your opening candle wrench low. As soon as it reclaim, that's where we're looking to get in." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3546s-3551s])

- **First-break vs waiting for retest - 84% rule reclaim** "Majority of the times, whenever you take the 84% of all, it's going to be on the reclaim. Like 90% of my trades, when I take the 84% rule is the reclaim of a trade because it doesn't always come back to retest. Right? A lot of times, if it breaks above the highs, we'll happen. It was probably just continue towards the upside. Sometimes it doesn't reclaim or sometimes it doesn't come back and retest. So majority of my trades are always the reclaim of a key area instead of waiting for the retest, because the retest doesn't always happen." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6356s-6385s])

- **Dip and rip vs break and retest** "if you want a higher probability, right, you're going to wait for that reclaim of this key area, and then you're going to be looking to risk off this swing low. Because sometimes if you look to buy that first dip, right, your risk in this case is it's technically just right at this key area where it can be a little bit harder, where if you wait for the break of structure, now you can risk off this swing low and it's a lot more clear" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3758s-3777s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** - The speaker does not use the term "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as a named concept. He refers to "opening candle prints" and "opening range" but not as a named rule.

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-Entries

- **84% rule as reclaim of key area** "if you want a super, if you want to make it, so it's higher probability, like the 84% rule, right, then you can wait for the reclaim of this." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3742s-3748s])

- **84% rule - reclaim is the primary entry** "Majority of the times, whenever you take the 84% of all, it's going to be on the reclaim. Like 90% of my trades, when I take the 84% rule is the reclaim of a trade because it doesn't always come back to retest." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6356s-6367s])

- **84% rule - reclaim of opening candle** "I had the low of the candle marked up because that's the reclaim level, right? Kind of your 84% roll as well. Once it reclaim, as you can see right here, that's when it started to show strength." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5741s-5752s])

- **84% rule - best reclaims** "Usually the best 84% roll in the best reclaims happened if it just kind of moves higher and just sudden to the gravitating towards the upside. But it could always very well just come back down towards the retouch and then set up. It's just going to take a little bit longer." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5769s-5785s])

- **84% rule - reference to prior lesson** "If you guys have any questions on the reclaim, I talked about the 84% rule last week. I think I explained it fairly well. So if you go watch the last week's lesson, it was on the 84% rule. I'll talk about kind of the pros and cons. What you should do on the reclaim, a couple other techniques as well for the 84% rule." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6415s-6432s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **Order blocks work best on ES/NQ** "If you're drawing out order blocks, they usually work the greatest. If you're trading like the ES and the NQ, because you have information that's 24-7, whereas when you're trading, let's say just for example, the QQ, you have a lot of these gaps in the chart. So that kind of messes up a lot of this information." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1317s-1331s])

- **Previous day highs/lows preferred over order blocks for QQQ** "I'm just going to use your previous day highs and lows. These are going to be your key areas. A lot of times, I'll kind of line up with order blocks. But the best ones are always, they're always going to be old highs and old lows on the chart." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1331s-1345s])

- **One minute order block** "You can also draw your one minute order block as well." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4602s-4603s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **Always the same levels** "Previous day highs, previous day lows, right, old highs, old lows. Really these are the exact same levels that we look to trade off of every single day." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [279s-288s])

- **Drawing areas vs levels** "I like drawing out areas as you guys know. Right? You can also draw levels about personally, right? When we do have so many touches, like this, right? We have one touch, two touch, three touch, four touch, five touch, right? I like drawing it in area." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [568s-582s])

- **Extended hours vs regular hours for levels** "Majority of my levels come from previous day highs and previous day lows. So in this case, you can find these exact levels as well on the regular trading hours. Right? They might not be as clear... I like using the extended trading hours... The only time I have the regular trading hours on is when I want to draw these gap levels. And then besides that, I'm always on the extended trading hours." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [916s-989s])

- **Pre-market levels vs previous day levels** "the pre-market levels, I don't really like drawing them out that much because they're not as key as levels that are very previous day highs and lows, where you can see these levels on every single timeframe, right, if you're on the 15 minute timeframe, everyone's going to see the previous day highs and lows, if you're on the one hour timeframe, everyone's going to see the previous day highs and lows, even the daily chart, everyone's going to see the previous day highs and lows. The only people that are going to see these pre-market levels are people that are on the lower timeframe." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1989s-2017s])

- **Gap fill statistics** "with the statistic is 70% of the time, within a 48 hour these gaps will look to fill." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1532s-1537s])

- **Small gaps fill faster** "if you have a very small gap with no news, it's gonna fill at day one. If it's a little bit of a bigger gap, it might fill later into the day or it might take the next day." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1689s-1704s])

- **Big gap with news takes longer** "if we are going to be looking for the gap, it might, it's probably going to take a while because we did have a pretty big gap in one day. It would be very unrealistic that this gap would fill in one day just because we had bearish news over the weekend." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1642s-1655s])

- **Levels used as both support and resistance** "these levels will be used as bullish and bear. So it's all about the reaction around these key areas." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1107s-1112s])

- **In a trend, levels have preferred direction** "if we're trading in a trend, right, let's say, for example, back here on the cues... we're going to be looking for the retests off your previous day high for continuation." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1127s-1168s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **Be cautious first 15-30 minutes** "I usually always recommend for people to be cautious that first like kind of 15 to 30 minutes. Because if you let a play out, usually have a lot more clear picture, whereas if you trade right off the bat, you really need some strict criteria." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5976s-5985s])

- **Red folder news at 10am** "we have red folder news at 10 a.m. So if you have red folder news at 10 a.m. and we're inside the range, there's probably not going to be that clear of a direction on the overall market." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4915s-4921s])

- **News is the first thing to look for** "the first thing that I really look for when coming into the pre-market is news. So if there's anything that's news driven in the markets, usually that's very important." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [211s-219s])

- **Earnings as fresh news** "something like earnings, usually anything that happens before earnings is not that impactful on the market. Because once that earnings drops, everyone's waiting for that." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [242s-250s])

## 7. Exits

- **Scaling/partials** "maybe had like a partial right here at this like 1023" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5003s-5010s])

- **Risk off swing low after reclaim** "if you wait for the confirmation or the reclaim, then you can risk really just off, you don't even have to risk off of low of day, right, in this case you're just risking off this swing candle" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [3795s-3807s])

- **Holding overnight** "If I have a really good entry, I'll hold overnight, but majority of the times I have to have a really good entry. In this market, I'd rather just be risk off. Because anything can happen" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4757s-4767s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **15 minute is best guide for day trading** "if we're day trading, which we do from closer to market open, our 15 minute time frame is one of our best guides. Because it gives us the most recent price action." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4724s-4735s])

- **Daily chart not useful in range** "the daily chart hasn't been too useful in the past month or so. So that's why we've been focusing more on the 50 minute and the one-hour chart." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [466s-471s])

- **HTF context for entry models** "if we have a higher time frame thesis, we have those news, then we can be looking for a trading opportunity in those real market." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5323s-5327s])

- **Look at past 2-3 sessions on 15min** "when you're on the 15 minute time frame, all you have to look for is really the past like two to three sessions. Because we're trading on the five and one minute time frame." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4708s-4718s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **Top watches based on news + HTF** "we do the pre-market preps is we look for what's, or top watches and play, what are like the top two to three watches that we're going to look for that are going to give us a higher probability trade thesis, and that's based off of news, and it's also based off of higher time frames as well." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5292s-5307s])

- **Clear thesis = tradeable; unclear = caution** "if it's clear, then it's very easy to develop a thesis, where most people kind of go wrong as if you can't really develop a thesis or you can't really explain why you're looking for something coming to market open. That probably gives you reason to be cautious off the right off the bat." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5951s-5967s])

- **Relative strength vs QQQ** "if Nvidia is pushing towards the upside and queues is inside the middle of the range, right, this usually indicates that like something is relatively strong. There's a reason why it's most likely moving and it's going to continue higher. That's what relative strength is for." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6087s-6096s])

- **News event trumps QQQ correlation** "whenever there's some ticker has a news event, let's say something like earnings or let's say a big news event that's going to impact the market, right, that's way more important than what's the correlation to the queues because there's a fresh news event." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6107s-6116s])

- **QQQ just needs to not go opposite direction** "all we need on the queues is for it to just not go in a complete direction towards the downside. Right, if it consolidates or even ends up pushing towards upside, that's great because that's going to help Nvidia end up pushing towards the upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6130s-6139s])

- **Ticker selection - out of previous day range** "it's out of the range from previous day and it's not just driving sideways. Like you can see that there's maybe a trend setting with it." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5938s-5945s])

- **Develop both bullish and bearish thesis** "you wanna develop basically a bullish thesis and a bear's thesis, because since there's a lot of volatility in the markets, right things can happen fairly quickly. It can flip on a dime." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [314s-325s])

- **If-Then statements** "if A&Stamins as well, which is kind of having a bullish A& bear's thesis. So if A&Stamins is, if price does this, we're gonna do this. So we're basically reacting to what price is gonna do." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [332s-342s])

- **5-minute opening range as mechanical entry** "the easiest entry model that we always look for is to kind of your five-minute open range. This is a really easy entry model that we always look for because it's nice and easy. You just draw your five-minute open range high. Five-minute open range law. We look for a retest off that for a continuation towards the upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4451s-4465s])

- **5min opening range is mechanical vs discretionary** "the five minute opening range... everyone can see that on our chart. Right? You can't go wrong and you can't kind of mess up the five minute opening range because all we look for is just the retest. Once it breaks above, right? Super mechanical. Once it breaks above, retest, continuation higher. Whereas if you have a previous day high retest, right? You can see the sometimes people work for the reaction. You might get in. Right? Whereas this one fairly mechanical." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [4637s-4662s])

- **Best 5min opening range has displacement** "the best one always happens when you have like a nice try. Something like Google, right, big strong candles, and right off the bat are usually just going to be a nice indication that price is going to continue in your actual direction" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5167s-5182s])

- **Without HTF context, 5min opening range win rate varies** "if you just mark out the high and low of the first five minute candle, right, you're going to get probably around, like, without any higher time for in context, right, depending on the market conditions, maybe in a hotter market, on the queues, you might get like 50, 60% win rate, in a slower market, you might get like 30, 40% win rate." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5234s-5248s])

- **Our edge is identifying higher probability** "the main thing that we always and what our edges is identifying, what's going to be higher probability, what things that we can look for" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5248s-5253s])

- **Don't trade reversal setups** "We don't teach reversal setups" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [2968s-2974s])

- **Reversal - breaker concept** "the best entry model is when you have, it looks something like this, right? It looks fairly quickly, but when I kind of reclaimed, it's something like this. Like it's called a breaker, a breaker, which is kind of this previous key area. And then this one right, it breaks structure, market structure right here. And then you can end up using this support for this continuation towards upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [2989s-3004s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Gap fill statistic** "with the statistic is 70% of the time, within a 48 hour these gaps will look to fill." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [1532s-1537s])

- **5min opening range win rate without context** "in a hotter market, on the queues, you might get like 50, 60% win rate, in a slower market, you might get like 30, 40% win rate." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [5238s-5248s])

- **84% rule - 90% of trades are reclaim** "Like 90% of my trades, when I take the 84% rule is the reclaim of a trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_5_Pre_Market_Plan.txt [6362s-6367s])
