# EXTRACTION: building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **First break vs late breaks - pre-market high already swept** "If it already takes out your previous day highs, we'll have to wait for... the retest of this previous day highs... If we're looking at this from just like a one-minute perspective... we already took out this key level, which is your pre-market highs... this retest... is going to be a little bit lower probability just because we already took out this key high." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1482s-1521s])

- **After pre-market high swept, wait for break above pre-market highs AND high of day** "What we usually like to wait for at least extra confirmation or price to break above your pre-market highs and your high day for continuation high because I already took out that level." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1521s-1531s])

- **When pre-market high is swept, be cautious of reversal** "Whenever it does that, you always have to be very cautious of an overall reversal in the market." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1538s-1543s])

- **Entry too close to high of day = lower probability** "This entry right here basically has to work, right? It's not really that much of a higher probability entry, at least the first time, because price is very close to high of day, right? The risk to reward in this case... it's so tight... This could be, this very well could work, but this is always lower probability, because your risk in this case is just a break underneath." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1644s-1669s])

- **Textbook break-and-retest** "A lot of the times you would like to see a perfect set up where it just breaks above, right? Reclames, right? Nice retouch off your previous day highs. These are textbook. Everyone can see these ones, and it's super clear to see, but it's not always like this." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1698s-1707s])

- **Break above, retest, continuation - classic pattern** "We always look for a break. Pre-visit high retest continuation." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2513s-2515s])

- **Break and retest off pre-market high - textbook example** "Break above. Retest of your pre-market highs... This looks textbook... Pre-visit high. Break and retest off of your pre-visit high day on Apple. Right? Nice hammer candle." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2467s-2482s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE (as a named concept)

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **84% rule - enter on reclaim, not retest** "I always get in on the reclaim because majority of the times the best 84% rules happen they just end up grabbing tending towards upside sometimes it does come back to retest... but majority of the times on the 84% rule it's a momentum trade and you really want to see a reclaim and just push back towards the highs right off the bat." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [954s-976s])

- **84% rule - strong candle closure required** "Body of that candle should close above the pivot level... for the reclaim." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1314s-1318s])

- **84% rule - wait for strong candle close** "That's why we wait for a strong candle close so once we had that strong candle close this is our indication that price is now setting back towards the upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1345s-1355s])

- **84% rule - stop is break underneath the key level** "I got in at this 10 on one candle the exact same stop as before which is just a break underneath its previous day highs." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [976s-989s])

- **84% rule - valid if structure holds (doesn't break low of day)** "In this case, the 84% was valid because it didn't break low of day. If it came all the way down towards low of day, it probably would have been invalidated." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1806s-1812s])

- **84% rule - best reclaim holds structure and creates higher low** "The best reclaimed in this case is if it holds structure and then just creates this higher low." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2110s-2116s])

- **84% rule - reclaim shows strength, better than first opportunity** "If it sets up on the 84% real, this is even better than the first opportunity because now it shows that it's a lot more, there's a lot more strength in this case." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2566s-2575s])

- **84% rule - news makes it higher probability** "The reason why I was so interested in looking for the reclaim on apple right just wasn't just because of the actual setup and itself it was because of the conditions which we had news... that's when you can be looking for continuation... this one for me is a higher probability 84% rule because of one thing and one thing only is just because of news." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1396s-1440s])

- **84% rule - after first trade stopped out, wait for reclaim** "Let's say for example you did take that first trade... you just get stopped out immediately and then you could always be looking for that 84% rule." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [840s-854s])

- **84% rule - loss of momentum when breaks underneath, reclaim shows buyers stepping back in** "Once it broke underneath your previous high day, that's where the momentum kind of starts slowing down... But as soon as it reclaimed back above, that's when you know the buyers are starting to step back in and that's when you can be more interested." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1930s-1942s])

- **84% rule - reclaim of previous day high = momentum in play** "If you wait for the reclaimed of your previous day highs, right? That's when you know momentum's in play, and that's when you can expect continuation back towards upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1964s-1968s])

- **84% rule - reclaim shows price most likely gravitates back to high day** "If it reclams your previous day high, that's why it's the 84% row and that's why it's a lot better this century because now price is most likely. There's a very good chance it gravitates back towards high day after reclams your previous day high." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1985s-1993s])

- **84% rule - tight stop because if it breaks underneath, not interested** "That's why it's a very tight stop. If it breaks underneath previous day high, I'm not too interested because as you can see, it slows down." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1998s-2006s])

- **84% rule - after first trade loss, wait for reclaim** "If you take the first trade, you take a small loss on it. That's fine. Because now you're waiting for that 84% real to set up." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2559s-2565s])

- **84% rule - sizing down on less clear setups** "I kind of sized down on these ones because I'm not overly convicted." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2336s-2340s])

- **84% rule - on Fridays, lighter size** "If I am going to take these shirts, I'm going to be on lighter size. If it works out, it's great. I'm really only looking for kind of this scalp back towards high day. If it doesn't, I'm just going to end up cutting it for like 30 cents on the comments." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2352s-2360s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **Order block definition - wick to body** "I always mentioned it's the full candle. That's what the order block is. So it's from the wick all the way down towards the body. Like this is your key area." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3227s-3236s])

- **Order block - most bullish case taps top of wick** "The most bullish case in areas, if it taps the top of the wick and we just continue towards upside. This is the most bullish case in area." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3236s-3242s])

- **Order block - can tap into body and still be bullish** "Sometimes it can come back down into a little bit of the body and still be very much bullish. It can even come back down all the way to the bottom of the body and still be very much bullish." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3245s-3254s])

- **Order block - draw as zone, give wiggle room** "That's why we marked with this whole key area. And we give it a little bit of wiggle room, right? Because it can always tap the top or it can tap the body and still be bullish." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3257s-3264s])

- **Order block - use for entries, specific levels for targets** "When I draw specific levels is when I'm just going to use it for like targets." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3265s-3269s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **Draw zones not lines - give wiggle room** "I just draw zones just to give it a little extra wiggle room. And then you'll see, they respect price a lot better." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3434s-3443s])

- **Key area when levels are close together** "If they're really, really close together, you can use like key areas in price. If you just draw it strictly, just a level. As a match, I usually draw a key error. So just give it a little bit more room." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3037s-3051s])

- **Multiple touches = draw area** "If you have multiple touches, right, where the previous day highs, or you'll see usually we have multiple touches around the previous day highs, that's when we can use an actual area." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3293s-3299s])

- **Draw area from wick to body for previous day high** "What I'm going to do is just draw this whole area. So I'm going to draw it from the body to the wick and drink this open." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [4258s-4266s])

- **15-minute level drawn out, then use lower timeframe for precision** "That's why I have this 15-minute level just drawn out, and then you can go back on to the lower time frames. All we're looking for is now this level to be now used as support once it breaks above for this continued push back towards upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3367s-3381s])

- **Previous day high is the only level that matters sometimes** "Really the only level that matters in this case... It's just your previous day highs... This is the only level that matters on the chart. Just your previous day highs." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1823s-1832s])

- **Ranging market - need break outside range for continuation** "Whenever you're trading in a ranging market... What we'll like to see if you want to see any sort of continuation on least on the higher timeframes is a break outside of the range." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [403s-416s])

- **Internal liquidity - trade back inside range when rejecting off key areas** "If we reject off these key areas... What we're going to be doing is looking for a trade back inside the range which is internal liquidity." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [425s-439s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **Day one news event - best for continuation** "Day one news event. In day one, there's usually always continuation. If you ever have a good news event, there's usually continuation on day one. That's kind of the playbook... earnings day one continuation. Right? Those are the best days. Is whenever you have fresh news day one." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2403s-2420s])

- **News momentum decays day by day** "Day two, you still have continuation off of day one. And then day three, potentially you have continuation, but it's going to be less and less. Right? Day one's always the greatest. And it kind of goes day two. The momentum's going to start slowing down. And then day three as well. Maybe now it's going to have a nice bigger pullback on the higher timeframes." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2420s-2434s])

- **Fridays - size down, be cautious** "On days where it's either less high probability, like on Fridays, we always talk about Fridays. I rarely, if I'm trading on Fridays, I'm sizing down and I'm going to be a little bit more cautious because based off my statistics, I don't really have any A plus setups on Friday." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [4024s-4035s])

- **Fridays - hard to hold even with A+ setup** "Majority of the times, especially on a Friday as well, even if I do have an A plus setup, it's going to be hard to hold it because it is a Friday, I'm trading zero days. I'm probably going to end up selling beforehand, before it ends up running. So Fridays are very, very hard to trade." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [4047s-4061s])

- **Red folder news - wait for it to play out before trading** "When you're at the time you're just going to wait for that red folder news event to play out before you take any trade." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [919s-925s])

## 7. Exits

- **Scaling - 25% at high of day, 50% higher timeframe, 25% back towards upside** "If I didn't take the first trade you can look to scale some at high day in this case you can do like 25% right higher time frames 50 and then 25 back towards upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1161s-1181s])

- **Hold longer when QQQ hasn't filled its objective** "That's where you can honestly hold on apple a little bit longer just because the queues hasn't filled its objective right the queues in this case the objective for the queues is going to be this gap fell back towards upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [1192s-1204s])

- **Scaling along the way - 25, 25, 25, 25** "I was kind of scaling on the on the long ways, but basically I was like 25. And then I was kind of scaling on the ways where I was just doing like 25. I was kind of just scaling along the way. So like 25, 25, 25, 25." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5080s-5089s])

- **Selling majority at high day skews risk-reward** "If I'm selling on pops, like if I'm selling majority of high-day, right, then my risk-or-war ratio is going to be really skewed. And I'm going to have to increase my win rate." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5211s-5220s])

- **Selling only 25% at high day - fine if it comes back to breakeven** "If I only sell 25% at high-day, and it comes back down, it takes me break-even, then I'm probably break-even on the trade, just with slippage, or I'm losing a little bit of money. And I'm fine with that." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5253s-5265s])

- **Selling 75% at high day limits upside** "If you sell 75% at high-day, and the thing runs super nice, then it's still going to be a nice winner because you still have your trailer's left, but you already sold 75% of your positions. Only thing you're working now is just 25 of your remaining trailers." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5269s-5286s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **Higher timeframe thesis - looking for external targets** "Most of my trades, I like higher time frame thesis. I like getting in and holding for those external targets." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2716s-2722s])

- **Zero days vs next week contracts for HTF holds** "If I am looking for a trade setup and I'm going to give it a little bit more room, I would play the next week's contracts. If you're playing something like zero days, right, just expect it, you want to probably cut it fairly quickly and just look to kind of re-enter on the 84% roll." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [2789s-2806s])

- **Ranging market on HTF - need break outside for continuation** "Whenever you're trading in a ranging market... What we'll like to see if you want to see any sort of continuation on least on the higher timeframes is a break outside of the range." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [403s-416s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **A+ setup = higher timeframe + news** "If you have the bigger picture, higher time frames, and you have any sort of news, right? If you have these two checked off, you have an A plus opportunity in the markets." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3637s-3648s])

- **A+ setup - prepare and focus** "If you have these two, your light, you should be like, okay, let me prepare, let me look at something in the markets. Let me really focus in on the ticker that has any sort of news or higher time frame. Because right, there's a potential big opportunity for big move in the markets." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3652s-3665s])

- **News injects liquidity and volatility** "Whenever you have a news event and injects liquidity into the markets and that just creates a volatility. So that just creates more opportunity. And that really means the price is going to move a lot more on a regular basis compared to a day without any news." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3685s-3698s])

- **Priority: bigger picture + news > technical analysis > relative strength** "If you have these two checked off, right, you're going to be looking for some sort of opportunity in the markets. And then after you have these two checked off, then you worry about right your trade management, which is your technical analysis... And then you also have relative strength and relative weakness. Right? These two don't really matter that much." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3698s-3721s])

- **Only technicals + relative strength = just a scalp** "If you only have technical analysis and you have just relative strength, the bottom two are just purely scouts." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3737s-3745s])

- **All four = A+ opportunity** "If you have all four, that's when you can have a potential A plus opportunity in the markets." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3745s-3749s])

- **Bigger picture and news are not discretionary** "These two are not discretionary, right? If you have news, there's only one way to really take it. If there's news, there's news. If there's higher timeframes, there's higher timeframes." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3828s-3835s])

- **Relative strength is discretionary** "Based off relative strength and weaknesses, while that's more discretionary. Everyone is going to, everyone's going to have a different opinion on what relative strength is or what the Qs is doing." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3813s-3819s])

- **Trade on clear days, not every day** "We want to trade on days that are a lot easier to trade and it's a lot more clear." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [3942s-3947s])

- **Not every day has nice price action** "Not every day is going to be, not every day we're going to get some nice price action like yesterday." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [4014s-4020s])

- **AMD - avoid long side without news** "AMD is like, all my ticker that I'm not going to trade towards the upside. So I'm going to stay off on AMD unless it has any sort of news. Higher timeference have been very bearish on this one. So it's been very hard to trade anything AMD towards the long side." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5418s-5435s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Risk-reward with scaling at high day - almost 1:1** "If you're risking 70 cents, let's say 75 cents and it hits high day. If you scale majority of your position at high of day, and you're only gaining a dollar per contract while you're risking 75. So right here, your risk or reward is... not much reward here if you're just scaling as soon as it hits high day." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5021s-5038s])

- **1:1 risk-reward requires 78% win rate** "If you have a one to one, that means you need over 50% remy realistically speaking, you need around like 78." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5059s-5068s])

- **Example: 5:1 risk-reward with scaling** "Entry at 55 risking 75 cents. And then let's say I gain on average, right, three dollars. And 50 cents a contract... almost a five-restro reward ratio." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5121s-5155s])

- **With 5:1 R:R, need 20% win rate to break even** "If I'm having a five-restro reward, I trade, that means I would be break even if I had... 20% or 20% run rate to be break even." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5171s-5183s])

- **With 2-3 R:R and 50% win rate = profitable** "If I have a two or three-restro reward ratio. And I have around a 50% run rate. That means you're going to be fairly profitable." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [5191s-5196s])

- **Losers often bigger than winners when scaling at high day** "Your losers are going to be bigger than your winners if you look at your statistics." (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_6_Building_Logic.txt [4877s-4882s])

---

- **One-candle-rule / opening-candle-retest** (as a named concept)
- **Order blocks: probability hierarchy, validity specifics**
- **Key levels: PDH/PDL hierarchy, PMH/PML specifics**
- **Exits: breakeven (when), trailing specifics**
- **Trade selection: B/C grading criteria, max trades/day, stop-when-green**
- **Concrete numbers: win rates (beyond the 50% mention), risk %, drawdown, gap fill stats**
- **A/A+ grading: verbatim criteria distinguishing A from A+ from B** (A+ defined as higher timeframe + news, but no A vs A+ vs B distinction)
- **QQQ alignment: index confirmation rules** (beyond relative strength concept - mentioned as discretionary)
- **Displacement/FVG: exact size definitions**
- **Stop-after-win / max trades/day: tier rules**
- **Reclaim setup: full definition + 84% mechanics** (84% rule mechanics covered, but "reclaim setup" as named concept not defined)

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Definition/Usage]** "the break and retest happens every single day on multiple time frames and that's exactly the system that we use every single day" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [813s-823s])

- **[Entry Model Role]** "the break and retests is just an entry model that we use. That makes it nice and simple. What matters more is the logic around it, the higher time frames" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3657s-3664s])

- **[Entry Model Role]** "The reason why we take the break and retests is because it's very simple for most people to understand. And also there's a lot of different variations of the break and retests. There's the immediate retests, order block... Usually majority of the times you can capture almost every majority of the trades, either through some sort of break and retest kind of setup" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3683s-3702s])

- **[Immediate Retest - Momentum Signal]** "if it has an immediate retest, it's kind of tipping a ten. Right? A lot of times, if you can see, if it's price, nice push higher, immediate retest, that usually indicates that there's some sort of momentum in the markets when you have an immediate retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3231s-3246s])

- **[Immediate Retest - Example]** "this is what's called an immediate retest. Whenever we talk about the opening range, kind of as we always have these marked up, it's just happened a lot quicker... we're going to be looking for the break above retest and continuation. And this is kind of what happened, but on a lot quicker time frame on the one minute chart" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3005s-3023s])

- **[Break + Retest vs Reclaim]** "I always like looking for the reclaim, because a lot of times it will happen, is if it reclaims a key level, it'll just start pushing right towards upside. Sometimes it has a retest, sometimes it doesn't, and sometimes it just continues towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3871s-3879s])

- **[Break + Retest - Immediate Example on NVDA]** "this is a break and retests this is just immediate break and retests that you look for you just got to be a lot quicker" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [7109s-7119s])

- **[Break + Retest - What We Like To See]** "usually if we are looking for a retest right what we like to see break retests continuation something like this" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [7143s-7150s])

- **[Last Leg of a Move - Best for Breakouts]** "the last leg of a move right before it takes a gravitate towards this key area is going to be the most explosive move this is what's called the last leg of a move and this is always what we look to try and capture because this last leg of move is when it's going to have the most buying pressure" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6754s-6769s])

- **[Last Leg - Best for Options]** "this is the best for options we capture a continuation when we're in a clear trend" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6804s-6809s])

- **[Last Leg - After Move is Slower]** "this retests after is going to be a little bit slower it's still going to be a potential that is trading opportunity but we already hit its main objective" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6789s-6796s])

- **[Flat Top - Favorite Setup]** "flat top that's why it flat tops one of my favorites you can see how big of an explosive move this was towards upside because it was building up right these dips right they're solid right you can look to trade them but you're just going to be in a little bit more consolidation but as soon as it has that flat top right multiple touches one touch two chest three touch we're both to set to have a big explosive move towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6867s-6891s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **[Opening Range - 5 Minute Best]** "I would consider the five minute time frame is by far the best bet, right? The five minute, if you mark the five minute high and low, if we're in a clear trend of the markets, the five minutes, one of the best opening ranges in this case" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3041s-3047s])

- **[Opening Range - 1 Minute Caution]** "The one minute time frame, sometimes, you can get a little bit chopped around, especially if the first one minute candle. It's not that clear reduction. So the first one minute candle is just like a very small candle. It doesn't really do too much. The one minute, the one minute opening range doesn't really matter too much" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3047s-3059s])

- **[Opening Range - Marked Up Always]** "we've marked up always your opening candle, right? Your opening candle range, and we're going to be looking for the break above retest and continuation" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3013s-3018s])

- **[Opening Range - Immediate Retest Example]** "you have the one minute opening range, which you can see price pushes towards the upside... the best entry would have been the retest. This is a quick one" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3064s-3081s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[84% Rule - Definition on NVDA]** "this is the 84% rule just on a very smaller time frame... push towards upside break retest as you can see failed so what are we looking for we're waiting for the reclaim of your 14280 retest of this for this continued push towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4833s-4857s])

- **[84% Rule - Logic]** "the reason why the 84% rule works is because thinking about this logically... if you can get back above this level close above right this very well is going to invalidate this kind of like head and shoulders and an invalidation of your head and shoulders is one of the most bullish patterns in the overall market" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4877s-4903s])

- **[84% Rule - Stop Placement]** "if it can break above then my stop would have been just a break underneath right 84%" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4968s-4973s])

- **[84% Rule - Scenario on Tesla]** "as it came down, stop up and then very well, 84% rule. And then you could have been looking for the reclaim and continuation towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3206s-3217s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **[Definition - Down Close Candles in Uptrend]** "in an uptrend we look for down close candles in a downtrend we look for up close candles. And that's the exact definition in my opinion of an order block" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [705s-713s])

- **[Definition - Support Function]** "down close candles and then when you push towards upside you want these down close candles to support price high. Right, and that's that's an order block" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [735s-740s])

- **[Terminology Note - Same Concept]** "I call it order blocks. That's how I always refer to it as is order blocks... I don't know what he calls it. I think he just calls it up closing down close candles. But it's the exact same thing" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [686s-721s])

- **[Validity - Close Above Required]** "the order block is valid when we close above its down close candle. So when we close above the candle, that's when the order block is valid" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3148s-3159s])

- **[Best Order Blocks - Wick to Body]** "the best order blocks... happened from the top wick all the way to the top of the body, right? At least on the downturn. Right? Potentially could dig a little bit deeper into the bottom of the body or to the bottom of the wick as well. But majority of the times the best order blocks happened when we hold the top of the range, which is the wick top of the wick to the top of the body and we continue towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [3179s-3197s])

- **[Best Order Blocks - Confirmed]** "the best order blocks my favorite order blocks is always this right the top of the wick to the body this is the best order box if you ever see any sort of any sort of bullish order block the best order blocks always happen from the top of the wick to the body" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4631s-4647s])

- **[Order Block - Invalidation Logic]** "if you have a down close candle and you have an upper-wick price closes underneath this is a bearish candle but if you can get a bucket that means basically this bearish candle is invalidated and then we end up using that as a retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4655s-4669s])

- **[Order Blocks - Respect in Clear Trends]** "when you have order blocks or whenever you use order blocks, it's a clear up trend and you have down close candles. When prices respecting these down close candles, it makes it very easy" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4079s-4088s])

- **[Order Blocks - Marking on Tesla Example]** "this is your first down close candle on Tesla, right, we always talk about this in an uptrend, what are we going to mark out down close candle's. I consider this... order block. So uptrend down close candle. So you want to mark this out. This is now your area that you like to use as support for a continuation towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [1931s-1952s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Pre-Market Highs/Lows - Usage]** "Do I use these on a daily basis, your premarket highs and pretty like American lows? No, only use it in logic and context with other things as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4214s-4218s])

- **[Previous Day Highs - Bullish Signal]** "if we're above your previous day highs, then we are very much bullish and we expect this to continue towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2317s-2321s])

- **[Previous Day Highs/Lows - Continuation]** "if we're above previous day highs, we're going to be looking for continuation higher. If we're below your previous day lows, we're going to be looking for continuations lower" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2321s-2325s])

- **[Key Levels - Selling At]** "you always want to sell based off of the actual chart in those key levels because I think too many people see like a one to two or maybe see high day but then they just look this scale right... you want to look for a pop and then continuation towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5385s-5407s])

- **[Key Levels - Marking]** "whenever you're marking key levels just always hold highs hold lows right previous day highs which would be your five three fifty nine I'm marker your three sixty one and then the next level" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5363s-5375s])

- **[Inside Bar Day - Built Up Liquidity]** "whenever you have an inside bar day, that means just the previous days inside of its range. So that means this day, we never took of the previous day highs or the previous day lows. So there's just liquidity being built up underneath, right? Locuity. There's also liquidity being built up here" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4173s-4184s])

- **[Inside Bar Day - Expect Bigger Move]** "whatever way it breaks, there's probably going to be some sort of bigger move in the markets because if you ever see inside bar days, what do we have? We just have built up liquidity. That's why it just works" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [4163s-4168s])

- **[3-5 Sessions - Intraday Reference]** "the really the only sessions you really want to look at is I'd say 3 to 5 you can look back 3 to 5 sessions... if you're intraday trading you're on on the one in five minute time frame you can just look at the past 3 to 5 sessions" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5050s-5077s])

- **[Market Regime - Scalpers Mentality]** "in this range right here that we've been in it's really been this kind of scalpers mentality. If you win right that's awesome if you lose ready you got to get out right you can't marry your bias. - **[Market Regime - When to Press]** "when the higher time frames are good. That's when we really want to kind of press on the gas as well and we can look be looking for higher time frame trades" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [1120s-1125s])

- **[Market Regime - Easy vs Hard]** "that's kind of how trading is is if it's hard... that's where you kind of want to take us that back and just be cautious right if you're set up, it makes sense because the market conditions right when it's easy. - **[Options - Best in Clear Trend]** "when you want to be trading options is when we're in a clear trend. It's nice and easy" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [1011s-1020s])

- **[Options - Difficult in Range]** "the past really a month and a half on the overall market has been very difficult to trade options just because there's a lot of range" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [1059s-1065s])

- **[Options - Need to Hold Longer]** "if you can't hold on to position for more than like a few hours. It's gonna be very difficult to make money especially in options because they can flood fairly quickly" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [1175s-1183s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling - 25/50/25 Pattern]** "This is what I usually do is like 25, 50, and 25. This is what I usually look for" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2129s-2134s])

- **[Scaling - Four-Way Split]** "I like scaling fours or you can scale right and when you scale in fours it's 25 25 25 25 or some people do like 50 and then like maybe 50 20 10 10 something like that" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5965s-5981s])

- **[Scaling - Personal Preference]** "I personally like more of the 25 and then this is probably what most people do 50 like 20 10 10" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5981s-6001s])

- **[Scaling - Looking for Continuation]** "I'm always looking for high day plus continuation so I'm majority of the times this is always what I'm doing 25 and I'm looking for a continuation higher" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5723s-5728s])

- **[Scaling - With 2 Contracts]** "if you only have two contracts... I'll be looking for at least a dollar on Tesla cons right either be at high day maybe before maybe a pop after I will look for a minimum right a hundred dollars on my first contract scale and then you can look to leave the rest for the next key target" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5602s-5626s])

- **[Scaling - With 2 Contracts Difficulty]** "if you have one contract compared to 10 it's a lot harder to scale" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5539s-5542s])

- **[Scaling - If Scaling 50% at First Pop]** "if you're going to look to scale let's say 50% or greater it really doesn't make probably too much sense to take this trade if you're going to scale 50% in greater right at the end of the day if you scale 50% at this first initial pop you probably wouldn't ended off the trade even though I had a super nice push towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5692s-5705s])

- **[Breakeven - Based on Underlying, Not P&L]** "we talk about moving stops to break even we're basically moving the underlying to break even so we're allowing the trade still more breathing room" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5802s-5812s])

- **[Breakeven - After Scaling]** "whenever I do take 25 off at high day that's just usually I do the fact that if it does come back down to my stop I'm either going to be basically break even or maybe a little bit red and I'm fine with that because I'm fine with taking break even trades or maybe a little bit small losses" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5835s-5851s])

- **[Bar-by-Bar Exit - For Last Runner]** "you already scaled up key levels you just want to leave your last one where bar by bar... as soon as the bar breaks underneath I always talk with as it kind of slows down because right after a big extension close underneath" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5486s-5505s])

- **[Minimum R:R Expectation]** "a minimum on Tesla most likely three are on this trade if you're getting anything below a two art then you probably ended up scaling something wrong" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5873s-5878s])

- **[R:R - Not Fixed, Based on Chart]** "I always base it off of the actual chart like you'll never it's a one to two risk your word ratio I'll never just sell it just because it's a one to two because the thing on the chart is not even actually what matters" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5319s-5330s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Trading Set Playbook - Hierarchy]** "Number one, if I have a trading set of playbook, this is something that I use every single day... If I have the first two checked off that I know I have, something good" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2208s-2219s])

- **[Playbook - Order of Importance]** "it goes from one, this basically a number, right, one, two, three, four. Right, this is a system that go off of, right, and that's kind of how it goes in order" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2817s-2820s])

- **[Playbook - Top Two Most Important]** "having news and being in an uptrend. Right, these two are the most important" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2803s-2808s])

- **[Playbook - Without Top Two]** "if you don't have these two, and you can have the best, you can have the most relative strength intraday. You can have a really solid break and retest. Without these two, this very well could just fail" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2781s-2789s])

- **[Playbook - Relative Strength is Last]** "the relative strength of weakness is really at the bottom of the thing that I look for. If I have the first two checked off, right, then I know have a good opportunity in the markets, and then I look at technical analysis and then the type of order of flow of slash volume. So order flow of slash volume, which is relative strength of weakness by itself, doesn't really mean too much" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2225s-2243s])

- **[Playbook - Confirmation]** "If I have the first two checked off, right, then I know have a good opportunity in the markets" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [2229s-2233s])

- **[Playbook - Fundamentals/News]** "any sort of big news event in the overall market earnings and then fundamentals of the actual company this doesn't matter as much because we're trading momentum... the main thing is just news right news and earnings" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [5164s-5203s])

- **[A+ Setups - Make Majority of Money]** "I make majority of my money on a few a plus setups a month right the rest of the time I'm scalping I'm looking for basics sometimes I win sometimes I lose right that's why I try and keep those loss of small. But whenever I have those a plus setups that's where I make majority of my money" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [589s-608s])

- **[A+ Setups - Frequency]** "I hit every a couple times a month" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [609s-613s])

- **[Trade Selection - Conservative vs Aggressive]** "the best would be the conservative right the conservative entry model right you can use the conservative or the most conservative what you don't want to use in this case is just your limit order right you want to choose one or two of these entry models" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6361s-6371s])

- **[Trade Selection - Later in Day]** "if you're later into the day you already broke underneath your previous love day now we want to see what now we want to wait for is extra confirmation because if it does want to stay heavy then we can be looking for a short position but we don't have to be as aggressive" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6341s-6356s])

- **[Trade Selection - No Weak Reaction = Skip]** "unless there was week price action then you wouldn't be remotely interested right I can see the thesis but there wasn't a single reaction so then you're just ditched the trade idea" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6380s-6391s])

- **[Trade Selection - Last Leg of Move]** "the last leg of a move... this is when you usually have the the best trading opportunities and that's what we look for" (building-your-profitable-system_Lesson_7_Futures_Vs_Options.txt [6796s-6809s])

- **[Trade Selection - Market Order vs Limit Order]** "I personally always use market orders if the candle lets it close that low day then you might have to reassure entry or maybe wait for a small pullback but I'll use market orders that's what i personally use" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Drive.txt [1639s-1649s])

- **[Trade Selection - Gap Fill vs Gap and Go Distinction]** "if you have a gap over the weekend right we can expect continuation or at least an opening play right off the bat right because after this gap up usually on day one let's say on a Monday where we're probably going to expand towards the upside and then later on it's going to continue back lower just because of the trend very rarely will you ever see right as soon as you have let's say day one gap or day one news reversal that you're just going to immediately reverse back towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Drive.txt [1983s-2009s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Displacement)
- **Displacement requirement** "The best opening range breaks and retests have displacement" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3004s-3009s])
- **Strong candle close needed** "You're going to have to have a strong candle closure for this potential push towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1575s-1584s])
- **Break requires price to move outside opening candle** "If you want to move higher, we have to break above the first candle... If you want to move lower, we're going to have to break underneath the opening candle range low" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1098s-1110s])

### Valid Retest
- **Retest after break** "Once it breaks that range and comes back to retest, then we could be looking for continuation in the general direction" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [330s-339s])
- **Immediate retest can fail** "I can show you plenty of examples where it broke above the high, consolidated there, had an immediate retest and failed" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1607s-1617s])
- **Retest success depends on HTF context** "The difference between a stock moving higher and a stock kind of having an immediate retest and failing, has to do with the higher timeframe context" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1617s-1626s])

### Entry Trigger
- **Entry after retest holds** "Wait for a break above or below the opening candle and wait for a retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [381s-386s])
- **Reclaim entry (84% rule)** "The best 84% rules occur when the time frame is still intact... higher lows are still being built" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4516s-4527s])

### Stop Placement
- **Invalidation is opening candle** "This is basically your invalidation if price breaks back above your opening candle" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1281s-1285s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **Clear objectives needed** "When we have clear objectives and prefer unmitigated liquidity... your previous day highs, or your previous day lows, or if you have unmitigated liquidity, like we have potential gap fills" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [391s-408s])
- **High of day as target** "All you're looking for is high of day" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4268s-4274s])

### First-Break vs Late Breaks
- **First candle importance** "That first candle is very important... once that first candle develops, it's good to give us a good indication if price wants to continue towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1026s-1039s])
- **Late entries are scalps** "If you get in a little bit later, you basically have to sell at your key levels to manage your risk" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4176s-4186s])

### When NOT to use Opening Range Break
- **Inside previous day range** "If we're inside of its range, don't look for any opening plays" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [620s-625s])
- **Opening in middle of range** "If you open up inside the middle of the range... it's going to be very hard for you to trade this one minute opening range" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [542s-554s])
- **First 15-30 minutes choppy** "The first 15 to 30 minutes of market open is usually fairly choppy" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [554s-560s])

---

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

### Named "Opening Candle" not "One Candle Rule"
- **Uses opening candle terminology** "I personally like using the opening candle" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1089s-1093s])
- **First candle of New York session** "Mark up the first New York session opening candle, right? The one five or 15 minute candle" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [372s-381s])

### Exact Specification
- **Mark high and low** "Mark up the high. We also want to mark up the low" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [417s-422s])
- **Break and retest required** "Wait for a break above or below the opening candle and wait for a retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [381s-386s])
- **First candle shows strength/direction** "If that first candle shows a lot of strength... within the first five minutes we're above your previous day highs" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3168s-3182s])

---

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

### Conditions for 84% Rule
- **Higher timeframe structure intact** "The best 84% rules occur when the time frame is still intact... higher lows are still being built" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4516s-4527s])
- **Reclaim after structure holds** "If the markets can hold structure, which is creating higher lows, then this is when a reclaim is valid" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4536s-4541s])

### Disqualifiers
- **Invalid when structure broken** "The only reclaims that don't work is if price comes up, breaks all the way back down and then sets back up. This is for me not a reclaim" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4527s-4536s])
- **After big push higher** "If price did this and then came all the way back up... that would have made too much sense because price already had a big push higher. This reclaim would have made too much sense" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4561s-4575s])

### Psychology / Outcome
- **Gravitates to main objective** "A lot of the times when you reclaim on an 84% you're now going to gravitate to your main objective, which is going to be high of day and above" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3297s-3307s])
- **Not just reject at HOD** "There's a good chance whenever you're in a trade reclaim... we're not going to just reject off high of day and head lower" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3307s-3317s])

---

## 4. Order Blocks

### Definition and Drawing
- **Down close candle** "An order block specifically... it's always going to be that down close candle. Those are full down close candles" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3730s-3738s])
- **Wick to body** "It's from the wick all the way down to the body" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3601s-3605s])

### Probability Hierarchy
- **Best order block** "As long as it respects the wick to the body, this is the best order block... I prefer you respect the top of the wick and you continue higher" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3623s-3635s])
- **Can dig to bottom** "Technically, it could dig all the way down towards the bottom of the order block. But preferably, you like to see it hold from your wick to your body" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3635s-3642s])

### Confluence
- **Multiple down close candles** "Every single down close candle, you want to have them marked out. It's just a way to manage it" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3652s-3658s])
- **Combine with opening range** "You can have other confidences like order blocks" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3333s-3337s])

### Entry/Stop/Target
- **Scalp entry on order block** "Whenever you have order blocks like this, it's just primarily scalp for a continuation higher" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4042s-4045s])
- **Stop below order block** "Your risk is just going to be a break underneath it" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3914s-3919s])

---

## 5. Key Levels

### PDH/PDL Hierarchy
- **PDH/PDL most important** "Put more emphasis on higher time frame levels, your regular trading hours, which is the best" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4762s-4767s])
- **PDH/PDL over pre-market** "I'm going to put more emphasis onto the previous day highs a lot more than this pre-market high level" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4639s-4645s])
- **PDH/PDL strongest** "The previous day high levels are the ones that are going to be always the strongest" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4777s-4781s])

### Opening Range
- **Timeframe options** "Preferably, we like the five, 15. You can also do it on the one minute as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [272s-276s])
- **New York session only** "Majority of times whenever we use the opening range, we use it during the New York session open, which is at 9:30 AM Eastern Standard Time" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [245s-252s])

### Zones vs Ticks
- **Draw areas not lines** "That's why I like drawing out areas" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3513s-3516s])

### Gap Fill
- **Gap fill as objective** "If you have unmitigated liquidity, like we have potential gap fills" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [405s-408s])
- **Best gaps on no news** "The best gaps happen... when you kind of gap down on no news. When you gap down on no news, there's a possibility that we can reverse really quickly" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [2928s-2938s])

---

## 6. Time-of-Day

### Trade Window
- **Opening plays within first 30 min to 1.5 hours** "Primarily, when we do look for trading opportunities, it's within the first 30 minutes to an hour and a half" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [311s-315s])
- **After 10 AM is intraday** "After 10 o'clock, we can classify these as intraday plays" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1515s-1523s])
- **Wait for first 15-30 min** "Wait for the first 15 to 30 minutes to play out. That's a good rule of thumb" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [813s-817s])

### Best Days
- **Friday considerations** "It's a Friday... you probably don't want to hold any trades over the weekend in this market" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1969s-1974s])

### Chart Timeframe Rules
- **Inside range: use 5/15 min not 1 min** "If we're inside the range... do not look for any opening plays... you can look for trading opportunities around 10 o'clock or after 10 o'clock, which is now going to be on a five minute opening range or a 15 minute opening range" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [614s-639s])
- **Outside range: use 1 and 5 min** "If we're outside the range, we can look for opening plays in combination with the opening range. In this case what we can use is the one and five minute" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [2993s-3004s])

---

## 7. Exits

### Scaling
- **Scale into strength** "If you're already selling up into this strength, and you have, let's say, 30% left, right, this is an area that you should just maintain" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3692s-3702s])
- **Sell majority at HOD** "You're going to sell majority up into towards high of day, like 80%" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4114s-4118s])
- **Keep 20% for runners** "You do want to hold some, maybe like 20% in case it runs" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4168s-4172s])

### Breakeven
- NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Trailing
- **Cut on slowdown** "Once it starts slowing down, you probably just want to cut it" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4118s-4122s])
- **Use order blocks to manage** "If you have your order block marked out, you already sold some, then you should maintain, look at these down close candles" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3706s-3709s])

### Position Management
- **Hold at key levels** "Stopping out right here doesn't make too much sense, because you're right at your key level. You might as well wait for this candle to close to see if price wants to continue higher" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3559s-3567s])
- **Full position harder to hold** "It might be a little bit harder to hold if you are holding your full position, because you can see it come back" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3683s-3688s])

---

## 8. HTF Application

### Weekly/Daily Levels
- **Start with HTF thesis** "We're going to first start off on the higher time frames. This gives us a lot more context and exactly what we're looking for" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3077s-3086s])
- **HTF makes intraday work** "What makes this so good is not because of the intraday price action is because it's what happens on the higher time frames" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [3408s-3417s])

### HTF Overriding Intraday
- **HTF context determines trade validity** "The reason why it continued higher is because of... the higher timeframes because we had bullish higher timeframe movement" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1626s-1636s])
- **Zoom out for clarity** "When in doubt, zoom out" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4593s-4599s])

## 9. Trade Selection

### Grading Criteria (A/A+/B/C)
- **Best entries at PDH/PDL** "The best levels as I always mentioned is always going to be around your previous day highs and your previous day lows. If you can get in entries as close to these levels as possible, these will always offer the best risk to reward" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [4217s-4227s])
- **Multiple confluence = higher probability** "You had that reaction around your opening range. You also had multiple confidences being your one minute opening range, your previous day lows. We also had your pre-market lows. So that's what made it like a lot more high probability" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [1304s-1319s])

### Confluence
- **HTF + news + setup + relative strength** "I always look for higher time frames first. And then I look for news. And then I look for the actual setup in itself. And then I look for the relative strength versus the indices" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [2105s-2116s])

### Max Trades/Day
- NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Stop-When-Green
- NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Market Regime
- **Continuation outside range** "If we're above its previous day highs, we're going to be looking for continuation higher. If we're below your previous day lows, we'd like to see continuation lower" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [905s-913s])
- **Inside range = choppy** "If we're inside of its range... you're going to get chopped around for the first 15 to 30 minutes" (building-your-profitable-system_Opening_Range_B_R.txt [838s-847s])

# EXTRACTION FROM: building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R_transcript.txt

## 4. Order Blocks

### Definition
- **Institutional order flow** "An order block is an area where large market participants have placed significant buy and sell orders" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [354s-362s])
- **Key levels on chart** "Order blocks are key levels on a chart and understanding the different types of order blocks and what makes a high probability for low probability order block" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [362s-377s])
- **Same as one-candle-rule** "The order block and the one candle rule is I consider the exact same thing" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [303s-315s])
- **Down close in uptrend, up close in downtrend** "For down close candles really the criteria just for any sort of order block, it's going to be down close candles in an uptrend" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [423s-430s])
- **Up close in downtrend** "For a downtrend what we like to see is... an up close candle in a downtrend" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [448s-454s])

### Drawing (Wick-to-Body)
- **Highest probability: top wick to body (bullish)** "The best order blocks you'll see occur from the top, wick to the body... the highest probability order block is going to occur when price taps the top of the wick and really closes above" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [647s-665s])
- **Second most bullish: top wick to top of body** "The second most bullish order block is we can draw it from the top of the wick to the bottom of the body... a very small zone" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [679s-693s])
- **Third most bullish: full down close candle** "The third most bullish order block is going to be that full down close candle... pretty wide of a zone" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [863s-880s])
- **Bearish: bottom wick to bottom of body** "When we have a bearish order block marked out, what we like to mark out is the bottom wick to the bottom of the body" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [880s-889s])
- **Second most bearish: bottom wick to top of body** "The second most bearish is drawing out from the bottom of the wick to the top of the body" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [889s-900s])

### Probability Hierarchy
- **Wick = clear zone, no wick = wide zone** "If we have a wick, then we can use the wick in the bodies, which make it a lot easier... this is a very tight zone where risk can be fairly clear" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [972s-1001s])
- **No wick = don't know where bounce** "If we have no wick... we don't know if price is going to bounce off the top of the wick, price could dig a little bit deeper into the middle... or price can dig all the way back down towards the bottom of the body" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [949s-972s])
- **Bodies tell the story, wicks do damage** "The wicks do the damage the bodies tell the story... wicks in between but the bodies are showing that buyers are stepping in" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2097s-2113s])
- **Close inside = not bullish** "If it has a candle close inside the order block it's not that bullish" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2083s-2096s])
- **Most bullish = doesn't close inside** "If price does not close inside the order block that is the most bullish order block possible" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2162s-2181s])

### Validity
- **Must close above to activate** "The way this order block is valid is once it closes above this down close candle" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4561s-4575s])
- **Bodies closing above = bullish** "If the bodies are respecting the order block and we're closing above right this is what matters" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2097s-2113s])
- **Closing inside = momentum slowing** "If it starts closing inside this order block... price starts slowing down... that just means the momentum is going to slow down" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2717s-2730s])
- **Not bearish until structure broken** "We're not bearish until we can have a candle close underneath this right here which also lines up with what our break of structure" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2730s-2742s])

### Entry / Stop / Target
- **Entry at top of order block** "You can have your entry order set at the top of the order block which you'll get in every time" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4644s-4650s])
- **Stop at bottom of order block** "The stop loss is always going to be at the bottom of the order block" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3841s-3850s])
- **Tighter stop = under candle close** "If you want to have a tighter stop... you can put it underneath let's say a wick" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3850s-3859s])
- **Target = draw on liquidity** "Understanding liquidity and where price's most likely going to gravitate towards is the most important" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2035s-2043s])
- **Target = next key level above** "If we can break above 260, where's the next logical area... this key pivot high back from the ninth" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [1327s-1350s])
- **Scale at highs, hold remainder** "Take 50% off at the highs... then you look to hold 50%... move your stop to break even" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [1790s-1806s])

### Confluence
- **Order block + opening range break = best** "When let's say the opening range break has an order block. Those ones are the best when they have multiple conferences" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [288s-303s])
- **Order block + key level + previous day high** "You have an order block one minute you have your 100 key cycle level you'll have your previous day highs... a lot of conferences" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [5030s-5049s])
- **Order block + higher timeframe point of interest** "It also coincides with this higher timeframe point of interest" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2226s-2241s])

### Draw on Liquidity (Most Important)
- **Direction trumps perfect setup** "You can have a perfect order block right and still fail because you have the direction wrong" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3791s-3798s])
- **Understanding where price gravitates** "The reason why some order blocks work and some order blocks don't work is understanding where the draw on liquidity is" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2062s-2081s])
- **HTF + news > entry model** "Higher time frames plus news... what's on the bottom is actual setup and itself so order blocks is just used as a potential entry model" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3818s-3826s])

### Healthy vs Unhealthy Trends
- **Healthy uptrend has down close candles** "A very healthy uptrend is when we have down close candles. So price can be supported towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [387s-402s])
- **Straight up = straight down** "Whenever we move up in a straight line without any down close candles... it's going to have to pull back in a straight line as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [402s-411s])
- **Explosive moves need consolidation** "To sustain the move it has to hold key levels because we don't have any down close candles" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3960s-3972s])

### Scalper vs High Probability
- **One confidence = scalper trade** "If you only have one confidence... this is more of a scalpers trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [1848s-1861s])
- **No external confluence = just break and retest** "If you have no other external conferences... every single order block all you're going to be doing it's just like a breaking retest" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4090s-4102s])
- **Multiple confluence = hold for bigger target** "If you got in at previous day lows no problem... you can hold it up towards this 274" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [1707s-1732s])

### Reclaim (Order Block Failure)
- **Fails order block, resets above = bullish** "If it fails the order block and resets back up or reclaims above your order block that just signifies that price wants to go to higher" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3130s-3139s])
- **Reclaim = breaks below then back above key level** "The reclaim is just something breaks below... so let's say on the higher time frames... we have this right here... reclaiming back above a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3157s-3188s])

### Timeframe Context
- **Daily levels first** "I'm first going to mark out the daily candles... once we're able to close above you can see this end up being used as a flip" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2400s-2418s])
- **Pre-market levels secondary** "After that maybe I can mark out a couple pre-market levels" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [2423s-2437s])
- **Multiple touches = key level** "We have about five touches here on Tesla... if we can break above 260 we're going to be looking for price to push back towards upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [1327s-1339s])

### Position Management
- **Scale on pops with options** "When you're trading zeros right you want to sell into strength... you just want to scale along the way in sell on pops" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3095s-3114s])
- **Sell 25% on first pop** "I was just selling like three on pops... I scaled up like 25% and then I just scaled along the ways" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3218s-3236s])
- **Move stop to breakeven after first target** "Once price hits new high day you can move your underlying to break even" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [1806s-1819s])

### Relative Strength Context
- **Tesla relative strength = momentum trumps** "Tesla was so relatively strong compared to the cues that this just like the tape was just so bullish that trumped anything" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4216s-4230s])
- **Momentum moves don't respect technicals** "If you have some momentum like this where you understand the Tesla and how runs when it runs... it doesn't matter anything about technicals" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4255s-4278s])

---

- **HTF + news > entry model** "Higher time frames plus news... what's on the bottom is actual setup and itself" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3818s-3826s])

### Multi-Day Holds
- **Swing setup into next week** "This to me right is going to be a really nice setup coming to next week... looking for the retest of your previous high pivot point" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4476s-4493s])
- **Overnight gap risk** "It's obviously harder to manage a swing overnight because right if you buy here... overnight you could gap down" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4493s-4510s])

## 9. Trade Selection

### Confluence Hierarchy
- **HTF + news + setup + relative strength** "Remember drawn the queries and most important... higher time frames plus news... what's on the bottom is actual setup and itself" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3818s-3826s])

### Market Regime
- **Consolidation = range high to range low** "If we're consolidating a little bit more here on Netflix all we're doing is just going from range high to range low" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [3367s-3384s])
- **Range break = momentum** "Once we finally broke outside of kind of this range and once we had that range break that's when you had that momentum kind of pick in" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4362s-4374s])

### Ticker Personality
- **Tesla momentum phases** "Tesla back here was like untradable very hard to trade it throughout here but trading it through these months were so nice towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4323s-4341s])
- **Hard to trade = fake outs** "Tesla's been really bad it's been hard to trade a lot of fake outs" (building-your-profitable-system_Order_Block_B_R.txt [4349s-4356s])
