# Extraction: building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade

## 1. Break-and-retest
- **Pop-and-fade as break-and-retest on higher timeframe** "This just looks like for me a perfect break in retest and this is exactly what we look for" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1299s-1303s])
- **Break-and-retest visible on 5-minute with extended hours** "if you go into the five minute chart you can see that this looks like a break and retest as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2941s-2945s])
- **Extended hours make break-and-retest cleaner** "I like drawing them on the extended trading hours because it makes it a lot more cleaner... plus right you can see right you had a nice little reaction here in the pre market as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2961s-2977s])
- **After previous day low is taken out, retest is lower probability** "Once we already take out the take out the main objective these ones are always a lot more lower probability" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1498s-1504s])
- **Do not look for retest after previous day low is taken** "after we take out your previous day low we do not want to be looking for the retest of this three oh three" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1515s-1521s])

## 2. One-candle-rule / opening-candle-retest
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** — The speaker does not use the term "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as a named rule. He discusses opening candle range/print as a level for entries.

## 3. 84% rule / re-entries
- **84% rule mentioned as re-entry after stop-out** "you could always hit it back with the 84% roll" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3575s-3578s])
- **84% rule as reclaim of key level** "You'll see sometimes right the 84% roll right we'll take momentum because it's a key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [872s-876s])
- **84% rule used after stop-out on Apple** "I took that trade and stopped it a later in 84% roll got again" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3642s-3648s])

## 4. Order blocks
- **Order block used as entry confirmation** "we end up using this order block and we just end up having continuation towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2023s-2029s])
- **Order block as support on slow grind up** "if it slowly grinds up then that means it's basically using like every down close candle support... you can see like it would do something like this" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3953s-3961s])
- **No order blocks = faster fade** "if you have a nice pop that just like immediately pops right into order key level that means it really just moved here fairly quickly. So if it were just were to reject it would move down fairly quickly because there's no levels of support" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3965s-3979s])

## 5. Key levels
- **Previous day highs/lows as primary targets inside range** "We always like to target your previous day highs and previous day lows just do the fact that there's a lot of liquidity resting above and below those key areas" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [589s-600s])
- **Opening candle range high/low marked every day** "I always mark out the opening candle range high and open candle range low" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1132s-1137s])
- **Opening print vs opening range candle** "Sometimes you'll see it respects the opening print sometimes it respects the opening range candle. I personally always use the opening range high and opening range low" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1153s-1162s])
- **Pre-market highs as key levels** "the premarket highs are good like especially momentum and you're just looking for quick moves in the market... premarket highs usually line up with other key levels as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1531s-1539s])
- **Previous day low as target for pop-and-fade** "your targets in this case would be your previous days low" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [567s-574s])
- **Higher timeframe key levels trump intraday levels** "I'd much rather use your previous day highs and lows... if there's the one minute and opening range in combination with your previous day highs and lows even better. But if it's just by itself, I'd much rather look for the previous day highs and lows first" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4106s-4132s])

## 6. Time-of-day
- **Opening play defined as first 30 minutes** "Anything within the first 30 minutes of market open" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [208s-210s])
- **Pop-and-fade occurs within first 30 minutes** "the time frame that we want to be looking for this trading opportunity is within really its first 30 minutes... preferably within around the first 30 minutes give or take" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [507s-516s])
- **Outside range pop-and-fade happens within first 10-15 minutes** "within the first kind of 10 to 15 minutes or within the first 30 minute time frame" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3043s-3051s])
- **Gap fill has no specific timeframe** "there's not actually a specific time... it's just within a 48 hour period. This can happen right off the bat it can happen later into the day" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4489s-4503s])
- **News timing matters for reversals** "with news like time matters as well and you have to look more on the indices... red folder news timing... the candles if there's like the eight 30 news if there's 10 30 news the time of day... time and week" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4529s-4544s])

## 7. Exits
- **Targets = previous day lows (inside range)** "your targets in this case would be your previous days low" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [567s-574s])
- **Targets = pre-market lows and below (outside range)** "your targets would be your pre market lows and then the next levels below which would be like an external level towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3072s-3079s])
- **Scaling/adding on retest** "I got in here. I think Tony got in on this candle... actually added into this position" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3369s-3375s])
- **Adding on retest after aggressive entry** "take a smaller starter maybe on the entry number two and then add if it retests" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3762s-3768s])

## 8. HTF application
- **Clear downtrend on 15-minute required** "a clear down trend at least on the 15 minute time frame" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [281s-290s])
- **Higher timeframe trend trumps intraday** "the higher time frames is always good at trump what happens in your day" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2793s-2797s])
- **Multiple timeframes required** "if you're on the one minute chart you want to have minimum right the narrative the 15 minutes... you want to have right the 15 minute time frame or the five minute time frame pulled up" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2106s-2120s])
- **Higher timeframe first, then lower for entries** "we go into the higher time frames first and then we go on to the lower time frames" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2846s-2854s])
- **15-minute for narrative, 1-minute for entries** "You want to be on the five in 15 minute time frame for your narrative and then you can go into the one minute time frame to look for your entries" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2129s-2135s])
- **Aggressive entry on 1-minute is actually break-and-retest on 5-minute** "it's technically a reversal trade but the reason why... if you're on the five minute chart this is just a break and we're gonna test on the five minute towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2146s-2155s])

## 9. Trade selection
- **Pop-and-fade criteria: clear downtrend + consolidation/retracement + pop into key level + rejection** "clear trend towards the downside... consolidation slash retracement... pop into a key level... rejection offer key area and then a fade" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [279s-391s])
- **Best pop-and-fades require downtrend** "the best pop and fades should happen when there is some sort of continuation... continuation towards the downside" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [305s-310s])
- **Pop-and-fade not for choppy/consolidating markets** "if it's chopping around then a pop and fade is gonna be fairly difficult to capture" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [293s-300s])
- **Speed of pop matters** "the best pop and fades happen when right we have an immediate pop... we usually like to see speed into our key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3973s-3985s])
- **Slow grind up = harder fade** "if it slowly grinds up... it's gonna be a lot harder on the way back down because it is supported by like order blocks" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4002s-4012s])
- **Market regime determines which opening plays to use** "the past couple of weeks we've been looking for the pop and fade because that's all what we've been doing... whereas if let's say in the event that we break above... we would only play what's in front of us" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4584s-4606s])
- **In downtrend, be aggressive with opening plays** "if I take an opening play in a downtrend and I want to be really aggressive because I'll consider opening plays a little bit more aggressive" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4621s-4630s])
- **In reversal/uptrend, be more patient** "I would much rather be open to playing like intraday setups like later into the day unless there's like it's screaming out at me" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4636s-4648s])
- **Apple personality: slow, fakeouts, eventually reaches target** "apple... it'll take it's nice slow time to get there" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3703s-3715s])
- **Tesla personality: momentum, quick moves** "Tesla nice momentum like in video these ones happen a lot quicker because they're more momentum moves" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3703s-3709s])
- **Focus on volatile names, not Apple** "usually like to focus on ones with more volatility whereas apple this is mainly for investments most people don't day trade apple" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3715s-3722s])

## 10. Concrete numbers
- **Gap fill: 70% within 48 hours** "the status of 48 hour period where it's 70% of the time will fill the gap" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4489s-4494s])

## Pop-and-Fade Specific Rules (Expanded)

### Definition
- **Pop-and-fade = pop-and-drop = same thing** "pop and fade pop and drop the exact same thing" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [269s-275s])

### Two Types
- **Inside the range** "pop and fade inside of the range... inside it's previous day highs and it's previous day low" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [397s-419s])
- **Outside the range** "pop and fade outside the range... we're actually underneath your previous day lows" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2996s-3006s])

### Inside Range: Three Entry Models
- **Entry 1 (Aggressive): Pop into key level** "first potential pop coming into market open is gonna be one of our most aggressive entries" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [722s-728s])
- **Entry 2 (Conservative): Break back underneath opening candle print** "the break back underneath it's opening candle... a breakdown of a key level which in this case would be your opening print" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [786s-890s])
- **Entry 3 (Confirmation): Retest of opening range** "the retest of our opening range candle... for this continue push down towards the previous day low" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [959s-985s])
- **Inside range: can be patient** "if it's inside the range you'll see usually we like to be a little bit more patient" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [742s-748s])
- **Inside range: pop may not fade** "it could very well pop and just continue towards the upside" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [759s-764s])
- **Inside range: always offers at least 2 entries** "it's guaranteed if a pop and fate is guaranteed always going to offer you these two entries right here... aggressive and conservative" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1054s-1064s])
- **Inside range: third entry (retest) doesn't always occur on downside** "it doesn't always have to have that confirmation because the downside... it can just head lower" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1064s-1068s])

### Outside Range: Two Entry Models
- **Entry 1 (Aggressive): Rejection off previous day low** "the rejection right off of the bat which is a really aggressive entry" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3120s-3127s])
- **Entry 2 (Aggressive): Break back underneath opening candle print low** "the break back underneath your opening candle print low... a little bit more of an aggressive entry because now your risk would be a lot more tighter" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3131s-3153s])
- **Outside range: only offers one solid entry usually** "it only offers you really one solid entry" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3098s-3103s])
- **Outside range: must be aggressive** "you have to be really aggressive on these ones" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3173s-3178s])
- **Outside range: retest of opening candle range low usually won't occur** "the retest of your open candle range low right this usually won't occur" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3165s-3169s])

### Pop Must Come First
- **Must pop higher first** "if it pops higher than we can look for a fade back towards the downside. If it just drops then this would not be considered a pop and fade" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [798s-809s])
- **Want to see rise, not drop first** "we don't want to see a drop first you want to see it rise higher" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1867s-1874s])

### Consolidation in Pre-Market
- **Consolidation in pre-market is ideal** "What we like to see generally speaking in the pre market is consolidation... after a big trending day towards the downside, what we want to see in the pre market is consolidation" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [474s-490s])
- **Do not want pre-market continuation lower** "if it continues lower than the pre market I'll have to look for something else" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [486s-490s])

### Downside vs Upside Trading
- **Downside: be more aggressive** "on the downside we have to be a little bit more aggressive" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4204s-4207s])
- **Downside: may only get one entry** "you usually have that one entry on the lower time friends and if you miss it then sometimes you miss that full trade" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4244s-4251s])
- **Downside: price stays heavy, no retest** "when price days heavy it stays heavy... sometimes it doesn't even come up to order blocks because that's how heavy price days" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4196s-4204s])
- **Upside: more re-entries** "we like trading towards upside because a lot of the times we can have a couple of re entries" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [4237s-4242s])

### Five-Minute Opening Range Context
- **Don't trade inside 5-minute opening range without HTF thesis** "on a regular day if you don't have any higher time frame thesis or if you trade in between the five minute opening ranges a good chance you're probably getting chopped around" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2690s-2702s])
- **HTF trend trumps 5-minute opening range** "if we have a clear trend in the markets I'm very much rather good to be looking at the chart and the overall trend than just looking at this five minute range" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2776s-2784s])

### QQQ Alignment
- **Correlation with broad market indexes for confirmation** "Discorrelation help with broad market indexes with confirmation for your move. Yeah, that's always something that we look for" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [1200s-1206s])
- **QQQ showing weakness = confirmation for short** "I was looking for the break underneath while the exact same time you can see right cue showing weakness" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2494s-2499s])
- **QQQ rejection at key level = confluence** "cues on this day nice rejection off that 519" (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [2532s-2534s])

### Trade Review Judgment
- **Apple: "I personally hate apple just because it does this"** (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3583s-3586s])
- **Apple: "apple sucks I agree"** (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3612s-3617s])
- **Apple: "I'm done with apple no more trading apple for me"** (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3638s-3641s])
- **Apple: "it literally looks every single time it looks perfect higher time frames beautiful and it's like all right let's now go into the one minute and then the one minute sucks"** (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3617s-3622s])
- **Apple: "only times apples really good is when we have news"** (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3684s-3690s])
- **Tesla: "nice momentum"** (building-your-profitable-system_Pop_Fade.txt [3703s-3709s])

# EXTRACTION: building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest_transcript.txt

## Pre-Market High/Low Retest

### Definition & Session
- **Pre-market defined as 4am-9:30am EST** "the pre-market starts at 4 a.m and goes to 9:30 a.m" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [216s-229s])
- **Pre-market levels used more than after-hours** "the pre-market levels are usually used more than the after-hours the only time we use the after-hours is if there's any sort of news or we do have earnings" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [238s-249s])
- **Earnings highs/lows are personal favorite** "This is my personal favorite is looking at these earnings highs and lows" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [566s-577s])

### When Pre-Market Levels Matter
- **Pre-market levels matter when OUTSIDE previous day range** "if prices in between our previous day highs and previous day lows then the pre market levels most likely won't matter as much" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [354s-362s])
- **Inside range = don't pay attention to pre-market levels** "if we're inside the range I'm really not going to be paying attention to the pre market levels as much" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [367s-371s])
- **Outside range = pre-market levels become important** "if there's let's say in the pre market we end up having this push above or maybe you're above previous day highs or below previous day lows then we can use our pre market levels" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [375s-386s])
- **Previous day highs/lows have most volume** "the previous day highs and lows have the most volume so if they have the most volumes those levels are gonna hold the most weight" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [444s-449s])
- **Pre-market levels inside range = less volume, need confluence** "if the pre market levels are inside the range then there's probably not that much volume and you don't want to pay attention to them as much you want to be looking at these levels in confidence with another setup or other levels as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [449s-459s])

### Earnings Highs/Lows (Most Important)
- **Earnings candle has most volume** "whenever we have earnings everyone's waiting for earnings we have a lot of volume" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [578s-586s])
- **Earnings high = first candle that pops** "I consider your earnings high that first candle that pops" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1594s-1597s])
- **Earnings high/low more important than previous day high/low** "We have something that's more important than this previous day high and low, which is your earnings high... whatever has most volume so in this case your earnings high is gonna be used because there's more volume here" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1520s-1535s])
- **Earnings levels stay relevant for days/weeks** "this level still get to be used a few days later just because it's a very key level" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1396s-1406s])
- **Earnings high/low = range, markets gravitate** "if the markets gravitate towards one I like low or high right it's gonna like to gravitate towards the upside right if it takes out the low it's most likely to go back to the high" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [2305s-2315s])

### News Candle High/Low
- **News candle high/low for CPI, PPI, unemployment** "news candle high and low in the pre-markets is generally red folder news... very important for specifically CPI and we also have PPI here as well" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [637s-659s])
- **Unemployment every Thursday at 8:30am** "unemployment happens at 8:30 as well this happens every single Thursday at 8:30 a.m Eastern standard time" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [663s-670s])
- **CPI and PPI are major movers** "Unemployment doesn't move the market as much besides right core PPI and core CPI these are the major ones" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [670s-681s])

### Drawing Levels
- **Draw areas, not exact lines** "I personally like drawing in an area just because we have multiple touches" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1163s-1168s])
- **When levels are close, mark whole area** "if the levels are close by each other your pre-market highs your previous day highs I'm just gonna mark it the whole area instead of having right like three lines" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [2102s-2110s])
- **Draw areas for tech names, precise lines for futures** "the only time I'll maybe just draw it strictly levels is when I'm trading the futures themselves but I'll always draw higher time frame areas when I'm trading the tech names" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3975s-3986s])

### How Far Back to Draw Levels
- **Three to five sessions on 15-minute chart** "a good rule of thumb is three to five sessions if you're on the 15 minute chart" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3625s-3631s])
- **If nothing in 3-5 sessions, go further back** "if you don't have three to five then you have to go back even further to whatever the most relevant information that we do have" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3659s-3667s])
- **Daily timeframe: look back 20-40 sessions** "for the daily timeframe I look looking at the past 20 to 40 sessions" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3673s-3677s])

### Trade Examples
- **Tesla: buy at previous day high retest, sell at pre-market high** "bought it down towards your previous day highs sold up into your pre-market highs" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1862s-1867s])
- **Break above pre-market high = acceleration** "as soon as it broke pre-market highs you can see the acceleration that we have just because now we're breaking up of your only level of resistance" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1909s-1920s])
- **No entry model off pre-market highs/lows directly** "there's not really an entry model off of your pre-market highs in pre-market lows" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [1936s-1941s])

### Key Concept: All Levels Are Old Highs/Old Lows
- **All levels are fundamentally the same** "they're all the same thing primarily they're just all old highs and old lows" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3886s-3896s])
- **Multiple touches = most important** "whatever has multiple touches is what we're gonna base it off of" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3902s-3906s])
- **Levels should "speak out to you"** "they should just speak out to you... your eyes should be magnetized to like these higher time frame point of interest old highs and old lows" (building-your-profitable-system_Pre_Market_High_Low_Retest.txt [3916s-3927s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Definition - Reclaim vs Break-and-Retest]** "The reclaim, you're basing more factors into it... The break and retest, it's just simply just kind of marked out the high and low, most people can see it." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3146s-3155s])

- **[Break-and-Retest as preferred over reclaim at range highs]** "In this case, right, usually this, I consider this not really a reclaim that you want to take, just because it's already at high day. In this case, you much rather wait for the break and retest." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3076s-3086s])

- **[Break-and-Retest for range breakouts]** "If it was just chopping around, let's say for the first hour and a half, we usually just teach, right, just wait for the break above highs, retest, continuation towards the upside. This is like classic, right, just waiting for the range to break." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3045s-3060s])

- **[Break-and-Retest vs Reclaim at high-of-day]** "If I'm trading the break and retest, I'd much rather wait for the break and retest above highs for a continuation towards upside. If I'm looking for the reclaim, I'd much rather look for the reclaim in anticipation that price is going to move higher instead of looking for the reclaim, right, as soon as the break set of high day. Because it doesn't make too much sense if you're going to buy a breakout, technically, that's what a reclaim is. You're buying it once a reclaims a key level at high day." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3092s-3118s])

- **[Break-and-Retest example - Palantir]** "So in this case, we were looking for the retest of your previous day highs. So you can see as price comes down, what do you want to see? We really want to see the whole developing range for this push higher. So this would be our number one scenario for palantir, right? The retest off of your previous day highs. This is what we're looking for, right? With the stop just to break below, right? Continuation higher." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1489s-1507s])

- **[Break-and-Retest - no reaction = no trade]** "In this case, right, we never ended up taking the trade because there was no reaction. Right? You can see, preferably what we like to see kind of the wick, right? Body step in, buyer step in and we continue higher. The reason why this maybe didn't work is because of the cues, right? Maybe other contacts, but basically the thesis is break retest continuation towards the upside. And you can see price, no buyer stepped in. So there's no trade." (building-your-professional-system_Reclaim.txt [1507s-1533s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-Entries

- **[84% Rule defined as best reclaim trades]** "The 84% rules are the best reclaim trades, but then you can always be looking for reclaim later into the day." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [95s-104s])

- **[84% Rule requires holding structure]** "As the 84% roll needs to hold structure, does it reclaiming to hold structure? Yeah. So the 84% roll needs to hold structure. That's why it's the 84%." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1915s-1922s])

- **[84% Rule vs later reclaim - probability difference]** "If you have a recline later into the session, then it's not going to be as high probability as the original one because this one now broke structure and now it's pushing back towards the upside. So this could very much, well, just reject off highs and just continue back inside of the range. But the reason why an 84% roll is the best is because of hold structure, whereas a reclame, it's still a reclaminant, it's the exact same level, but the structure looks a little bit different than on the one minute timeframe. So this is the reclame, but this is going to be less high probability than the original thesis, which would be an 84% roll reclame." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1922s-1953s])

- **[84% Rule - market structure intact = clear momentum]** "What's going to be the best reclaimed is going to be the 84% rule because market structure would still be intact for this push towards the upside right off the bat. And that would be clear momentum." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1569s-1580s])

- **[84% Rule - best momentum when structure intact]** "But the best reclaimed and the best momentum is going to occur when the market structure is still intact." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1597s-1602s])

- **[84% Rule - tight stop at key level]** "A lot of times, if we reclaim a key level, that's where that 84% real comes in, we can have a super tight stop and then once price reclaims a super key level, then we can look for it continuation back towards the upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [311s-322s])

- **[84% Rule invalidated - example Tesla]** "So this year 84 percent roll. Right. And now the 84 percent roll is invalidated because it never broke back above your woman order block and you end up pushing back towards the downside." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2188s-2197s])

- **[84% Rule - favorite reclaims]** "The reclaims, as I mentioned, my favorite reclaims are the 84% rule." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2296s-2301s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **[Order block drawing - wick to body]** "I like to mark the wick to the body. So if I have this, it gives us a little bit more confluence as I can go off of." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3312s-3323s])

- **[Order block as support in healthy trends]** "If you're on the one minute timeframe, you would have to go on to you would can mark out your one minute order blocks. Right? These will be used as support." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3533s-3541s])

- **[Order blocks vs inversions - tech names vs indices]** "I would much rather use order blocks, but a lot of the times when you're trading the tech names, that's what you're going to be using. You're going to be using order blocks and then when you're using if you're trading the spine Q's inversions are great just because it's more algorithmic and it's more mechanical on that." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3441s-3456s])

- **[Down-close candles as order blocks - healthy trend indicator]** "We'll be always talking about order blocks down close canos. Support price towards the upside. That's a healthy trend. If you go on to any sort of trend higher, right? The healthiest trends are when you have down close canos." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3507s-3522s])

- **[One-minute order block example - Tesla]** "I personally waited for one-minute order block. I talked about this last week but one-minute order block drawn this out the reason why the only reason why we're able to take Tesla here was because higher time frame was bullish." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [4069s-4085s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Previous day highs/lows - best liquidity]** "The previous day highs and previous day lows are the ones that have the best liquidity level." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1338s-1343s])

- **[Reclaim requires a key level with multiple touches]** "It has to be some sort of strong level with multiple touches." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [389s-395s])

- **[Common levels for reclaims]** "The common levels that we can be looking for reclames, previous day highs and previous day lows, your opening range, candles, which is basically your opening print. This is a really good one that a lot of people look for where we always look for these ones. Primark at highs and lows, right, and key level reclames as well." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1226s-1245s])

- **[Intraday levels preferred over pre-market]** "You always want to be using your intraday levels because that's where the most volume is at right pre-market levels doesn't have that much volume." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [5185s-5194s])

- **[Pre-market levels only when nothing else]** "The only time I'll use your external levels is something like this when you have nothing else to use." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [5153s-5162s])

- **[Earnings highs as key levels]** "The earnings key levels always important. So that's why you have this level marked out your earnings high." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [5177s-5185s])

- **[Daily level on 1-minute chart - need wider zone]** "If this is a daily level... If I'm on the one minute, and you go to think about it, if it's a daily level, sometimes it's going to have a lot of fake oats and stuff... You have to give it a my opinion, a little bit more room, or you're going to have to kind of widen up your level just because you're on the daily chart." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3235s-3285s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Reclaims work best before 11am EST]** "Reclames generally work best before 11 am EST." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1252s-1258s])

- **[No exact time - context based]** "I wouldn't say there's like a general time. Like it really depends as I mentioned, context based." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1258s-1264s])

- **[First 30-90 minutes choppy]** "Sometimes if you're looking for a reclame right now, right, it's a little bit more choppy right during first 30 minutes to an hour and a half." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1264s-1268s])

- **[After consolidation - better movement later]** "If we consolidate for the first hour to an hour and a half, then generally speaking later into the session, we're going to have, right, better movement." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1295s-1304s])

- **[Earlier reclaims for opening plays on 1-minute]** "If you're on the one minute timeframe, you can be looking for right opening play plays for reclamet off the bat." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1328s-1335s])

- **[Later reclaims need higher timeframe]** "If you're more on the higher timeframe, so you can be looking for reclams later into the session, but you're now going to be on the higher timeframe. You're going to be minimum on the 5 and 15 minute." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1323s-1333s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Target = high of day after reclaim]** "If you're long, your targets are going to be high of day." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [573s-574s])

- **[Short seller stops = high of day = long target]** "If your short, your stop is going to be at high of day here as well. So that's where you're going to place your stop. So that's the reason why if we're able to reclaim this level, we really want speed and momentum back up towards your high of day because that's where the short seller stops are." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [574s-593s])

- **[Scaling example - sell some at key level, sell some at next]** "Potentially, you could have been looking for let's say buy here, sell some and then sell some as it pushes up and towards the next key level." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2340s-2355s])

- **[Taking profits at logical levels - all-time highs]** "Everyone that was buying these dips they're looking to sell up into our to 125 130 right that's where everyone's looking to be looking to take profits that's where I took profit just because that's a logical level." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [4453s-4466s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Higher timeframe thesis required for reclaim]** "The best reclames are going to occur when there's a lot of fake oats on the lower timeframes, but the higher timeframes is still intact. If you look for a reclame and there's no higher timeframe thesis, sometimes a majority of the times will occur is that it'll just end up being a fake out." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [643s-661s])

- **[Reclaim = higher timeframe hold, 1-minute fakeout]** "Really a reclaim a lot of the times is just a higher timeframe hold, but it's just a one minute fake out. So that's why these reclaims work so well is because the market structure is still intact on the higher time frames, but on the lower time frame, it potentially could fake you out." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1707s-1718s])

- **[HTF trend determines bias - Palantir example]** "If everyone's bullish on the higher timeframe, we don't have to come into the day looking for, let's say potential shorts towards the downside. So we're already bullish. So we already have our thesis slash bias coming to market open. So all we're looking for is trading opportunities towards the upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1385s-1401s])

- **[HTF trend determines whether reclaim works later]** "The reason why Tesla worked out in this case was because of the higher time frames." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2255s-2260s])

- **[HTF - last leg of move most explosive]** "Whenever trading the last leg either on the lower timeframes or the higher timeframes that's when you have these most explosive moves." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [4317s-4322s])

- **[HTF - understanding where price is going]** "If you can understand where the higher time frames is going you can position yourself fairly nicely." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [5069s-5073s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Context and logic over entry model]** "What matters most is context and logic and kind of the draw and liquidity. Whereas, price for you most likely going to go. Because if you understand that, you can trade a break out. You can trade a reversal. You can trade a breaking retest. You can trade any entry model possible. If you have a thesis, and you understand where prices most likely going to gravitate towards, that's the main thing." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3185s-3205s])

- **[Reclaim is more discretionary than break-and-retest]** "The reclaim, that's why sometimes it's a little bit more discretionary because you're looking at more confluences, there's other things that go into the break and retest. It's just simply just kind of marked out the high and low, most people can see it." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [3139s-3155s])

- **[Best reclaims have strong push right after reclaim]** "The best reclames as I mentioned are going to reclaim. And we're going to have a strong push right off the bat up and towards your high of day key level." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [612s-620s])

- **[Choppy reclaim after reclaim = not great]** "If we reclaim the key level, you have a really nice push towards the upside. Instead of breaking above your reclame level. And then we just end up chopping around. This is not really what you want to see for a reclame." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [598s-609s])

- **[Two scenarios - continuation or reversal]** "There's really only two scenarios that you can be looking for when you're trading this setup is that you're either going to get that continuation right off the bat for a push higher or there's a potential reversal play. So that's why you have to be, that's why at least for these trade setups, it's either going to work right away or you're going to have to cut it right away." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [827s-844s])

- **[Wait for candle close 90% of time]** "90% of the time, you always want to wait for the candles to close. That's in most scenarios as well. 90% of the time you want to wait for the candles to close unless you have some sort of external factor, which potentially could be, it would be more discretionary." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1005s-1020s])

- **[Getting in before candle close is discretionary]** "Getting in before the candle close is discretionary. And Georgia times you always want to wait for the candles to close because it can always work. Right, what's through the damage bodies tell the story. When you start getting in before the candles close, that's going to be discretionary based off of maybe higher time frames or relative strength of weakness. You want to try and keep it as mechanical as possible." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1020s-1041s])

- **[Reclaim doesn't require retest]** "A lot of the times the best reclames, what will occur is that price will end up reclaiming key level and will have really strong price pushed back towards upside. The reason why the reclames, we don't have to wait for the retest, is because due to the fact that everyone knows it's bullish." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [513s-533s])

- **[Wait for strong price action at reclaim level]** "You have the way for strong price action. So really it's based off of number one the higher timeframes." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [638s-647s])

### Displacement/FVG
- **[Inversion defined - impulsive move with no wicks]** "Basically what an inversion is, is that price has an a very impulsive move towards the downside, usually with no wicks. It usually is just your body, and we have continuation." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2569s-2587s])

- **[Inversion - mark body, reclaim above = strong signal]** "So a lot of people what they'll mark out is this body right here. They'll mark out this wick. And this is basically what you'd like to see reclaim above. If you want to see any sort of continuation back towards upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2605s-2622s])

- **[Impulsive move lower + reclaim = strongest reclaims]** "If we have an impulsive move lower, let's say, we kind of do this and we have a big drop. We have a big drop towards the downside and we end up breaking underneath these keys lows impulsively. This shows a lot of sellers and everyone's going to be looking to potentially go in short because if it moves down lower quicker and then it can actually show that they're strength on the reclame. This is going to look really good because right, this was an impulsive move lower. A lot of shorts are now trapped and then we end up having this big push higher because it basically induces shorts to think that it's going to have lower. But this reclame is going to have a lot of speed and moment to back towards the upside just because you have a lot of impulsive reclandals." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2744s-2784s])

- **[Last leg most impulsive - exhaustion move]** "The most impulsive leg happens at the very end of the move. So a lot of the times when you have this really impulsive move lower, this usually signifies that it's potentially going to end because this kind of has the fastest move right down towards a key level." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2802s-2824s])

- **[Healthy trend vs straight-line move]** "What a healthy trend looks like is when it's like holding down close candles right and we're holding price and pushing higher. What an unhealthy technically would unhealthy is, is if it just kind of moves up in a straight line because if it moves up in a straight that usually signifies that it's potentially could have a reversal back down in a straight line." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2892s-2912s])

### Stop-After-Win / Max Trades/Day
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Reclaim Setup - Full Definition
- **[Reclaim definition]** "In trading, a reclaim refers to when the price of an asset drops below a key level, such as support resistance. But then quickly moves back above it, signaling a potential false breakdown or break out in a shift of momentum." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [222s-237s])

- **[Reclaim = false breakdown]** "Basically what a reclaim is is if we have a key level being a support level, for example, if we break underneath the support level, generally speaking, that means that the buyers were not able to hold the key level support. And then what happens, right, people most people think that we're going to break lower, which in this case, right, you're right, right, we're going to break lower, but if we can reclaim back above your key level support, that signifies as a potential false breakdown." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [242s-268s])

- **[Reclaim = reversal setup]** "Generally speaking, a reclaim is usually more of a reversal set up." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [283s-291s])

- **[Reclaim traps breakout traders]** "A reclaim often traps traders who bet on the initial breakout leading to a sharp reversal." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [286s-292s])

- **[Reclaim shows strength - stronger than original base]** "Once it reclaims this signifies that buyers are back in control. So basically once we break underneath your previous day highs, we're less strong, but if you can reclaim that just shows a lot more strength, then the original base back above your previous day highs." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [436s-452s])

- **[Reclaim invalidates short thesis]** "Once it reclames, it invalidates the short thesis and then the long thesis, it shows that buyers are stepping in because we reclame back above your key level and then we continue back up and towards the highs." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [622s-632s])

- **[Reclaim = same thesis, different entry model]** "It's a very similar thesis, right, the thesis is still in tack, but it's just the entry model." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1850s-1860s])

- **[Reclaim vs 84% Rule - structure difference]** "The 84% roll needs to hold structure. That's why it's the 84%. If you have a recline later into the session, then it's not going to be as high probability as the original one because this one now broke structure and now it's pushing back towards the upside." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [1915s-1937s])

- **[Later reclaim - lower probability, needs more confluence]** "You can be looking for a reclaimed later into the session using the similar thesis, but that's only if there's right later into the session, less high probability. You have to really be looking at the spine cues and the overall context of the market." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2296s-2309s])

- **[Reclaim - time gap depends on HTF staying intact]** "How much time can lap in between breakdowns of reclaimed before that, de-a-brake's down. So this is a good question. So this happens Todd as long as the higher time frames is still intact." (building-your-profitable-system_Reclaim.txt [2358s-2372s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Pivot significance - single down close candle vs strong pivot]** "This is a single down close candle in the uptrend... it's not even really like a pivot high or low really... this isn't really a very strong pivot" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [614s-633s])
- **[Strong pivot defined - price balanced/consolidated]** "Price actually balanced here... came down in a downtrend it actually kind of looks like it almost reverse... there is an actual pretty strong balance here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [694s-708s])
- **[Danger of marking too many levels]** "If you want to mark out everything you want to be extra careful on every single thing that you do, you're gonna have so much stuff marked out and it's probably gonna just confuse you if anything" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [799s-804s])
- **[Deleting levels - when price has gone through them]** "Long story short pretty much when prices already gone through them... I'm not gonna use this high anymore... we're just gonna re-mark out this high as prices already gone through it" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [913s-926s])
- **[Exception - don't delete old highs/lows not yet touched in RTH]** "The one time that you're not gonna take out those old highs or lows... is if in regular trading hours prices not touched there yet" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [991s-998s])
- **[Using both lines and zones]** "I use zones... I think it's nicer to just to make sure you're not like trading into strong resistance" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1074s-1084s])
- **[Zones vs lines - Tesla example with many wicks]** "Have zoned right here big upper wicks... very large upper wicks so it's kind of tough to do this when you have so many levels... in this case lines would be make it a little bit easier for you" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1096s-1113s])
- **[Best of both worlds - zones and pivots]** "Use both both both yeah zones and pivots... I think it's the best of both worlds" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1124s-1127s])
- **[Alerts - most underrated feature]** "People really underestimate the alert feature like it's one of the best features of trading view and it's so simple... set alerts for not only your pre-market game plan for your intraday stuff, but also higher time frame stuff" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1394s-1410s])
- **[Alerts have made enough money to pay for TradingView]** "The alerts have made me enough money to pay for trading view a couple times over just say that" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2208s-2217s])
- **[How to not miss moves - set alerts everywhere]** "That's how you don't miss the moves just set alerts have alerts everywhere on all your tickers do it to every single one" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2178s-2183s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Daily levels for swing trade ideas - trend + key levels]** "For like swing trading and getting trade ideas... your main things that you're looking for are like trend... you're looking for like key levels to break right and then jump to the lower time frames" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2810s-2820s])
- **[Multi-timeframe alignment - daily + 4hr + 1hr + 15min]** "If the daily is bullish the four hours... same thing with the 15 minute and the one hour... usually like they're fairly similar so if like all of those things are bullish or bearish... you're trading the trend there on all four and you're also trading the trend on the lower time frames" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2829s-2844s])
- **[HTF trend + entry model = ~50% win rate]** "And you're also pairing that with like an entry model... you know you're gonna have probably like a 50% win rate at least" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2847s-2850s])
- **[Neutral HTF = hard to trade - Tesla example]** "When you have tickers that look like this... it's very hard... until it hits like one of these these key points and has a reaction... it's gonna be very difficult to trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1939s-1971s])
- **[Avoid guessing on neutral HTF]** "You gotta wait right because you're never gonna get those like really really really nice moves unless you just kind of guess right what you don't do that don't just guess. That's gonna happen. It's not what trading is" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2001s-2007s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Watchlist - tickers with clear HTF trend vs neutral]** "Some of these other tickers... they're actually a little bit tougher to mark out... Google Microsoft Tesla Amazon meta all these really don't have as strong the trend recently as some of these semis do" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1195s-1207s])
- **[Avoid or short weak HTF tickers]** "You either want to avoid those things or you probably want to just play those like downside trades" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1219s-1227s])
- **[Long side semis, short side weak tickers]** "Maybe you just want to go short side and then just use your semis as like long side tickers" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1227s-1232s])
- **[Apple - not worth trading]** "Apple I would say apples just I just take it off the list never traded again... I don't think I'll ever trade apple again... the bars are just... there's just no volatility" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [1722s-1745s])
- **[Set alerts for tickers not actively watched]** "You know usually you're focusing in on day trading for a select few of them... but that doesn't mean that you can't take advantage of some of these other higher time frame moves... maybe it's for swings maybe not... maybe it's for like the entry day trade once it does get to that level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2193s-2206s])
- **[Don't rely on live trading calls as crutch]** "The issue stems that they rely on it and they don't practice on their own... when you join the live trading every day... you have the music a crutch" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [3273s-3288s])
- **[Test yourself - don't join live trading some days]** "Don't join the live trading let's just say Thursday or Friday and go on your own" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [3307s-3314s])
- **[Confidence boost from own trades working out]** "The confidence boost that you get when they do work out... it's a different level right you'll start to build this like rhythm you'll start to build patterns" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [3459s-3465s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[~50% win rate with HTF trend + entry model]** "You know you're gonna have probably like a 50% win rate at least" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2847s-2850s])
- **[Most students have >50% win rate]** "Most of you guys do have like over 50% win rate... it's just always the risk reward is a little bit skewed on a lot of trades" (hayden-s-coaching_How_To_Properly_Pick_A_Great_Watchlist_To_Trade_Q_A.txt [2856s-2865s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Scaling with break-and-retest - options vs stocks/futures]** "I don't think that you're really gonna be managing the positions the same with futures or stocks at all... Similarly to options they just don't move the same... you don't have to scale into strength. They don't move exponentially" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [178s-191s])
- **[Futures/stocks scaling - key levels or fixed R:R only]** "For futures and stocks... You are pretty much just using key levels... you're pretty much a selling your whole position at your higher time frame targets or you're using a fixed risk to reward" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [203s-212s])
- **[Futures/stocks - scaling not worth it]** "I just really don't think it's worth it. I don't think you're gonna get the same P and L if you're scaling with futures as you are with options" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [221s-232s])
- **[Futures gain linearly - no exponential benefit to scaling]** "futures just gain Linear basically... futures contracts don't move exponentially Neither do stocks so there's not really a purpose of doing this" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [253s-281s])

- **[Key levels used for exits with futures/stocks]** "You are pretty much just using key levels... you're pretty much a selling your whole position at your higher time frame targets" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [203s-212s])
- **[HOD/LOD as technical target - if hit and reverse, cut to breakeven]** "If you have a setup like this... it hits high of day And reverses back down and you haven't taken profit yet... if it doesn't get there if you don't get the risk rewarded at your trade right out the bat... And it hits this level and starts coming back down reversing you're gonna cut the straight break even" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2961s-3021s])
- **[Next key level for runner position after 80% sold at 2R]** "Next key level... you see what I'm getting out here you guys like so like you're using this as a reference point" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2675s-2696s])

## 7. Exits
- **[80% of position off at 2R - golden rule for scaling with options]** "Rule of thumb is... At two are you should have like 80% of your position taken off of my opinion" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2534s-2538s])
- **[1-3 contracts - close full position at 2R, no scaling]** "For the first like three cons that you're trading you know you're you're just closing out your whole position here... you're just closing out majority of your trade at this level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2698s-2708s])
- **[4+ contracts - implement scaling: 3 sold at 2R, 1 at next key level]** "At the four at the four mark you have a 75 right so you have three and one so 75 and 25% obviously so if you really wanted to with four contracts you could implement some scaling... So you go three sold at to our And then Next key level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2658s-2681s])
- **[5 contracts - 4 sold at 2R, 1 at next key level]** "For five right for five contracts you do the same thing... Four sold at two are... And then one next key level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2683s-2690s])
- **[If using 1.5R instead of 2R - 60-70% of position off]** "With one point five I would say like you know 60% of your position is probably Ideal right depending on how you're gonna scale I'd say 60 to 70%... you don't want to take off too much because you haven't really got two times your risk at this point" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2748s-2780s])
- **[Cut to breakeven when technical target hit and price reverses]** "If it hits this level and starts coming back down reversing you're gonna cut the straight break even... this is how you're gonna basically solve your your bigger losing trades as well" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3013s-3027s])
- **[Use break of structure / change of character to define exit point for calculating average R]** "You need to kind of use a Break of structure Style or change character style kind of reference point or else you're gonna have like really really really high-resural or trades which you kind of wanna avoid" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [1877s-1888s])
- **[Change of character is best exit reference]** "A change of character... or a break of structure one of the two I would say change a character by the best one right when it turns from an uptrend To a downtrend" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2037s-2045s])
- **[Full candle closure outside level = cut trade]** "Realistically a full candle outside of it is Technically better... if it hits hive day And there's a full candle closure like outside the level at that point I would probably cut the trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3613s-3646s])
- **[Three-candle rejection pattern to cut trade]** "You could even use like a three-candle process... Which you say you have the green candle up and do it maybe like a red candle like this and then this breaks the last candle... If it starts rejecting off of that right and this candle closes down you could cut this trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3081s-3101s])
- **[Refuse to lose - cut when main target hit and price reverses]** "I'm cutting the trade like I'm not even I'm not taking the loss on it right there's just no way... if it hits a main target and it's coming back down I'm not given it that any room... I refuse to lose" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3179s-3196s])

- **[Make trading boring - turn brain off, same thing every time]** "You want to set up these systems in place so like when it comes a trading you're kind of you can just kind of turn your brain off... you just kind of show up and you just kind of wait there for things to happen And you just do the same thing and that's why when when Tony and Jack talk about make trading and boring It's kind of what they're talking about" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3947s-3967s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[1-2% risk per trade is ideal, 3% is too much]** "I would say one to two percent of your account... This is the general error three is a bit much... if you lose three trades in a row... you're here down like almost 10% on your account" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [671s-698s])
- **[~$100 risk per trade is good general number]** "Let's just say our one honor to like $100... This is a good general number and also a lot of people are actually just risking around $100 per trade... you can risk about like 30 cents fish on most of these contracts and you can get like three of them and you can be adding $90 risk" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [615s-644s])
- **[Over 40% win rate is very good]** "If you guys are around over 40 I want to say like that's really good... Like over a 40 percent win rate is very very very good" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [811s-818s])
- **[Most students have 44-71% win rates]** "48... 55 58 44... 67... 662... you guys all have a fairly good percent winner rate... - **[Average win vs loss is where most traders fail]** "average win versus average loss... This is where I think most traders fail which is why we're doing the session today" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [828s-833s])
- **[If average win/loss is above 1 with 50% win rate = breakeven]** "If this number is above one... let's just say above one and you have a 50 percent win rate You're gonna be break even" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [845s-854s])
- **[With 50% win rate, average win/loss of 1.1 = profitable]** "If you have a 50... if you're average when lost portray it is like anywhere above like 1.1 you're you're profitable" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [968s-970s])
- **[With 67% win rate, can have 0.7-0.8 average win/loss and still profit]** "Robert if you're over 67 you can have like probably like you know 0.7 maybe or 0.8... your losses can outweigh a lot of the winners and you can still make money" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [1001s-1005s])
- **[If average R is above 2R, can get away with 33% win rate]** "If you are above this to our... You can kind of get away with a win rate of anything above 33" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2127s-2139s])
- **[80% of position off at 2R is golden rule]** "80% in position should be gone on to our so... I do like that rule. I've always kind of stuck to it" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [2870s-2878s])
- **[Tony sells some below 2R - works because high win rate]** "Tony will take some off the yeah one hundred percent he will take off some lower than two are... It depends on your wind rate right Tony's got a pretty high wind rate so you can get away with it" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3972s-3982s])
- **[If win rate ~45% and selling most at 1:1 = losing money]** "If you're wind rates a little bit lower like let's just say it's like 40-ish or like 45... if you're selling a lot of your position off like let's say a 45 someone right... And you're selling a lot at one to one or most of your position off at one to one you're selling a whole thing... you're actually gonna be losing money" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_build_a_scaling_process_with_options_trading.txt [3988s-4013s])
- **[With 65% win rate, selling at 1:1 works]** "If you have a little bit of a better wind rate like if say let's say you're at like 65... # Extracted Trading Rules

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Bodies vs Wicks, Displacement)
- **Candle closure above level required**: "it can't a closure above the level let's just call it that candle break a candle closure above level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1056s-1062s])
- **Displacement candle must be fully outside the zone**: "This candle is fully outside of the zone. There's space here and the reason why I say this is not just for the confirmed break out" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1127s-1139s])
- **Displacement defined**: "All you all it is is essentially except you have a zone... you are looking for a full candle outside of it" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1751s-1760s])
- **Without full break and closure outside level, no confirmed breakout**: "Until you have a full break in closure outside of the level you don't really have a confirmed break out" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1119s-1126s])
- **Must have a down close candle to define the high**: "there has to be a down close candle... you have to have some sort of pivot high right can't just be green candles over and over and over again" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1027s-1034s])

### Valid Retest (Touch/Zone, Max Wait)
- **Do NOT enter on the retest candle itself**: "Zero percent of the time you're gonna want to enter in on the retest candle... There is very little chance that you know exactly what's going to happen after you do have three confirmations" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1293s-1310s])
- **Decision made on candle after retest**: "You have to make a decision on the next candle after the retest candle you have to make a decision here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1362s-1373s])
- **Max candles from breakout to retest**: "Over eight candles form from candlestick one the three break it to retest" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2989s-3000s])
- **Ideal range is 3-8 candles**: "three to eight candles from one... From candlestick one... three or... break out to retest... this is ideal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2939s-2972s])
- **Avoid immediate retests**: "just don't take immediate retests like you just basically displacement" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [4779s-4781s])

### Entry Trigger (Hammer/Shooting Star)
- **A+ entry: wait for fourth confirmation candle**: "when you wait for this fourth confirmation candle you are essentially giving yourself one more checklist" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1408s-1413s])
- **Wait 45-60 seconds on the confirmation candle**: "you probably want to wait at least 45 to 60 seconds here if you are going to enter or if you're not going to enter" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1425s-1434s])
- **Sniper entry on fifth candle**: "as soon as this fifth candle forms... if it takes out this candle high you know at this point prices going to move to this target" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1472s-1482s])
- **Entry on candle four requires it to break above candle three**: "If you decide to enter in on this fourth candle... if you do it needs to break above... you should also wait 45 to 60 seconds" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1539s-1554s])
- **Entry trigger is candle five taking out previous candle high**: "Candle five takes out the previous candle already after ten set yes 100%... As soon as that pops" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1885s-1895s])
- **If fourth candle shoots up immediately, do not enter**: "if you are waiting for this candle to form the fourth candle and it shoots up right away you're not entering this trade anymore" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1586s-1591s])

### Stop Placement
- **Stop loss should be the retest candle low**: "the stop loss should just be the time to close stop right the higher low... it should just be the retest candle night" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1833s-1840s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **Target is next liquidity/seller zone**: "if we can break and hold above this level... it's gonna gravitate towards this high up here because that is the next point where sellers are" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2093s-2106s])
- **High of day is the minimum target**: "we know at least we're gonna we're gonna probably hit high of day and the next target whatever it is based on our trade idea" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2141s-2147s])

### First-Break vs Late Breaks
- **If HTF objective already hit, don't take the retest**: "if this are it's this higher to get's taken out and you see a pullback... you're essentially knocking along anymore you're not gonna buy down here it already hit the main higher time frame objective" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3099s-3112s])
- **After main objective hit, reversal setup preferred**: "it already took us a liquidity the chances are you're probably gonna have a move down you'd rather show you to reversal set up in this case rather than a break and retest" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3113s-3124s])

### Best Level for Break-and-Retest
- **Best trades are off high of day or low of day**: "every single time the best trade is gonna be off high day and it's for one reason... typically the structures just intact always you're never trading to the wrong side" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [594s-604s])
- **90% of trades are break-and-retests**: "90% loss of my trades are breaking retests because it's the easiest most simple most importantly it's repeatable" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [395s-400s])

### When NOT to Enter
- **Risk-reward below 1:1**: "if high of day is less than one to one versus through a ward I would say that are not entering" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2506s-2518s])
- **Breakout/displacement candle has large upper wick**: "breakout slash displacement candle as large upper... be careful of that breakout displacement count all right like look logically at the chart" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2709s-2751s])
- **Too many candles from breakout to retest (over 8)**: "Over eight candles form from candlestick one the three break it to retest" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2989s-3000s])
- **HTF level already hit**: "higher time frame objective... is already... hit" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3059s-3063s])
- **Trading into HTF resistance**: "not trading long into high time frame resistance" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2564s-2574s])
- **Structure broken on lower timeframe**: "you can't have a set up where okay you reject and then you have something like this... this is just a no go... trend shifts on the low-time frames" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2882s-2901s])
- **Entry too far from level (below 1:1 R:R)**: "if you were trade is above a one to one risk or award... you are no longer going to enter this trade... or sorry below a one to one risk or award" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1610s-1627s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **Order blocks are one way to mark the level**: "Whether you mark out as a order block with purple with like a purple zone... it's the same exact idea we're looking for sellers. That's it right" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [870s-947s])
- **Order blocks affect stop loss placement**: "They just affect the stop loss yes true" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [996s-998s])
- **Personal preference**: "I just use order blocks at high of day. Tony may just use a line you may use the zone you might add in the body" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [927s-933s])

## 5. Key Levels

### PDH/PDL, PMH/PML, HOD/LOD
- **Break-and-retest applies to all levels**: "Whether it's previous day high... whether it's a pre-market high... when to actually enter it at any point... technically wise that's the same for all of the setups" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [323s-343s])
- **Best trades are HOD/LOD breaks**: "every single time the best trade is gonna be off high day... you're never gonna be on the wrong side of the trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [594s-604s])
- **PDH setups benefit from lower timeframe reaction**: "with the previous day high setups I do like to see like a lower time frame reaction to... to see like okay, it's still valid like sellers are still here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [4683s-4692s])

### Zones vs Lines
- **Lines, zones, order blocks are all the same**: "Whether you mark out a line like this yellow line is here whether you mark out as a order block... it's the same exact idea we're looking for sellers" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [870s-947s])
- **Personal preference**: "I like to use zones in 15 minutes kind of personal preference on auto-neus lines. They're all the same levels" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2049s-2056s])

- **10am caution mentioned but not a hard rule**: "you could say news 10 o'clock... I wouldn't I wouldn't always say that maybe you want to just be cautious of 10 a.m. but that's not really... going to be like a yes or no" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3492s-3512s])
- **If A+ trade at 10am, still take it**: "if there's like a equals trade in its 10 o'clock like I'm gonna take the trade so most likely like 99% of time" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3513s-3529s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **HTF levels lining up with LTF makes it better**: "when you have like let's just say this is a lower time frame level but this is also previous day high like that's that's even better" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2289s-2297s])
- **Don't trade long into HTF resistance**: "not trading long into high time frame resistance and they vice versa or... support" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2564s-2578s])
- **If HTF objective already hit, don't take the trade**: "higher time frame objective... is already... hit" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3059s-3063s])
- **HTF level more important than LTF setup**: "is this higher time frame level more important or is this one minute down close candle more important" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3167s-3169s])

---

## 9. Trade Selection

### A/A+ Grading Criteria
- **A+ trade has all confirmations (break, displacement, retest, confirmation candle)**: "we have five reasons to believe that price is at least gonna hit high of day... we have the candle break... we have the displacement candle we have the retest candle" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2159s-2173s])
- **A+ setup is the ideal drawn example**: "this is like an ideal a plus setup" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1810s-1814s])
- **A+ trades are rare**: "There's never gonna be like there's like one like a plus plus plus plus trade like a month right" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2551s-2557s])

### Market Regime Impact on Trade Selection
- **In current market, need A+ for break-and-retest**: "if you are a break and recess rate right now... you need to have like basically an A plus trade for as a workout" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [4714s-4727s])
- **Reversals allow more aggressive entries currently**: "if you're a versatile trader kind of the opposite... you should be a little bit tougher to trade the reversals now you're getting a lot more setups... you can be more aggressive you don't have to have like the A plus criteria" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [4736s-4754s])
- **Can't take B+ break-and-retest trades currently**: "you can't take B plus break and retest rate now it's not very easy" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [4764s-4766s])

### Checklist Approach
- **Build criteria of good vs bad points**: "you could mark out all the good things you could mark out your displacement the break... and then you could mark out all the bad things" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [4064s-4076s])
- **Outcome doesn't matter, process does**: "The outcome doesn't matter is did you follow your rules did you follow your plan?" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3256s-3260s])

- **1:1 minimum risk-reward**: "if high of day is less than one to one versus through a ward I would say that are not entering" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2506s-2518s])
- **Example trade had 1.33 R:R**: "I have a 1.33 risk reward that means that this trade makes sense to take" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1708s-1713s])
