- **Over 8 candles = don't enter**: "Over eight candles form from candlestick one the three break it to retest" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [2989s-3000s])

### A/A+ Grading: Verbatim Criteria

- **QQQ trend less important than HTF levels**: "you just want to be very cautious of the higher time frame levels more so than anything and also probably just high day and low-day" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [3985s-3992s])

- **Space between candle and zone**: "This candle is fully outside of the zone. There's space here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_break_and_retest_trade.txt [1127s-1139s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **System is fundamentally key levels**: "our system is technically just this... Just key levels... That's it right as we do we all understand that so we just try to key levels whether it's five minute high whether it's five minute low whether it's a previous day high... Whether it is like a higher time frame key level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [201s-217s])
- **Liquidity drives markets through highs/lows**: "liquidity is how markets move highs and lows are really liquidity lies... That's how orders work it just is what it is" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [229s-237s])
- **Three trade ideas all under key levels**: "under key levels... our three main trade ideas... break and retest... A reclaim... Or a reversal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [248s-270s])
- **High of day and low of day are the go-to for one-minute**: "high day and low days pretty much the go to for the one minute always" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3125s-3130s])
- **Always have two take-profit levels**: "You always have two levels for take profit no matter what if you trade right... high of day... that's always your first level... Number two is gonna be your higher time frame level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3240s-3278s])
- **Previous day high/low are strongest reactions**: "where are the strongest reactions typically they're always high of day and low of day or previous day high previous day lows... For them or key levels" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3110s-3125s])
- **Previous day high is best for reclaim trades**: "previous day high previous day low... those are obviously probably gonna get the best reclaim trades" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2873s-2879s])
- **Just because it's a previous day high doesn't mean no other resistance**: "just because it's previous day high doesn't mean that there's not other resistance that's on the chart... We know that that's the most recent resistance... but there's also other resistance as well right so we got to think about how much room that it really has to go" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2914s-2930s])
- **Trading in range is hard due to multiple levels**: "that's why trading in range is so hard because... if you're in previous days range... trading right into pre-mark high we're trading right into this previous day high... We're trading into all these levels of resistance" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3334s-3389s])

## 7. Exits

- **Always have two targets**: "You always have two levels for take profit no matter what if you trade right... high of day... that's always your first level... Number two is gonna be your higher time frame level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3240s-3278s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **Use three entry models to capture lower time frame entry for HTF move**: "I'm gonna use the three entry models that we teach the break and retest the reclaim and the reversal... I'm gonna use those three models to capture a lower time frame move to get the best entry possible for the higher time frame move... That is how you build high probability trades at the fundamental level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2249s-2261s])
- **HTF bias + LTF entry model + risk reward = highest probability**: "It's by using a lower time frame entry model with the higher time frame bias with the risk reward... That's the bottom line" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2488s-2499s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **A+ reclaim setup defined**: "I'm simply going through an A plus trade what would be a like text book like as perfect as you can get... Reclaim trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [876s-885s])
- **A+ reclaim criteria - structure intact**: "ideally it does not break structure at all... Ideally and maybe it has a reaction off this candle right here it holds this level the first pull back... from that original high that we created" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [985s-993s])
- **A+ reclaim criteria - higher low formed**: "in an ideal perfect world right in in this A plus setup we do form like a higher low here... So you have... fire step in... You have a pull back and then a break above that level... That is going to be ideal so structure is still intact" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1063s-1085s])
- **A+ reclaim criteria - close above the level**: "this candle ideally should should close... you should wait for the closure... we do want to close above that... we need the closure above... Just the body to close above that's ideal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1135s-1200s])
- **A+ reclaim criteria - displacement not required**: "You don't need displacement... we just need the close above... not a full candle outside it's not what I mean but we need the closure above" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1186s-1199s])
- **Not every reclaim trade looks like A+**: "not every reclaim trade has to look like this... I'm simply going through an A plus trade... just a heads up so like if your reclaim trade doesn't look like this and you're wondering is it a reclaim trade like it probably is do not worry" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [876s-897s])
- **A+ trades don't happen every session**: "Are you gonna be taking only A plus trades these probably aren't gonna happen every session" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [411s-420s])
- **Need A+ standard to know what A looks like**: "if we don't know what an A plus setup looks like then we don't know what any setup looks like" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [425s-431s])
- **Don't trade long when SPY is pushing resistance**: "don't long... when spy... is pushing... resistance... Don't long short when spy is pushing... support... if you're trying to take a reclaim trade on a ticker and spy is pushing up into previous day high... that's not gonna be a good case scenario that goes for all trades though" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2528s-2559s])
- **Don't enter if risk-reward less than 1:1**: "if from entry to high of day... is less than... 1 to 1... Don't enter" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1808s-1821s])
- **1:1 minimum can be flexible on A+ setups**: "you can still enter in less than one to one risk reward I want to say on an A plus setup... you're probably not gonna do this like if it's a true true A plus trade you're gonna have probably a decent risk reward to high of day" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1834s-1855s])
- **Don't enter if price already pushed close to target**: "if price is already pushed up very very very close to the high the risk reward is never there... you don't want to put your stop loss right here... you also don't want to enter in and then just use two targets anyways and now your take profit is way way way higher because the likelihood of it working doesn't make sense anymore" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1947s-1971s])
- **Don't enter if main objective already hit**: "we already just took out one of the main objectives like that's... we had a pretty strong reaction" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3063s-3065s])
- **Don't enter if too many candles from breakout to reclaim**: "from breakout candle to reclaim candle... I just say no more than... Probably 15 candles... with the break and retests it's like eight... it's like eight to ten max you don't want to go over that from breakout to retest because at that point you just lost momentum" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2085s-2117s])
- **Reclaim is more lenient on candle count than break-and-retest**: "this is a little bit more lenient than the break and retest is that's why I'm gonna say 15 candles" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2184s-2188s])
- **If ticker has done nothing for 15-20 minutes, move on**: "it's like are you even gonna look at this ticker anymore... why are you still on this ticker if it's done nothing for 15 20 minutes right like there's something not right here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2195s-2202s])
- **Don't enter if first candle after reclaim is down close**: "on the reclaim... these candles this is the first candle goes yeah, you're probably staying away from this right... because we don't have any structure at this point anymore it just took this out now... trying to take this out again... We need to be patient" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2374s-2392s])
- **Don't enter if structure is broken**: "The structures broken is a good one too. Yeah, you don't want to use that" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2314s-2319s])
- **If trade works, it happens right away**: "most of the time if you have a really good setup and the trades gonna work out... It's gonna happen right away... as soon as we reclaim this level it's gonna shoot right up to this level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3175s-3186s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Reclaim max candles from breakout: 15**: "from breakout candle to reclaim candle... I just say no more than... Probably 15 candles" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2085s-2109s])
- **Break-and-retest max candles: 8-10**: "with the break and retests it's like eight... it's like eight to ten max" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2110s-2117s])
- **1:1 minimum risk-reward for reclaim**: "if from entry to high of day... is less than... 1 to 1... Don't enter" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1808s-1821s])

## Special Audit Questions

### A/A+ Grading: Verbatim Criteria for Reclaim

- **A+ reclaim = structure intact + higher low + close above level**: "ideally it does not break structure at all... in an ideal perfect world... we do form like a higher low here... You have a pull back and then a break above that level... this candle ideally should should close... we do want to close above that... Just the body to close above that's ideal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [985s-1200s])
- **A+ reclaim = displacement NOT required**: "You don't need displacement... we just need the close above... not a full candle outside it's not what I mean but we need the closure above" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1186s-1199s])
- **A+ reclaim = reaction off the level on first pullback**: "Ideally and maybe it has a reaction off this candle right here it holds this level the first pull back... from that original high that we created" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [985s-993s])
- **No verbatim distinction between A vs A+ vs B for reclaim provided** - speaker only defines A+ and says "not every reclaim trade has to look like this" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [876s-897s])

### QQQ Alignment

- **Don't long when SPY is pushing resistance**: "don't long... when spy... is pushing... resistance... Don't long short when spy is pushing... support... if you're trying to take a reclaim trade on a ticker and spy is pushing up into previous day high... that's not gonna be a good case scenario that goes for all trades though" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2528s-2559s])

### Displacement/FVG: Exact Size Definitions

- **Displacement NOT required for reclaim**: "You don't need displacement... we just need the close above... not a full candle outside it's not what I mean but we need the closure above" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1186s-1199s])

### Reclaim Setup: Full Definition

- **Reclaim defined - breakout without retest**: "The reclaim same exact thing we make a high... This is the idea you break out so it actually breaks out of the high we don't get a retest... There's no retest here, all right, but we reclaim the level... So you're entering in here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [579s-598s])
- **Reclaim entry trigger - close above the level**: "we do want to close above that... we need the closure above... Just the body to close above that's ideal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1184s-1200s])
- **Reclaim entry - wait for candle close**: "this candle ideally should should close... you should wait for the closure" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1135s-1139s])
- **Reclaim entry - aggressive entry possible at 30-45 seconds**: "if you want to be a little more aggressive... I would consider this fine if you enter in during this candle... I would probably 30 45 seconds if anything... at least 30 for sure" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1145s-1154s])
- **Reclaim entry - safer to wait for close**: "if you want to keep it simple if you want something repeatable... You're probably waiting for the candle to close because then it's confirmed and you want a good closure" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1168s-1175s])
- **Reclaim entry - be more aggressive with HTF room**: "if you have a setup like this on the higher time frames... then as typically if his relative strength as well... You typically want to be a little bit more aggressive like you might enter in like as it's forming" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1511s-1521s])
- **Reclaim entry - in normal day, wait for close**: "in a normal trading day for reclaim setup you're probably... waiting a little bit it is a lot safer to just wait for the candle close generally speaking" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [1535s-1540s])
- **Reclaim max candles from breakout: 15**: "from breakout candle to reclaim candle... I just say no more than... Probably 15 candles" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [2085s-2109s])
- **Reclaim stop loss implied below the level** - not explicitly stated but implied by risk-reward discussion
- **Reclaim targets - HOD then HTF level**: "You always have two levels for take profit... high of day... that's always your first level... Number two is gonna be your higher time frame level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reclaim_trade.txt [3240s-3278s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Reversal vs break-and-retest - same three-candle process but different**: "for the reversal trades it's gonna be a little bit different but the same thing applies if that makes sense" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [162s-169s])
- **Reversal entry requires more patience than break-and-retest**: "it's not like a break and retest where you're gonna enter in right as it starts forming down below you do need to wait for a little bit more confirmation here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1147s-1155s])
- **Break-and-retest vs reversal - structure difference**: "in the break and retest we broke above and we formed a higher low" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2750s-2755s])
- **Reversal vs break-and-retest - entry timing difference**: "Whereas the higher time frame retest one or the higher time frame reversal should say you do need to wait for this candle... So there is a difference" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2861s-2871s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Reversal requires higher time frame level - not just intraday high**: "This is a higher time frame level right here... This level this is not from the session. This is from another day. It could be multiple touchpoints... this has to be a pivot high on the higher time frame for our reversal trade to the short side right" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [367s-398s])
- **Without HTF level, reversal does not apply**: "if this is just a high intraday... This does not work" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [415s-417s])
- **15-minute used for drawing key levels**: "I always use 15 minute to draw the my key levels" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [461s-467s])
- **Pre-market highs on SPY/QQQ are important levels**: "pre-market levels on spy and cues because they're technically the markets are open for ES and NQ... They are a little bit more important than let's just say like Tesla or AMD" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1888s-1903s])
- **Best trades are reactions at HOD/LOD or HTF levels**: "you're only best trades are gonna be reactions at this level and this level... In between here you're not really gonna have anything fancy happen" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2374s-2385s])
- **No trade ideas between HOD and LOD**: "Your trades are gonna be right here or right here... You're not looking for anything in the middle. There's no trade ideas here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2407s-2419s])
- **Extended trading hours view**: "Extended trading hours and then regular trading hours just put on extended to view what pre-market levels... blue for after hours... pre-market would be yellow" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2198s-2213s])

## 7. Exits
- **Reversal targets - next pivot low**: "the main target is kind of that whatever the next kind of pivot low is" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1198s-1204s])
- **Reversal targets - can hold some contracts for further lows**: "technically you could hold some more contracts for this low down here too as well" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1214s-1217s])
- **Reversal targets opposite of break-and-retest**: "For the break and retest we talked about two targets... It's kind of the opposite with reversals" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1227s-1246s])
- **Reversal stop loss - lower high is the stop**: "I want to argue the lower high is going to be your stop loss... That's why I put it there for most trades" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1089s-1096s])
- **Reversal stop - not one-size-fits-all like retest**: "It's not like the retest candle where every time your stop loss is gonna be the retest candle... Every time it's not gonna be very specific" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1053s-1088s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **Reversal trades mostly off higher time frame levels**: "been trying out more reversal style trades mostly off higher time frame levels... not really taking any intraday five-minute reversals" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [272s-287s])
- **HTF level must be a pivot high for short reversal**: "this has to be a pivot high on the higher time frame for our reversal trade to the short side right" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [391s-398s])
- **Structure shift alone is not enough without HTF level**: "We do see the structure shift. We do see the change of character here. Yes... That doesn't mean that we're always gonna enter the trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [425s-432s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **A+ reversal setup - must take out the HTF high**: "it has to actually take out that high... It can't just form below it. It can't bounce off the zone" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [518s-533s])
- **A+ reversal - candle 1: takes out HTF high with reaction**: "the higher time frame level of liquidity is taken and we do have a reaction off this" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [594s-596s])
- **A+ reversal - candle 2: down close candle**: "We need to see some sort of down-close candle... It can't be an up close candle because that would just mean eventually the trend will just keep going up" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [597s-610s])
- **A+ reversal - candle 3: displacement from the level**: "you want displacement from the level this would be very ideal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [648s-655s])
- **A+ reversal - must build a lower high**: "we need to build this first the lower high... This cannot close above right that's gonna make this trade technically not solid" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [705s-728s])
- **Lower high only confirmed when structure breaks**: "this only becomes a lower high though at this fifth candle... because with the context right here... Nothing is saying that this isn't just gonna go higher yet" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [741s-757s])
- **Structure shift requires breaking the higher low**: "we need the structure shift... For that to happen we need to break this higher low... This is the level to now the structure has shifted" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [762s-775s])
- **A+ reversal - all criteria must mostly be present**: "if you want to grade your own trade based off of some sort of a plus setup... all of these are gonna probably have to happen most of these" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [659s-676s])
- **A+ reversal - can be A if missing one thing**: "could it be like A and it's missing like one of the things for sure" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [672s-676s])
- **Reversal entry - wait 45-60 seconds into candle**: "at this entry candle you probably want to wait at least 45 to 60 seconds" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [809s-815s])
- **Reversal entry - cannot enter at candle start like break-and-retest**: "This is where it gets a little bit different from... break and retest where you can kind of enter in right at the start of this" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [819s-835s])
- **Reversal entry - aggressive entry possible at break but risky**: "If you're really aggressive and you want to take the entry right as it breaks you could... But it's still a pivot low, right this could still be support" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1161s-1177s])
- **Poor man's reversal - lower time frame, no HTF level needed**: "This is kind of like a poor man's reversal trade... If you're not gonna get the best like high probability trades here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2998s-3012s])
- **Poor man's reversal - only with strong bias**: "I would not take this otherwise... if you have a massive short bias like... today with meta... I was very very very short bias meta" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [3013s-3036s])
- **Poor man's reversal - entry at candle 5, can enter as it forms below**: "this fifth candle you can enter in on like right as it starts forming below" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2854s-2860s])
- **Reversal trades are harder than break-and-retest**: "these trades are harder... 100% because... you're basically shorting an uptrend" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [3402s-3415s])
- **Don't take reversal trades on live account without practice**: "I wouldn't go really nearly trying to take reversal trades try to paper trade them... do not take them on your live account" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2901s-2916s])
- **Max candles between candle 1 and 5: ~15**: "probably like more than 15 candles at this point you're probably just gonna avoid the trade... That's probably just ranging at that point" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1339s-1354s])
- **If reversal works, it works right away**: "if they work right away they're going to they're gonna work if they don't they're not" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1357s-1364s])
- **Consolidation = avoid reversal**: "if it's consolidating, you know, that's a very good sign that you're probably gonna stay away from the setup" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1373s-1378s])
- **Liquidity can be taken with only wicks**: "liquidity can be taken with only wicks in my opinion" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [953s-959s])
- **Wick above candle 2 - still valid for lower high**: "if it does end up like... you want to see the lower high form if it's a wick... Honestly, it's kind of just giving you a little bit I would say more confirmation" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2662s-2683s])
- **Don't take reversal if another pivot low is very close by**: "If there is another pivot low very close by on the higher time frame you also might not want to... pre-market low... if they're very close by and you're trading a tight range. I know you definitely don't want to do that" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2043s-2055s])
- **If something above key level on left, don't take reversal**: "if we look left and there would be something above the key level you'll don't take it as a reversal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2055s-2062s])
- **Reversal works best when ranging on both tickers**: "I want to say it's gonna work best like when you are in range on both tickers" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [2148s-2153s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Reversal entry wait time: 45-60 seconds**: "at this entry candle you probably want to wait at least 45 to 60 seconds" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [809s-815s])
- **Max candles between candle 1 and 5: ~15**: "probably like more than 15 candles at this point you're probably just gonna avoid the trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [1339s-1354s])
- **90% of trades have been break-and-retest**: "for the past like year and a half I would say 90% of my trades have been break and retests" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_enter_the_reversal.txt [269s-271s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Three things at key levels - break and retest is primary]** "You have three things which is your breaking retest you have your Reclame which is very similar and then you have your reversal... So these are the three main things that you are going to be looking for at your key levels" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [623s-640s])

- **[Level must break first]** "the level has to break... That's a must right that's a must so our level does have to break for all of these setups to make sense" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [648s-655s])

- **[Break and retest is 80-90% of trades]** "if you have a long bias you're looking for break and retests... That is our bread and butter that's what 90% of our trades are I would say maybe 80% and then the rest like 15 is like reclaimed and then baby a couple reversal trades here and there" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [661s-675s])

- **[Break and retest model - 1-2-3-4-5 candle structure]** "If you look at this from like the one to five kind of standpoint from the break and retest model... I guess you would have the break the immediate retest... Maybe confirmation candle... I guess would be four you're missing the displacement here... so I guess it would be one... three... four and then I guess these could both be like the five candles right the flip here... be a entry little bit aggressive" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [815s-839s])

- **[Break candle, displacement, retest candle, confirmation candle]** "The break handle I guess you could call this the break candle too if you really wanted to the displacement... If you retest candle confirmation candle arguably you're probably entering here... You don't really need to wait for the fifth candle as it did flip the candle did flip and it closed nice... You're waiting 45 60 seconds" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [975s-992s])

- **[Targets after break and retest - HOD then next key level]** "Where are your two targets right you have your high of day and you also have just like 745... There's also sellers up here... So they're sellers right here. They're sellers up here if we break this level and we hold this level... You know where do you expect price to go theoretically right or or literally?... You know price naturally is going to gravitate at least most of the times right towards your high of day... And then to your actual next key level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [707s-747s])

- **[Room before next level is critical for setup quality]** "Setting up proper trades where you do have a good bit of room before the next key level right like it's not as if we're trading a pre-market high where it's right here and we're the previous day highs right here. Not really going to be the growth best best setup because you know... There's multiple levels of resistance that we're looking at... That you're you're trying to long basically into resistance" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [748s-764s])

- **[We don't trade breakouts - need retest]** "We still need to find entry though right we don't trade breakouts" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [789s-793s])

- **[Immediate retest can be entry]** "If you had been to like the break and retest session that we had ran you guys know this could be considered one of your entries" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [801s-809s])

- **[Downside moves don't always give retests]** "To the downside those moves are gonna happen fast... Like to the downside those moves are going to not always give you retests and that's why I'm not the greatest short side trader" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2645s-2656s])

- **[Downside - staircase up, elevator down]** "The downside trades are very tough because it's basically like you know staircase up elevator down staircase up... Elevator down like that's what happens basically with spy and cues in the indices" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2669s-2684s])

- **[Downside - need to be more aggressive]** "The downside always happens fast right you gotta be a little bit more aggressive" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2707s-2712s])

- **[Only two break and retest entries on SPY today]** "You had your basically five and opening range retest which was like over here... And then you had maybe your pre-market high if you were a little bit cheeky and you entered in right off the bat... Those are really the only two trades today up until right now right for me for like our for my break and retests entry model... That is it" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2056s-2083s])

- **[No trade in whole downside move]** "From this whole move downward right with the short bias, you know below your low of day... Blu everything right there's no trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2087s-2101s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **[Opening candle as directional reference]** "Very good indication of whether to go long or short is if just you're above or below your opening candle by the way... Just your first one minute candle... Just just for reference by the way like you could probably save yourself a lot of losing trades by doing this... Just by so like literally your opening candle if you're long above it and you're short below it... That's a very easy way to just kind of you know have a little bit of direction to start off" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1809s-1833s])

- **[Opening candle - don't short above, don't long below]** "You should never really... Like if you if you trade the break and retest... Right you should never be like shorting... above here... You know what I mean they're what why are you shorting above your opening candle?... If trying to reverse is intraday like I get it, but it's just not gonna be your best trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [3722s-3743s])

- **[Opening candle - not named "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as a formal setup name]** The speaker refers to "your opening candle" and "your first one minute candle" as a directional reference, not as a named setup called "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" in this transcript.

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 4. Order Blocks

- **[Prefer key levels over random order blocks]** "I would rather use this previous day high level rather than just this random one minute order block" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1290s-1294s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Best reactions at daily highs/lows, pre-market highs/lows]** "Your best trades and your number one reactions are gonna come from like obviously like daily highs... Daily lows maybe pre-market highs pre-market lows... Those are gonna be your main key levels. Those are where you're gonna have the biggest of reactions" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [315s-332s])

- **[15-minute timeframe for key levels]** "We're gonna come into to market open. We're just gonna go to the 15 minute time frame because that's... Generally speaking what most of us I would say are using for key levels" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [226s-237s])

- **[Line + zone marking]** "I've been doing this a little bit more so recently where you have the actual key level as... As your line and then I use a zone just to be cautious right if you want to long into this... Probably being a little more cautious right now" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [395s-406s])

- **[Look left - only nearest pivot highs/lows matter]** "Directly below this pivot low here... Because realistically prices already moved through all of the other pivot lows. We don't need to worry about these... We don't need to worry about these highs right here price already moved through those" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [438s-449s])

- **[Key level hierarchy - move down sequentially]** "This is what you do with key level. You're just gonna move down so below here... We look left our next pivot low" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [458s-466s])

- **[Long bottom wicks - use lines not zones]** "Honestly you kind of need to try your best and I would honestly maybe argue you're just gonna use lines for these really really really long bottom wicks" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [471s-484s])

- **[Unconfirmed levels - no buyers/sellers yet]** "There's no confirmed buyers here right so we have green buyers red sellers pretty straightforward... So we don't actually know yet whether this is gonna be buyers or sellers there is a chance just like the break and retest right when price comes back down... We don't know whether there's... You know buyers or sellers yet we just know that we're looking for the reaction there" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [552s-573s])

- **[Three things happen at key levels - break and retest, reclaim, reversal]** "Our system is all built off key levels... Within those key levels and you could refer these as the quantity... You could refer these as like highs or lows the same thing they're all they all mean the same thing... You have three things which is your breaking retest you have your... Reclame which is very similar and then you have your reversal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [606s-640s])

- **[Level must break for all setups]** "The level has to break... That's a must right that's a must so our level does have to break for all of these setups to make sense" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [648s-655s])

- **[Level only valid once - if it doesn't reclaim, invalid]** "If this does not reclaim right off the bat, you know this levels probably invalid now" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1372s-1376s])

- **[Level already broke and retested - remove it]** "It's already broke once it's already broke or it's already retested it work we're not gonna use this anymore... We're gonna take that out now" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1390s-1398s])

- **[Holding a level ≠ trade setup]** "Just because you hold key level does not mean that you're gonna take a trade off it... If we are looking for break and retests and our strategy off of key levels... Right is the break and retests... Reverse or... Or let's just say reclaim... None of these... Makes sense to to take at this level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1686s-1712s])

- **[HOD and LOD are always best levels to trade off]** "Your low and your high are always gonna be like really good really good... Levels to trade off of" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2447s-2453s])

- **[Thesis is done when price hits your target level]** "If you were thesis is I'm gonna trade Tesla above previous day high... It's gotta be into pre-market high... Right, cuz that's your next level that's your next sellers... So if if you were thesis is this and... Tesla breaks previous day high... And it hits pre-market high your thesis is done... thesis done" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [3331s-3352s])

- **[After thesis done, new thesis for next level]** "The flip side of this is to say okay... It already took it pre-market high now my trade idea is above pre-market high into... Your 426 which I believe was like uh yeah or 424... The Friday's high so that is that's your thesis now" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [3391s-3408s])

- **[If level taken out and no retest - thesis becomes HOD/LOD]** "If this gets taken out and we're down here right there's no thesis anymore... Now it's it's gonna be high of day now right if there's no retest... Um, because there's nothing else I can I can do" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [3505s-3512s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Two targets - HOD then next key level]** "Where are your two targets right you have your high of day and you also have just like 745... There's also sellers up here... So they're sellers right here. They're sellers up here if we break this level and we hold this level... You know where do you expect price to go theoretically right or or literally?... You know price naturally is going to gravitate at least most of the times right towards your high of day... And then to your actual next key level" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [707s-747s])

- **[2R target above previous day high]** "You draw maybe like two r just above five day they're just above your previous say hi" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1001s-1008s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Always have higher time frame thesis]** "Always have a higher time frame thesis like don't just use your one minute candle and just go off that" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [3755s-3760s])

- **[Reversal trades need HTF pivot]** "For a reversal like a true... You know a plus reversal trade it's gotta be some sort of a higher time frame pivot that's like taking out... Right it's gotta be this high taken out and then the trend reverses and you're taking it to the downside... That's that's the key" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2756s-2769s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[A+ trade perspective - need context]** "If we're looking at this from an a plus trade perspective like as these candles are forming like are we going to... take a setup down here. I would argue you know you probably would if we got buyers to step in if we had this level hold" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1300s-1315s])

- **[Not an A+ trade - not enough context]** "It's not like a a plus trade or anything you could argue maybe... This but I still don't really think that like there's not a lot of context here" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2421s-2430s])

- **[Game plan saves you]** "The game plan is so important and it helps me so much like every day... It basically right off the bat AMD was a no trade Intel no trade MU no trade... Nvidia nothing... Tesla no retest right we just looked at Tesla... SDK was the only thing that that broke my key level in that gave us setup and it was the only one that worked out... So it's like it I'm telling you like you got to kind of set these things up and and just... Go follow the game plan it'll save you so much I promise" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [3601s-3635s])

- **[Avoiding bad trades is key]** "Through every single last bit of these... A candles we just avoided bad trades... There's no key reaction points" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1990s-2004s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Break and retest = 80-90% of trades, reclaim = ~15%, reversal = few]** "That is our bread and butter that's what 90% of our trades are I would say maybe 80% and then the rest like 15 is like reclaimed and then baby a couple reversal trades here and there" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [664s-675s])

- **[Nobody catches entire move - 1 in 100-200 trades]** "You're never going to do this. I'm never going to do this... You know with scaling... You're still not gonna do this. It's maybe like a one in every... You know a hundred trades... 200 trades where you're gonna catch like with scaling, you know, a move that that happens like this" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2193s-2205s])

- **[Only 2 trade opportunities on SPY today]** "Those are really the only two trades today up until right now right for me for like our for my break and retests entry model, you know... That is it" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2075s-2083s])

- **[No trade in whole downside move on SPY]** "From this whole move downward right with the short bias, you know below your low of day... Blu everything right there's no trade" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2087s-2101s])

## 11. One-Minute Trend

- **[One-minute trend shift - lower high + lower low]** "If this if this breaks above at this point we're hitting a new high of day if this breaks pivot low... The trend is now reversed right... So this this would be your lower high and your lower low at this point" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1239s-1250s])

- **[Always note one-minute trend + HTF alignment]** "When you're looking at price and when you're reading this in real time always note... One minute trend and is it is it does this align with the higher time frames right like does this make sense" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1331s-1340s])

- **[Best reclaim trades have small trend shift]** "The best reclaim trade has that like little small trend shift" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1574s-1580s])

## 12. Reversal Trades

- **[Reversal - third setup type]** "You have three things which is your breaking retest you have your... Reclame which is very similar and then you have your reversal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [623s-640s])

- **[A+ reversal needs HTF pivot taken out]** "For a reversal like a true... You know a plus reversal trade it's gotta be some sort of a higher time frame pivot that's like taking out... Right it's gotta be this high taken out and then the trend reverses and you're taking it to the downside... That's that's the key" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2756s-2769s])

- **[Speed of reversal candles is important]** "The speed of the candles like how fast it actually does reverse this is important... Right over here where it's kind of just like breaks down kind of it's all they're eating you don't really know what's happening... There's no real direction it's just buyers and sellers fighting" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2922s-2937s])

- **[Reversal - strong candle close at level + fast]** "This one's actually really nice... This is actually like a really good reversal trade because we don't know how being a very very very strong... Candle close at the level and another nice candle close even on the second push up... And it's fast it's like you know a couple minutes" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2906s-2922s])

- **[Reversal - 10-12 candles of consolidation = momentum gone]** "If you're taking this over here for the reversal it has been probably... What 15 candles... It's been 12 candles... Or like 10 to 12 candles since you would have even 11 as well since you would have debated entering this trade at that point the momentum probably isn't there" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2939s-2963s])

- **[Reversal targets - 2R or previous day low]** "When you take the reversals off these targets you're just you're targeting most of the time... You know... For like originally probably just your two r if there's no other target but you could even target up here right for the first trade... Or a reversal right at this point you have your previous day low still" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2996s-3013s])

## 13. Reclaim Setup

- **[Reclaim - similar to break and retest]** "You have three things which is your breaking retest you have your... Reclame which is very similar and then you have your reversal" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [623s-640s])

- **[Reclaim - about 15% of trades]** "That is our bread and butter that's what 90% of our trades are I would say maybe 80% and then the rest like 15 is like reclaimed and then baby a couple reversal trades here and there" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [664s-675s])

- **[Best reclaim has small trend shift]** "The best reclaim trade has that like little small trend shift... Right when you when you do reclaim the level which is good in this case be probably want to see that there" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1574s-1585s])

## 14. QQQ/Index Confirmation

- **[Indices have more reversals and liquidity draws]** "The indices are a great... Great resource for this because... This is kind of like they have more reversals. They have more like draws on the liquidity... I would say then a lot of tickers they will gravitate towards lows and towards highs... quite easily I would say you have a lot more reversals on... Your indices than you would maybe for some of your tickers" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [202s-226s])

## 15. Displacement/FVG

- **[Displacement needed in break and retest model]** "I guess you would have the break the immediate retest... Maybe confirmation candle... I guess would be four you're missing the displacement here. That's the thing" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [822s-836s])

- **[Displacement candle + retest = entry]** "We have the break and we have the displacement already... So here we do have a potential for another trade setup... You can see we do get the displacement candle the third candle and then it does come back down... I believe for retest eventually" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [931s-949s])

## 16. Trade Review / Judgment

- **[Nobody catches entire move - don't beat yourself up]** "Nobody's catching all these right it's okay, right there wasn't even entries for a lot of these I double checked... Even right now we just went through all this right for this whole move right here. There's not a single entry... So you know don't beat yourself up right so times you just you don't get it sometimes the downside happens fast" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [1923s-1944s])

- **[Nobody shorted top and sold bottom]** "I can almost I can almost like for 100% certainty... Say that not probably not a single person... Shorted anywhere up here and... Sold down here... Probably not a single person in this entire planet... Probably did not do that" (hayden-s-coaching_How_to_read_price_action_live_transcript.txt [2155s-2182s])

- **[Neto (SPY trader) got 1 trade at 10:14]** "Neto the guy that trades spy every single day... Every single day he shorts spy he got it to the trade at what?... Like 10 15 late 10 14... - **[Sell at bid or ask depending on market direction]** "when I'm selling I might sell out the bid or I might sell out the ask but if I'm getting stopped out... I will be selling at the bid or below the bid" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [335s-348s])
- **[Uses limit orders to control slippage]** "I trade with limit orders because it's just a little bit it actually helps me when I'm getting filled because it won't fill me over a certain price or I won't fill me under a certain price" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1781s-1798s])
- **[Market orders fine for large caps like Apple, Tesla, SPY, QQQ]** "market orders I still do right especially when you're trading large caps like market orders are completely fine like apple Tesla... meta spy... QQ like those are a large caps. You're not gonna really experience as much slippage unless your order is extremely extremely big" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1861s-1889s])
- **[Does not recommend hard stops for options - uses mental stops]** "I personally don't do hard stops. Right. I like to have mental stops... I think a hard stop especially when you're trading options sometimes the option contract will dip a little bit just into just enough to get into that stock loss and then shoot up but the actual stock or the underlying asset you'll see on the chart hasn't really moved that much" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2408s-2443s])

## 13. Options-Specific
- **[Out of the money contracts move more volatile than at the money]** "out of the money contracts will move much much more volatile. Therefore if you can capture a bigger move... you'll have a bigger P and L boost however if that once again goes against you you'll have a big loss at the money is a little bit safer than out of the money and then of course in the money is the safest" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [520s-542s])
- **[One options contract = 100 shares of underlying]** "one single options contract for us will represent 100 shares. So if you buy one... spy option contract you are actually buying 100 shares of the underlying asset" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1131s-1156s])
- **[Options can go to zero even if underlying doesn't - especially OTM]** "the underlying just has to drop enough for the contract and based off the DTE and you can easily go to zero right especially especially if you're playing out of the money contracts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1245s-1259s])
- **[Options can give 71-116% returns on 5-6% stock moves]** "this was 71% moved for the underlying asset only moving like five to six percent right" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [3066s-3072s]); "this was once again a hundred and 16 percent trade as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [3083s-3085s])
- **[Options are best way to grow account quickly]** "options are a great way to make a lot of percent to just especially if you're a small account medium account whatever large account even... it's one of the best ways to grow an account really really quickly in my opinion" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [3086s-3098s])

## 14. VIX / Volatility
- **[VIX over 20 = keep in mind]** "whenever the VIX is over 20 you want to keep it in the back of your head" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2468s-2474s])
- **[VIX and SPX are opposite]** "VIX and the SPX or SPY are simply the opposite of each other so whenever the VIX is moving up the SPX will be usually be moving down and vice versa" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2479s-2485s])
- **[VIX does not work with technical analysis - only whole psychological numbers]** "the VIX does not do well with technical analysis. It just likes its whole psychological numbers... it simply likes whole psychological numbers if you look at the whole psychological numbers on the VIX you know just have those charted at least and you'll see that it likes to bounce off those much more than technical analysis" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2492s-2523s])

## 15. Broker / Platform
- **[Recommends Interactive Brokers for best fills]** "Interactive brokers does a really really good job with that" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1093s-1100s])
- **[Does not recommend Robinhood]** "brokers like Robin and for example not a good broker if you're trading with Robin I honestly recommend you to get off of Robin and go to any other broker right other than Robinhood" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1074s-1086s])
- **[Trades with cash account, not margin]** "I just trade with a cash account so I don't trade with the margin account anymore" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1744s-1748s])

## 16. Risk Management
- **[Do not trade with big margin/leverage as a beginner]** "I do not recommend you do that. That's one of the worst things to do especially in the beginning" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1760s-1767s])
- **[New traders should trade stocks with tight bid-ask spreads and high volume]** "for all new traders try to focus on stocks that have the lowest bid and ask spread with a lot of volume" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [882s-889s])
- **[New traders should not trade SPX - trade SPY instead]** "it's really important to for new traders at least to not trade SPX rather trade SPY with the smaller bid and ask prices" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [872s-882s])

## 17. Market Makers / Market Manipulation
- **[Market makers are not targeting retail traders' small positions]** "I promise you they are not looking at your five option contracts that you bought they're not looking at the ten option contracts you bought they might not you they're not even look at the hundred option contracts you bought right market makers role is much much bigger than that for large cap options at least or large caps in general" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1993s-2014s])
- **[Market isn't rigged - bad entries cause losses]** "the market isn't rigged okay it's just simply because you had a bad entry of bad stop and overall a bad trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2021s-2028s])

## 18. Alerts / Signal Services
- **[Does not recommend buying alert services]** "do not buy alerts in general my opinion" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2131s-2140s])
- **[Out of the money low volume option alerts = pumping = illegal]** "if someone is ever alerting you a very very out of the money contract that has very very low volume it's really easy to tell if they're pumping that right and you know you definitely don't want to buy those type of things" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2114s-2125s])

## 19. Earnings / Straddles
- **[Straddles/strangles for earnings lose money 90%+ of time]** "if you've ever tried to do that... 90% of the time you're not gonna make money. Okay, unless the stock has a really really really big move... I say even more than 90% of time" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2934s-2951s])
- **[IV crush kills earnings trades]** "the IV is so high on those option contracts the day or two before the actual earnings happens... you'll notice that the IV's too high and you got IV crush" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [2951s-2977s])

## 20. IPOs
- **[Trading IPOs = 100% gambling]** "I personally think when you're trading IPO you're 100% gambling because you have no clue what's going on unless you have insider information really" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [1681s-1689s])

## 21. Learning / Note-Taking
- **[Best way to learn = listen, see, regurgitate in own words]** "The best way to learn is to listen see and then re-gurgitate it into your own language or into your own like you know simple terms" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [126s-136s])
- **[Pen and paper recommended for retention]** "I like pen and paper because you have that retention right when you're actually learning something" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Introduction_to_Options_Key_Terms_My_Trading_Setup_transcript.txt [122s-126s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Days to avoid - Quadwitching** "Quadwitching refers to simultaneous expiration of four different types of financial contracts... It occurs quarterly and can lead to increased volatility in the markets... you really don't want to trade those days." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [441s-474s])
- **Days to avoid - Jerome Powell speeches** "Jay powtays... every single time Jerome Powell talks, you'll see that the market's going to spike to one side or another and that's also days I don't like to take any trades... before he talks, you'll see a lot of choppy range... after he talks, you'll see big moves to one side or another... the majority of the time there won't really be a proper trend." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [475s-512s])
- **Days to avoid - Earnings** "I don't like to trade when stocks are nearing their earnings or their earnings have already occurred, it's the next day... there's too much volatility to one side unless there's a trend... most of the time you'll see that a lot of people are confused with the earnings news and you'll just see like range in the stock or a lot of chopiness the next day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [513s-554s])
- **Personal trading frequency** "I trade probably two to three days a week max... I like to trade with a very, very chill mindset." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [414s-435s])
- **Live trading schedule** "I'm only going to trade three days of the week... I usually trade three to two days a week." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1794s-1810s])

## 7. Exits
- **Scaling out in 10-contract increments** "I like to sell in 10, 10 contract sizes... it lets me... it's just an even number for me to sell... just slowly reducing my size rather than selling the whole position on a pop." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [980s-1006s])
- **Sell market button for quick exits** "I'll click sell market... just to get into profits really quickly... sell 50... half the position... sometimes I do it for the opposite as well... if it's going against me really quickly, I'll click sell market and get out of that trade pretty quickly." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1063s-1102s])
- **Close position button** "close position... that'll just close my full position and hold on a little bit below the bid." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1052s-1055s])
- **Trailing stops for walkaway** "I'll use trailing stops... as the stock keeps going up... I'll get stopped out if it goes down 10% after moving up 20% or something like that and I can walk away from the stock and that's the only way I'll use that." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1463s-1492s])
- **Mental stops** "A mental stop is simply just where you want... on the chart you know exactly where you want to exit the stock... you don't actually have a hard stop in there... you have to exit the stock yourself." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1344s-1366s])
- **Day trading only - same day exits** "We're still day trading at the end of the day, right? So we're still getting out on the same day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1528s-1536s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **Instruments traded** "Apple, QQQ, SPX... those are the only things I trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [700s-710s])
- **Option contract selection criteria** "over a thousand volume is key for me and an over a dollar bid is also key for me as well." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [657s-665s])
- **Strike selection** "I'm just looking for the ones that's one out of the money on either side." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [686s-697s])
- **DTE recommendation for new traders** "For new traders, I always always always recommend to buy weekly or anything that's longer... definitely not zero DTE's because they are extremely extremely risky and they can go down extremely fast." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1394s-1419s])
- **DTE for experienced traders** "If you're a little bit more experienced, you can buy those zero days and you can make a little bit more profits that way, however, that's only after you've been trading for quite a while." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1419s-1431s])
- **Friday trading recommendation** "If you're a newer trader, I would recommend you buying next Friday on Fridays. It's just the safer way to go in my opinion." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1492s-1499s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Starter position size** "My starter position on most trades is 50 contracts every time I say I'm buying a starter position. It's 50 contracts." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [875s-888s])
- **Buy button buffer** "I'm buying 10 cents above that ask price... that just allows me to get filled 97% of the time, 98% of the time." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [888s-904s])
- **Sell button buffer** "I'm selling 10 contracts... limit bid below 10 cents... I usually always get filled on the bid or a little bit higher than the bid." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [947s-1016s])
- **IV threshold to avoid** "I think above 50 to 60 is extreme levels of IV and you really shouldn't be trading that stock." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [357s-366s])
- **Trailing stop parameters** "I'll get stopped out if it goes down 10% after moving up 20%." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1480s-1492s])

## Options Greeks (Methodology Context)
- **Delta relevance** "The main thing that I think you guys need to worry about is delta and theta." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [304s-310s])
- **Greeks not needed for day trading** "When you're day trading options, you genuinely don't need to know this... I forget the last time I was really looking at Greeks in a lot of detail, because they really don't matter too much when you have the volume on that option contract." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [30s-76s])
- **Theta importance for zero DTE** "When you're trading those zero DTEs... it's really important to measure how much of a value the option might lose each day or hour as it comes closer to expiration." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [244s-261s])

## Platform Setup (IBKR)
- **Charts used** "Apple five minute, Apple one minute, S&P 500 one minute, and then the QQQ one minute... I have the five minute up to see what overall the general consensus of what it's doing... what Apple is doing in the trend." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1156s-1184s])
- **TradingView for charting** "TradingView is what you want to use for actually charting because IBKR's charting is absolutely, absolutely terrible." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1614s-1622s])
- **IBKR not linked to TradingView** "I don't have my TradingView linked to my IBKR... I think it would be really slow to execute as well." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1644s-1652s])
- **Monitor setup** "I have three monitors right now... I think three is good, you know even two is good as well." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [1211s-1225s])

Excellent. Continuing extraction from the new transcript. This is a core strategy lesson, so I will be verbose and capture all new material, confirmations, and contradictions.

---

### 1. Break-and-retest
- **Core Definition** "a break and retest trading setup is one of price breaks a certain level of support or resistance. And then retest that level. The goal of this trading setup is to buy and sell after the price breaks a level and retest it." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [95s-112s])
- **The Problem with Most Traders** "the problem I think that most people have is when they're trying to play the break and retest setup, they don't identify the proper levels. Right, so if you don't have the proper levels when you're playing the break and retest setup, you're genuinely trading blind." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [112s-127s])
- **Universality of Setup** "you can trade any stock, any market, and any time frame. That's what I love about this setup." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [214s-221s])
- **Market Tweaks Required** "In this market, some people from the previous, you know, mentorships will know that, you know, I do have a little bit of a tweak in this specific market currently. And we'll talk about that during live trading a little bit more, right? However, with this setup rate, you can trade in any stock." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [235s-252s])
- **Patience is Key** "the main part of this strategy and why some traders aren't as good as trading the strategy is because they simply cannot wait. They just don't have patience." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [415s-420s])
- **No Retrace = No Trade** "Sometimes that price will never retrace. Okay. That is really, really crucial to mention. Sometimes the price will never retrace to that level. And if it doesn't, that means you simply do not trade that day. Okay. You do not trade that day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [438s-455s])
- **Displacement Confirmation** "displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken the move after that key level break... this displacement of the stock shows that this is a really good example for a downside move" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1802s-1815s])
- **Entry Trigger Candle** "I personally like to look for hammer candles when I'm going long I like to look for inverted hammer candles or shooting star candles when I'm going short" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1884s-1892s])
- **Entry Trigger Candle Rationale** "what they show you right is someone or not someone but buyers try to bring this stock above this level and as we can see we touch this level but the second we touched it we had liquidity sitting there right we had sell order sitting here and therefore we broke back down and we couldn't break above so we actually came back down sellers were in control and we closed the candle alone" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1892s-1923s])
- **Stop Placement** "your risk level will always be a break of a key level or a break of the trend or market structure." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [474s-482s])
- **Wait for Candle Close on Stop** "with risk level, we're going to wait for the candle to close. Okay. So every time we have a risk, it should be the exact same thing." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [482s-492s])
- **Stop Loss Buffer** "me personally I don't like to have you know a stop loss that's right you know right at that level I like to give it a little bit of room" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1959s-1964s])
- **Opening Price as Stop Level** "opening price is a big factor as well opening price is something that the stock loves to respect during the day because that's just simply where the stock opened up during that day and so we're going to use the opening price right here and that's where we're going to put our stop loss" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1969s-1983s])
- **Profit Targets** "the profit targets for this specific strategy should be high of day, low of day, key levels and V1." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [725s-731s])
- **HOD/LOD as Primary Target** "high of day and low of day momentum trades are the best trades in my opinion to have. Sometimes key levels, you guys saw that sometimes I'll take a key level trade once in a while. Those trades will be once in a while. But overall, right, your profit target should be high day and low of day. That's really what I aim for. You guys will see that's what I aim for 95% of the time." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [761s-780s])
- **V1 Use** "I don't really use V1 too much other than just to determine if the stock is above or below V1 to see the trend in the day, right? We should go calls or puts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [744s-754s])
- **First Break vs Late Breaks** "on the same day right on the same day let's say retest here again it's just going to be a weaker level" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4166s-4173s])
- **Trend Must Be Established** "you want a trend to be developed before you take anything" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3632s-3640s])
