### 5. Key levels
- **Drawing Process (HTF to LTF)** "when you're drawing key levels, you want to start off on the daily time frame, then go to the four hour time frame, the one hour time frame, and then finally the one minute time frame." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [977s-988s])
- **Skipping Timeframes** "I skip all the way to the one minute. I don't go on the five minute. I don't go on the 10 minute. I skip to the one minute." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [996s-1002s])
- **HTF Patterns** "On the daily four hour and one hour, I'm looking at patterns." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1006s-1010s])
- **Previous Day Pivot Levels** "key pivot levels from the previous day. So from the previous day, when we see a higher low, lower high that has two to three touches, we're going to draw out those key levels for ourselves. Right. Because if it had touches back then, that means there's liquidity within those levels." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1025s-1045s])
- **Intraday Levels** "When we're looking at levels to draw in the intro day, we're simply just going to be drawing the higher highs, the higher lows, or the lower lows and lower highs, depending on which way the socket is trending. And those are the only key levels that I draw when the actual market has opened" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1058s-1074s])
- **Levels Must Be Obvious** "your key levels should be so obvious that, you know, if I was to tell you how to draw the key levels, which I will be in the upcoming slides. And if I tell you how to draw the key levels, you should instantly know when you're looking at the chart within about 30 seconds, exactly which key levels to draw and which ones are not appropriate to draw." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [622s-643s])
- **Forcing Levels = Forcing Trade** "If your key level is not obvious, right, that means you're forcing it. And if you're trying to figure out where the risk is in your trade, that also means you're forcing the trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [648s-656s])
- **Body vs Wick Drawing** "I believe you should draw it based off of context. Okay? A lot of traders, right, they only draw it on one. So they would only draw it on the body or they would only draw it on the wick. I don't do that. I draw both, right, depending on the context of the trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1432s-1450s])
- **Less is More** "less is more right? So I like the least amount of levels possible." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1552s-1557s])
- **Max Levels** "most of the time you'll see about how much is this one two three four five like around five to six levels at most." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1568s-1573s])
- **Pre-Market High/Low** "You can also draw pre-market high and pre-market low as well. Right? You can always draw those pre-market high and pre-market low as well." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1584s-1594s])
- **Levels are Subjective** "key levels have that subjectivity to them. Even, you know, as my canoes, you want to be, they're still going to be a little bit subjective." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [186s-196s])
- **Levels are Imaginary** "you got to understand that these key levels are at the end of the day they're imaginary okay... even though you see the key levels here okay you traders will have you know some traders will have this key level like right here right other traders will have it like right over here okay so realistically every key level is technically his own" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3924s-3950s])
- **Color Coding Levels** "if you chart the night before with color coded actual horizontal lines or trends or whatever, you're going to remember why you have them the next day as well." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1287s-1296s])

### 6. Time-of-day
- **Trade Window** "I don't trade after 11 o'clock so that's enough for me right so I'll trade from 930 to 11 a.m. Eastern and that's it for me after that I will not be taking any single trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4360s-4371s])
- **Personal Preference for 1-min** "Me personally, I'm a one minute trader. I like to be done my day before 11 o'clock to 11 30." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [348s-352s])
- **News Days (FOMC/CPI)** "I don't like to play the news. I don't like to play, you know, quadwitching. I don't like to play j-pout days. I don't like to play earnings. The reason I like don't like to do that is because it creates more volatility. And while more volatility could make more profits, like we talked about in the previous lesson, it can also increase your risk." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [568s-582s])

### 7. Exits
- **Scaling Out** "if you have 3 contracts right you can take 1 out at 423 33 you can take 1 out of 423 19 and then you can let your rest ride to 422 29" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2014s-2020s])
- **Breakeven Trigger** "we're not going to change our stop loss in till until we break this low because once we break this low then our stop simply just becomes break even okay" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2025s-2037s])
- **Breakeven Rationale** "once we break this low we break we create a new lower low and this becomes a new lower high so if we break this the stock will actually just reverse right theoretically it will reverse and therefore we can just break even on this trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2048s-2061s])
- **First Scale = Breakeven Trade** "the second I get my first scale right that trade is really just a break even trade for me depending on the type it's set up okay" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2635s-2640s])
- **Runners are Subjective** "the runners is really subjective depending on you know if you have left trailers and you just walked away from the screen or if you're sitting on the screen and you can actively monitor it" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2163s-2172s])
- **Trade Duration** "most of my profitable trades are usually within 10 to 15 minutes like much, much below 15 minutes" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [2611s-2622s])

### 8. HTF Application
- **HTF Trend Alignment** "on the higher time frame this trend is the exact same bearish okay so on the higher time frame we're bearish and then we have this trend established as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3102s-3111s])
- **Patterns on HTF** "pattern identification is really important on those higher time frames because whatever way a stock is moving with a pattern rate and it breaks to that one level especially if it's on a higher time frame it has more strength and volume behind it therefore the trend on the lower time frames will also follow that higher time frame." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4198s-4212s])
- **Main HTF Patterns** "the main patterns that I'm looking for are really just a bull flag and a bear flag on a higher timeframe sometimes the flat top as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4276s-4287s])

### 9. Trade Selection
- **A+ Setup Definition** "this retest right here is an A plus setup retest okay this is an A plus setup retest the reason this is because look displacement on this move" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1791s-1802s])
- **A+ Setup Criteria (Displacement + Trend)** "we have a very strong trend established rate and also on the higher time frame this trend is the exact same bearish okay so on the higher time frame we're bearish and then we have this trend established as well so simply what we're looking for is a retest from here" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [3097s-3116s])
- **Risk Confusion = Not A+** "if you're trying to enter a trade. And the risk is confusing to you. You don't know where the risk is. You don't know where you should put your stop loss. You don't know exactly where the trend is breaking. That means that's not an A plus trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [683s-698s])
- **Edge from QQQ** "our edge comes from QQQ when we're trading Tesla and Apple." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [814s-821s])
- **QQQ Alignment for Entries** "if I see Q's reversing from low of day and Tesla's still above VW, for example, in Q's below VW, but it's reversing from low of day, I'll try to enter into Tesla because it has relative strength." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [869s-881s])
- **QQQ Disagreement = No Trade** "I see that let's say I'm going to try to buy calls on Tesla. And I see that QQ is actually at low of day. I may not want to buy those calls on Tesla, right? Because QQ is at low of day and it's pushing down. Meaning if the whole sector of QQ is pushing down, Tesla has relative strength. I'm not going to try to buy Tesla or force Tesla there." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [844s-862s])
- **Instruments Traded** "Apple, QQQ, SPX... those are the only things I trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [700s-710s]) -- CONFIRMED
- **Option Strike Selection** "the price that I go for is one out of the money okay... so I'll probably buy 423 puts right so just one out of the money if I was buying calls at 424 I'd buy 425 calls just one out of the money every single time that's just what I do" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4506s-4527s]) -- CONFIRMED

### 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Win Rate** "when you look at my win rate for the strategy over a long period of time it's going to be around that 65% mark. Okay? So this strategy is accurate about 65% of the time." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1647s-1655s])
- **Average R:R** "on average if you look at my trades over a long period of time it'll be a one to two to one to two point two ish around Ristor Award strategy." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1634s-1641s])
- **Minimum R:R** "when we're looking at break and retest set ups by the way what I'm looking for is at least a one to two restore word." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1614s-1619s])
- **Starter Position Size** "My starter position on most trades is 50 contracts every time I say I'm buying a starter position. It's 50 contracts." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_1_Part_2_transcript.txt [875s-885s]) -- CONFIRMED

### SPECIAL AUDIT QUESTIONS
- **A/A+ grading**: "this retest right here is an A plus setup retest okay this is an A plus setup retest the reason this is because look displacement on this move" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1791s-1802s]) -- **NEW: A+ requires displacement after the break.**
- **A/A+ grading**: "if you're trying to enter a trade. And the risk is confusing to you. You don't know where the risk is. You don't know where you should put your stop loss. You don't know exactly where the trend is breaking. That means that's not an A plus trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [683s-698s]) -- **NEW: A+ requires clear, obvious risk level.**
- **QQQ alignment**: "our edge comes from QQQ when we're trading Tesla and Apple." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [814s-821s]) -- **NEW: QQQ is the primary edge for individual stock trades.**
- **QQQ alignment**: "if I see Q's reversing from low of day and Tesla's still above VW, for example, in Q's below VW, but it's reversing from low of day, I'll try to enter into Tesla because it has relative strength." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [869s-881s]) -- **NEW: QQQ reversal + stock relative strength = entry trigger.**
- **Displacement/FVG**: "displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken the move after that key level break" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [1802s-1808s]) -- **NEW: Displacement is the size of the move *after* the break.**
- **Stop-after-win / max trades/day**: "I don't trade after 11 o'clock so that's enough for me right so I'll trade from 930 to 11 a.m. Eastern and that's it for me after that I will not be taking any single trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels_transcript.txt [4360s-4371s]) -- **NEW: Hard cut-off at 11:00 AM ET.**
- **Reclaim setup**: NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

# EXTRACTION FROM NEW TRANSCRIPTS

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **Trend Development Required Before Break** "we don't want to enter a trade until we break to either side and this is why some of you guys don't have the high probability traits for the break and retest setup the reason you don't have high probability traits for break and retest setups is because you're not... Trading within a high probability environment you're not trading on new highs you're not trading on new lows you're not letting the trend develop you're trading within this chop right taking a retest within this chop is not something you want to do" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [442s-469s])

- **Chop Zone = No Trades** "This is where most traders fail right because they're gonna be trying to look for break and retest within here when this is something you definitely should not do right this is noise. This is where you're gonna get chopped" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1218s-1238s])

- **Fakeout Liquidity Before Real Break** "Everyone else on the chart saw the same thing right everyone saw that this is where you know your stop should be for the continuation to the upside so what did they do they're gonna fake you out and they're gonna move the stock right back up" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1154s-1165s])

- **Clear Break vs Liquidity Fakeout** "This now is a clear break right this is a clear break right this isn't any liquidity break out or liquidity fake this is a clear break right" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1257s-1265s])

- **Retest Entry with Weak Price Action Candle** "right here what are we getting this is a great retest why is this a great retest... Inverted hammer candle right week price action off of our key level low of day right always a high probability trade when it's a low of day level right low of day retest inverted hammer very weak handle" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [627s-674s])

- **Stop Loss at Break of Previous Structure** "we would put our stop loss at the break of this previous level okay this comes in at 238 75s right because we can see that we tried to hold this level right here the low at 237 74's right we can see strong buying action kind of occurring here... if we break back above this the likely to the stop coming back down is not likely right so that's where you would put your stop loss" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [723s-772s])

- **Stop at Break of Consolidation/Flat Top** "I would put my stop loss at the break of the previous low right and that low right we got put in right here and this consolidation right so this two 40 87 level right why would I put it here? What the reason I would put it here is because we can see right here flat top break above the flat top consolidate before that high of they move consolidate here right so what does this show us right you have to think about the context here guys right this shows us that buyers try to step in... buyers are going to be trying their hardest to defend this level at 240 87 so they're gonna be trying their hardest to defend this level right and therefore that's where you want to put your stop" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1341s-1396s])

- **Profit Targets at Round Numbers** "where would your profit target be you can have it a high of day you can also have it at whole psychological numbers as it's kind of going up so 244's right 245 246" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1399s-1417s])

- **A+ Break and Retest = Continuing Trend** "a break above this level right the previous high of day right a retest of high of day and then we can continue that move to the upside right that would be an A plus break and retest that up for me right and that's simply because it's continuing that trend to the upside" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1090s-1106s])

- **No Trade Until High or Low of Day Breaks** "I would only take a trade today if we broke the high or low of today which we didn't do when I was training until 1030 okay" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [398s-416s])

- **Wait for Momentum and Clear Trend** "You want them momentum to occur you want to see a clear trend" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1233s-1241s])

- **Key Levels as Zones, Not Exact Ticks** "When I'm looking at these key levels it's not that exact level right it's a little bit of a zone right like most people think it's like it's to the tick right and sometimes it is to the tick right because my levels are pretty accurate however let's say this this kind of level here right you want to kind of like it's like you know it's like a small little zone right it's not the complete level right" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2282s-2303s])

- **Consolidation = Not a Retest** "This isn't a retest. This isn't a retest at all. There's no displacement. This isn't a retest at all. This is just consolidation to the downside" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2340s-2348s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **No Previous Day Levels Used** "every time I draw Q levels it's a brand new chart brand new chart every single day. I don't have previous day levels. I don't I don't have previous levels that I've used ever I always start with a brand new fresh chart every night" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2433s-2441s])

- **Pre-Market High/Low Use Depends on Context** "Depends on the key levels I draw right sometimes those pre-market high and pre-market lows for example pre-market high and pre-market low the market gaps up overnight. I'm gonna be using pre-market high pre-market low levels right however if we open up in a range and I already have previous day levels right maybe those pre-market levels aren't as important because more liquidity is sitting within those actual key levels" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2503s-2522s])

- **VWAP Used Only When Near Key Level** "VWAP is only used for me if we have a key level near that level as well... VWAP is a more of an indicator for above VWAP calls if we're below VWAP puts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2616s-2631s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **No Trade Until 10:30 if No Break** "I would only take a trade today if we broke the high or low of today which we didn't do when I was training until 1030 okay" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [398s-416s])

## 7. Exits

- **Profit Targets at Round Numbers** "where would your profit target be you can have it a high of day you can also have it at whole psychological numbers as it's kind of going up so 244's right 245 246" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1399s-1417s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **Trend Structure: Higher Highs/Higher Lows** "when we have for example right an up trend right what does an up trend consist of a consists of the opening price high right a higher low higher high higher low right very very simple concept" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [56s-78s])

- **Trend Intact Until Structure Breaks** "The trend structure is still to the upside until we break this low" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [145s-169s])

- **Real-Time Trends Never Textbook** "in real time trading you'll never get something that you're going to be doing that's you know a plus or textbook okay that's super important to mention" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [116s-125s])

- **Sellers in Control After Failed Breakouts** "sellers are much much more in control than buyers because buyers did try to come up and break out of this level not once but twice and rejected both times so therefore sellers are more in control you have to understand this context when you're trading okay" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [573s-586s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **QQQ as Edge for Individual Tickers** "my edge like we talked about in the previous video comes from QQ understanding what's going on with QQ" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1721s-1730s])

- **QQQ Context Saves Bad Setups** "is this the greatest break and retest to the downside? No right however... here's what's going to save you and what saves me all the time and that's adding in QQQ right adding in QQQQ to your examples" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1609s-1636s])

- **QQQ Strength Prevents Downside Move** "the reason you wouldn't take this trade is because... you see that there's a very strong push on the markets right and therefore if we look at Tesla right it kind of chops around... as Q's is kind of pushing here look at what Tesla does it just chops around right you never kind of reach that low of that low of day profit target" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1814s-1856s])

- **QQQ Used as Reference, Not Traded** "I don't trade Q Q by the way I want to make that very clear. I only use Q as a as a kind of like a reference point" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1944s-1951s])

- **Relative Weakness: Stock Drops Faster Than QQQ** "Q's made a for it's first rejection candle and look at how big in a video's rejection candle is right NVIDIA's already a low of day in three candles where Q's is still above you up... this is showing me relative weakness in NVIDIA" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2045s-2066s])

- **Need QQQ Weakness to Confirm Short** "I need what now I what I need to see now is Q's show me weakness as well because if Q shows me weakness NVIDIA is the best ticker to play for relative weakness" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2067s-2083s])

- **QQQ Retest Confirms Entry on Individual Stock** "what would give me the confidence to enter into this retest... look at what Q's is doing this is why I would enter this trade look at what Q's does look at the opening print this is opening print Q's retesting opening print right do you see how Q's is retesting that opening print what a good candle for that retest of the opening print right below VWP we broke structure to the downside very impulsive move to the downside right very strong move first retest is opening print retest with a weak price action candle right and so if Q's does potentially keep moving down here... NVIDIA is a great entry here" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2148s-2234s])

- **Relative Strength/Weakness Determines Which Ticker to Trade** "which if that if for example if the market is looking weak and Tesla is showing more signs of relative weakness and apple right which means Tesla is having a more impulsive move to the downside than apple I'm gonna be training Tesla if if Tesla is or if for example vice versa if apple if the market is right and Tesla is weak why would I trade Tesla right I wouldn't I would trade apple or spy" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2539s-2568s])

- **Four Tickers Up for Context** "I have four tickers up right I'm looking at the overall markets" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2770s-2778s])

- **No Level 2, No Book, No Footprint** "I don't look at level two. I don't look at book. I don't look at footprint all right I only look at the actual price action that's happening on the charts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2691s-2698s])

- **Momentum Trader - Quick In/Out** "I'm a momentum trader and I'm not trying to waste it around and wait for the range to break I'm I'm looking for a quick in quick out" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2711s-2718s])

- **Opening Range Strategy Exists But Not Yet Covered** "There is a way to trade the open right I have a strategy on how to play the open and we'll get into that strategy. ## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Option Strike: First Out of the Money** "I always trade the first out of the money first out of the money. So for example if we're looking at a video here in this example right and it's 486 I'd be trading the 485 puts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2445s-2456s])

- **Same Strike When Scaling In** "If I'm scaling into trades in and out of yeah, I'm buying the same contract so let's say I for example let's say um it would right here. It was a 486 right on mine 485s let's say we retest again here at 482 37 then it's a really good retest for me to enter into I'm still buying the exact same ones same contracts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2912s-2930s])

- **SPX Options: Same Day or Week Out** "if it's spies sometimes I'll do the same day, but I recommend the week out" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [2791s-2797s])

- **A/A+ grading**: CONFIRMED - "a break above this level right the previous high of day right a retest of high of day and then we can continue that move to the upside right that would be an A plus break and retest that up for me right and that's simply because it's continuing that trend to the upside" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [1090s-1106s]) -- **NEW: A+ = continuing trend direction**
- **QQQ alignment**: EXTENSIVELY COVERED - QQQ is the edge, used as reference not traded, must show weakness for shorts, retest confirmation on QQQ needed for entry on individual stocks
- **Displacement/FVG**: CONFIRMED - "displacement simply means after a key level the move which you get after a key level so the break right and this break is a very very nice break indicating to us that the trend is indeed to the downside" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Read_Indexes_transcript.txt [602s-626s])

--- Chunk 4 ---
# EXTRACTION FROM NEW TRANSCRIPTS

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Entry/Exit Simplicity** "Simple break, retest, hammer candle, inverted hammer candle, strong price action, weak price action, high day low day or key levels. Very, very simple." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [659s-666s])
- **Entry/Exit Must Be Identical Every Time** "Every single time that I take a trade, it will be the exact same trade, it will be the exact same entry signal, it will be the exact same exit signal" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [649s-659s])
- **Break and Retest is the ONLY Strategy** "All my strategies are basically based off B&R in some way, shape or form" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3507s-3513s])
- **Step-by-Step Process** "We wait for momentum and trend to develop. Look for the stock to break a key level. Once broken, look for a retrace. On retrace, look for a hammer candle. Once PA is confirmed. Buy into stock, looking for high-adads exit or V-Ops, slash key level as stop loss." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [763s-776s])
- **Without Identical Entry/Exit Criteria, You're Gambling** "until you actually consistently look for the exact same entry and exit criteria, you're gambling, right? Because you don't have a system." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [791s-801s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Profit Targets at HOD/LOD or Key Levels** "within my system, I know that I can take profits at high every day, low of day, or key levels. That is where I take profits 99.999999% of the time." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2341s-2357s])
- **Stop Levels at Key Levels, VWAP, Market Structure** "Have them at key levels, VWAP in market structure. That's what my stop level has always been." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2407s-2417s])
- **VWAP Alone Not Enough for Stop** "VWAP alone will not be where I stop out. But VWAP combined with something is where I can stop out." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2420s-2430s])
- **VWAP as Reference Only** "VWAP is simply on my chart because it helps me look at where a stock will potentially bounce from if there's a key level there... VWAP will not affect my strategy or how I hold my stock" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3071s-3091s])
- **Psychological Numbers as Targets** "How do you choose a whole psychological numbers for targets? It's just whichever one is kind of the nearest at the time, right? So if it's like if stocks are like 100, if 101's near, right, I'll choose 100 or 101" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3655s-3667s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Trade Window: 9:30 AM to 11:00 AM ET** "I only trade from 930 a.m. to 11 a.m. Eastern time" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1098s-1105s])
- **Never Trades After 11:30 AM** "they have never ever seen me take a trade after 1130 a.m. ever." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1111s-1120s])
- **Time Rule Based on Backtesting** "I know that my setup and my specific strategy has the most probability of working within those times and those are the times that I backed. I did in back test 1 p.m. on the day. I didn't backed as 2 p.m. on the day. Right? I backed as is from 930 a.m. to 1130 a.m. and I will only trade that time" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1127s-1145s])
- **Friday: No Trading** "I won't be trading. There's in Friday, right? I try to trade there's in Friday by myself to ensure, you know, I kind of have a couple of days for myself." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3496s-3507s])

## 7. Exits
- **Scaling to Hold Winners Longer** "if we scale to contracts here and then we scale 1 out high of day and we stop, we have our stop loss now at a 1 to 1... we can hold for the next profit target up here." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2797s-2822s])
- **Psychological Benefit of Scaling** "once you sell 1 out high of day, what will happen psychologically is you're like, okay, I have a guaranteed realized profit for the day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2840s-2853s])
- **Scaling Example: 2 Contracts** "Let's say we have three contracts. We scale one at a high of day. And we leave two for another key level somewhere down the road." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2478s-2488s])
- **Market Conditions Dictate Scaling Strategy** "In a very bullish market... you can hold that trade much longer after the high a day... At the same time, let's say there was... chopiness... would you hold your trade? Probably not" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2463s-2540s])
- **Choppy Market: Sell Out Quick** "when it's choppy, you really want to sell out quick. Okay? You really want to sell out quick." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2573s-2578s])
- **Breakeven After First Scale** "if we scale to contracts here and then we scale 1 out high of day and we stop, we have our stop loss now at a 1 to 1" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2803s-2809s])
- **Monitor Both Option Premium and Underlying** "you do have to monitor both. It's very important because of course our money is tied up in the actual option contract. However, for me, when I'm looking at the actual stock, it's important to look at the technicals" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3008s-3024s])
- **More Emphasis on Underlying Asset** "you want to put more emphasis on the underlying asset." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3045s-3052s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **Max Trades Per Day: 3** "I only trade three like at max on a day. Like this is my maximum level of trades in the day. I can only take three trades in a day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1436s-1442s])
- **Max Losses in a Row: 2** "if I have two losses, I'm not going to take the third trade just because I know with experience and over time that my strategy isn't simply not working that day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1458s-1468s])
- **Max Loss Rule for New Traders** "if you lose those two trades, you're done. Right? You're done for the day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1483s-1486s])
- **Max Stop for the Day = Daily Profit Target** "if you're aiming to make $100 a day. Well, your max stop for the day should also be $100 a day." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1197s-1203s])
- **Max Position Size Rule** "if you're trading with one to two contracts, your max position size is two contracts. That's it. Every single time you can only trade two contracts, you can't scale in more." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1406s-1410s])
- **Stick to One System Only** "Only trade that one system. That's it. That is it. You cannot trade another system after that. The only system that you are allowed to trade from now on is that one system and stick to it." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1920s-1933s])
- **Shiny Object Syndrome Kills Profitability** "the number one reason traders don't become profitable and never reach out profitability is a lack of discipline and conviction in their system" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2012s-2017s])
- **Don't Change Systems After Losses** "people change their systems based on foam or food routes... you just lost all that progress because now you're going to go follow the next person" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2028s-2067s])
- **System Must Be Simple Enough to Explain** "Have it to a point where you can explain to your younger cousin or your kids or anything like that, you know, where you enter an exit to chart and they understand what you're saying" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [700s-709s])
- **Entry/Exit Must Make Sense with Trend** "If you're trying to buy a breakout trade... for high of day, but the trend is low of day, right? That's simply not an effective entry and exit signal because your trade doesn't make sense. You're not following market trend." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [731s-748s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **New Traders: 1-2 Contracts Only** "only have one to two option contracts. Okay? The reason this is because you will lose money and this way you will lose the least amount of money" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1595s-1604s])
- **Experienced Trader Sizing: 50-70 Contracts Currently** "I'm taking 50 contracts to 70, 80 contracts max right now. Right? Like 100 contracts will be, if I say, I really, really good trades setup, but right now I'm only taking 50 to 70 contracts." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1720s-1729s])
- **Bullish Market Sizing: 150-200+ Contracts** "when of the market is extremely bullish, I'll go in with a lot of contracts. Like we're talking about 152, 200 plus contracts." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1683s-1688s])
- **Zero DTE: 50-60 Contracts Max** "I'll slowly slowly enter into zero DTEs with smaller size. Let's say 50 to 60 contracts max" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1835s-1838s])
- **Risk Per Trade Should Be Consistent for New Traders** "The amount of risk you take per trade should generally be the exact same." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1770s-1777s])
- **Experienced Traders: Risk Per Trade Varies by Market** "I manage my risk per trade, right? It's not every single trade is the exact for my personal risk management... for me, personally, at the level I am, right, it's per trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3364s-3392s])
- **New Traders: Keep Constant Contract Amount** "for new traders, you want to keep that constant" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3399s-3404s])
- **Have 1 Year of Expenses Before Going Full-Time** "for a trader. Have a year's worth of expenses. Right? And that's really, really important to help you be consistent and profitable and not fear every single time you're entering a trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2717s-2726s])
- **Profitable Traders Can Lose 2-3 Months in a Row** "I know profitable traders that have lost for two to three months in a row before. Okay, with their strategy. But that doesn't mean anything to them because they know that they have that long term vision." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2677s-2690s])

## 11. Zero DTE / Options-Specific Rules (NEW TOPIC)
- **Zero DTE: Not for New Traders** "Zero DTEs should not be traded in my opinion if you're a new trader. Okay? You do not trade zero DTEs." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1783s-1789s])
- **Zero DTE Volatility Explained** "if that option goes against you even a little bit... you will lose 40 to 50 percent of that option contracts value." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1808s-1818s])
- **Thursday: Go Next Week, Not Friday** "On Thursday, should you also go next week or just proud of it? I personally, you're you're fine on Thursday's realistically, just go friday, right? Friday, just go next week." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3303s-3313s])
- **Options: Don't Buy Breakouts** "if you're trading options, trading breakouts is probably one of the worst things to do in my opinion, because when you're buying the top of an option contract, the second it dips a little bit... you're going to lose so much money" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [452s-468s])
- **Swing Trading Options for 9-5 Workers** "If you're a person who has a family, you work a nine to five... You may be better off swing trading options over a one to two week period rather than scalping them" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [476s-488s])

## 12. Trading Style / System Creation (NEW TOPIC)
- **Momentum Trader Identity** "We are momentum traders. I trade momentum. I'm looking for a high-a-day low-of-day type of things" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [226s-232s])
- **Choose One Style and Stick to It** "choose one style, stick to that style and stick to that style only after you understand what kind of commitments you have in your real life" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [578s-587s])
- **Commitments Dictate Trading Style** "The commitments you have directly correlate with the trading ability you have... if you have a full-time work commitment... You cannot be a scalper" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [279s-297s])
- **System Must Be Backtested** "without backtesting, your system is simply won't work." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [838s-843s])
- **Backtesting Increases Confidence, Conviction, Experience, Decreases Fear** "What backtesting will do is increase your confidence, it'll increase your conviction, it will increase your experience to charts much faster, and it will decrease your fear." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [886s-892s])
- **Continuous Refinement Required** "refining your system will be a life long process. And that's something you need to understand. It is a life long process." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2178s-2184s])
- **Journaling Identifies Weaknesses** "Journaling will help you identify weaknesses within yourself and strengths within yourself. It will help you identify weaknesses within your system based off the market and strengths of your system with the market." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2160s-2171s])
- **Journaling Only Works With One Strategy** "journaling isn't effective when you have 50 different strategies that you're trading. Journaling is only effective when you have one" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [829s-833s])
- **Track Performance with TradeZilla or Excel** "You can use tradezilla, right? I use tradezilla personally. That's where I have my journal. However, I recommend in the beginning to use Excel." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3188s-3201s])

## 13. Market Conditions / Regime (NEW TOPIC)
- **Drawdown Periods Are Normal** "there are periods within your system that it just won't work, right? And it's because of the market conditions" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2110s-2116s])
- **Must Continuously Backtest Even When Profitable** "even if you become profitable, it doesn't mean that you know, you can just pick your feet up and relax. That's not how I work... You need to continuously backtest your system." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2122s-2131s])
- **Market Conditions Dictate Sizing** "you have to manage your risk based off of the market you're in, is your training setup working, if your trading setup is working, really, really effectively right now, maybe you can size up slowly." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1733s-1741s])

## 14. Psychology / Discipline (NEW TOPIC)
- **Emotions Cannot Be Eliminated** "Your emotions will not be mitigated enough to zero... Every human no matter how long you've been trading. That's the one thing you're going to have to deal with for the rest of your trading career, which is mitigating your emotions." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [160s-178s])
- **Fear Causes Missed Entries** "Has this ever been you? Have you seen a trade that's within your setup? 100% perfect trade... you're like, oh wait, let me just wait for the entry... That's you actually experiencing fear because you are not entering the trade even though every single one of your rules or your entry criteria are met." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [960s-996s])
- **Missed Entry Leads to Revenge Trading** "now you can't enter the trade and you see the trade hit your profit target and now you're frustrated. Now the fact you're frustrated, you're going to go and enter into a subpar trade right after just to try to make that profit on the day" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1016s-1028s])
- **System Makes You Mechanical, Not Emotional** "a system essentially allows you to become more mechanical within trades rather than going off of your emotions or gut feelings" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [32s-39s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Pre-market high as key level for confirmation** "we have a pre-market high level of 292 62 on Q's we break above that on Q's but on spy we have a pre-market high that we're not breaking above" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2467s-2479s])
- **Pre-market high break as momentum signal** "I want Q Q to break above the pre-market high for me to be able to take this retest on apple" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2691s-2698s])
- **Cyclical levels as profit targets** "So for example here right I would be looking for that 285 cycle level on Q's" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2770s-2777s])
- **High of day as profit target** "our profit targets for example on Q's here would be high of day" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2523s-2527s])
- **VWAP as trend filter** "So whatever stock I'm trading has to be above view up. Okay, or above view off or calls below view up for puts" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3233s-3241s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

### QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation
- **QQQ as confirmation for individual stock trades** "I don't personally trade QQ. I only look at QQ for confirmation on whatever other stock I'm trading" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2916s-2922s])
- **QQQ break of key level gives conviction for stock entry** "I need Q Q Q to be above its pre-market high to show momentum" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2723s-2726s])
- **QQQ as edge for stock trades** "you have to use Qs with the stock that you're trading to see what the overall market is doing" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3044s-3049s])
- **QQQ break + stock consolidation = strong momentum** "if Q's does push here to new high of day and Tesla breaks this flag you're going to get a lot of strong momentum" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2071s-2075s])
- **QQQ holding level allows scaling in on stock dip** "if QQQ is still pushing higher... the chances of apple coming back or the probability is a little bit higher" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3028s-3040s])
- **QQQ can be used as stop loss reference** "you can use that correlation of Q's to understand why apple is dipping into that stop loss and actually save yourself from being stopped out" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3116s-3122s])
- **QQQ as profit target indication** "you can use QQ as the indication of your stop loss and your profit target as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3010s-3015s])
- **QQQ ranging = don't take the trade** "if Q's is ranging for example... then we don't want to take this trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2181s-2183s])
- **QQQ flat top rejection gives conviction for short** "the reason I would enter this trade based on the fact that we just rejected at 1034 we rejected the flat top" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2324s-2330s])
- **QQQ consolidation = minimal risk** "the risk is minimal here because we're in a consolidation phase on Q's" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2290s-2295s])
- **QQQ at VWAP with higher low + stock retest = good trade** "if cues is consolidating at view up for example and it's making a higher low... and Tesla is coming back for a retest after making new highs that would qualify for a really really good trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3258s-3268s])

# EXTRACTION: mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Why the pattern works** "It works because stock moves up from a consolidation period, it moves up above its resistance or support. It breaks that rate where buyers or sellers are present near those levels because that's where it had a strong break above. And so when it comes back and pulls back into that area, it should have it buyers or sellers, depending if you're going along or short, right, to actually move the stock in the direction that is desired for us. That's why the break and retest setup works." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1141s-1170s])
- **Stop loss placement at impulse move origin** "it would be two, 16, 70s on Tesla here, right, because this is where the impulse of move came from, the break of that move came from, right? And so this is where you would want to put your stop loss, because if we did break below this, right, this stock would have come back into that, it would break that previous high, right, and this is the impulse to move in the impulse of candle that actually broke that new high" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [444s-467s])
- **Stop loss on body vs wick depends on confluence** "it depends on what has more confluence, does a body have more confluence, or does a wick have more confluence, if there's equal body candles, I'll do it there, if the wick is small, I'll do it on the body, if the wick, if there's two equal wick lows, I'll do it on the wick, right? So it just depends on the context." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2119s-2133s])
- **Wait for candle close before exiting on stop loss** "this candle right, technically, if it closed, right, it would have closed down here at about 170, 82's, right? However, it didn't close, it didn't close and when it did close, right, it's closed as one of the strongest candles within the day. Higher volume, very, very strong hammer candle, right? And this is exactly why this is exactly why we wait for those candles to close. Okay, it's so you don't get whipped out" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2455s-2480s])
- **Backtesting results depend on candle close** "whenever you are back testing your setups right, you're waiting for those candles to close anyway. So those back testing results of however much money you could have made or however many percentage points you would have gained or lost, they are dependent on your candle close or not dependent on if you would have entered exited before the candle close or entered up before the candle close. And that's why I wait for candle close." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2487s-2504s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** — No verbatim teaching of "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" by name.

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **Definition of 84% rule** "if you have an entry, like an entry criteria, let's say it's a break and retest rate, a stock breaks high of day, comes back, retest to key level, right? Looking and you're looking for whatever a new high of day or a new key level, inner stop losses at the break of market structure. Let's say you break that market structure, right? But then you come back later on in the day, this can be like 5, 10, 15, 20 minutes, whatever. You come back to that same area later on in the day, that same key level that you first entered on, and you have the exact same trade setting up, right? So you have the same break of that key level, you have the same retest of the key level, you have, you know, a hammer candle still there, you have the same stop loss you could put in before, and you have the same profit target as before. Well, that's called the 84% rule" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [68s-115s])
- **84% probability of second trade working** "theoretically what the 84% rule indicates is 84% of the time, if the criteria is the same as your first trade and the first trade criteria and the system obviously has to be correct, but which is, which ours is, if the criteria is the exact same, 84% of the time, this second trade should work out, right?" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [115s-129s])
- **Psychological benefit of 84% rule** "that's like a rule that you know, you can apply when you stop out of trades, right? If it does come back, you can always reassess and reenter the trade, right? And that'll help you stop out of, you know, all the trades that your stop loss and not have that foam of what if I, you know, miss out on a specific trade." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [135s-148s])
- **Re-entry requires reassessing context** "if this trade ever came back, right, if this trade ever came back here and then retested, right, we could always look at this again, we can always reassess, right, see what the queue is doing, see the context of the market, and we could always reenter, right, for that, for a good trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [786s-803s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Trade first hour for most volume** "the first hour is what has the most ballroom? It has the most volatility. And that's something that I'm comfortable trading. And as you guys can see it's a very profitable way to, you know, really finish off today quick before the chop comes in." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1979s-1992s])
- **Trade until ~11:00 AM based on volume** "Most of the time, if you just look at volume, volume will die down after 11 o'clock. So you can see here 11 o'clock is when we got the most volume. And throughout the day here, right, you can see much, much more volume than the rest of the day, right? You can see based off that chart here, there was more volume here. There was some volume, once again 11 o'clock, and then after that, right, there was less volume. Therefore, the biggest moves occur during that first one to two hours or one to two hours of market open. So I like to trade till about 11 o'clock. After that, the volume simply dies down, and therefore the moves take longer to form." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2624s-2663s])
- **Do not trade last hour** "Do you ever trade the last hour of the date? Nope, I do not. After I promise you every single day, after 1130, after I leave the voice session with you guys, I don't come back to the market unless I'm making a trade review for you guys." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2278s-2291s])
- **If can't trade first hour, use 5-minute timeframe** "if you can't trade for the first hour of the day, I would simply trade the five minute time frame, right? So later on in the day, if you were to trade, I would trade the five minute time frame instead of the one minute. And you can still trade the break and retest on the five minute time frame. It just takes long as to, it takes longer to actually see those moves occur." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2088s-2109s])

## 7. Exits
- **Always stop out — never move stop loss** "you need to stop out of every single trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [803s-804s])
- **Moving stop loss leads to catastrophic losses** "you now have a 0.5 risk to our ward. So let's say you were supposed to lose what $500, you know, that break of structure right there, you now probably lost over $2,000 to $3,000, because you simply did not want to exit the trade when you had to" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [681s-695s])
- **Example of moving stop loss psychology** "you saw my thought process when I was talking about it, right, you saw like how most new traders think about these trades, like I thought about it like that too, when I was a new trader, I used to think like that, like man shit, like, you know, it broke here, you know what, let's just have our stop here, it makes sense, because that's the next key, key level, right, it'll always bounce from there, okay, it bounced a little bit, right, it showed up bounced a little bit from here, all right, so that means, you know, there is buyers coming in, but maybe he needs one more dip, he needs one more dip before we can, you know, exit out, so let's just put our stop here. And first stop, start here, it might as well be low of day and it keeps going and going and going, right, and now you're, you know, what should have been a very, very small red day, a man, a manageable red day is the biggest red day you've had within the month" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [704s-746s])
- **Wait for candle close to avoid whipsaw** "this is exactly why we wait for those candles to close. Okay, it's so you don't get whipped out and you understand that, hey, if I do get stopped out and let's say we would have stopped out here, that's completely fine. It's within our system." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2475s-2487s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **One setup focus** "find a setup that matches your personality and ensure that setup is profitable long term. Back to that setup to gain confidence and only play that one set up. A lot of people, right? They focus on like four different setups or it's not even for, right? They focus on what's a two. I wouldn't even focus on two, right? I would only focus on one set up that matches your personality" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1466s-1493s])
- **Setup must match personality** "if you want to genuinely be a successful trader that makes, you know, a lot of money in the future, right? This isn't a hobby, right? You pay money to be in this route, right? Like, you know, this isn't a video game, right? You need to actually focus and find a setup that aligns with your personality." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1493s-1515s])
- **Break and retest matches his personality** "the break and retested matches with my personality because not I don't like break-out trading, right? It's it's I've tried break-out trading. I don't like it. But I can still trade momentum by buying those little pullbacks near the breakout, right? For the high of day moves, I like doing that. Okay? I like buying the pullback because it shows me that there's a little bit less risk in that trade, right? And I have a very defined stop loss. I have a very defined profit target." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1515s-1541s])
- **Trade minimum 4 months on one setup** "trade a setup for like two, three, four, five months, at least you need to trade it for four months minimum in my opinion, four months, one setup, trade it on a daily basis, okay? And that's how you'll figure out if, first of all, if it's profitable, of course, you can back test it, but you'll figure out if it matches your personality as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1638s-1660s])
- **Learn charts first before psychology** "The first thing is charts. All right. So I would focus on charts first and how to read that price action, right. So I think this one's really important because without this right, like you can have the best psychology in the world. But if you don't know how to read chart rate, like realistically, you're not really going to be a good trader." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1003s-1021s])
- **Psychology is 4th priority, not 1st** "A lot of people put psychology as their number one thing that they would focus on when they're starting right, especially on social media, everyone's talking with psychology and how it's so important and, you know, of course, psychology is important and that's why it's number fourth, 100%, once you understand the basics of trading, right, because it doesn't really honestly, like it genuinely does not take long to actually learn how to trade per se, it really doesn't, right, you just have to learn the charge, you have to learn how, trans structure works in self-adaptant, like realistically that, you can do that in less than a year. This psychology part will take you years, on years, on years, years" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1230s-1273s])
- **Don't focus on psychology if you don't understand charts** "in the beginning, you don't even understand how the trade, how this stock actually moves, like a lot of people, and I know even a couple of people in this room, they're focusing on psychology and think they have, you know, the charting figured out, but then they send me trades, and it's like the worst trade I've ever seen" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1278s-1293s])
- **Choose 1-2 mentors with same strategy** "I recommend Nutriors to always choose one or two mentors that have the same strategy, right? So let's say you want the break and retests, right? Choose one to two people, it could be me, could be someone else, right? Choose one or two people, focus on what they say, focus on what they have, right? And just really, really learn their strategy, and then back to the figure yourself to be self-sufficient." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2176s-2199s])
- **Too many mentors causes confusion** "I think when people have too many different mentors, too many different people, so I'm going to do some people are telling them to wait for the don't trade the first 15 minutes, some people are telling them to trade the first 15 minutes, you know, there's a lot of different roles, and that's why it's super super important to have one or two mentors that have the same strategy, kind of alignment, and you can follow them. That's that's the fastest way to success in trading, in my opinion." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2199s-2219s])
- **Don't force trades that aren't there** "this is you're drawing a random key. And I talked about this before in previous workshops, you know, you're, you're forcing trades that just aren't there here" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2600s-2609s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Minimum 1:2 risk-to-reward target** "I try to aim for one to two, risk to a word at a minimum." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2255s-2260s])
- **1:1.5 R:R still acceptable for A+ setup** "if it wasn't a plus set up, I most likely would take the trade, even if it was one point five" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2267s-2273s])
- **Don't need more than $50,000 for options account** "With with options, you've genuinely don't need more than $50,000. You really don't need more than a $50,000 account." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2339s-2344s])
- **Withdraw anything over $100,000 monthly** "every single month, anything over a hundred thousand, I try to take out, and I haven't done that in a month or two, but I try to take out anything over a hundred thousand in my account. And that's every month. So I just basically pay myself a month" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [2358s-2374s])
- **Trade one setup for minimum 4 months** "trade a setup for like two, three, four, five months, at least you need to trade it for four months minimum in my opinion, four months, one setup, trade it on a daily basis" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1638s-1650s])
- **Don't quit job until 2-3 years expenses + 1 year consistency** "you can not quit your job in my opinion until you have at least two to three years of expenses set aside. Okay? Until you have two to three years of expenses set aside. And for at least one year you're a consistent trader. Like people like they'll be consistent traders for a month and be like I'm done. I'm done. I'm done. My job. Like no, that is not something that I recommend personally. I personally believe that you should not quit your job until you see at least a year of consistency and have three years of expenses backed up." (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Part_2_transcript.txt [1938s-1974s])

# CONTINUED EXTRACTION

## 7. Exits
- **Exit majority or all at HOD in V-shaped recovery markets** "I need to ride that momentum to high of day, but I need to exit the majority or if not all that position at high of day because I know I can expect a reversal because of the market sentiment" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [645s-651s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **Market sentiment determines position sizing, not fixed** "market sentiment determines position size... I cannot give you a one to one risk management session and tell you exactly what works for me will work for you" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [470s-485s])
- **In good market: 150-200+ contracts; in poor market: ~50 contracts** "in a very, very good market, I'm trading over 200 contracts, 150, 100 contracts... Currently, right, I've trade about 50 contracts. The reason that is is because the market sentiment at this specific time isn't the best context" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [518s-535s])
- **In poor market context, only take A+ setups with lower size** "Is it a time to push in the markets or is it a time to play defensive and really just take those eight plus setups with lower size, right? Because the probabilities are simply not within your favor" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [556s-566s])
- **Adapt stop loss tightness to market type** "in those markets, I have to manage my risk and understand, like, hey, I do have to take a little bit more aggressive entries within this market because we understand that we trend mostly all day. And I need to have my stop loss very tight because I'm taking those aggressive entries" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [588s-603s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Minimum 1,000 volume on option contracts** "I need at least a thousand volume on the option contract that I'm trading, right, on these large caps stocks" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [248s-254s])
- **90% of time large caps have sufficient liquidity** "most of the time, I'd say 90% of the time that's completely fine, right? Because these are large caps stocks" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_Pa_transcript.txt [255s-262s])

9. Trade selection (only market sentiment sizing covered — no A/A+/B grading criteria, confluence, max trades/day, stop-when-green)
10. Concrete numbers (only volume minimum covered — no win rates, R:R, risk %, drawdown, gap fill stats)

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **Playing the open is similar to break-and-retest but different** "it is similar to the break and retest, but it is a little bit different, right? There's a little bit different, little things that you have to do" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [52s-59s])
- **Break-and-retest requires more waiting; playing the open requires faster action** "the break and retest, you know, you need to wait, right? There's a lot of waiting with the break and retests. But with playing the open, you have much, much, much more emphasis on key levels, so you don't have to wait for a retest per se" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [229s-241s])
- **Break-and-retest gives leeway because you wait for retest; playing the open requires entering extremely quickly** "with the break and retests, it gives you a little bit of leeway because you have to, you know, wait for that retest to happen, right? And even with the, the how to play the open, you stuff the way for the candle close" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [251s-262s])
- **Still wait for candle close on any strategy** "with this whole strategy, any strategy I trade, I still wait for the candle close" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [262s-267s])
- **Break-and-retest: wait for trend to confirm intraday before entering** "with break and retest, we are waiting for the trend to confirm... you can't enter into a trade one to two minutes in, because you need to know which way the trend is in today" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [346s-357s])
- **Break-and-retest: enter after trend is clear on the day** "we enter after the trend is clear on the day" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [362s-365s])
- **Break-and-retest: risk level is break of key level or break of trend/architecture** "for break and retest, your risk level is always the break of key level or a break of trend or architecture" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [421s-426s])
- **Break-and-retest: can risk off previously formed intraday levels** "because we have that trend that's already developed on the chart, we have that higher low, or we have that higher high. So we can always risk off of those levels" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [442s-457s])
- **Break-and-retest: stop loss at break of structure** "you have your stop loss at a break of structure for a reason for the break in retests" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [536s-540s])
- **Profit targets same as break-and-retest: high of day, low of day, key levels, pattern higher low** "for our profit targets, basically the same as the break and retes rate, high of day low of day key levels, and then the pattern higher low" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [611s-621s])

- **Profit targets: high of day, low of day, key levels** "high of day low of day and key levels are really what I'm looking at when I'm trading those higher timeframes" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [627s-633s])
- **Pre-market low as key level for entries** "I would mark it out the pre-market low level" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1012s-1013s])
