## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Playing the open: enter 1-5 minutes into the open** "we entered into this trade about 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 minutes into the open" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1238s-1241s])
- **70% of trade worked out before 10:30 AM** "Most of the trade, I'd say 70% of the trade worked out right before 1030" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1244s-1245s])
- **Massive volatility in first five minutes** "there's so much volatility in the first five minutes" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [570s-573s])
- **Playing the open strategy is mostly for entering into the day; thesis validated or invalidated within 30 minutes** "Later on in the day, and by later on, I mean, you know, let's say half an hour in, right, your thesis would either be validated or invalidated already" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2029s-2041s])
- **Can trade playing the open after first five minutes too** "we can trade after, right? It doesn't have to be the first five minutes" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [591s-595s])
- **Never use limit orders before market open** "Would you ever have a limit order in place? No, never, never, never, never. I want to make that very clear, never. I would never have a limit order in place before the market opens" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2055s-2065s])

## 7. Exits
- **Scaling is essential to hold to full profit target** "the reason we scale is once again, so we can hold it to that full profit target, right? Because if I didn't scale in and out of this trade, I simply wouldn't be able to hold it to that profit target" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1156s-1162s])
- **Scales at key levels help you sit through consolidation** "Notice how we do have to sit through a little bit of consolidation here. And this is exactly why you want to take those scales. It's very, very important that you take those scales. So you can sit through this kind of consolidation period" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1693s-1701s])
- **Can use profits from scales to add to position** "you can, and there's examples in the trader abuse... sometimes I'll take a low day scale, and then I'll enter in more of my position either like a lower lower lower, lower higher, whichever way the stocks actually are trending at that time" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1870s-1890s])
- **Adding to position depends on market strength** "That really depends on how strong or weak the market is and if we're in a training market" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1891s-1896s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **Use Heikin Ashi (HA) candles on higher timeframes to filter noise and identify patterns** "I like to use the high canashi candlesticks... Takes the average price of the stock's candle... To create candlesticks and filter out market noise" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [93s-107s])
- **HA candles are NOT for drawing key levels or trading on** "It is not to draw key levels. It is not to, you know, trade on the high canashi, but it's simply to identify patterns" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [214s-219s])
- **Look at 4-hour, 1-hour, and daily timeframe for patterns** "I'm looking at the four hour, I'm looking at the one hour and I'm looking at the daily timeframe" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [180s-183s])
- **Higher the timeframe, the better the move** "the higher the timeframe, the better the move is going to be" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [857s-859s])
- **Playing the open: enter before clear intraday trend forms because HTF trend is already clear** "playing the open, you enter before that clear trend in today is made... We have a clear trend on the higher time frame. Therefore, we do not have to wait for an intraday trend to form" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [381s-393s])
- **HTF thesis gives highest risk-to-reward entering on key level** "it gives us the highest risk to reward to enter in based off of our higher time frame, thesis on a key level" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [400s-407s])
- **Playing the open: risk is break of another key level or HTF pattern** "your risk will be a break of another key level or a higher time frame pattern" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [431s-435s])
- **Most common risk for playing the open: risk off the candle itself** "what I like to do most of the time is to just risk off the candle, right? Risk off the higher low of the candle that I'm entering and on" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [481s-488s])
- **Playing the open: need to give trade room to breathe due to volatility** "you need to give the trade room to breathe. This is very important... there will be volatility in the first five minutes, and you need to give a little bit more time for the trade to develop or the trade to breathe" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [508s-584s])
- **Check HA candles every morning as routine** "every morning, basically when I wake up, I go flip through the HHA candles just to see if there's any pattern... it takes like 10 seconds" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1919s-1938s])
- **Personal preference: flag patterns on higher timeframe** "the one I personally like the most is flags, okay, I personally like flag patterns the most on the higher time frame. That's what I'm really really good at trading" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1956s-1965s])
- **Any pattern works on HTF, but flags are preferred** "Any pattern theoretically works with the, you know, the higher time frame, though, because there's more emphasis on it. But for me, I like trading the flag pattern" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1965s-1975s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **Don't have bias going into day, but have thesis from HTF** "I don't like having a bias going into the day, right? However, seeing these higher timeframe patterns will help you understand to put more emphasis on specific stocks" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [42s-50s])
- **Thesis is fine; bias clouds judgment** "the thesis is fine to have, right, the thesis is definitely fine to have, but having a bias really helps, a bias isn't good going into the day, right, because it judges a, it clouds your judgment" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1851s-1861s])
- **Edge comes from higher timeframe + market context** "the edge for this really comes from the higher time frame, and you do need some market context within the trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [642s-651s])
- **Ensure market aligns with thesis** "you want to ensure that the market also aligns with your thesis" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [655s-657s])
- **If everything is ripping opposite to your thesis, step back** "if everything is ripping right into open, right? You want to step back and really, you know, ensure that you're not rushing into that trade, right? You're not forcing that trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [679s-686s])
- **If market is normal/no thesis and stock has HTF pattern, can be more aggressive** "let's say Qs is just having a normal day... there's no real thesis, everything else is having a normal day and Tesla has a bear flag. Those days, you'll see that, you know, you can be a little bit more aggressive without that market context and Tesla will still, you know, work out within your probability" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [696s-710s])
- **If market is sideways, no reason to enter** "if the market is moving sideways, right, then of course, there's no reason to enter the trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2019s-2024s])
- **Always trade price action presented, but put more emphasis on tickers with better HTF patterns** "I will always trade the price action that's presented to me, but I will have a little bit more emphasis on different tickers if they have a better higher timeframe pattern" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1773s-1779s])

# EXTRACTION: mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline + mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **A+ entry is when candle actually breaks level, tries to reclaim, and fails** "the A plus entry and A plus entry like you guys have seen we talk about before is when the candle actually break your level tries to see and the reason this is this is an A plus entry by the way is because if a candle actually breaks the level and still can break and comes back down it shows that there's too many sellers for the buyers to actually push it up rather than when it's just consolidating underneath that level" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [189s-215s])

- **Break of market structure on QQQ + retest on QQQ + retest on stock = low risk entry** "we break below that 292 44 level right showing that we broke to the downside on QQ we come back and retest the 292 26 which is a previous pivot low on QQ and what do we do on Tesla we retest VWAP plus the 201 80 which is a low on Tesla okay with a very very very nice inverted hammer candle okay so you can enter into Tesla here looking for low of day" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [267s-303s])

- **Do NOT enter on break of bear flag — wait for retest** "you still wait for that retest setup right we're looking at a plus retest setups right the bear flag is just to give you a little bit more conviction on which way the stock is going to trend too because you do have that bear flag in a break of the bear flag when indicate that it does break to the downside right but that does not mean uh to enter on the break of the bear flag because there's no retest happening" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1089s-1119s])

- **Preferred entry candles: hammer, inverted hammer, shooting star** "inverted hammer candles or regular hammer candles or shooting star candle those are just some of the like the best trades that I have come from those specific candles" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1014s-1026s])

- **But it's about context and price action, not just the candle shape** "it's all about context really an understanding price action right it's not so much about the hammer stick uh candle but that's just something that I prefer on the woman to chart I prefer if it's uh if it's a strong hammer candle or inverted hammer shooting star" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1048s-1062s])

- **Long wick on hammer is fine if body closes near key level** "if the candlestick wick wicks up and the body candle closes like down here right based off of that context I wouldn't enter so I would say that that has a little bit more to do with context" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1215s-1224s])

- **Scale out when QQQ hits target (e.g., low of day)** "the way I would scale on this is basically when QQ hits a low of day that's where I would scale on Tesla as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [349s-356s])

- **Stop loss only changes when market structure changes** "our stop loss doesn't change a lot of people change their stop loss our stop loss doesn't change you guys have seen this with me as well I only change stop loss when we change market structure" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [902s-914s])

- **Even A+ setups don't always hit full profit target** "sometimes even A plus setups don't work out in full set in that you know the full technically profit target you know where you can let those trailers rad it doesn't work out like that sometimes but this is still a really good trade for the risk to reward" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [397s-414s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **Pre-market levels are used when gapping up overnight** "when we're gapping up overnight we understand that we're playing pre-market levels" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [441s-448s])

- **Key level + fair value gap gives more conviction** "having that fair value gap plus a key level we just give you a little bit more conviction right that's all really really is" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1150s-1155s])

- **Key level is more precise than fair value gap** "for me a key level is a little bit more precise right the fair value gap is in as precise as a key level but you could technically have both of those kind of aspects playing in this trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1341s-1351s])

- **400 level on SPY is a key level used almost every day** "400 sake level like we know on spy gives us a really good risk to reward just because that's that level has been used as resistance and support on spy so much in today if you guys go look back at the charge rate has been a level that has been used almost every other day essentially when we're near in this range" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [869s-885s])

- **Cut rest of position when market structure breaks on QQQ** "you'd cut it when it broke below that 295 5445 for the rest of the trailers at about 1035 okay so about this candle right here that's where the market's special broke on TOT on QQ and that's where you can cut the rest of your trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [661s-674s])

- **Stop loss only changes when market structure changes** "our stop loss doesn't change a lot of people change their stop loss our stop loss doesn't change you guys have seen this with me as well I only change stop loss when we change market structure" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [902s-914s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **Only trade A+ or A trades — nothing below** "for me I'm not trading anything unless uh basically it's an A plus or A trade I'm taking anything below that I'm not even taking" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1293s-1297s])

- **B, C, D trades are not even in your setup** "B or C D trades like you don't even want to know about those right because those aren't even in your setup at that point right those aren't even in your setup" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1283s-1293s])

- **A+ setup requires: relative strength/weakness, strong momentum, QQQ context, proper candlestick, key level** "this was an A plus trade with the candlesticks with the relatives strength with QQ forming a pattern as well above the wall upon the chart that's actually trading and then the higher time frame profit targets" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [709s-723s])

- **Relative weakness into open is important for A+ short setup** "The number one thing that we see on Tesla is we have relative weakness right into the open. Now this is very important for an A plus setup is that this stock your trading has really strong momentum because as you know where momentum traders and we need that strong momentum" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [89s-106s])

- **If you don't understand the setup, don't trade — just watch** "if it's option to that you don't understand completely. Don't trade just watch right just watch those live sessions again right rewatch the live sessions right and don't trade just watch until you understand and can create that a plus setup" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [1196s-1212s])

- **If you understand but are scared to lose money, you need to start executing** "if you do understand and you're simply just scared to lose money right and you're seeing these a plus setup Just go by you need to start executing you need to start executing and following your rules" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [1219s-1228s])

- **Rating trades: depends on check marks — below VWAP, key level, candlestick, QQQ context** "it depends right it depends how many check marks you have right is there it is a below VWOP is hitting a key level well how was the price at how was the candlestick um you know what was Q's doing at the time to have realistic strength and weakness right it's all those things that uh that that's how you read the trade from A to A to D right" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1259s-1278s])

- **No personal rating system — just knows what A+ is** "I don't really have a personal rating I just know what my A plus trade is and then I can tell you what like A A minus is great but like B or C D trades like you don't even want to know about those" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1278s-1287s])

## SPECIAL AUDIT QUESTIONS

### A/A+ Grading
- **A+ entry: candle breaks level, tries to reclaim, fails** "the A plus entry... is when the candle actually break your level tries to see... if a candle actually breaks the level and still can break and comes back down it shows that there's too many sellers for the buyers to actually push it up" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [189s-215s])
- **A+ requires: relative strength/weakness, strong momentum, QQQ context, proper candlestick, key level, HTF profit targets** "this was an A plus trade with the candlesticks with the relatives strength with QQ forming a pattern as well above the wall upon the chart that's actually trading and then the higher time frame profit targets" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [709s-723s])
- **Only trade A+ or A — nothing below** "for me I'm not trading anything unless uh basically it's an A plus or A trade I'm taking anything below that I'm not even taking" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1293s-1297s])
- **No formal rating system — just knows A+ vs everything else** "I don't really have a personal rating I just know what my A plus trade is and then I can tell you what like A A minus is great but like B or C D trades like you don't even want to know about those" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1278s-1287s])

### QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation
- **Use QQQ to confirm break of market structure for stock entries** "we break below that 292 44 level right showing that we broke to the downside on QQ we come back and retest the 292 26 which is a previous pivot low on QQ and what do we do on Tesla we retest VWAP plus the 201 80... with a very very very nice inverted hammer candle" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [267s-295s])
- **Scale out when QQQ hits target** "the way I would scale on this is basically when QQ hits a low of day that's where I would scale on Tesla as well" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [349s-356s])
- **Cut rest of trade when market structure breaks on QQQ** "you'd cut it when it broke below that 295 5445 for the rest of the trailers at about 1035... that's where the market's special broke on TOT on QQ and that's where you can cut the rest of your trade" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [661s-674s])
- **QQQ stop loss can be used as reference for stock stop** "what we can use for QQ... as a stop loss is this break of this Lover rate here 292 65 rate looking for low of day on QQ okay and then we can play that same scenario on Tesla" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [332s-349s])
- **Relative weakness on stock + QQQ breaking structure = A+ short** "we see Q's dropping below making a new low of day here and then coming right back up to high of day where on Tesla we see that it drops and then it slowly just kind of fades here right" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [111s-126s])
- **QQQ holding key level = stock can push to HTF targets** "if Q's essentially breaks underneath this 29457 which it does look like chop right now... if it basically just held that 29457 that we had pre-market we understand that Q's is going to be strong enough for Tesla to push right the Tesla for Tesla to push to the higher time frame levels" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [559s-580s])

### Displacement/FVG
- **Fair value gap: impulsive break between candle close and next candle open** "the fair value gap would come from the close of or the bottom of this green candle to the top of this red candle right here this would all be within a fair value gap" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1336s-1346s])
- **FVG is not as precise as a key level** "for me a key level is a little bit more precise right the fair value gap is in as precise as a key level" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1341s-1351s])
- **FVG is not required — just a concept to understand** "fair value gap for me isn't something that I'm like you know it has to be there for me to trade it's just some a concept that I understand" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1356s-1361s])
- **FVG + key level = more conviction** "having that fair value gap plus a key level we just give you a little bit more conviction right that's all really really is" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_Part_2.txt [1150s-1155s])

## DISCIPLINE (Methodology — from Lesson 8 Part 1)

- **Three types of discipline: preventive, supportive, corrective** "preventive discipline is establishing expectations, guidelines and behavior... supportive... occurs after a mistake has occurred a warning maybe in place... corrective discipline is if you fail to change behavior after supportive discipline, this is when corrective discipline must be used this has consequences" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [276s-441s])

- **Preventive discipline = creating rules** "preventive discipline is establishing expectations, guidelines and behaviors... this is for your rules, right? So in trading, this is when you create those rules" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [302s-311s])

- **Supportive discipline = warning after first mistake** "this would be a yellow card, right? This would be a warning. Don't do this again. If you do this again, you're okay" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [385s-415s])

- **Corrective discipline = consequence after repeated failure** "for me, corrective discipline is... every single time that I would, I wouldn't, I wouldn't follow my rules after supported discipline, I would have to run five kilometers every single time no matter if it was raining outside" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [501s-526s])

- **You want to fear the consequences of your actions** "I would simply hate that stop because I have that discipline because I fear the consequence of my actions. Right? You want to fear the consequences of your actions" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [583s-588s])

- **Five steps to create discipline: (1) write down 1-year goal, (2) understand strengths, (3) understand weaknesses, (4) focus on fixing weaknesses, (5) start small** "Number one is right down where you want to be in a year... number two understand your strengths... then that needs to understand his weaknesses... number four he needs to focus on fixing the things that he struggles with his weaknesses... number five start with small size" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [601s-823s])

- **Once you fall off, it's very hard to get back on — so start small for consistency** "once you fall off it's very very hard to get back on... that's why it's super crucial that you do start with that smaller size right to actually have that consistency" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [961s-988s])

- **Making rules and following them is the only thing differentiating you from a gambler** "making those rules and sticking to them is the only thing making those rules and following them is the only thing that is differentiating you from a gambler" (mastermind-1-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [1013s-1042s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Valid break definition - displacement]** "displacement simply means after a key level the move which you get after a key level so the break right and this break is a very very nice break indicating to us that the trend is indeed to the downside okay because we broke and held on multiple candles here right" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [602s-626s])

- **[Valid retest - weak price action candle]** "right here what are we getting this is a great retest why is this a great retest can anyone explain why this is a good retest... Inverted hammer candle right week price action off of our key level low of day right always a high probability trade when it's a low of day level right low of day retest inverted hammer very weak handle" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [627s-674s])

- **[Stop placement - break of previous structure]** "for this example here we would put our stop loss at the break of this previous level okay this comes in at 238 75s right because we can see that we tried to hold this level right here the low at 237 74's right we can see strong buying action kind of occurring here... if we break back above this the likely to the stop coming back down is not likely right so that's where you would put your stop loss within this scenario" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [723s-772s])

- **[Stop placement - break of consolidation/flat top]** "I would put my stop loss at the break of the previous low right and that low right we got put in right here and this consolidation right so this two 40 87 level right why would I put it here? What the reason I would put it here is because we can see right here flat top break above the flat top consolidate before that high of they move consolidate here right so what does this show us right you have to think about the context here guys right this shows us that buyers try to step in... buyers try to step in and ensure that the stock was above this previous flat top for that move up right we can also see you know a flat top we can kind of see this consolidation for that move up as well right therefore buyers are going to be trying their hardest to defend this level at 240 87" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1341s-1396s])

- **[Targets - HOD/LOD and psychological numbers]** "where would your profit target be you can have it a high of day you can also have it at whole psychological numbers as it's kind of going up so 244's right 245 246" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1399s-1417s])

- **[A+ break and retest - continuing trend]** "a break above this level right the previous high of day right a retest of high of day and then we can continue that move to the upside right that would be an A plus break and retest that up for me right and that's simply because it's continuing that trend to the upside" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1090s-1106s])

- **[Don't trade break and retest within chop/range]** "the reason you don't have high probability traits for break and retest setups is because you're not trading within a high probability environment you're not trading on new highs you're not trading on new lows you're not letting the trend develop you're trading within this chop right taking a retest within this chop is not something you want to do" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [442s-477s])

- **[Noise vs. clear break]** "This is where most traders fail right because they're gonna be trying to look for break and retest within here when this is something you definitely should not do right this is noise. This is where you're gonna get chopped. You want them momentum to occur you want to see a clear trend" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1218s-1241s])

- **[Clear break vs. fakeout]** "There we go here's a break right we see another candle close above as well. This now is a clear break right this is a clear break right this isn't any liquidity break out or liquidity fake. This is a clear break right" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1242s-1265s])

- **[Entry trigger - hammer candle]** "within here right what do we see very strong hammer candle very strong by very strong hammer candle very strong. Bye, okay, both show you a very very clear side of by" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1274s-1287s])

- **[Not a retest - no displacement]** "Why didn't you not take really right here. This isn't a retest. This isn't a retest at all. There's no displacement. This isn't a retest at all. This is just consolidation to the downside" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2337s-2348s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[HOD/LOD - only trade after break]** "someone asked me today within live trading when I would take a trade and I explained to them I would only take a trade today if we broke the high or low of today which we didn't do when I was training until 1030 okay" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [398s-416s])

- **[Levels are zones, not ticks]** "When I'm looking at these key levels it's not that exact level right it's a little bit of a zone right like most people think it's like it's to the tick right and sometimes it is to the tick right because my levels are pretty accurate. However, let's say this this kind of level here right you want to kind of like it's like you know it's like a small little zone right it's not the complete level right" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2282s-2303s])

- **[Pre-market high/low vs. previous day levels - context dependent]** "Depends on the key levels I draw right sometimes those pre-market high and pre-market lows for example pre-market high and pre-market low the market gaps up overnight. I'm gonna be using pre-market high pre-market low levels right however if we open up in a range and I already have previous day levels right maybe those pre-market levels aren't as important because more liquidity is sitting within those actual key levels" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2503s-2522s])

- **[VWAP usage - only with key level nearby]** "VWAP is only used for me if we have a key level near that level as well. So if VWAP is there and then we also have a key level near that level. Bastion we time amusing VWAP mostly VWAP is a more of an indicator for above VWAP calls if we're below VWAP puts" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2616s-2629s])

- **[Fresh chart every day - no previous day levels saved]** "every time I draw Q levels it's a brand new chart brand new chart every single day. I don't have previous day levels. I don't I don't have previous levels that I've used ever. I always start with a brand new fresh chart every night" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2433s-2441s])

## 11. QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation

- **[QQQ as reference, not traded]** "I don't trade Q Q by the way. I want to make that very clear. I only use Q as a as a kind of like a reference point right because it's it's a really really good reference" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1944s-1951s])

- **[QQQ is the edge for individual tickers]** "When you're looking at the overall when you're looking at the individual ticker my edge like we talked about in the previous video comes from QQ. Understanding what's going on with QQ right" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1721s-1730s])

- **[QQQ context saves bad setups]** "Here's what's going to save you and what saves me all the time and that's adding in QQQ right adding in QQQQ to your examples" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1623s-1636s])

- **[QQQ strength prevents downside trade on weak stock]** "The reason you wouldn't take this trade is because... you see that there's a very strong push on the markets right and therefore if we look at Tesla right it kind of chops around... as Q's is kind of pushing here look at what Tesla does it just chops around right you never kind of reach that low of that low of day profit target" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1819s-1860s])

- **[Need QQQ weakness to confirm short on weak stock]** "I need what now I what I need to see now is Q's show me weakness as well because if Q shows me weakness NVIDIA is the best ticker to play for relative weakness" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2067s-2079s])

- **[QQQ retest of opening print confirms entry on individual stock]** "look at what Q's is doing this is why I would enter this trade look at what Q's does. Look at the opening print this is opening print Q's retesting opening print. Right do you see how Q's is retesting that opening print what a good candle for that retest of the opening print. Right below VWP we broke structure to the downside very impulsive move to the downside right very strong move first retest is opening print. Retest with a weak price action candle right and so if Q's does potentially keep moving down here right because we're not trading Q's the retest is really nice on Q's yes, but we're not trading Q's but if Q's keeps moving down here NVIDIA is a great entry here" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2184s-2234s])

- **[Four tickers up - QQQ, Tesla, Apple, SPY]** "I have four tickers up right I'm looking at the overall markets if spy does show weakness compared to the overall markets right I'll trade that" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2770s-2780s])

- **[Gauge stock to play by relative strength/weakness vs QQQ]** "That's how I gauge what stock to play right because we have Q Q Q. We have Tesla. We have Spy up. We have Apple up. That's how I gauge what stock to play. It's whichever has relative weakness and strength compared to Q's" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2100s-2112s])

## 12. Trend Structure / Market Structure

- **[Basic uptrend definition]** "an up trend right what does an up trend consist of a consists of the opening price high right a higher low higher high higher low right very very simple concept" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [56s-78s])

- **[Basic downtrend definition]** "vice versa for the downside right all you want is higher lower lows and lower highs right so for the downside you just want lower lows and lower highs" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [86s-109s])

- **[Trend still intact even without breaking previous high]** "we could have you know a high we have a higher low right we could get the next you know pivot point as a lower high right and then the next pivot point can be a higher low however this trend structure is till to the upside right even if we didn't break that previous high. The trend structure is still to the upside until we break this low" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [125s-169s])

- **[Fakeout liquidity - sellers in control after double rejection]** "we did break right now that we did break this fake out to the upside right the sellers are much much more in control. Okay, sellers are much much more in control than buyers because buyers did try to come up and break out of this level not once but twice and rejected both times so therefore sellers are more in control you have to understand this context when you're trading" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [565s-586s])

- **[Fakeout liquidity - everyone sees the same stop level]** "everyone else on the chart saw the same thing right everyone saw that this is where you know your stop should be for the continuation to the upside. So what did they do they're gonna fake you out and they're gonna move the stock right back up" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1153s-1165s])

- **[Structure break defined by previous pivot low]** "we break structure if we break 238 89... because this is the pivot low that we've made therefore if the stock comes back the low where we see aggressive buying you guys need to understand where aggressive buying occurs. This is where aggressive buying came in right and because of this this is where the structure would break. It would be the previous pivot level" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1045s-1089s])

## 13. Options / Contract Selection

- **[First out of the money]** "I always trade the first out of the money first out of the money. So for example if we're looking at a video here in this example right and it's 486 I'd be trading the 485 puts" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2445s-2456s])

- **[Week out recommended for SPY]** "it is one out of the money it is if it's spies sometimes I'll do the same day, but I recommend the week out" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2791s-2797s])

- **[Same strike when scaling in]** "If I'm scaling into trades in and out of yeah, I'm buying the same contract so let's say I for example let's say um it would right here. It was a 486 right on mine 485s. Let's say we retest again here at 482 37 then it's a really good retest for me to enter into I'm still buying the exact same ones same contracts" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2912s-2928s])

## 14. Tools / Indicators

- **[No level 2, no book, no footprint]** "Do you look at level two no I don't look at level two. I don't look at book. I don't look at footprint all right I only look at the actual price action that's happening on the charts" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2691s-2698s])

## 15. Wick vs. Body at Key Levels

- **[Prefer wick above level - shows sellers stronger]** "I personally like it when you see these strong wicks right so when you see like even on the video here when you see this wick right when you see this kind of move up and then you see this wick I like when it actually does wick above the level because that shows that seller you know we did try to actually test that level and you know sellers were stronger than buyers" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2808s-2824s])

- **[Sometimes stock doesn't reach level - market dependent]** "sometimes if it the stock is really has a lot of relative strength which you guys might see on Tesla Tesla back in the day you just have a lot of relative strength and weakness where if Tesla you know let's say this was a retest level right let's say the retest level was like right here or something Tesla would sometimes only come to here and make the move down right and that's just different markets" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2824s-2847s])

- **[Current market - wait for close below key level]** "in the market that we're in currently right I'm always waiting for you know that that close but below that key level" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2847s-2855s])

# EXTRACTION: mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[HTF break-and-retest as entry without LTF confirmation]** "we see that we had this bottom on the video... we consolidated it and then we broke below that bottom... And now we're coming back, pre-market, and we're retesting that low... this means this is a simple break and retest on the higher time frame that we could enter into... Based off of the key level, without having to wait for the break and retest on the lower time frame, because we understand it's breaking retest on the higher time frame" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [792s-826s])
- **[Aggressive entry on HTF pattern + opening dip]** "we could enter in on the potential of this trade right here because the higher time frame and the very nice opening dip" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [276s-282s])
- **[Retest of wedge pattern - bearish candle + rejection]** "we actually reject that wedge pattern right to the downside... still a bearish candle, still a retest, and a really, really good retest for that move to the downside" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [639s-661s])
- **[Stop loss on HTF break-and-retest - above previous high]** "our stop loss in this specific scenario would simply be just a breakup of, right, this previous 20902 level, or just a break a little bit above this previous high that we made right here" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [701s-714s])
- **[Stop loss on pre-market low break - close above pre-market low]** "your stop loss here, would be a close, simply above this level right here now... With that 9.31 candle can be your stop... because if we do break back above this level a pre-market low, right, we could see some consolidation before we see another move down" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [318s-340s])
- **[Stop loss on bear flag entry - break of day high]** "Our stop loss here would have to be a break of that high, right? So a break of the actual days high, because until we break that, we still have that bearish sentiment on the stock" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [886s-894s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Pre-market high/low as primary levels for open]** "I would mark out the pre-market low level, which is right here... I would simply wait for the stock to come back into this level right here" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [253s-261s])
- **[Pre-market high/low as confirmation levels for wedge break]** "I would mark out the pre-market low here, and then I would mark out the pre-market high, right? And Tesla would have to break either of these levels to show some strength or weakness within the stock" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [547s-554s])
- **[Previous day levels as profit targets]** "we can have profit targets here at previous day levels as well, right? So, and then previous day levels right there as well, right? So 434,09, and then 433,88" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [850s-860s])
- **[Key levels from previous month's data - not further unless breakout]** "I'm only taking the net, like, you know, previous two weeks of data or anything like that, maybe a month... I usually take, you know, the previous month's data, right, unless we're gapping up or down... when we're still in this range, right, I really don't care, right? I really don't care, and I'm only taking, you know, the previous month's data outmost" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1884s-1924s])
- **[Levels drawn at wicks of HA candles - same height as bar candles]** "if I'm usually putting them at the wicks of the HA candles, right? And that kind of helps me not have to move them when I'm actually using the actual candles six as well... because if you put them at the wick, for me I've just noticed, it's the exact same height" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1245s-1263s])
- **[Opening in range - no extra levels needed]** "when we open in between a range, I don't have to draw anymore key levels. I don't have to draw anything because we're opening in between a range" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1732s-1742s])
- **[Gap up - only draw pre-market high as extra level]** "If we gapped up, right, pre-mark it, we opened above 19598, I would probably draw the pre-mark a high, right? That's the only level that I draw extra" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1747s-1756s])
- **[Levels should "bounce out at you" - simple identification]** "you have the key level should almost, you know, bounce out at you, right? Like, you should look at the chart and be like, oh, this is a level, this is a level, this is a level... It should be very, very simple" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1564s-1576s])
- **[Give levels room - not to the exact wick]** "you got what you want to give it a little bit more room, right? It's not to the wick" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1606s-1613s])
- **[Daily charting process - mark daily high/low, then swing high/low]** "the next thing that I'm going to mark out is the daily low, right, that we made yesterday... And then the high... We have the high, we have the low, we have the low swing and we have the high swing" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1477s-1492s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Enter within first 5 minutes of open]** "we entered into this trade about one, two, three, four, five minutes into the open" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [481s-482s])
- **[70% of trade works before 10:30]** "Most of the trade, I'd say 70% of the trade worked out right before 1030, and then you did have to wait for the final scales" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [485s-491s])
- **[Pre-market preparation - thesis before live trading]** "I always talk about what my thesis is going into the date... during pre-market, before live trading" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [996s-1001s])
- **[Night-before preparation - look for patterns, set levels]** "I'll look for a pattern on the night before... the only thing that I'm going to be doing is going back on my charts and looking at any patterns, you're just setting up those key levels for tomorrow. So by the time I wake up tomorrow... I already have those levels and kind of know what's going to go happen on the charts" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1190s-1213s])

## 7. Exits / Position Management

- **[Scaling at key levels to hold to full target]** "we would scale F420s here as well, and then we would probably have one more scale out around 420s... and then we would hit our full profit target" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [351s-361s])
- **[Scaling enables holding through consolidation]** "the reason we scale is once again, so we can hold it to that full profit target, right? Because if I didn't scale in and out of this trade, I simply wouldn't be able to hold it to that profit target" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [397s-403s])
- **[Scales at round numbers / key levels]** "420, 74's, 420 key level scale, right? You could have a 419 scale because that's another cycle level" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [459s-466s])
- **[Scaling essential to sit through consolidation]** "Notice how we do have to sit through a little bit of consolidation here. And this is exactly why you want to take those scales. It's very, very important that you take those scales. So you can sit through this kind of consolidation period" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [934s-942s])
- **[Profit targets from HTF structure]** "our profit targets here were this structure form, right? So this structure formed based off of these candles right here. So that's where our profit target would be" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [290s-297s])
- **[Closer profit target + HTF target for scaling]** "my profit target would be a little bit closer as well just so I can get some scales out and we can have that previous day level there as well. For another profit target, so we can actually hold for this complete move" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [303s-311s])
- **[Scale on new low of day - fake wick]** "There we go, we made a new low of day, right? You can scale on that low of day, that wick was fake" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [722s-726s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[HTF = 1 hour and 4 hour for patterns]** "higher time frame would be one hour and four hour, by the way, that's what I'm usually looking at patterns for, right? I'm looking at the one hour time frame and the four hour time frame" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1156s-1164s])
- **[Daily for key levels, 4H/1H for patterns]** "I'm looking at the daily as well, right? And the daily is just for, you know, any key levels that I'm going to draw out, but the four hour and one hour, mostly what I'm looking at for higher time frame breaks your patterns" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1166s-1177s])
- **[HA candles on HTF for pattern identification]** "I only look at HA candles on the higher time frames. So one hour, four hour" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1153s-1159s])
- **[HA candles block noise - makes patterns textbook]** "the HA candles block out enough noise that it makes it seem like it's a really, really high quality pattern... And it makes it seem like it's textbook, right? It's a little bit more easier to identify" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1137s-1146s])
- **[HTF pattern gives aggressive entries on LTF]** "we can take those aggressive entries because we understand the higher time frame aligns with our shorter time frame entries" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [223s-227s])
- **[HTF pattern = emphasis but still trade price action]** "if there's a very strong, high timeframe pattern and set up that's going on, I will talk about it and I will put a little bit more emphasis on that pattern. Once again, I will always trade the price action that's presented to me, but I will have a little bit more emphasis on different tickers if they have a better higher timeframe pattern" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1006s-1020s])
- **[HTF patterns happen often but not every day]** "these higher timeframe patterns happen all the time, not every single day, well they occur, but they do happen all the time" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1024s-1026s])
- **[Gap up/down - HTF pattern explains direction, trade retest of pre-market levels]** "if the stock gaps up or gaps down... The way we would trade that is we understand on the higher time frame, we did move break to the upside. So the trend for the day will be to the upside... I would simply be waiting for a retest of one of those levels, right? Looking at price action, once the candle closes, let's say at pre-market low it closes with a very strong hammer candle. I'll just enter in for calls, risking low of day or another key level, looking for higher day or pre-market high" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1286s-1315s])
- **[Daily patterns take very long to break - ignore until breakout]** "the higher timeframes, right? Like the very high, like the daily timeframes that, you know, you see these patterns on, the problem with that is they take a very, very long time to break... That is not until we, you know, actually break above that, I really don't care about them because they'll usually just chop right on the daily" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1848s-1868s])
- **[Post-market doesn't matter]** "this post-market matter, no, post-market honestly doesn't matter too much" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1873s-1879s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[No bias going into day - but emphasis on HTF pattern]** "I don't like to have a bias going into the day, but if there's a very strong, high timeframe pattern and set up that's going on, I will talk about it and I will put a little bit more emphasis on that pattern" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1003s-1011s])
- **[Need break of pre-market high/low for confirmation on wedge]** "Tesla would have to break either of these levels to show some strength or weakness within the stock" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [552s-554s])
- **[No trade until confirmation - below pre-market low but wedge holding]** "this wouldn't give me enough confirmation to enter the reason this is because we held that wedge, yes, but we are still below that pre-market low level... there is no trade yet, right? I want to make that very clear, there is no trade yet" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [582s-599s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Example trade: 9.17 R trade]** "this complete trade would result in about 9.17 trade" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [315s-318s])
- **[Example trade: 8.23 R trade]** "this would be at 8.23 restore trade" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [348s-351s])
- **[Example trade: 4.35 R trade]** "this would be a 4.35 risk to our word trade" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [717s-719s])
- **[Example trade: 2.0 R trade]** "this would be about a 2.0 risk to a war trade" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [904s-907s])

- **[Market context important but not shown in examples]** "this is not to mention we're not seeing the background of what's going on with QQQ, right? Or market context... So it's really important to have that market context in mind as well" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [946s-978s])

## 12. Trend Structure / Market Structure

- **[Bear flag on HTF - strong move then consolidation]** "we have a very, very strong move and then the bare flag forming" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [191s-193s])
- **[Wedge pattern on HTF - can break either direction]** "the wedge could break in either direction, and therefore, for us, what we would be waiting for to play the open or the higher time frame is a break of that wedge pattern" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [520s-528s])
- **[Flat top on HA candles = resistance]** "we see it's almost like a flat top, right? Some people could also, you know, depending on, you know, you can also put that flag pattern, right? But I like to flat top a little bit better" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1529s-1536s])
- **[HA candle color changes = trend changes within range = chop]** "when you see a little bit more green and red candles, because you understand that every time it changes from green to red, that means usually it's actually changing trends. So if it changes trends, you know, once twice, three, four, five, six, seven, eight times within the same range, that usually means, you know, we're chopping around" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1372s-1385s])

## 13. Options / Contract Selection

- **[Trading one week out - Friday contracts]** "I am trading one week out, right? So today is currently what the deal 11th. So this Friday's contracts, right? That's what I'm trading. I'm trading one week out contracts. And I'm taking one out of the money" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1644s-1655s])
- **[SPY sometimes zero DTE - but recommend one week out]** "if I'm trading spies, sometimes I'll trade zero DTEs, but once again, for most traders, I recommend the one DTEs" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1655s-1661s])

## 14. Tools / Indicators

- **[HA candles only on higher time frames (1H, 4H)]** "I only look at HA candles on the higher time frames. So one hour, four hour" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1153s-1159s])
- **[HA candles for pattern identification, not key levels]** "I use them mostly for pattern identification" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1661s-1669s])
- **[HA candles help newer traders see textbook patterns]** "for the newer traders, right, I mentioned the HA candles because it's very, very easy to look at patterns... because they're looking for those textbook examples... that we don't get textbook patterns, especially in the real market... But the HA candles block out enough noise that it makes it seem like it's a really, really high quality pattern" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1116s-1141s])

## 15. Wick vs. Body at Key Levels

- **[Look at body candles when wick action isn't real]** "just look at the body candles, just because there is some wick action going on that isn't real" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [735s-739s])
- **[Fake wick on low of day - scale there]** "There we go, we made a new low of day, right? You can scale on that low of day, that wick was fake" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [722s-726s])

## 16. Charting / Level Drawing Process

- **[Daily timeframe first - mark daily high/low, swing high/low]** "the number one thing I look at is I go on the daily time frame... the first thing we see right is that chart that we've talked about right, so we can mark that out as red... the next thing that I'm going to mark out is the daily low, right, that we made yesterday... And then the high... We have the high, we have the low, we have the low swing and we have the high swing" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1431s-1492s])
- **[Four hour next - use HA candles]** "next four hour, four hour timeframe is next... on the four hour, we can go to the high canashi to help you guys kind of understand this concept a little bit more" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1496s-1504s])
- **[One hour - if nothing, move on]** "on the one hour timeframe, I really personally don't see anything" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1547s-1552s])
- **[One minute - previous day action only]** "the one minute, all I'm looking at is the previous day action" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1556s-1561s])
- **[Irrelevant data until breakout of range]** "this is irrelevant to me until we break out. Until we break out of this range, all of this is irrelevant" (mastermind-2-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1457s-1467s])

# EXTRACTION FROM mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Valid break requires solid close below, then retest]** "for us, what we need is a solid close below just like this, but then we also need that retest, because on that retest is where we actually see if it's their strength or weakness on the stock" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [664s-672s])
- **[Do not buy the breakout - wait for retest]** "this is exactly why we don't buy the break out" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [677s-680s])
- **[Liquidity fake out - sellers try to come in strong, then buyers return]** "this is called a liquidity fake out... sellers try to come in here strong. This is exactly why I also don't break out trade" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [635s-646s])
- **[After fake out, stop can be tighter - simple break back into zone]** "whichever way we break now, our stop loss can literally just be a simple break back into the zone" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [696s-702s])
- **[After fake out, zone between levels is fair price - no one in control]** "in between here is fair value or fair price. Now when there's a fair price on the stock, no one is in control. Therefore, whichever way we break and get a retest of, if we do come back into this zone... no one is in control" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [754s-767s])
- **[Preferred entry candle: inverted hammer / hammer]** "We get a very weak inverted hammer candle. You guys know this is the candle that I love personally, right? This is the candle that I trade 90% of the time" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [864s-869s])
- **[Inverted hammer signifies sellers came back and closed at low]** "what this candle indicates is sellers came back and they closed it below or at the low of the candle. Okay, and this is why it's such a strong... short side" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [910s-927s])
- **[Should wait for bearish candle retest if shorting]** "Should we wait for a bearish candle, retest if we are shorting? Yes, 100% yes" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [941s-947s])
- **[Stop loss placement - break of previous pivot high]** "my stop loss would just simply be a break of this previous pivot high" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1030s-1034s])
- **[Need to give trades room to breathe]** "you need to get because it could potentially have just a little quick fake out and then make a move to the downside. So you want to give it a little bit of room to breathe" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1016s-1023s])
- **[Profit target - pre-market low]** "my profit target personally, okay, would simply be pre-market low" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1084s-1088s])
- **[First break vs late breaks - can enter on strong candle after break]** "you could enter technically on this candle right here, right? Because it's such a strong uptrend. And then we come back and we test and we still close a green. You could, secondly, enter earlier here" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1892s-1901s])
- **[Need displacement for valid break - not just wick]** "this isn't, there's no displacement. There's no actual retest. We need the retest, okay? And this is exactly why I wait for the retest" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2207s-2215s])
- **[Impulsive candle breaking key level + fair value gap = sellers took over]** "this candle is literally one of the most bearish candles you can get trading. Right, we broke a key level, impulse of candle, made a fair value gap, technically, as well. And just a very impulse of candle to the downside, breaking both levels" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2255s-2271s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
- **[Opening candle high is significant - everyone looks at it]** "this high right here is the opening candle high. This is a very, very significant candle because everyone looks at the first opening candle to see if we can break above the opening candle" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [475s-481s])
- **[Bodies closing below opening candle high = traders using that level as high]** "because both bodies of these candles close below this, this means that most other traders are using this level as a high" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [485s-491s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Only draw significant levels that other traders see]** "you can have a level here, you can have a level here, who cares, no one else is looking at those levels. Those aren't significant levels other than to you. Okay, the significant levels to other traders are these levels that I'm showing you" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [506s-515s])
- **[Opening candle high is significant because everyone looks at it]** "this high right here is the opening candle high. This is a very, very significant candle because everyone looks at the first opening candle" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [475s-481s])
- **[Bodies closing below level = traders using that level]** "because both bodies of these candles close below this, this means that most other traders are using this level as a high" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [485s-491s])
- **[Psychological numbers matter - 183 level bounce example]** "why do you think there's random candle came here? This came here because it's a whole psychological number" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1199s-1211s])
- **[High of day, opening candle high, and low are key levels]** "Number one level, it would be high of day... the second level that I would draw is going to be this level right here... this was the first high of day level. And this is also the opening candle high... and then finally we would just draw this low right here" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1423s-1460s])
- **[Previous day levels = where draw on liquidity sits]** "this is the previous day, the previous day low and after breaking the previous high, the previous high here, right, previous high day, this is where technically the most liquidity is sitting" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1683s-1691s])
- **[Levels must stick out like a sore thumb]** "the previous day levels that I see and this is, it's got to kind of stick out like a sore thumb" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2456s-2458s])
- **[Level with more touches is preferred]** "You could have it either this level right here, or this level right here. This level just had more touches, so I kind of like this level a little bit more" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2484s-2489s])
