## 7. Exits / Position Management
- **[Scale in and out of positions]** "I scale in and out of my positions, right? Therefore, I could take some potentially off at 183 to lower my risk or at 18266 completely" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1100s-1108s])
- **[After first PT hit, can move stop to new pivot]** "you could technically start moving your stop, right? Because we do have this new pivot point, right? So your stop would be this new pivot point at this retest" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2572s-2580s])
- **[Scaling example - 3 contracts at different targets]** "you could technically enter three contracts, one here, one here, one here. This is a 3.69 risk-to-reward trade total. This would be a 2.23, and this would be a 0.63" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2506s-2511s])
- **[Uses mental stops, not hard stops]** "I personally don't use hard stops. I personally like to use mental stops. That's just how I trade" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1330s-1335s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[Higher timeframe flat top - if broken, reaching all-time highs]** "on the higher time frame, this 4 27 level... this was a flat top that we needed to break. And if we broke this, we were reaching all time highs and I knew Q was going to push" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3363s-3374s])
- **[HTF trend used to determine which stocks to avoid]** "Tesla today was on the downtrend. Okay. So I didn't want to trade Tesla today in the morning. I did, but later on in the day, I didn't want to trade Tesla because it was on the downtrend" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3403s-3423s])
- **[HTF downtrend + retest = only going to draw more]** "Tesla's down trending on the day, the higher time frame is down... if we retest this previous low right we know that Tesla's only going to draw more because the higher time frame on Tesla is still to the downside" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3782s-3805s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Do not trade chop - no real trend formed]** "This is all noise. This is not something you wanna trade it. This is where you're gonna... edge erosion or my win rate becoming lower, yeah, it's becoming lower because you're trading this. Why would you wanna trade this? There is nothing there to trade. There's no real trend being formed on the actual stock yet" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [579s-594s])
- **[Only like to trade trend, not chop]** "I only like to trade trend" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [318s])
- **[No trade if risk-to-reward isn't enough - buying too close to high of day]** "this is a no trade, right? Because the risk to reward just isn't enough for me personally... we're buying so close to high of day" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1743s-1769s])
- **[Must gauge risk-to-reward based on closest draw on liquidity]** "you have to also gauge your risk-to-reward based off of that level" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1870s-1872s])
- **[Even if candle is strong, if RR is bad, don't take trade]** "even if we did get a strong candle the risk-to-reward on this trade was huge" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1888s-1890s])
- **[Market context overrides good market structure - QQQ correlation]** "market trend is really good, but the market context is terrible. So you need to be able to combine both of those. That's what creates your edge" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3082s-3088s])
- **[Simple trades - downtrend, below VWAP, weak price action, key level retest]** "this is a very clear downtrend downtrend below the VWAP... price action price action key level key level and retest okay very very simple" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3835s-3840s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Example trade: 1.9 R trade]** "this is a 1.9 risk-to-war trade" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1125s-1126s])
- **[Average R:R is 2]** "my risk-to-war on average is two. 1.9 is very doable for me. Okay, 1.9 is doable for me. I'd say 1.85 is doable" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1137s-1145s])
- **[Example trade: 1.17 R - not taken]** "this is a 1.17R trade. Are we taking this trade? ... No trade" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1718s-1734s])
- **[Example trade: 1.55 R max possible]** "1.93 would be like the max level you could probably hit on this trade. And that would be what the 3.0 points on Tesla, which would still make it 1.55" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1754s-1760s])
- **[Example trade: 0.24 R - not worth it]** "That would only give us a 0.24 trade. And overall in the day, right, not the greatest trade" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1820s-1824s])
- **[Example trade: 3.69 R total with scaling]** "This is a 3.69-ristor word trade total. This would be a 2.23, and this would be a 0.63" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2508s-2511s])
- **[Example trade: 1.85 R - decent]** "this gives us 1.85 R trade decent, right? This 1.85 is a very decent trade to get it on" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2910s-2913s])

## 11. QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation
- **[QQQ is the market - individual stocks under QQQ]** "QQQ is the market, right? Apple is the individual stock. The market isn't in the downtrend... QQQ is in the downtrend. Apple is relatively strong on the day. However, because of how strong of a downtrend we're in, right? I'm not buying apple" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2997s-3010s])
- **[Need market to give signs of strength before buying relative strength]** "We need the market to give me at least a little bit of signs of strength. Right. So when I'm buying relative strength or weakness, I need the market to either give me the lowest risk possible entry. Or I need the markets to give me signs of strength or weakness a little bit" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3015s-3025s])
- **[Only trades tech stocks under QQQ or SPY]** "I only trade either tech stocks QQQ or SPY... I only trade personally... QQQ, Tesla, Apple, anything under the QQQ basically the QQQ built. Actual QQQ stock or SPY" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3124s-3152s])
- **[When trading individual stocks, correlate with QQQ]** "when you're trading individual stocks, the only thing you have to correlate that with is QQQ" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3195s-3200s])
- **[When trading QQQ/NQ, correlate with individual stocks in QQQ]** "to trade NQ or QQQ, you have to actually correlate that with the individual stocks" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3190s-3195s])
- **[QQQ range used to play relative strength of individual stocks]** "I'm trading the range of QQQ to play the relative strength of Nvidia" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3596s-3598s])
- **[QQQ at low of range + Nvidia retesting high of day = low risk trade on Nvidia]** "when Nvidia came back and retested its previous high of day... look at where Q's was Q's was at the low of the range... because of this, I could have entered this Nvidia trade because this was such a low risk trade" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3459s-3490s])
- **[Stop on Nvidia trade = if QQQ breaks range low]** "the second Q's breaks for 29 12's as a new low of day and closes that candle were done the trades done" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3514s-3524s])
- **[Both QQQ and stock downtrending + retesting = simple trade]** "both down trending both below VWAP. And both putting in weak price action candles as well right so you could even have waited for QQQ for example potentially to put a weak candle in" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3805s-3834s])

## 12. Trend Structure / Market Structure
- **[Higher lows and higher highs are only potential until confirmed]** "every higher low that you have on your chart is potential. It's not a concrete higher low until we actually develop a proper trend. And you only know that after the next higher low is made, okay, you do not know a higher low is made until after the next higher low is made" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [205s-220s])
- **[In real time, all structure is potential - not concrete]** "When you're actually trading in the real markets, it's all potential" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [235s-237s])
- **[Trading will always have discretionary elements]** "even though we want to make our trading as mechanical as possible, there will still be discretionary trading involved" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [126s-130s])
- **[Chop between levels = no trade zone]** "in between here, not too much, okay? So therefore we only have two levels" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [454s-457s])
- **[Wait for break of range to determine trend]** "The trend will form once buyers or sellers have decided we're either breaking to the upside or we're breaking to the downside" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [594s-600s])
- **[Sometimes you get stuck in consolidation no matter how good you are]** "no matter how good you are at reading market trend, market context, whatever. Sometimes you will still be stuck in this, right? There's literally sometimes no way to avoid this" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1793s-1801s])
- **[Stop loss in uptrend must be at recent low]** "market trend wise, our stop loss here has to be this previous higher low. It literally has to be, right? Because this is an uptrend" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1635s-1650s])

## 13. Options / Contract Selection
- **[Strike price always one out of the money, 99% of time]** "strike price of the trade that I'm taking, every single time, 99% of the time, will be one out of the money" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2604s-2613s])
- **[Weekly contracts recommended - not zero DTE for new traders]** "for you guys, always weekly contracts or the next, or if you're playing the Fridays, you can even play the next week contracts" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2643s-2646s])
- **[One out of the money or at the money]** "it needs to be one out of the money, or at the money if you want to be a little bit more risk to say" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2649s-2651s])
- **[Does play zero DTE sometimes but doesn't recommend for students]** "I do play zero DTE sometimes. Do not recommend it to you guys, but I'm just, you know, being transparent" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2637s-2640s])
- **[Zero DTE makes more money if moves in direction, but new traders can't manage risk]** "if they move in my direction properly, I just make more money on them. However, for most new traders, they just can't manage that risk properly" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2662s-2668s])

## 14. Tools / Indicators
- **[VWAP only used to see if above or below - not essential]** "VWAP, I only have it on my chart to see if it's above or below. VWAP, technically, you don't need VWAP. I don't even use really VWAP that much. It's just to make my chart look cleaner" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1913s-1922s])
- **[Doesn't look at volume much - candlesticks tell everything]** "I don't really look at volume too much. I'll look at it sometimes when it's very significant on the chart and like it sticks out to me, but for the most part, I'm not really looking at volume too much. The candlesticks will tell me all I need to know really" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2858s-2866s])

## 15. Wick vs. Body at Key Levels
- **[Drawing levels depends on context - opening candle high uses body]** "why did I draw the body, slash the top look of this and why did I draw the low of this? This depends on context" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [462s-470s])
- **[Opening candle high is significant - bodies closing below = traders using that level]** "this high right here is the opening candle high... because both bodies of these candles close below this, this means that most other traders are using this level as a high" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [475s-491s])

## 16. Charting / Level Drawing Process
- **[Only draw levels formed in the day - not previous day levels for trend analysis]** "We're not gonna use any key levels. Okay, so we're not gonna use previous day levels or anything. We're only going to use the levels formed in the day" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [330s-337s])
- **[In range, only two levels - high and low]** "for me personally on this chart currently... I would only have two key levels... a high here... and then a low, right here" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [419s-439s])
- **[In uptrend, three levels - high of day, opening candle high/potential higher low, and low]** "we would draw three levels... Number one level, it would be high of day... the second level... this was the first high of day level. And this is also the opening candle high... and then finally we would just draw this low right here" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1418s-1460s])
- **[Levels in middle of range - personally wouldn't draw]** "you could potentially put this right here at 49345s, however, in my opinion, that's still in the middle of this range. Right, so I personally wouldn't do that" (mastermind-3-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2124s-2131s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Green lines definition** "the green lines are higher time frame resistance. So this is going to be the daily, the four hour, the one hour. That's what the green lines indicate. The indicate higher time frame resistance." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1952s-1959s])
- **White lines definition** "The white lines are previous day levels, right? So these are going to be previous day pivot levels on the one minute chart." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1959s-1967s])
- **Red lines definition** "And then the red lines are higher time frame levels of support." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1967s-1971s])
- **White lines clarification** "They're just, they're just pivot levels." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1993s-1995s])
- **Gap definition** "A gap is a significant price difference between two consecutive trading sessions. So this morning, for example, J, J, was like, oh, you guys want to mark out your gaps. What was he talking about? He was simply saying that the, the underlying stock, for example, Q, Q, if yesterday, or if on Friday, it closed out a hundred dollars. And this morning, it opened up regular trading hours at 101. Well, it made a dollar gap on the daily timeframe. So that gap, most likely, we will get a fill. But that is a gap level." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5586s-5615s])
- **Liquidity concept** "The stock is literally looking to fill wherever the most liquidity is. So if there's liquidity sitting at high of day, the stock will gravitate towards high of day before going anywhere else. If there's liquidity sitting below a key level that the stock needs to get to, it will get to that key level and then bounce off that key level." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6326s-6335s])
- **Liquidity levels location** "they're most likely higher timeframe levels" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6340s-6343s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Trade window** "I only trade from 9.30 to 11 o'clock Eastern time." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [2035s-2038s])
- **Why morning** "I am trading in from 9.30 a.m. to 11 a.m. in the morning where there's the most volatility, there's the most volume" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8431s-8437s])
- **Days to avoid - Quadwitching** "quadwitching... occurs quarterly and can lead to increased volatility in the market... you'll either have a bunch of chop or a bunch of moves that have no trend behind them to have no continuation behind them. And you want to avoid quadwitching." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8890s-8922s])
- **Days to avoid - J-Pow/FOMC** "you want to make sure you don't trade on days that have Jerome Powell or FOMC, their low probability days. Those are days that you're going to notice in the beginning of the day, you're going to have a lot of chop and then when two PM when Jerome Powell does start speaking, there will be a lot of volatility... if you want to be a disciplined trader, you want to be here for the long term. You're not going to trade Jerome Powell or FOMC days, right? Because they're just chop. It's slow probability days." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [9021s-9056s])
- **Days to avoid - Earnings** "you don't want to trade into earnings... number one, the reason you don't want to trade for earnings is because I V is way too high. Okay? So the contracts are not going to move well. You can trade the day after earnings. The day after earnings is the day you want to trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [9056s-9086s])
- **General principle** "you don't have to trade every single day. That's a huge mistake I see at everyone do. As a trader, you do not want to trade every day. You want to only trade days that give you high probability setups." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8941s-8957s])
- **Red folder news** "any day that there's red news events, red folder news, that is after nine o'clock, I tend to avoid those days. Now that means it has to be somewhat important red folder because there's some red folder news events that are just like home data or something like that doesn't matter too much. But if it's red folder news where it's like FOMC quadwitching, you know, things like this, I'm going to tend to avoid it." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8957s-8977s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Position sizing** "most of my position size is going to be anywhere from like $52, $100. Usually it's going to be $5,500. 50 is usually a starter than a hundred is usually like kind of almost full sometimes I'll go $1,5,200." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6009s-6014s])
- **Account size needed** "I can trade with a $50,000 account and still consistently made you know like $2, 3,000, $4,000 a day consistently." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5996s-6002s])
- **Maximum entry size** "the maximum entry is like 50, 60, 70,000 max, absolute max. Right? With the average position size, being like 25,000." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7855s-7861s])
- **Option contract volume requirement** "it needs to have over a thousand volume traded" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7202s-7209s])
- **IV threshold** "you want the stock, whatever stock you're trading, you need the IV below 100. If you're trading a stock that has an IV, that's even near 100, right? The option contract is simply not going to move." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8641s-8649s])
- **IV recommendation** "I always recommend have your IV never train stock that has IV over even like honestly like I say like 75, 75 is even too high for IV. Right? You want to be as the lower the IV, the better it'll move." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8671s-8678s])
- **Option leverage example** "the underlying stock. So Tesla underlying moved 2% 2% 2%. Okay. But the option contract itself jumped 71%." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7963s-7984s])
- **Personal track record** "I've had one red day in the last month and a half live in front of all those accelerator students" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [758s-761s])
- **Trading journey timeline** "I started trading in February 2017... I lost for about three years... I started seeing consistency in January 2020" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [261s-660s])
- **Profitability duration** "I've been profitable for three going on four years now" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [660s-672s])

## 11. QQQ Alignment / Index Confirmation
- **Index definition** "the Q Q Q index... it's a tech index" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5656s-5670s])
- **Why watch indices** "if the indices is moving in a specific direction, let's say I'm moving to the downside, and Tesla, for example, let's just use Tesla for this example. And Tesla is showing clear signs of a move to the upside, and it has relative strength. Exactly the trade we took this morning. Then you want to buy that pull back on Tesla, because if Q's bounces off a key support level from the bottom, then Tesla will be the first stock to trend all day." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5694s-5721s])
- **Full edge explanation** "that is my full edge in the markets, how I'm able to determine QQQ compared to every other name. That's what I'm really good at, and that's why I've made the money that I made in the markets, it's through that, through those simple sentences that I just explained to you" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5838s-5851s])
- **Reverse also true** "Yes, reverse is also true." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5823s])

## 12. Option Contract Selection
- **Standard contract** "the option contract I trade is one out of the money, weekly expiration." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7229s-7238s])
- **For calls** "Every single time I am buying going long, every time. For calls, the block is out of the money. So I'm looking at the first block one, which is 265." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8834s-8840s])
- **For puts** "If I said, I'm going short, I'm looking at the first block one for puts, which is out of the money. I'm simply buying the first out of the money, weekly contract with less than 100 IV" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8840s-8851s])
- **Zero DTE recommendation** "I personally don't recommend training zero DT. I usually always recommend one DTE, which is next week expiry for new traders." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [4803s-4810s])
- **Friday trading for new traders** "on the Friday that it's zero DT, I recommend you guys to go ahead and trade the next week's option contracts, right? So on the Friday, I recommend you guys go to the next Friday, because the zero DTs is still a little bit volatile" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7295s-7309s])
- **Personal Friday trading** "I personally buy zero DTs, right? So whatever week it is, I've usually buying the same week contracts... on Friday, I'll still trade zero DT" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7281s-7315s])

## 13. Entry Execution
- **Buy on ask** "when I enter into trades, I enter into the trade based off of the ask price... I try to get filled on the ask price every single time that ensures that I get filled." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [4379s-4469s])
- **Sell on bid** "when I sold it at high of day, I was getting filled on the bid price." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [4486s-4491s])
- **Limit order setup** "my limit order is always over 10 cents... And then for my bid or like what I'm selling my bid order is under 10 cents. So if the bid is at a dollar, for example, my bid and how my broker has it with my limit and my hotkeys is its under 10 cents. Okay. So whenever I click that button, I will get filled instantaneously." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6076s-6104s])
- **Market orders for new traders** "for most people, especially when you're new, you guys can trade a market order and it'll become completely fine." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6405s-6418s])
- **Limit orders for size** "for someone that's trading a lot of size, right? Sometimes you won't have the luxury of getting into a market order because you're going to get filled underneath the asscore above the bid... to decrease slippage on trades, I use limit orders." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6426s-6438s])

## 14. Stop Losses
- **Mental vs hard** "I personally use mental... a hard stop for me is not good. A mental stop is much better." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6881s-6984s])
- **Discipline requirement** "with mental stops, you have to be much, much, much more distance. You have to be way more disciplined than actually stop out of the trip when you need to." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6984s-6995s])
- **Based on backtesting** "when I'm back testing my data, am I waiting for the cano to close based off the the back testing that I'm doing the back testing results? Or am I simply selling while the option contract? Sorry, not the option. While the underlying is still developing, well, based off my back tested data, it's based off of cano closes. Therefore, I know that it's priced in even if I have like I guess stopped out on a huge red cano at one point. It's priced in, right? Within my strategy, it's priced in." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6920s-6961s])

## 15. Broker Recommendations
- **Recommended brokers** "the two brokers I recommend for you, if you do are, or you're planning to switch, is I be care and think or swim." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5120s-5131s])
- **Avoid Questrade** "if you're on Quest Trade, right, I would recommend to change the I'd be care as fast as possible. I was personally on Quest Trade, right, Quest Trade, it cost $10 to actually buy one contract. So for the contract that you even make money, you have to make $10 in the commission fees where I'd be care, it's $1 per contract. So you're literally saving, you know, $9 per trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [3945s-3969s])
- **Avoid Robinhood** "we're not day trading on Robin, guys." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [3937s-3939s])
- **No margin on options** "On options, there is no chance you should be using margin, right? No chance you should be using leverage on options." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5951s-5957s])
- **Cash account for options** "you can literally use a cash account with options and therefore there should be no need for a margin account unless you're trading futures" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6235s-6243s])

## 16. Order Types
- **Day orders only** "I only personally use day orders... you're going to only get executed during that specific day. So once that day is done, you're not going to get, you're not going to get filled anymore." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5216s-5230s])
- **Avoid GTC** "always have a day order, right? Don't have a good till cancel order." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5315s-5322s])

## 17. Greeks
- **What matters** "I personally look at is Delta and Theta and I honestly don't even really look at Delta too much." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8421s-8425s])
- **Why Greeks don't matter much** "the theta is not going to affect me too much because I'm literally trading right in the morning. The theta only affects the or all these optionary. So honestly, only affect you if the IV is too high during that day for the time period that we trade. If you trade, obviously later on the day than they will affect you. But from 9.30 to 11. It's not the only reason the Greeks should affect you is if the IV is too high." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8441s-8462s])
- **Delta definition** "Delta is how much the option contract will go up by if the underlying move... if the Delta's base off a dollar move by the way, so the underlying has to move a dollar. So let's say, you know, a Delta's as 0.5 or something... That means for every dollar move of the underlying stock, the the option contract will move by 0.5 which is 50 cents." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8095s-8132s])
- **Theta definition** "theta is the decay in an auction price every day as the expiration gets near... theta measures the dollar change in the option price based on the time that passes." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8462s-8491s])
- **Rho irrelevance** "I literally could not tell you the last time I've ever even thought about row... row is something that you don't really have to. And especially the way we trade rows not something you need to understand in detail" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8559s-8577s])

## 18. Market Structure / Liquidity
- **Market maker role** "a market maker's only role is simply to provide liquidity in the markets. That's it. Okay. They're not looking to, a lot of people, they're like, oh, the market maker stopped me, you know, they didn't, they weren't looking at your five contracts. I promise you, they had no care in the world for your five contracts." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6306s-6321s])
- **Price movement driver** "The only reason the stock moves from point to point is based off of liquidity." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [6321s-6326s])

## 19. VIX
- **VIX doesn't respect technicals** "the bics doesn't respect any level. The bics, technical doing technical analysis on the bics is basically like watching paint dry. It's not going to rip. It's not going to do anything for you. Not a single thing." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7060s-7071s])
- **VIX recommendation** "do the psychological numbers on bics. Right? Charter with psychological numbers and pivot levels that are near psychological numbers. Right? That's what the bics likes to represent. It does not support technical analysis or it does not support, for example, like flag pattern, your double tops. That's not what the bics is for. The technical analysis does not work and it only works based off key levels." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7085s-7112s])

## 20. Learning Methodology
- **Best way to learn** "the best way to learn is to number one, watch the content. Right? So you watch the content from watching the content. You want to read about the content. So by reading it doesn't mean like you have to read the book about the content. But even if it's a video, for example, just read the transcript, right? Like sometimes in the transcript, you'll see things that you didn't hear... And then write in your own words, like rewatch the video and write in your own words, what the summary of the full thing." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [2812s-2858s])
- **Teach to retain** "when you're writing down the information that you learn, try to present it to a class like, try to pretend like, pretend you're presenting it to like this resume room or something. Right? Because then when you speak it out, that's when you 100% retain that information." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [2928s-2948s])

## 21. Psychology / Mindset
- **Stick to one system** "you have to stick to one thing to make money, right? One of my mentors back in the day told me... when you're doing one thing at a time in business, just in general... if you spread yourself too thin where you're trying to learn everything, you're trying to do everything, right? The one main instrument that's supposed to make you the most money, you'll never make it." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [852s-881s])
- **Diversification is bad early** "in the beginning diversification is something that's actually gonna hold you back" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [898s-900s])
- **Focus on consistency not dollar amounts** "Notice how no one here is saying a dollar remote. No one's saying I want to make this much money. I want to make $100,000. I want to make a million dollars... Everyone's saying how they want to see consistency." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1113s-1128s])
- **Don't chase signals** "if you're taking the trade based off the signal without understanding the underlying strategy behind it, right, you won't be profitable. That's such a short term way to think" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1192s-1199s])

## 22. Trade Reviews / Journaling
- **Trade review value** "once you learn the system, the actual trade reviews is what's going to help you refine your strategy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [2593s-2597s])
- **Trade Zilla access** "you get literally a free account. If you just go into trades, I left, you click this link right here and then you go choose a plan and then you put in this code Scarface 224, you literally get a free account." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [1808s-1822s])

## 23. Live Trading Structure
- **Recording policy** "every single time that JW and I go live in the morning for live trading, right? We record those sessions. Every single second of our live trading is recorded. There is no hindsight, there is no, you know, after the fact like, oh, yeah, I wasn't that trader. Oh, yeah, I was doing this. No, everything is live. Everything is in real time." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [2480s-2501s])
- **Learning curve** "it'll take you probably two weeks to just get used to the live trading. Right? It'll take you about two weeks to get used to the live trading." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [2622s-2631s])

## 24. Weekly Forecast
- **Sunday preparation** "the upcoming week forecast on Sunday, Jayda makes those... he goes over exactly what he's expecting for the week. In the PDF, he has exactly what he's expecting for the week. Any news events that are there for the week, any red folder events that are there for the week and all the charts and what they're looking at for the weekly outlook." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [3124s-3155s])

## 25. Option Greeks - Gamma
- **Gamma definition** "Gamma measures the change in Delta when the stock price is moving. Okay. And this is once again because Delta is not constant, it ranges." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8185s-8197s])
- **Gamma is rate of change** "Gamma is the one that's the fixed rate of, no Gamma is still the rate of change of Delta. Right. So you have to understand when Gamma's the the the value that Gamma's at, right, is what's going to move the Delta? So Delta's the one that's moving." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8241s-8253s])

## 26. Option Greeks - Vega
- **Vega definition** "Vega measures change in option price when volatility moves. Okay. So let's say you know a 1% move up or down in the implied volatility, right. So the IV moves up 1% or down 1%. That means that if the Vega is for example, like if Vega's you know 0.6 or 0.5 or 0.2. That means the overall the underlying, sorry, the option contract itself will move the volatility will move based off Vega." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [8267s-8299s])

## 27. IV Crush
- **Definition** "IV crush. What that means is the IV just went from 100 to like 30 or 40. And because of that, the actual contract itself, if it was, you know, if it was like IV juiced up to like $20, it just dropped to like, like, let's say $7. So you actually don't make money on either side." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7745s-7760s])

## 28. Spread / Bid-Ask
- **Good spread stocks** "a good spread trades are any stock that the underlying has decent amount of volume, but it's around $150 to $250, right? They'll usually have good ask bid and ask spreads. Like so, for example, like Tesla has good bid and ask spread. Apple has really good bid and ask spread." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [4659s-4670s])
- **Avoid high spread** "if your bid and ask quote is the spread is too high, you really don't want to take that trade, especially for options" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [4567s-4572s])

## 29. Option Risk
- **Options can go to zero** "with options, you don't have to. You can lose 100% within like 10 minutes after untree." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7804s-7808s])
- **Size smaller on options** "because of that factor, we have to size less on options than we would with underlying stock." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7814s-7820s])

## 30. Deep in the Money Options
- **Safest option** "if you want to play the safest option contract, right? If you want to play the safest option contract, you want to play an option contract that is deep in the money. This is because that option contract, the value of the option contract really will move too much. And because if it has enough volume, that option contract will essentially honestly move basically very, very similar to the stock itself. And you won't have too much gains, but you won't have too much downside risk either." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5337s-5359s])
- **Deep ITM expensive** "the problem with deep in the money is they're usually more expensive than any other, you know, option contract" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5364s-5369s])

## 31. Straddles/Strangles
- **Earnings warning** "90% of the time you will lose. Why? Because the, though IV is so juiced up right before earnings" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7632s-7639s])

## 32. Relative Strength/Weakness
- **Definition** "when you have Tesla in the morning, showing relative strength, meaning that Tesla is moving up very aggressively, whereas QQQQ is moving down, and it's moving to a key level support where it could potentially bounce from. Well, when you see a pullback on Tesla into a break and retest level, that should give you more confluence that Tesla is going to be the stock that is going to trend all day" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [5779s-5798s])
- **How to determine** "Just watch the charts." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_1...txt [7315s-7319s])

# Extraction from mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Core Definition
- **[Setup = Break and Retest]** "The setup is a break and retest trading setup... when price breaks a certain level of support or resistance, then retest that level. The goal of the trading setup is to buy or sell after the price breaks the level and retest it." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [122s-188s])

### Valid Break (Displacement)
- **[Displacement Required]** "What we want to see here, right? We wanna see the retest because this retest right here is an A plus setup retest. The reason this is is because look at the displacement on this move. Displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken, the move after that key level break, okay? So this displacement of the stock shows that this is a really good example for a downside move." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3279s-3304s])
- **[Displacement = Impulsive Move After Break]** "We had an impulsive candle... broke out strong displacement after the breakout, which means after this breakout level, we had an impulsive move still continue to the upside." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5328s-5339s])
- **[No Displacement = No Trade]** "Why was there no trade on the initial breakout retest? Because this didn't have enough displacement move." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4372s-4374s])

### Valid Retest
- **[Retest of Broken Level]** "What we want here is a bearish candle, right? We don't want to have neutral candle. We want a bearish candle to enter off of." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3977s-3981s])
- **[Wick Below Level is OK for Longs]** "A wick below your level is okay. A wick below is technically the candle close above, right? Like you have to understand that. Like this candle, you kind of want the wick below, right? The candle has to close above or below your level. So when we're looking at a long position, the candle has to close above your level, right? The candle has to close above your level, but if it wicks below your level, that's fine. That's honestly better." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5554s-5578s])
- **[Wick Below Gets Rid of Rookie Traders]** "I personally prefer when it wicks below the level and then comes back because when it wicks below the level, it gets rid of the rookie traders, but it also allows the short sellers to enter in because they think they're going to enter into a breakout short, but then it comes back in and therefore now everyone's looking for that long position and therefore it'll actually move to the upside." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5515s-5534s])

### Entry Trigger (Candlestick Confirmation)
- **[Wait for Candle Close]** "As a new trader, you always wait, you always wait for the candlestick to close, okay? The reason we wait for candlestick close is because when we're back testing, we're back testing based off candlestick closes. So therefore, when we actually go ahead and trade in the real markets, why are we not waiting for that?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1189s-1204s])
- **[Hammer for Longs / Shooting Star for Shorts]** "I like to look for hammer candles when I'm going long. I like to look for inverted hammer candles or shooting star candles when I'm going short. And the reason that I like these candles so much more and I prefer them is because what they show you, right? Is someone or not someone, but buyers try to bring this stock above this level. And as we can see, we touched this level, but the second we touched it, we had liquidity sitting there, right? We had sell orders sitting here and therefore we broke back down and we couldn't break above." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3375s-3409s])
- **[Bearish Candle for Short Entry]** "We see buyers trying to come in, trying to take the stock above the 265.70 level. They were, they couldn't do so, right? We came back, sellers came back and closed it below and made a shooting star slash inverted hammer candle." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3988s-3999s])

### Stop Placement
- **[Stop = Break of Key Level or Structure]** "Your risk level, like I talked about, will always be a break of key level or break of the trend or market structure." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1242s-1245s])
- **[Stop Should Be Obvious]** "If you're trying to figure out where the risk is, then most likely you're trying to force the trade. You should be able to look at the chart for a couple of seconds and be able to determine the risk. If it's not clear, there's most likely a reason for that." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1254s-1262s])
- **[Give Stop a Little Room]** "I don't like to have, you know, a stop loss that's right, you know, right at that level. I like to give it a little bit of room." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3449s-3454s])
- **[Opening Price as Stop Reference]** "Opening price is something that the stock loves to respect during the day because that's just simply where the stock opened up during that day. And so we're gonna use the opening price right here and that's where we're gonna put our stop loss." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3459s-3470s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **[First Scales at HOD/LOD]** "I'm looking for my first scales at high of day or low of day every time, right? Every time. I'm looking for the high of day slash low of day." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1519s-1528s])
- **[HOD/LOD = Liquidity]** "This high of day is where the liquidity is sitting for this specific day now, right? This is where the liquidity is sitting because it's high of day. Obviously, that's where if the stock does move back up, sellers might want to sell some here just to lock that in because it is high of day." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1561s-1574s])
- **[HOD Scale Allows Holding]** "That high of day scale allows me to hold. And therefore, even if it comes back down near our stop loss, which our stop loss always remains constant until we break that structure, right? We can come down to the stop loss. But because I locked in high of day, this means even if we come back to our stop loss here and break what worst case scenario, I'm going to lose only a little." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1617s-1640s])
- **[Psychological Numbers as Targets]** "When we're looking at the key level aspect of the profit targets, we're going to be looking at key levels that are either whole psychological numbers. You guys have 100% heard me say that, right? Those whole psychological numbers, that could be, you know, if you're trading Apple, for example, and it's at 90 bucks, right? Then the whole psychological number of, you know, 91, 92, 93, those are whole psychological numbers." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1693s-1712s])
- **[Previous Pivot Levels as Targets]** "Number two are going to be previous pivot levels, right? So those are going to be higher time frame levels that we're going to exit on. For example, on the four hour, if we have a level of resistance, you know, that we've drawn out previously, right? So that's going to be our final profit target or something like that." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1753s-1768s])

### First Break vs Late Breaks
- **[First Retest is Best]** "You want to look for the first retest, right? You don't want something that's been retested multiple times. You want to look for the fresh level, right? I always look for the fresh level. I don't like something that's been retested multiple times." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4268s-4280s])
- **[First Opening Drive Play is Best]** "The best case scenario for me is always the first opening drive play is always going to be the best play, right? Because if you catch that and you catch the trend for the day, that's going to be the best play for the whole day, right? So for me, I always like to try to capture the first opening drive play." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5898s-5912s])

### VWAP Usage
- **[VWAP for Direction Only]** "The only reason I have VWAP on my chart now, honestly, is just because it makes the chart not look completely plain. I just kind of like it for the aesthetic, to be honest. But I also use VWAP for one main purpose. If the stock is above VWAP, right? If the stock is above VWAP, then you're looking for a long position. If the stock is below VWAP, you're looking for a short position. Why? Because we're momentum traders. We're not trying to buy the reversal of the trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1792s-1818s])

### Market Context for Break-and-Retest
- **[Works Best in Trending Markets]** "I personally like to trade when we are in a trending market. Right? It's either we're trending to the upside or to the downside. But I'm only trading it in a trending market. Why? Because it works the best for momentum trading." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [720s-727s])
- **[Choppy Markets = Bad Retests]** "You guys saw today. Today was a choppy day. And you saw some of the retest that we had coming back. They just weren't good retest. Why? Because they weren't holding. Why weren't they holding? Because the market was chopping." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [729s-738s])
- **[Sideways = Lower Size]** "Sideways action. You want to size. You want to only look for the most A plus setups if you even want to take it. And if you do take it, you take it with low size." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [784s-791s])

### Works on Any Market/Timeframe
- **[Works Everywhere]** "The break and retest, the fundamental strategy behind the break and retest works in every single market. OK, every market, any stock, any time frame." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [573s-581s])
- **[Tech Names Preferred]** "I personally trade the stock on where the tech names are. Sorry, this setup. I only trade this setup on the tech names. Why? Because that's what I've been trading for years at this point. OK. So the tech names are Tesla, Apple, Nvidia, AMD, anything under the QQQ belt." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [665s-676s])

## 5. Key Levels

### Drawing Process (Hierarchical)
- **[Start with Daily]** "First, higher time frame. I'm looking at the daily chart and on the daily chart, I'm simply marking out the candlestick high and the low for the previous day and I'm looking at any other pivot levels that may be near on the daily chart that I need to mark out." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2271s-2292s])
- **[Then 4H and 1H]** "After I mark out the high and low of the candlestick, I'm going to the four hour and the one hour time frame, right? So I skip everything else, but I go from the daily to the four hour. And on the four hour, I'm going to once again mark out pivot levels that I see that are completely obvious or I'm going to look for patterns." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2319s-2333s])
- **[Patterns: Flag and Flat Top]** "For me personally, the patterns I trade the most, honestly, it's just the flag and the flat top, right? Or bear flag and flat bottom or bull flagger and flat top, right? But those are the two patterns that I trade the most, right? And 95% of the time I'm trading those patterns." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2339s-2354s])
- **[Then 1M for Precision]** "Then I go down to the one minute timeframe. On the one minute timeframe, I only mark out levels from previous day, or the day that just occurred, right? So I'll draw out the levels for the day that just occurred, and I'll draw out the pivot levels for the actual specific day." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2503s-2520s])

### Color Coding
- **[Green = Resistance, Red = Support, White = Previous Day]** "The green lines on my chart are the higher timeframe resistance. The red lines on my chart are the higher timeframe support. The white lines are the previous day one minute levels." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2523s-2532s])

### Number of Levels
- **[4-7 Levels Max]** "On average, I'll have anywhere from like four to at most seven, right? I'll have anywhere from four to seven key levels." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1300s-1308s])
- **[Less is More]** "I only draw the most important ones. How do you know they're the most important ones? Because they're the most obvious ones." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1345s-1352s])

### Body vs Wick for Drawing Levels
- **[Draw Based on Context]** "One thing I wanna mention about the body or wick, where should you draw the pivot level at a body or wick? I believe you should draw it based off of context, okay? A lot of traders, right, they only draw it on one. So they would only draw it on the body or they would only draw it on the wick. I don't do that. I draw both, right, depending on the context of the trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2918s-2938s])

### Pre-Market High/Low
- **[Pre-Market High/Low = Key Levels]** "You can also draw pre-market high and pre-market low as well, right? You can always draw those pre-market high and pre-market lows as well." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3074s-3079s])
- **[Pre-Market High/Low Definition]** "The pre-market high and low is from 4, as you can see here, 4, 4 a.m. Eastern time, 4 a.m. Eastern time to 9.29 a.m. Eastern time. The high and low that's formed from 4 a.m. Eastern to 9.29 Eastern." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5637s-5653s])

### Fresh Levels Daily
- **[Delete Levels Every Day]** "Every single day, I have a fresh start, okay? Every day, every single morning or every night, depending on when I do it, I will get rid of all the levels I had before, come into the day, see what price action occurred on that specific day because one day of price action will change a lot." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1468s-1484s])

### Draw Levels as Day Progresses
- **[Draw New Levels Intraday]** "After I have those key levels that I draw the previous day, or the day before or whatever, then I will draw levels as the day progresses, right? So as the day progresses, and we have new pivot levels intraday, right? Or we break those higher timeframe resistance that we had before or whatever, right? And we're having new levels, like new high of day or low of day, I'll draw out those new high of day and low of day levels." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2570s-2592s])

### Learning to Draw Levels
- **[Compare to Tony's Levels]** "Every single night or morning, whenever you draw your key levels, make sure you have your key levels before I post them. And what you want to do is simply compare your key levels to mine. And that is going to be how you're going to learn how to draw key levels the most effectively every single day." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [986s-1003s])
- **[It Takes Time]** "You will understand exactly each single one of my key levels as time progresses. It may take some people two weeks. It may take some people two months. It may take some people six months. But I promise you, it will come to you. And one day it'll kind of just click." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [935s-947s])

---

## 6. Time-of-Day

### Trade Window
- **[Most Profitable Trades Within 10-15 Minutes]** "Most of my profitable trades are usually within 10 to 15 minutes. Like they're much, much below 15 minutes. And that's just most of my profitable trades, right? And that's why I don't like sitting around waiting for trades." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4104s-4114s])

### Best Days
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### News Days
- **[Avoid Crazy News Events]** "This is assuming a normal trading day and not some weird news driven the market, 100%, right? So every time we're trading, right, we're looking for a normal trading day. We're not looking for crazy news events or anything like that." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1449s-1459s])

### Chart Timeframe Rules
- **[Bias on Daily/4H/1H, Entry on 1M]** "I'm making my thesis or my bias, as some people call it. I'm making it on the daily four hour and one hour time frame. And then from there, I'm actually taking the trade every single time on the one minute time frame. I may monitor, right? I may monitor the trade on the five minute, but I'm taking it on the one minute." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [797s-810s])

---

## 7. Exits

### Scaling %
- **[Sell 40% at HOD/LOD]** "Let's say we sell the first let's say we sell 40 percent at high of day. OK, the remainder, the remainder of the 60 percent that actually ends up riding will make so much more money than this 40 percent." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [496s-504s])
- **[Sell Into Strength on Options]** "You want to sell into strength on option contracts. You always sell into strength, right? You always sell into strength on option contracts. The reason that is because when you're selling into strength, other people are buying that at those high prices... When you're selling into strength, you're going to get the best price for that contract." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4421s-4440s])

### Breakeven (When)
- **[Don't Move to BE After First Scale]** "After my higher day scale, that doesn't mean I'm gonna let my stop go break even. My stop loss was still the same." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5748s-5752s])
- **[Only Move Stop When Structure Changes]** "You only move your stop loss when market structure changes. You don't move it before based off your trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5771s-5774s])
- **[Move to BE After Breaking Structure]** "We're not gonna change our stop loss until we break this low because once we break this low, then our stop simply just becomes breakeven, okay? Because once we break this low, and this is based off of trend too, this doesn't happen every single time because sometimes it really depends on the chart context." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3516s-3534s])

### Trailing / Position Management
- **[Trailers Depend on Market Context]** "For the trailers, it'll depend on the actual context of the market. Is QQQ trending? Is the market overall in a trend? Are we in a consolidated market, which means if we're in a range market, then I'm going to take the full position off at high of day. If we're not in a range market, if we're in a trending market, then I'll only take off 20% and let the rest ride." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5954s-5971s])
- **[Trailers Can Make More Than Full Trade]** "Those trailers could potentially have made more than the full trade if it actually did end up, you know, having a crazy day, right? And that's why you let those trailers sit there, right? Because the trailers can make you more than the whole potential trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5703s-5716s])

---

## 8. HTF Application

### Weekly/Daily Levels
- **[Daily Candle High/Low as Key Levels]** "On the daily chart, I'm simply marking out the candlestick high and the low for the previous day and I'm looking at any other pivot levels that may be near on the daily chart that I need to mark out." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2283s-2292s])

### Multi-Day Holds
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### HTF Overriding Intraday
- **[HTF Thesis Guides Direction]** "Overall on the day, on the daily timeframe, we're going in with a bearish thesis. Okay, we're going into a bearish thesis on Tesla with the daily timeframe. So we should be looking for a short position because this is a very good example on the daily timeframe of being short." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3722s-3734s])
- **[HTF Patterns = Higher Significance]** "On the higher timeframe, you have to understand most hedge funds, most institutions, they're looking at those higher timeframes. And though they're not looking at patterns like us, the pattern helps us understand like, hey, the stock was in an uptrend, it's consolidating now. Therefore, if it breaks out of this consolidation period, it'll continue its move to the upside." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2458s-2476s])

---

## 9. Trade Selection

### A/A+/B/C Grading Criteria
- **[A+ Setup = Strong Displacement + Retest]** "What we wanna see here, right? We wanna see the retest because this retest right here is an A plus setup retest. Okay, this is an A plus setup retest. The reason this is is because look at the displacement on this move." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3279s-3290s])
- **[A+ = Strong Trend + HTF Alignment]** "This would be an A plus setup. We have a very strong trend established rate. And also on the higher timeframe, this trend is the exact same bearish. Okay. So on the higher timeframe, we're bearish. And then we have this trend established as well." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4836s-4850s])

### Confluence
- **[Edge = Comparing Stock to QQQ]** "My edge comes from understanding market context and market structure... For me, it means being able to identify intraday relative strength and weakness by comparing individual stocks with the indices. You need to understand that not all break and retest are equal, and that's where understanding market structure and context comes into place." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1870s-1912s])
- **[QQQ Weak + Tesla Weak = Short]** "If QQQ is strong on the day, right? And Tesla's weak on the day, right? The second QQQ hits a level of resistance where I believe that QQQ can drop from, and Tesla shows a break in retest that Tesla's retesting a previous level of resistance. I'm entering into Tesla short there because if QQQ drops, this is gonna explode Tesla to the downside." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2061s-2080s])

### Max Trades/Day
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Stop-When-Green
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Market Regime
- **[Trending = Size Up, Sideways = Size Down]** "When we do come back into a sideways market or consolidation market, right on the higher time frame, then I will obviously step back, lower my size and obviously not size up as aggressively as I am on trades right now just because I'm winning." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [758s-770s])

### Come in with Thesis, But Stay Open
- **[Thesis + Flexibility]** "I'll always come into the day with a thesis. You'll notice whenever I'm live trading, I come into the day and I'm like, you know, even this morning, right? I had those, you know, I'll always have those levels where I'm like, guys, I'm looking for a retest level here, whatever, blah, blah, blah, right? So I'm coming in with a thesis, but that does not mean that I'm not going to go to the other side." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5593s-5609s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

### Win Rate

### R:R
- **[Average 1:2 to 1:2.2]** "On average, if you look at my trades over a long period of time, it'll be a one to two to one to 2.2-ish around risk to reward strategy." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3125s-3132s])
- **[Minimum 1:2]** "When we're looking at break and retest setups, by the way, what I'm looking for is at least a one to two risk to reward." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3103s-3108s])

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

The probability is high of a gap getting filled, but it's not always." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5657s-5667s])

Here are the extracted rules from the new transcript, building upon the previous extraction notes.

### 1. Break-and-retest
- **[Stop Placement = Below the Impulsive Break Candle]** "the candle that broke with an impulsive move to the upside above 180 64s... it's the impulsive candle that broke the level okay so buyers are going to keep that candlestick in mind okay because it is the candlestick that broke" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [813s-826s])
- **[Stop Placement = Below Pivot Level for Room to Breathe]** "if you want to give your trade a little bit of room to breathe... the stop would simply be a little bit above this pivot level... but then to give the trade a little bit of room to breathe near that stop area right it would be the 177 29 level which is this pivot low and this pivot" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1620s-1634s])
- **[Stop Placement = Above Pivot High for Shorts]** "our stop is going to be right here because once again this is the pivot high right so of course this is going to be the pivot high this is going to be the stop" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3686s-3690s])
- **[Retest Entry = Requires Speed]** "on the one minute time frame you need to have the ability to trade fast... it gives you a split second to enter and if you're not in the trade right then just you have to skip that trade because then all of a sudden you're buying the breakout other than the retest" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1440s-1453s])
- **[Retest Entry = Requires Speed]** "if you're someone that lacks the ability to make decisions quickly right i really recommend you to start trading on the five minute or higher time frames" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1425s-1435s])

### 4. Order blocks
- **[Order Block = Candle that Held a Retest]** "this candle also created an order block now if you guys don't know what an order block is just yet don't worry this weekend j-dub will be going over all those concepts like order block" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1665s-1679s])

### 5. Key levels
- **[Low of Day = Key for Stop Management]** "once we get a close below previous low of day right so this is the low of day that we put in for the day once we get a close after low of day so we get a close of the candle after low of day then you can potentially move your stop loss okay before then you never move your stop" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1757s-1770s])
- **[Low of Day = Key for Stop Management]** "we don't move our stop loss until we break the candle or until we break the low of day and we actually have a candle close below along with having the big red candles as well" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1816s-1822s])
- **[Previous Day Low = Profit Target]** "this low of day push because this reverses like a v-shape this definitely has to correlate with something else... this low of day uh two days ago right this two a day two days ago low of day right so that could have been your potential final profit target" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2195s-2215s])
- **[Liquidity = Buying Liquidity at Previous Lows]** "there's a 90 chance it's going to hit 173.92 why because this is where all the buying liquidity is if the trade does want to reverse it's obviously going to get go to the buying liquidity first it's going to bring in new buyers to reverse the trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2524s-2538s])

### 6. Time-of-day
- **[News Days = Cause Consolidation]** "we had 10 o'clock news today so that's also why we had some consolidation as well" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3026s-3029s])

### 7. Exits
- **[Stop Management = Do Not Move Stop Prematurely]** "we don't move our stop loss until we break the candle or until we break the low of day and we actually have a candle close below along with having the big red candles as well" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1816s-1822s])
- **[Stop Management = Move Stop After Structure Break]** "every time you see a low of day being created with the candle close below you start moving your stop right" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1882s-1887s])
- **[Stop Management = Move to Previous Pivot High After Break]** "once the candle closes below that previous low of day we move our stop to the previous pivot the lower high" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [3773s-3779s])
- **[Stop Management = Move to Break Even After Structure Break]** "from here right we can move our stop to simply break even now break even by break even i simply you're actually not moving into break even completely actually you're moving it to the the the top wick of that previous high right" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1862s-1871s])
- **[Stop Management = Do Not Move Stop Based on Risk Alone]** "you don't move your stop based off of your own risk management that's a mistake i see a lot of people making where they're like oh yeah just because it hit you know uh it came near low of day i'm gonna move my stop to break even why the market structure has not changed" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1774s-1783s])
- **[Stop Management = Do Not Move Stop Based on Risk Alone]** "you don't move your stop based off of your own risk management that's a mistake i see a lot of people making" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [1774s-1778s])
- **[Stop Management = Context Determines Stop Location]** "it really all depends on the context of the trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [364s-369s])
- **[Stop Management = Context Determines Stop Location]** "it all comes down to understanding the context of the trade as well right so understanding the context that oh look this was the low of day we broke that we came back up our stop loss obviously moved to the high of this candle" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2002s-2017s])
- **[Stop Management = Context Determines Stop Location]** "you just have to think about the market context at this point this is the low 174.73 you have your profit target here which is the two day previous day low right so why would you end up moving your stop loss here right the reason i didn't move my stop loss is based off market context" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2489s-2503s])
- **[Stop Management = Context Determines Stop Location]** "you just have to think about the market context at this point this is the low 174.73 you have your profit target here which is the two day previous day low right so why would you end up moving your stop loss here right the reason i didn't move my stop loss is based off market context" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes.txt [2489s-2503s])

### GROUP 24
Files: mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Fundamental Strategy Definition]** "the fundamental strategy is simply a move up, a displacement move here... And then we just get the retest in the most basic terms. That's the strategy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [530s-547s])

- **[Displacement Requirement]** "we need a displacement move up" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [540s-541s])

- **[Flat Top Breakout]** "we had a flat top, right? So what does the flat top signify? It signifies that there is this is a resistance. And if we break above this with an impulsive candle and have a strong displacement, right, it shows that buyers are buying the stock up and obviously sellers are weak above this level. Therefore, the retest of this level will be strong." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2058s-2078s])

- **[Retest Entry Trigger - Hammer/Wick]** "the price action for me, you guys know I'm either looking for a strong hammer stick candle close or I'm looking for a wick to develop right underneath" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2108s-2113s])

- **[Wick Below Level Confirmation]** "whenever it's a key level, I like to actually see the wick go below and then close above because that shows that sellers did try to bring it down, but buyers brought it up really aggressively" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2117s-2124s])

- **[Entry Timing - Next Candle]** "once this candle closes on the second candle, I'll be looking to enter in right on the second candle once I see a little bit more strength and I enter in" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2126s-2132s])

- **[Entry Execution]** "when I enter the trade, because the first candle has to close, right? So it's the next candle. It's the next candle that I'm entering on. It's usually going to be the open of the next candle" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6086s-6093s])

- **[Stop Loss Placement]** "the stop loss is obviously going to be right below this level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2083s-2085s])

- **[Stop Loss = Break of Market Structure]** "our stop level always has to make sure that it is a break of market structure... I'm gonna get stopped out because it broke market structure" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5206s-5226s])

- **[First Profit Target - High of Day]** "your first profit target is always going to be high day where you scale" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2091s-2093s])

- **[Second Target - Key Level]** "and then your second key level or whatever" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2094s-2095s])

- **[Market Condition Refinement - Choppy Market]** "if it's a choppy market... rather than taking, let's say 20 to 40% off a high of day, now we're taking 100% off and we're just scalping the strategy rather than holding the trailers" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [607s-614s])

- **[Market Condition Refinement - Trending Market]** "if it's a trending market... you always get the break in retest. But then after that, when you make that new high of day, you're going to obviously only take, let's say 20% off here, and then you're going to hold the majority of your position because you're in a trending market" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [624s-642s])

- **[Levels are Zones, Not Ticks]** "every level is a zone, right? You have to consider the levels like a zone. Like it's not like the trade is going to come down to your level specifically... it might just dip right below the level and then rip" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2392s-2415s])

- **[Zone Size]** "it can be like anywhere from like a two to ten cents zone" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2421s-2424s])

- **[Consistency of Entry/Exit Signals]** "you guys can go back to trade reviews from, you know, two years ago from today. And you'll see that I had the exact same entry and exit signals back then that I do now and I use them every single time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2146s-2156s])

- **[Changing Signals = Losing Money]** "the only time you change your entry and exit signals, you'll notice is the time you're going to lose money in the markets that shouldn't be lost" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2164s-2171s])

---

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **[Opening Play Strategy Mentioned]** "There is another strategy we're still gonna learn. It's the opening play strategy. And we'll talk more about that when we have to, I think next week." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6043s-6048s])

**NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** - No specific rules for "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" provided in this transcript.

---

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[Basic Definition]** "Basically, what it is is when a trade comes to one level with the same thesis and it doesn't work the first time, the second time, if it has the same thesis, it will work." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5948s-5955s])

- **[Reference to Full Explanation]** "I have a full YouTube video on the 84% rule. There's also in the accelerator course, right? So you can go through and watch that as well, 84%." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5940s-5946s])

- **[84% Rule as Conviction Booster]** "let's say it had the 84% rule to even increase the chances, right? So 84% of the time it's gonna work" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3938s-3941s])

---

## 4. Order Blocks

**NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**
