## 5. Key Levels

- **[Levels as Zones]** "every level is a zone, right? You have to consider the levels like a zone" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2392s-2394s])

- **[Zone Size Range]** "it can be like anywhere from like a two to ten cents zone" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2421s-2424s])

- **[Profit Target at Key Levels]** "We take profits at high of day slash low of day and key levels" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5157s-5158s])

- **[Stop Loss at Key Levels]** "We stop out at key levels, VWAP, and market structure" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5164s-5165s])

- **[Higher Timeframe Level as Final Target]** "my final kind of profit targets might be this higher timeframe level... this higher timeframe level... that's where the liquidity is gonna, that's where the most liquidity is sitting in, so that's where the stock most likely has to head" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5102s-5121s])

---

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Trade Window - 9:30 AM to 11:00 AM Eastern]** "the time I trade is 9.30 a.m. Eastern to 11 a.m. Eastern" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3097s-3099s])

- **[Reason for Trade Window]** "The reason I choose from 9.30 a.m. to 11 o'clock is because the volatility and the volume is the highest during that time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3132s-3135s])

- **[Ignore Later Moves]** "what about if a bigger move happens at like two o'clock today? It's like, I don't care, because I know on average, the biggest moves happen from 9.30 to 11 o'clock, because that's when the most volume in the markets is inflowing" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3141s-3152s])

- **[Consistency of Trading Time]** "you trade that time of day every single day consistently. You don't change the time of day you trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3120s-3126s])

- **[Red Folder News - Avoid Trading]** "how many times have you seen me go, oh guys, we've got to wait till either 10 o'clock or we have to just not trade that day based off the news, right? Because it'll be too volatile" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5312s-5320s])

- **[FOMC Days - Low Probability]** "you start to understand why it's such a low probability reason to trade on FOMC days or days that we have red folder news that actually impact the markets" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5452s-5460s])

- **[News Can Destroy Technical Analysis]** "if we do have that 10 o'clock news, that news can potentially get rid of any market structure you have, get rid of all the market structure, get rid of any technical analysis you know" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5345s-5355s])

- **[Zero DTE on Fridays - Use Next Week]** "on Fridays when it is zero DTE, just go to the next week contracts" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4709s-4711s])

- **[Zero DTE Not for Beginners]** "for most people, I don't recommend zero DTEs until you have that experience where you can trade it" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4719s-4722s])

- **[Theta Not an Issue in Trade Window]** "since we trade from 9.30 to 11 o'clock, right? The theta doesn't really affect us too much" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5871s-5874s])

---

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling at High of Day - Always]** "whenever we have a break and retest, right, high of day, I'm selling some... I'm scaling some at high of day" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5081s-5093s])

- **[Scaling at Psychological Numbers]** "you can scale at whole numbers. For example, this 436.50 level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5685s-5687s])

- **[Three-Contract Scaling Example]** "Let's just say we had three contracts, right? We sell one contract here, we sell one contract of 436.50, and then our final target... the realized profits we have from these two is already more than the realized profits we would have had just holding to a high of day. So now with the unrealized profits of the third one, we're able to hold that trade much longer into our profit target" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5706s-5735s])

- **[Stress-Free Holding After Realized Profits]** "we were able to hold that stress-free because we have realized profits, and so this, you know, the final contract, we're able to ride it because it's stress-free" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5763s-5771s])

- **[Mental Stop Loss Recommended]** "I always say you want to have a mental stop-loss" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5937s-5938s])

- **[Mechanical Stop for Final Target - Depends]** "It depends if I'm trading still, or if I'm not trading, then I'll just have my profit target set on my IBK, and I'll just walk away" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5884s-5892s])

- **[Stop Loss = Break of Market Structure]** "I'm gonna get stopped out because it broke market structure" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5223s-5226s])

---

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Higher Timeframe Thesis for Momentum]** "the edge comes from understanding which trades have the higher timeframe thesis. And based off that higher timeframe thesis, we can go and trade those because they have momentum... I find the momentum on the higher timeframe then trade the lower timeframe" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2924s-2937s])

- **[Hold Longer in Trending HTF]** "you want to trade or hold your winners longer in market conditions that we're trending on the higher timeframe" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4789s-4793s])

- **[Sell at HOD in Choppy HTF]** "if we're consolidating or chopping around in the higher timeframe, obviously you wanna sell the majority at high of day" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4794s-4798s])

- **[Final Target = HTF Level]** "my final kind of profit targets might be this higher timeframe level... that's where the liquidity is gonna, that's where the most liquidity is sitting in, so that's where the stock most likely has to head" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5102s-5121s])

- **[Future Transition to Swing Trading]** "I'm probably going to transition into swing trading... taking more size on swing trades with the exact same strategy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1683s-1698s])

---

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[A+ Setup Mentioned]** "A plus setup trade... this is like A plus in my textbook" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3930s-3935s])

- **[A+ Setup + 84% Rule]** "let's say it had the 84% rule to even increase the chances, right? So 84% of the time it's gonna work" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3938s-3941s])

- **[Max Trades Per Day - 1 to 3]** "I only take one to two trades a day on average. Technically within my rules, I can take one to three" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3451s-3457s])

- **[Max Losses in a Row - 2]** "if I lose two trades in a row, what that shows me is don't go for the third trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3487s-3490s])

- **[Reason for Max Losses Rule]** "if I just lost two trades in a row? That simply means that either A, my psychology isn't on point for the day or B, the market is just chopping around or consolidating or it's not a market that I wanna trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3495s-3502s])

- **[One Trade at a Time]** "Most of the time, I'm usually in one trade at a time." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6053s-6056s])

- **[QQQ Alignment Required]** "when you see something retesting on the left side, you simply look over at QQQ if QQQ is also aligning. It's simple. You take the trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1801s-1806s])

- **[Ticker Personality Matters]** "it does help understanding personalities of tickers 100%... on days that Tesla wants to run... sometimes you have to be really aggressive with Tesla on those days... on days that Tesla doesn't want to run and recently how the higher timeframe is just in consolidation, you know, Tesla is something you don't even want to touch" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2829s-2862s])

- **[One Instrument in Beginning]** "you want to trade one instrument as well... your system in the beginning should only be... one dimensional... you only have one system or you only have one setup. You have one instrument that you're trading" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1365s-1386s])

- **[Don't Be a Jack of All Trades]** "a lot of traders come in and especially new traders, they try to be a jack of all trade. They try to learn Forex. They try to learn crypto. They try to learn... everything. But in the beginning, it's important for you... you want to trade one instrument" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1349s-1366s])

- **[Good Trade Defined by Plan, Not Result]** "The trade's result of winning and losing does not define a good trade. What defines a good trade is your pre-plan... A good trade is defined by your actual setup and how well you follow your plan" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2189s-2205s])

- **[Win or Lose Doesn't Matter - Follow Plan]** "Win or lose does not matter as long as you follow your plan every single time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2210s-2213s])

- **[Stop When Green - Implied by Max Losses]** "on trending days, I would win my one trade or whatever and I'd call it a day because I'm like, oh, I'm green, let me call it a day because I took one green trade and I'm good" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3431s-3435s])

- **[Market Regime - Size Adjustment]** "if we're in an uptrending market, then I'm gonna go ahead and I'm gonna take 150, 200 contracts maybe... However, if I'm in a choppy market and we're consolidating, I'll take like 50 contracts" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3385s-3398s])

---

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Risk Multiple - 2R]** "my risk multiple is two" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1071s-1072s])

- **[Risk to Reward - 2:1]** "if we're risking one hundred dollars, I'm looking to gain about two hundred dollars" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1097s-1100s])

- **[Scalper R:R - 1:1]** "a scalper might go one to one, which means if you're risking one hundred dollars, they're only looking to gain one hundred dollars" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1091s-1096s])

- **[Max Stop for Day = Average Daily Win]** "if you, on average, make $100 a day... then your max stop should be $100... You shouldn't lose a bigger amount than you win" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3199s-3212s])

- **[Max Losses in a Row - 2]** "if I lose two trades in a row, what that shows me is don't go for the third trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3487s-3490s])

- **[Max Trades Per Day - 1 to 3]** "I only take one to two trades a day on average. Technically within my rules, I can take one to three" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3451s-3457s])

- **[Beginner Position Size - 1 to 3 Contracts]** "go down to the most minimal contracts... you only wanna take one or two contracts, maybe two to three... three is like that sweet spot for the scaling aspect... but in the beginning... you wanna trade one to two contracts" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3349s-3372s])

- **[84% Rule - 84% of the Time]** "84% of the time it's gonna work" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3941s])

- **[Red Day Management - Under $3K]** "every time I had a red day, it was very, I would manage it very well... I try to make it under a 3K red day" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3035s-3043s])

- **[Green Day Range - $3K to $20K]** "my green days... Some days were like $15,000, $20,000... Some days were, you know, $3,000, $4,000" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3045s-3050s])

- **[Risk Per Trade - Personal Financial Situation]** "it's based off of per trade basis and it's based off of exactly how much money you're personally willing to lose" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4003s-4008s])

- **[Example R:R Improvement - 3.18 to 7.08]** "your original risk to reward was a 3.18. To increase your risk to reward, which this is a 7.08" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5651s-5657s])

- **[Time to First Scale - 3-5 Minutes]** "from that retest to high of day, that usually works out within like three to five minutes on average" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1525s-1529s])

- **[Experience Before Discretion - 5+ Years]** "Only after you've been trading for, I want to say, five plus years with the same system and the same setup, and you've been seeing consistency only after then can you be like, okay, yeah, no, I have a, you know, based off the experience that I have" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [786s-799s])

- **[Beginner Must Be Robotic 100%]** "as a new trader, 100% of the time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [878s-880s])

- **[Experienced Trader - Follow Plan 99.9%]** "you still, at the end of the day, you want to follow your plan 99.9% of the time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [874s-876s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Entry trigger - QQQ confirmation]** "I want QQQ to break above the pre-market high for me to be able to take this retest on Apple on that consolidation slash stalling level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3956s-3964s])
- **[Entry trigger - QQQ confirmation]** "for me to buy this entry, I need QQQ to be above its pre-market high to show momentum" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3974s-3978s])
- **[Breakout vs break-and-retest distinction]** "technically it did take the break in retest. However, if I technically, if I didn't see that retest, you're technically buying the breakout, so probably not" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [4150s-4159s])
- **[Breakout vs break-and-retest distinction]** "realistically the best entry would have came technically at this retest, but based off of Q's breaking out at the same time on that higher timeframe, and Tesla's still right below it, technically that's a good entry for a breakout, but it wasn't a break in retest entry" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [4170s-4183s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **[84% rule definition]** "The 84% rule is the retest again" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2103s-2104s])
- **[Psychology of re-entering after loss - warning]** "you already lost a hundred dollars here, right? You might as well take the trade again because what if it goes back up? You've seen this happen before multiple times on the chart. What if it goes back up? You take the trade again at this level. Now the trade comes up, comes up. You never take the profits because it didn't hit high of day and boom, right? It goes back to your stop loss, but this time because this is your second time, you really want to win the trade. You don't take that stop, but then you take the second stop and now you're down 200 bucks" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2113s-2139s])
- **[Psychology of re-entering after loss - escalation]** "now you're down $300. However, now off of this level, it sky rocketed. Like it literally does a V-shape recovery, sky rockets back up to your level. This time you're like, this time it's definitely going to hit my profit target. But all of a sudden it's so, so, so, so close and boom, straight down" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2144s-2162s])
- **[Chop day identification]** "This day represents chop. This day literally represents what happened today on QQQ until we took that AMD trade, right? Today QQQ was looking exactly like this, right? It was the day you didn't want to trade. It was just consolidation and chop" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2164s-2178s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Pre-market high as key level]** "we have a pre-market level on QQQ at 283.95s" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3912s-3914s])
- **[Pre-market high as key level]** "we have this level here, right, at that pre-market high and we wanna break above that on Apple" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3925s-3927s])
- **[Flat top as key level]** "we see this flat top at 294.70s with a bottom wedge" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3501s-3505s])
- **[Psych level as target]** "the next level here would be the 294 psych level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3787s-3788s])
- **[Psych level as target]** "I would be looking for that 285 psych level on Q's" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [4023s-4025s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Late trade example]** "I took a late trade at like 10.35 or 10.40 and you know, it was super late and it was a great trade, right? It clearly worked out really, really well" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [16s-26s])
- **[FOMC - no live trading]** "Wednesday, I won't be live trading just because of all the FOMC stuff going on" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [4297s-4300s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Scaling out on strength]** "if you do have like three, maybe sell one at 190, right, to lock in profits if we do come back down" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3326s-3330s])
- **[Stop loss discipline - holding losses]** "if something's not in your strategy, if you're holding something based off of pure, you know, luck or hoping, you will lose that money 100 percent. 100 percent. If you don't lose in that trade, you will lose in the next trade. If you don't lose in the next trade, you'll lose in the trade after" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [1901s-1916s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[QQQ as higher timeframe confirmation]** "You can be a great technical trader, but until the actual markets align with your thesis, you're never going to, you know, have that complete edge in the markets. And my edge came from the fact that once again, we broke that 1027 level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3374s-3384s])
- **[QQQ as higher timeframe confirmation]** "you really have to see with Qs what's happening on Qs, right? To see that bigger picture and what was happening in the markets" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3457s-3462s])
- **[QQQ ranging = no trade]** "If Qs is ranging, for example, right? Then we don't wanna take this trade rate, for example, because then this could consolidate. So that's why we wanna wait for the breakout of Qs at that 308, 86 level to take that trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3464s-3474s])
- **[QQQ break as edge for individual stock]** "the secret sauce comes from QQQ, right?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3370s-3372s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Max trades/day]** "within our system, we only take one or two trades, right?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2205s-2207s])
- **[Stop after loss - ideal scenario]** "The one A plus trade, you would have lost $100 and you just would have walked away from the day" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2208s-2212s])
- **[Overtrading as cause of losses]** "the reason you were losing money in this phase was most likely due to over trading" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [1700s-1703s])
- **[Overtrading fix]** "you just overtrade, like just stop trading, like take one to two trade a day. Like it's that's not that hard to do" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2014s-2019s])
- **[Subpar trades have lower win rate]** "when you trade too much and you're not taking A plus setups, well, you're only taking these subpar trades and those subpar trades have a much lower win rate than 50 percent" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [1748s-1756s])
- **[Trade selection - QQQ alignment]** "I want QQQ to break above the pre-market high for me to be able to take this retest on Apple" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3956s-3964s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Consistency before sizing up]** "you wanna see at least three to four months of consistency before you even think of sizing up" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3172s-3176s])
- **[Consistency before sizing up - detail]** "you wanna see three to four months of consistency, you know, on the one to three contracts you're trading before you even decide to jump up to the next level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [3182s-3189s])
- **[Contract size range]** "I take anywhere from 50 to 150 contracts. The most I'll usually take is 200, but that has to be in a very trending market, and I need to have a lot of confidence in that trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [4272s-4285s])
- **[Timeline - journey phases]** "from this step to the losing step, this may be three to six months. This step right here might maybe three months. Right. You may have three months of crazy wins and then you start losing. Right. From the losing step to the pivoting. Right. This may take another six months. Right. Now, from the losing to the pivot, or say from the pivoting to the continue slash start working. This is probably the longest part on the chart. This is probably two to three years" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [1333s-1355s])
- **[P&L calendar pattern - small greens, one big red]** "small green, small green, let's just say, you know, $50, $60, $30, $50, $60, $30, $50, $60, $30, right, really, really green. And then all of a day, one red day minus $600, right? So it'd be 50, 50, 50, 40, 30, minus 600, right? Now all of a sudden my psychology is messed up, next day minus 1200" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2291s-2313s])
- **[Biggest red day removal]** "if I just got rid of my biggest red day every month when I was a new trader, I would have been profitable. I literally would have been profitable in a year" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel.txt [2337s-2345s])

# EXTRACTION FROM mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Following plan over FOMO]** "if you took Tesla as it was falling, but it was in a break and retest, or it wasn't within your strategy, right? And you FOMO'd in because even me and JDubb were on voice today. We were like, oh yeah, Tesla's going to drop. Tesla's 100% going to drop, right? But the thing was, we didn't have an entry. We couldn't enter into the trade because it wasn't within our plan, right? We didn't have a strategy to enter into a breakout. We're not breakout traders. We needed that retest, right?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1528s-1550s])
- **[Break and retest works on every timeframe]** "the break and retest works on every single timeframe, right? But obviously, for this one, you have to wait a little while." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2255s-2259s])
- **[Daily timeframe break and retest example]** "you can see that you pushed up, we came back, retest on a hammer candle entry, and then new high of, I was going to say new high of day, but new push to new highs, right? And this is clear because the momentum's there, we have a clear breakout, we have a clear retest based off of that previous high, and now we're pushing back up, right?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2264s-2280s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
- **[NOT named "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" in this source]** The speaker references a "five minute opening range break" strategy but does not name it as "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1800s-1804s])

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **[Re-entry after stop out with 84% rule]** "when the stock moves back up, okay, and it retests again with the 84% rule per se, right? Then you can re-enter that trade for that move to high of day or wherever you're looking for, okay?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1614s-1628s])
- **[Re-entry example - limiting loss then capturing move]** "this way you were able to limit your loss to let's say $100 here, but then you were able to once again capture the 84% rule, right? And if this was a two-hour trade, even though you lost $100, you still made $200 on this trade, so you came out of the day with $100" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1643s-1659s])

## 4. Order Blocks
- NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Stop loss at low of day for opening range break]** "Where would our stop loss be? Our stop loss would be simply low day." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1816s-1820s])
- **[Previous day low as pivot level]** "we have this pivot level, which is a previous day low right here at 173 17th." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1900s-1906s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Trade window 9:30-10:30]** "from 930 to 1030 when I was trading today" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [49s-54s])
- **[FOMC - not trading]** "we have FOMC tomorrow and therefore, I am not trading tomorrow, right?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [952s-956s])
- **[Five minute timeframe after 10:35/10:45]** "after about 1035, 1045, you know, that's fine if you're entering on the five minute rate" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [87s-91s])
- **[First hour focus for new traders]** "for the first hour, I would only trade when I'm trading. For example, just listen to my commentary and also see if I can see a trade myself from nine 30 to 1030 to 11." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [4196s-4202s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Stop loss - body close below, not wick]** "you always wait for the body to close below your stop loss. The reason you wait for the body to close below your stop loss is because if you get a wick, right? If you get a wick below your stop loss... what that indicates is that sellers did try to bring this daily time frame candle below the level. However, they weren't strong enough and there are some significant buyers sitting there" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2326s-2356s])
- **[Body close signals seller strength]** "we always wait for the body to close to signal to us that the sellers are more stronger here than buyers." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2363s-2369s])
- **[Mental stops vs hard stops]** "Me personally, I use mental stops. In the beginning, you can use hard stops. If you're someone that literally, you just can't respect your stop loss. You've tried multiple times. You are someone that you can put in hard stops." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2393s-2403s])
- **[Hard stops difficult for options]** "for options, it's pretty hard to put hard stops. It's pretty hard. All right. Like you have to basically guess where to put the hard stop. So I tend to avoid and do mental stops." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2403s-2414s])
- **[Hard stops only for trailers]** "the only time I'll use a hard stop is for my trailer positions" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2430s-2435s])
- **[Trailer percentage - 5-10% average]** "on average for trailers, you want anywhere from five to 10%. You can even do 20% on some trailers if the market's really hot." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2494s-2505s])
- **[Trailer after full profit target]** "after this high of day, let's say this was my final kind of profit target right here. After this, this remaining move, I'll probably just have a trailer where it continues to go up along with the trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2440s-2449s])
- **[Trailer as P&L management not technical]** "that's just based off of the risk management of the trade at that point and not the risk management of the actual trade or that's risk management of the P and L size of the trade rather than the technical side of the trade, just because we've already technically had our full profit target. So now we're just trying to milk the trade as much as we can without losing too much more money." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2461s-2475s])
- **[Walking away only for trailer positions in profit]** "the only time I walk away is only for trailer positions that are already in profit. I don't walk away while I'm not in the trade personally" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3678s-3683s])
- **[Walking away after first profit target hit]** "by walking away, what that means is, realistically, it's after your first profit target has been hit, right? It's after your first profit target has been hit because what happens is if the trade starts to consolidate or if the trade starts to move back to your stop loss, you may do things that you wouldn't have done." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3504s-3528s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[Higher timeframe trending makes entries forgiving]** "even on the one minute timeframe, if your entries are a little bit off, if the higher timeframe is with you and it's your, your trading with the higher timeframe momentum, even if your entries are off, you will still have good trades because the higher timeframe will make the trade. It's like a magnet. It'll make the trade work." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2163s-2187s])
- **[Sideways HTF = don't want to trade]** "Do you think Tesla is a trending B or say trending to the upside B trending to the downside or C sideways action? C, C, C, C, C. Exactly. And so this is one of the reasons I don't want to trade Tesla." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2046s-2060s])
- **[Nvidia clear uptrend example]** "on Nvidia, right? This low right here and Nvidia came back, pulled back and then has had an uptrend ever since. So it's so, so easy to trade because on the higher timeframe, it's trending." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2144s-2158s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Trade frequency - 3-5 per week]** "it's about anywhere from, it's around three to five. It's around three to five. That's on average, right?" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1211s-1223s])
- **[One A+ per day max]** "If I'm taking one A plus quality trade per day, right, that's five trades in total. However, some days I might take two trades, some days I might take zero trades" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1228s-1237s])
- **[Trade frequency and size not correlated]** "Your trading size has nothing to do with your trade frequency, right? Your trading size has nothing to do with your trade frequency in terms of your psychology or the market sentiment. Those are two factors that are interchangeable, right? You can have bigger size and still only take one trade. Your trade frequency and the size that you have for trade does not matter. They're not correlated." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1370s-1400s])
- **[Market sentiment determines position size]** "the market sentiment determines your position size." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1158s-1168s])
- **[Size down in choppy markets]** "When we're chopping around in the market, then obviously we want to size down." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1138s-1142s])
- **[Trending market = best to trade]** "if you are in a trending market and you're in a trending market for us, it's going to be the best market to trade." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1130s-1136s])
- **[Choose one setup and master it]** "choose the one thing that you want to get really good at and become a master at that. May that be flags, double top, break and retest, five-minute opening range, opening gap. You know, that's up to you what time frame you want to trade. It's all up to you at the end of the day. I'm giving you the framework. You have to curate the framework to yourself." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3093s-3111s])
- **[Not a signal service - learn to trade yourself]** "this isn't a signal service. Like if you want signals every single day, if that's your goal... if you copy someone else's trades, you can see a little bit of success. Long term, it's just 100% is not going to work. I've never seen someone copy someone's trades for years and remain profitable." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1302s-1332s])
- **[JDub screen sharing - good for beginners, then stop using as crutch]** "the screen is good for new traders in the beginning, 100%. I really do agree that his screen sharing is good in the beginning, but after that, you want to trade by yourself. Look at your screen and just listen to the commentary in the background, right? Because then you're going to have to apply it to yourself, and you're going to have to actually take those trades by yourself without looking at JDub" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1444s-1463s])
- **[Stop after big win - next day caution]** "the next day I came in and I'm like, guys, we just came off of a solid trade today. Unless an A plus opportunity presents itself, I'm not going to trade again because I didn't get overconfident, I didn't get cocky." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3627s-3638s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Max account size for learning - $10K]** "10K is all you need when you're learning to start maximum. Like we're talking about maximum amount of money you need." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3918s-3925s])
- **[Too much money in account warning]** "for the people that have over $10,000 in their account, please, please get rid of that. Like put that money, whatever you have over $10,000, put it into like another investment account and just put it into like the spy or something." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3861s-3877s])
- **[Play with house money to learn faster]** "take the initial money you had, take that out and play with house money, play with house money in your account. I promise you, you will learn faster playing with house money because you don't care about the house money as much because it was never technically yours to begin with." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [4044s-4062s])
- **[Trailer percentage - 5-10% average, up to 20% in hot markets]** "on average for trailers, you want anywhere from five to 10%. You can even do 20% on some trailers if the market's really hot." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2494s-2505s])
- **[NQ moves $5 per tick]** "NQ does move $5 per tick." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3228s-3231s])

## Special Audit Questions

### A/A+ Grading
- **[A+ quality trade - one per day]** "If I'm taking one A plus quality trade per day, right, that's five trades in total." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [1228s-1232s])
- **[A+ opportunity - wait for it]** "you don't get the A plus setup that you've been waiting for, even though you have the thesis, you can't enter because it's not in your system" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [30s-38s])
- **[A+ opportunity - next day after big win]** "Unless an A plus opportunity presents itself, I'm not going to trade again" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [3631s-3634s])

- **[QQQ at all time highs - difficult to trade intraday upside]** "Because we're at all time highs on queues, of course, as we can see, it's extremely difficult to actually trade intraday when we're looking at, you know, obviously profit targets to the upside." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2197s-2215s])

(mastermind-4-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader.txt [2215s-2231s])

# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Definition as fundamental strategy]** "The break in retest... That's the overall like setup or that's within the system. That's the overall fundamental strategy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [295s-308s])

- **[Open strategy vs break-and-retest - key difference]** "The break in retest fundamentally is very simple... However, when we're playing the open, the open strategy is going to be a little bit different than the break in retest you were taught" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [658s-672s])

- **[Open strategy = higher timeframe break-and-retest]** "if you take the same chart, right, and you look at the higher time frame version of that chart on the higher time frame, what might have been happening is that it's retesting the higher time frame level, right? So like a four hour level. So though on the one minute, it looks like it's coming back. It's coming down into a key level and it looks like a reversal trade on the higher time frame. We're still trading that break in retest" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [331s-354s])

- **[Open strategy = lowest risk entry]** "this is just going to be the lowest risk for us to enter based off of the one minute chart" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [354s-356s])

- **[Open strategy - emphasis on key levels, speed, conviction]** "the strategy is different than the break in retest just a little bit because there's more emphasis on key levels. You need to act even faster than you would with the break in retest. And we're looking at retest of levels or patterns rather than looking at the break and retest" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [712s-726s])

- **[Open strategy - no displacement move needed]** "you don't have a strong displacement move. You're simply coming into a level of support. You have to see if the price action is strong there. And then you're you're buying for the move to the upside" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [925s-931s])

- **[Open strategy - time window]** "this type of trade, realistically, the best setup usually occurs within the first 15 minutes of market open. Right. So the first 15 minutes of market open is when we want to look for this. And then after that, after the first 15 minutes, we're only strictly looking for the break and retest. OK. After that, we're strictly looking for the break and retest. This is only for the first 15 minutes" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1003s-1023s])

- **[Open strategy - thesis different from break-and-retest]** "With the break and retest, you're waiting for the trend to confirm. We enter after the the trend is clear on the day. However, playing the open, you enter before a clear trend or intraday price action has occurred. This is because we have a thesis from the higher time frame" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1052s-1073s])

- **[Open strategy - entry trigger = strong price action candle]** "The only thing you're waiting for near that key level. Right. So near the key level that we're coming back down to. The only thing you're waiting for is strong price action. Right. So if you get a hammer stick candle, right, you enter into that trade and you're looking for that potential move to the upside" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1177s-1189s])

- **[Open strategy - V-shape recovery]** "if it's a good setup, it'll come into your level and then V shape recovery out of it" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [861s-863s])

- **[Open strategy - buying low of day potential]** "if you're able to capture this, you know, this, you know, candlestick, you're basically if the trend continues all day, you're basically able to buy the low of the stock, like low of day and ride it all the way" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [881s-892s])

- **[Break-and-retest - normal entry time window]** "sometimes when the break in retest comes, it gives you a couple of minutes right here just to kind of analyze" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [824s-828s])

- **[Break-and-retest - example with time to enter]** "NVIDIA basically pulled back like NVIDIA broke the level and then it pulled back into the level. And it gave you literally like five to 10 minutes to enter in here" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [842s-849s])

- **[Higher timeframe break-and-retest example - Nvidia]** "we see that we had this bottom on a video, we consolidated it, and then we broke below that bottom, right, which was a low on a video, right, it was a lower low, we broke below it, and now we're coming back pre-market and we're retesting that low, what does this mean? This means this is a simple break and retest on the higher timeframe that we could enter into, right, right into the opening of the day, based off of the key level without having to wait for the break and retest on the lower timeframe, because we understand it's a break and retest on the higher timeframe" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2400s-2433s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[Re-adding after scaling - personal preference]** "when you take profit on partial and it bounces back to your entry, why can you not add more? You could add more. That's depending on you and your personal system within your strategy. For me personally, I just, it's just the cycle. That is more about psychology. Are you comfortable adding more knowing that you could go straight back to your stop loss? Or are you comfortable knowing that if it hits your stop loss, you're still be break even for the day? That depends on you and your own psychology, right? You can do that 100%. However, for me personally, right? I like the peace of mind of knowing that if it comes back to my stop, I'll still be green on that trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2748s-2782s])

- **[Re-adding - scenario-dependent]** "Now it depends on certain scenarios. I will add more on certain scenarios, different trades, but for the simplicity of this, right? We're just gonna talk about one entry every time rather than scaling in on your entries" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2782s-2794s])

## 4. Order Blocks

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Pre-market and after-hours included in levels]** "every time I chart the pre-market and after hours is on. So every level that I draw everything, it's with it's including the pre-market and the after hours" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [411s-419s])

- **[Open strategy - key level = higher timeframe level]** "The key level support that we made on the higher time frame level, if we see strong price action here, we need to enter much faster than we would enter a break in retest" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [812s-821s])

- **[Open strategy - levels to watch]** "pre-market low, pre-market high even, you know, this could be a previous day support level, whatever it is" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [378s-382s])

- **[Profit targets - same as break-and-retest]** "high of day slash low of day key levels and pattern high and low" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1441s-1445s])

- **[Profit targets - HOD/LOD too close at open]** "high of day might be a little bit too close. Therefore, we need to make sure that the profit target is obviously not going to be a negative risk to reward. Therefore, sometimes it might not be high of day slash low of day compared to the break and retest because high of day and low of day might be too close going into the open" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1570s-1583s])

- **[Profit targets - higher timeframe key level]** "The key level we're looking for is going to be a higher time frame key level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1583s-1586s])

- **[Profit targets - pattern high/low]** "the pattern high and low is going to be if, for example, if you have a flag in the top of that flag pattern might be the final profit target you have" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1591s-1597s])

- **[VWAP - not used]** "VWAP is I don't even use VWAP in my regular trading. So I'm not going to use VWAP for this either. Right. Once again, I've told you I've said this before multiple times. VWAP is only there for me to buy above and sell below. Right. Like VWAP is really something that I for my personal trading. I rarely look at VWAP" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1251s-1275s])

- **[VWAP - minimal importance for open]** "VWAP could matter less" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1285s-1287s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Open strategy - best setup in first 15 minutes]** "this type of trade, realistically, the best setup usually occurs within the first 15 minutes of market open" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1003s-1006s])

- **[After first 15 minutes - break-and-retest only]** "after that, after the first 15 minutes, we're only strictly looking for the break and retest" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1014s-1020s])

- **[FOMC days - avoid]** "I'm not allowed to play FOMC days on most FOMC days, especially when it's like big days like today, for example" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [92s-98s])

- **[FOMC days - choppy]** "historically it's going to be choppy. So I didn't want to risk that" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [87s-92s])

- **[FOMC days - journaling tip]** "during big news events like today, I would take screenshots of the chart... one out of five FOMC days might have a trending day. But, you know, the other four are going to be choppy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [148s-193s])

- **[Earnings days - avoid]** "earning day you avoid. If it's the day before earnings, you avoid that day" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3493s-3498s])

- **[Simple news events - okay]** "if it's like a news event and it's like a pretty simple news event, you don't have to avoid the news event. That's a pretty simple news event, right? And we're talking about earnings. You should avoid earnings, FOMC, stuff like that. But if it's like a simple news event that's already kind of factored in pre-market, then that's fine" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3442s-3461s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling - purpose]** "the reason we scale is once again, so we can hold it to that full profit target, right? Because if I didn't scale in and out of this trade, right, I simply wouldn't be able to hold it to that profit target" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2004s-2012s])

- **[Scaling - example with psych levels]** "we would scale F420s here as well and then we would probably have one more scale at around 420s, that whole psych number, and then we would hit our full profit target" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1958s-1964s])

- **[Scaling - helps sit through consolidation]** "Notice how we do have to sit through a little bit of consolidation here, right, and this is exactly why you wanna take those scales, right, it's very, very important that you take those scales so you can sit through this kind of consolidation period" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2537s-2546s])

- **[Scaling - peace of mind]** "I like the peace of mind of knowing that if it comes back to my stop, I'll still be green on that trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2776s-2780s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Heiken Ashi for HTF pattern identification]** "I like to use the Heiken Ashi candles. Heiken Ashi takes the average of the stock candle to create candlesticks and filter out market noise. This helps clear the noise and identify patterns on the higher time frame much more clearly" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [468s-480s])

- **[Heiken Ashi - not for trading]** "I personally right don't use the Heiken Ashi candlesticks to actually trade. OK, I if if I was to use it right, it's only for pattern and chart identification" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [593s-600s])

- **[Heiken Ashi - smooths chart]** "it blocks out a lot of the noise, a lot of gaps that you see on the on the daily time frame. And it'll just smooth out the chart so you can see the pattern" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [603s-609s])

- **[Heiken Ashi - price action not readable]** "the price action on the Heiken Ashi candles are not good. Like you can. There's not really, you know, anything that you can read from price action, in my opinion, that gives you an edge from Heiken Ashi candles. Right. It's simply to identify trend and pattern identification" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [623s-635s])

- **[HTF timeframe - 1 hour or 4 hour]** "one thing that I personally like to use for myself when I'm identifying patterns on the higher time frame is simply just the one hour or four hour. And then I like to look at the high Kenashie candles. Now, of course, the higher the time frame, the better the move is going to be" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1695s-1707s])

- **[HTF pattern identification - Heiken Ashi makes patterns visible]** "it shows you very, very clearly different type of pattern setups and recognition of those patterns that you simply could not see on the regular chart" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1724s-1732s])

- **[HTF pattern - bear flag example on SPY]** "you can clearly see this bear flag forming on SPY here. We have a very, very strong move and then the bear flag forming. Now with Heiken Ashi, we were able to identify the pattern" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1797s-1804s])

- **[HTF pattern - aggressive entries justified]** "we can take those aggressive entries because we understand the higher timeframe aligns with our shorter timeframe entries" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1830s-1834s])

- **[HTF pattern - wedge example on Tesla]** "On the one hour timeframe, we see this wedge to the downside, and then we also see this upper trend line on the wedge as well. Overall, Tesla does have a very, very strong downtrend, and we are forming a wedge. Now the wedge could break in either direction, and therefore, for us, what we would be waiting for to play the open or the higher timeframe is a break of that wedge pattern" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2114s-2135s])

- **[HTF - thesis before day]** "I always talk about what my thesis is going into the day. Now, as you guys know, I don't like to have a bias going into the day, but if there's a very strong higher timeframe pattern set up that's going on, I will talk about it, and I will put a little bit more emphasis on that pattern" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2606s-2618s])

- **[HTF - not every day]** "these higher timeframe patterns happen all the time, not every single day will they occur, but they do happen all the time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2630s-2634s])

- **[HTF - gives conviction]** "the higher timeframe, you need to know it no matter what. Even if you're playing the open or the break and retest, it just gives you that more conviction to enter in or not enter into the trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3163s-3173s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Beginners - focus on break-and-retest only]** "in the beginning, if you're just focusing on the break in retest, right, then only focus on the break and retest. Do not go and look for other strategies. If you're not comfortable playing the open, you don't have to play the open" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [680s-692s])

- **[Open strategy requires more experience]** "you're going to have to have a little bit more experience to play the open. I personally believe that you're going to have to have a little bit more experience playing the open with this strategy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1193s-1198s])

- **[Price action is key for all strategies]** "The price action is what's telling me if buyers and sellers are there to actually move the stock. OK. Therefore, for us, we need to have strong price action candle for anything break and retest higher time frame. Five minute strategy. Any strategy within my playbook requires strong price action" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1204s-1225s])

- **[A+ setup example - Nvidia]** "this was a really an A plus setup. If I had personally, like if I was trading today, and it wasn't an FMC day, this was such an easy trade to capture right here. Because you literally just wait for a strong price action candle. You get that strong price action candle at 955. Your stop loss here literally just had to be this pivot right here, this pivot high at 934. So you had a pretty tight stop loss right here because the trend is still to the upside. And then your profit target was literally just that all time highs play on Nvidia. And then you could obviously ride the trailers, which would have gave you a little bit more, but this was a 3.11 trade based off of the higher timeframe breakout" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3124s-3161s])

- **[A+ setup - FOMC disqualifier]** "I did talk about that Nvidia A plus setup that we had today. Now, because it was FOMC, it wasn't an A plus setup for me just because of the news" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [76s-83s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Example trade - 9.17 R]** "this complete trade would result in an about 9.17 trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1919s-1923s])

- **[Example trade - 8.23 R:R]** "this would be an 8.23 risk to reward trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1953s-1955s])

- **[Example trade - 4.35 R:R]** "this would be a 4.35 risk-to-reward trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2321s-2324s])

- **[Example trade - 2.0 R:R]** "this would be about a 2.0 risk to reward trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2508s-2511s])

- **[Example trade - 2.8 R:R]** "this would have been a 2.8 art rate" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3091s-3093s])

- **[Example trade - 3.11 R:R]** "this was a 3.11 trade based off of the higher timeframe breakout" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3158s-3161s])

- **[Risk potential - small risk for large reward]** "technically we're only risking $100, $200 to make a potential of two, three, four R" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2738s-2741s])

- **[FOMC - 1 out of 5 trending]** "one out of five FOMC days might have a trending day. But, you know, the other four are going to be choppy" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [190s-193s])

- **[Trade timing - 70% before 10:30]** "Most of the trade, I'd say 70% of the trade worked out right before 10.30" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2091s-2093s])

## 11. Stop Loss (Open Strategy)

- **[Stop loss - higher timeframe level, not intraday]** "the risk level that we use for playing the open is going to be a higher time frame level. OK. And this is different because the chart has informed. Therefore, break of structure hasn't been identified. Or change of character intraday. Therefore, you need to give the trade room to breathe" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1359s-1372s])

- **[Stop loss - need room to breathe at open]** "because it is the open, the stock will dip... right below and then push up. Right. And that's because right into the open, there's more volatility. There's more volume. So the stock is going to be a little bit more volatile to have that dip. And therefore, you need to, especially in the open, even if you don't give your stock, your trade room to breathe later on in the day, which I 100 percent think you should. But even if you don't do that and you've been seeing success with very, very tight stop losses. Right. With the break in retest or sorry, with the play the open, you need to give a room to breathe just because it's right into the open" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1382s-1418s])

- **[Stop loss - example: break above pre-market low]** "your stop loss here would be a close simply above this level right here now, right? And you can have your stop above the previous candle that broke, right, with that 931 candle can be your stop. OK, and this is because if we do break back above this level of pre-market low, right, we could see some consolidation before we see another move down, right? Or we could come back all the way up here and retest this previous higher timeframe level and therefore we simply just don't wanna be in that trade after it does break above" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1926s-1953s])

- **[Stop loss - example: break of day's high]** "our stop loss here would have to be a break of that high, right, so a break of the actual day's high because until we break that rate, we still have that bearish, bearish sentiment on the stock" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2492s-2500s])

- **[Stop loss - example: pre-market low for Apple]** "your stop loss in this scenario right here, literally just had to be that low, the pre-market low right here, right? So pre-market low looking for two tens. So that gave the trade some room to breathe. But at the same time, you still had at least a two R multiple" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2999s-3012s])

## 12. QQQ / Index Confluence

- **[QQQ - edge still comes from QQQ]** "our edge still comes from QQ. However, the higher timeframe, if the trend continues, especially after a breakout pattern, right? It'll have the best risk to reward" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2724s-2734s])

- **[QQQ - important but more emphasis on HTF for open]** "Yes, it's important to look at QQ, and I talked about that as well, right? It's important to look at QQ for the overall price action of the day as well. However, with the higher timeframe, especially playing the open, we're putting a little bit more emphasis on the higher timeframe, right? We're putting it on the higher timeframe rather than QQ" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2709s-2722s])

- **[Market context important]** "it's really important to have that market context in mind as well, however, just playing the open with the higher timeframe is a very, very good way to get a high probability trade-in right into the open" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2584s-2593s])

## 13. Heiken Ashi - Additional Details

- **[Heiken Ashi - how it works]** "Heiken Ashi takes the average of the stock candle to create candlesticks and filter out market noise" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [470s-476s])

- **[Heiken Ashi - trend identification]** "if the trend is just continuing to the upside, it will stay the color it is. And it helps to identify trend much more easier than, you know, looking at it from a regular candle" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [563s-568s])

- **[Heiken Ashi - do not trade with them]** "We don't want to trade the Heiken Ashi candlesticks" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [581s-584s])

- **[Heiken Ashi - use for pattern identification first, then mark levels on regular chart]** "you can do this first and then mark out your key levels and everything like that. But I like to just go on the AJ candles just to identify any patterns that might be forming that I simply cannot see within the actual bar chart" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1774s-1785s])

# EXTRACTION FROM: mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[A+ entry definition - candle breaks level and fails]** "an A plus entry like you guys have seen me talk about before is when the candle actually breaks your level tries to see and the reason this is an A plus entry by the way is because if a candle actually breaks the level and still can't break and comes back down it shows that there's too many sellers for the buyers to actually push it up rather than when it's just consolidating underneath that level because then you know the those breakup buyers will come and they'll push up the candle" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3485s-3508s])
- **[Displacement - key for A+ break]** "the first time tesla came up and broke it didn't have any displacement right so right here no displacement but the second time we broke out which is right here right this is the second time we broke out on tesla and this time you can see that we actually had this displacement move which means that the we actually broke above the level and a candlestick isn't touching the level where all of these candlesticks are touching the level and then we pushed back down with a strong price action candle" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4617s-4647s])
- **[First break vs second break - displacement difference]** "notice how this first one wasn't a solid A plus break and retest trade the reason this is because though it broke up with an impulsive candle it consolidated right so theoretically it can still use it as resistance because it's still touching the level but when it breaks above and it actually breaks without touching the level and it shows impulsivity then it comes back and pulls back and it closes above 180 858 then it becomes an A plus move for the upside" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4666s-4691s])
- **[Entry timing - wait for candle close]** "I would enter in on the 955 candle so I would wait for the 954 to close but the 955 it doesn't really matter I would just enter in on the 955" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4701s-4712s])
- **[Stop loss - only change when market structure changes]** "our stop loss doesn't change a lot of people change their stop loss our stop loss doesn't change you guys have seen this with me as well I only change stop loss when we change market structure" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4195s-4206s])
- **[Stop loss - break above VWAP/level for shorts]** "our support where we would put our stop is basically a break above that vwap slash 202 level and that's just because this is where it's came down stalled down to right so if we broke above that we would have to cut the trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3706s-3714s])
- **[Scaling - when QQQ hits target, scale on stock]** "the way I would scale on this is basically when qqq hits a low of day that's where I would scale on tesla as well" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3642s-3652s])
- **[Scaling - take some off at key levels, not all]** "we do want to still have some key levels that we want to scale out on" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3661s-3665s])
- **[Cut remaining when QQQ breaks structure]** "you would cut it when it broke below that 295.54.45 for the rest of the trailers at about 1035... that's where the market structure broke on on qqq and that's where you can cut the rest of your trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3952s-3967s])
