## 5. Key Levels
- **[Pre-market levels - gap up scenario]** "when we're gapping up overnight we understand that we're playing pre-market levels" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3732s-3736s])
- **[Previous day high as resistance]** "this level of resistance at 162 17s right because that was the previous day high" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4316s-4321s])
- **[Opening candle print as key level]** "market structure broke on spy when it broke below this 401 27 level right the opening candle print" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4033s-4038s])
- **[Psych level as profit target - SPY 400]** "that 400 psych level like we know on spy gives us a really good risk to reward just because that's that level has been used as resistance and support on spy so much intraday" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4164s-4174s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Summer - lower volume/liquidity]** "it used to be where every time we go into summer there's a lot there's very little volume low liquidity because most people are just on vacation... you do want to kind of have a chill summer overall like you don't really want to size up too much in the summer" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4878s-4904s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Trailers - mental stops with alerts]** "the trailers are usually going to be the mental stops as well I just have alerts on my chart... for the trailers on my trailer positions those stop orders are usually just going to be mental as well" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4801s-4817s])
- **[Market orders vs limit orders for exits]** "market orders are fine honestly like it depends if you don't have big contract size market orders are completely fine you may get filled like five cents under which is fine I just use limit sells just because if I'm coming in with big orders I will definitely experience that slippage but in the most terms if you're selling into strength market orders are going to be fine" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4777s-4801s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[HTF thesis - entry must not invalidate it]** "if it gives me the lowest risk and doesn't invalidate the higher time frame thesis yes I would enter" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4951s-4959s])
- **[HTF levels as magnet targets]** "the higher time frame profit targets that can be used as magnet levels" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4012s-4017s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[A+ setup definition - requires QQQ context + HTF thesis + HTF level]** "a regular break and retest you could enter based off of without qqq context so an A plus setup would have to have a qqq context a higher time frame thesis a higher time frame thesis a qqq context and the level you're entering on is a higher timeframe level as well. Whereas a regular break and retest may have the QGQ context, but it's missing one of those main factors." (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [5125s-5150s])
- **[A+ setup - requires strong momentum]** "this stock you're trading has really strong momentum because as you know we're momentum traders and we need that strong momentum to ensure that the stock that we're trading has you know it works out right away" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3387s-3402s])
- **[A+ setup - relative weakness/strength]** "the number one thing that we see on tesla is we have relative weakness right into the open now this is very important for an A plus setup" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3382s-3391s])
- **[A+ setup - even A+ setups can fail to hit full target]** "sometimes even A plus setups don't work out in full in that you know the full technically profit target... it doesn't work out like that sometimes but this is still a really good trade for the risk reward" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3693s-3701s])
- **[A+ setup - must have entry signal, even if thesis is correct]** "we may have a proper thesis but if our risk reward isn't there if our if it's not aligning with our strategy even if it works out with what we said and it's not giving us an entry signal we still don't enter" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4498s-4512s])
- **[A+ setup - must be within defined strategies]** "if the break and retest isn't there right if it's not within one of my strategies which is the either the opening play opening drive like any of the opening plays or within the break and retest five minute pre-market high or whatever right if it's not within those strategies I will not take the trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4529s-4547s])
- **[Ranging market - switch to futures or stop trading]** "if the stocks that I'm trading are in a ranging market I most likely will switch over to the future side of trading if that's also ranging then I'll probably honestly just stop trading for a bit" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [5025s-5036s])
- **[Most money comes in 1-2 month spans per year]** "the most money you make as a trader will come every year within a one to two month span you'll make a lot of money... it'll be like two weeks you'll do really really really good make a lot of money then you'll kind of chill out" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [5046s-5069s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE (no new win rates, R:R, risk %, drawdown, or gap fill stats beyond what was previously extracted)

## 11. Psychology / Discipline (NEW TOPIC)
- **[Three types of discipline - Preventive]** "preventive discipline... is when you're establishing expectations guidelines and behaviors... it's just a rules checklist" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [737s-839s])
- **[Three types of discipline - Supportive]** "supportive discipline occurs after the problem has occurred and a warning may be in place... if you take over those two trades per day let's say you take the third trade or the fourth trade if it's your first time doing it... you give yourself a yellow card" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [839s-1050s])
- **[Three types of discipline - Corrective]** "corrective discipline if you fail to change behavior after supportive discipline this is when corrective discipline must be used this has consequences" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [956s-978s])
- **[Corrective discipline - 5km run example]** "every single time that I broke my rules or if I break my rules right what I do is I go on a five kilometer run... I don't want to do a five kilometer run that's something I really genuinely... it's good for me at the same time" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [1102s-1131s])
- **[Corrective discipline - should benefit you long-term]** "you need to think about something that you can do that would technically benefit you in the long run however you really genuinely don't want to do it" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [1164s-1181s])
- **[Journaling as corrective discipline]** "whenever I used to take a lot of trades in the day... I knew I had to journal them at the end of the day and for me that journaling was such a rigorous process... that itself made me want to take less trades" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [1211s-1337s])
- **[Focus on weaknesses, not strengths]** "I would personally focus on the weakness... if you're already good at something why would I focus on it more... the return on your investment is much greater because if you can increase this weakness to a strength then all of a sudden you really don't have that many weaknesses" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [2102s-2186s])
- **[Start small - scale up only after consistency]** "you always want to start off small with whatever you do... if you can get to your desired state then you can increase in scale up" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [2492s-2509s])
- **[Don't try to make back losses quickly]** "in the beginning you don't even worry about making back the losses just worry about being consistent" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [2690s-2696s])
- **[Execute A+ setups - no excuse if you have edge + backtesting]** "if your rules are there if your strategy is there if your profitable edge is there if you've backtested if you've done everything that I've told you so far there should be no way that you do not execute an A plus trade" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [2919s-2933s])
- **[Don't trade and just watch - observe option contracts]** "open up your broker have whatever option contract I'm going to trade open... have your chart open... look at how the option contract moves and what I want you to do with the chart is look at how the chart moves as well... see how much of a percentage right you're missing out on because of the fact that you're simply too scared to enter" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3159s-3225s])
- **[Real account teaches emotions paper can't]** "I think the real account will teach you emotions that the option contracts or the paper account wouldn't" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3252s-3260s])

## 12. QQQ / Index Confluence
- **[QQQ context required for A+ setup]** "an A plus setup would have to have a qqq context" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [5130s-5134s])
- **[QQQ market structure break confirms entry]** "a break of market structure rate would essentially occur on qqq if you break below this level right here" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3521s-3533s])
- **[QQQ stop loss - break of level]** "what we can use for qqq as a stop loss is this break of this level right here 292.65" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3630s-3636s])
- **[QQQ holding support = stock can push]** "if q's essentially breaks underneath this 294.57 which it does look like chop... but if it basically just held that 294.57 that we had pre-market we understand that q's is going to be strong enough for tesla to push" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [3850s-3867s])
- **[QQQ and SPY - can use both for relative strength/weakness]** "you can use once again if you're playing like for example qqq you can use spy to see relative strength and weakness vice versa" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4068s-4078s])

## 13. Displacement / FVG
- **[Displacement - candle not touching the level]** "we actually broke above the level and a candlestick isn't touching the level where all of these candlesticks are touching the level" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4633s-4643s])
- **[No fixed displacement value - per trade basis]** "what is a meaningful displacement value given the stock's price are different so there isn't really a value that I would say it's more you have to you just have to look at the stock... it's a per trade basis type of thing on the displacement I personally don't have a value that I would give a stock for the displacement" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4832s-4870s])

## 14. Indicators
- **[No indicators - price action only]** "I don't use any indicators personally I just use price action strictly" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [5112s-5119s])
- **[Heiken Ashi - not used anymore]** "I usually use just candles I used to use ha candles a little bit more back in the day I don't use them as much now I only use candles for me myself personally" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [4744s-4758s])

## 15. Backtesting
- **[3-4 months enough for conviction]** "realistically you want to do as much as possible right even even doing the last three to four months will give you enough data in my opinion and enough data to have conviction not to give to show a profitable edge but to have conviction in your trading" (mastermind-4-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline.txt [5008s-5022s])

## 6. Time-of-day
- **[Days to avoid - Quadwitching]** "quadwitching... simultaneous expiration of four different types of financial contracts... this happens four times... the market becomes a little bit more choppier... trading momentum is one of the worst things you can do" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [10397s-10450s])
- **[Days to avoid - J-Pow/FOMC]** "j pow days and fomc days you do not want to trade... from 9:30 to basically 2 p.m. when that news event occurs you see chop and then the second the news event occurs you'll see a big push a big drop and then chop again" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [10472s-10496s])
- **[Days to avoid - Earnings]** "earnings... you don't trade this it's because you get iv crush" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [10515s-10522s])
- **[Friday trading warning]** "on fridays you want to be very careful with your size and your entries... do not trade zero dte after 1 to 2 p.m." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [7516s-7533s])
- **[Live trading schedule]** "JDubb starts his live trading at 8 AM Eastern every single day for the pre-market session... we actually go on voice at 9:15 Eastern for the actual live trading" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [3692s-3700s])
- **[Workshop schedule]** "Monday to Friday... it will be 5:30 PM Eastern time for the live workshops" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [3674s-3688s])

- **[Stop loss - candle close rule]** "I only get stopped out on the close of the candle... once the candle closes body is below my stop... if it wicks... I'm not getting stopped out on the way" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [8626s-8669s])

## 9. Trade selection
- **[Goal - seeing setups]** "gain the confidence and the ability to see setups in real time" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [4986s-4994s])
- **[Goal - end of month]** "you should be able to fully 100% do what I do when I do it... understand where a good entry is, why there's a good entry, where your profit target should be, where your stop loss should be, how to mark out key levels, how to look for good A+ setups, how to avoid bad setups" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_1.txt [5096s-5118s])

# Extraction from mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Bodies vs Wicks, Displacement)
- **Break confirmation via candle close** "if a candle close does not occur, okay. Candle itself, right. Let's just draw a candle right here. Right. Pretend this is a candle. Right. If this is a candle close and this is the resistance, if the candle, the resistance, that's fine. Right. Because this shows that buyers have confirmed the move." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [434s-452s])
- **Wick break vs body break distinction** "if you see that same kind of trade, right, where you have this big wick now and the body can't low that level of resistance, right. This means a seller still step back. Right. Sellers still stepped in here. Buyers are here too. Right. Buyers are that body. Right. So the body's telling us that the buyers are indeed here. However, sellers are still stronger." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [457s-478s])
- **Displacement definition** "Displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken, the move after break." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4018s-4028s])
- **Displacement as A+ indicator** "The reason this is, is because look at displacement on this move. Displacement simply refers to the fact that after a key level has been broken, the move after break. Okay, so this displacement of the stock shows that this is a really good example for a downside move." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4014s-4037s])

### Valid Retest (Touch/Zone, Max Wait)
- **Retest of previous resistance turning to support** "the best risk to reward comes where? This previous resistance. Right. So the stock has to come back down to this previous resistance. This turns into support." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [526s-540s])
- **Why resistance becomes support** "when we have this resistance. Right. Hold the stock down. Why? Because their stop loss is right above. Right. Their stop loss is right above. Okay. The same thing happens when we come back. Buyers are trying to defend this pivot point because their stop loss is right below right here." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [571s-594s])

### Entry Trigger (Hammer/Shooting Star)
- **Hammer candles for longs** "I personally like to look for hammer candles when I'm going long." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4104s-4109s])
- **Inverted hammer/shooting star for shorts** "I like to look for inverted hammer candles. The reason that I like these candles so much more, and I prefer them, is because what they show you, right, is someone buyers tried to bring this stock above this level, and as we can see, we touched this level, but the second we touched it, we had liquidity, sell order sitting here, and therefore, we broke back down, and we couldn't break above. So, we actually came back down, sellers were in control, and we closed the candle at the low." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4109s-4141s])
- **Wait for candle close before entry** "we want to wait for the candle to close before we take that trade, and we want a bearish handle. Okay, both those things need to happen." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4042s-4057s])
- **Neutral candle disqualifier** "We don't want a neutral candle. We want a bearish candle to enter off of." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4700s-4706s])

### Stop Placement
- **Stop loss = break of key level or trend/market structure** "your risk level always will be a break of a key level, right? Or of the trend or market structure, right? So your risk will always be a break of a key level or a break of the trend. Notice how I didn't say your risk level is based off your risk to reward." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1586s-1605s])
- **Wait for candle close for stop level** "we have to wait candle to close for our risk level." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1623s-1631s])
- **Mental stop loss, not hard stop** "I don't have a hard stop loss. Okay. I do not loss rather I have a mental stop loss." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1792s-1799s])
- **Give stop a little room** "I don't like to have, you know, a stop loss that's right, you know, right at that level. I like to give it a little bit of room." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4176s-4185s])
- **Opening price as stop reference** "opening price is a big factor as well. Opening price is something that the stock loves to respect during the day, because that's just simply where the stock opened up during that day. And so, we're going to use the opening price right here, and that's where we're going to put our stop loss." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4185s-4204s])
- **If risk level not obvious, don't take trade** "if you don't know where that risk is, if you should put your stop loss, you are forcing the trade, okay? You are forcing the trade and you should not be in that trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2168s-2178s])
- **Should determine risk in seconds** "you should be able to look at the chart for a couple seconds and be able to determine the risk. If it's not clear, like I said, there's no reason made." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2189s-2196s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **Profit targets = high of day, low of day, key higher timeframe levels** "profit targets for myself are a high of day, low of day, key level, and VWAP, right? Now VWAP, this actually shouldn't be here anymore, because this I don't use this anymore. So let's get rid of VWAP. However, high of day, low of day, and key higher timeframe levels. Okay, these are going to be my profit targets." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2308s-2328s])
- **First scale always at HOD/LOD** "my first profit targets are high of day slash low of day every time, okay? And then from there, profit targets are going to be higher time frame key levels." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2399s-2414s])
- **Scaling percentage varies by market** "if the higher time frame or the market itself is ripping in general, right, on high of day, I might only take 20%, 20 to 30%, sometimes even 10%, right? Depending on how strong the market is... However, if the market is consolidating like this, right, and it's a choppier market, and we come up to take at least 50, right, 50 to 60% off just at high of day itself." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2464s-2507s])
- **RR must remain 2R minimum** "you're not flexible with your RR, your RR still needs to remain a 2R, right? So for at least your final targets, right, it's still multiple, but you need to be flexible with your scaling." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2524s-2533s])

### First-Break vs Late Breaks
- **Both examples can be good in their own scenario** "Both of these examples are good in their own scenario. Okay. Now reason why number one is good is because what happened in number one, right? Though yet, oh yes, we were uptrending right in the beginning. Okay. We broke this level of liquidity here. Okay. We broke this level of liquidity, but what happened when we broke, right? Came in, right? Sellers were strong and they came in and we broke what? This previous higher low. When you break this happens, you now have a change of character, right? Therefore this confirmed to us that yes, the move is to the downside and their risk entry right here, or this pullback, right, is pulling back into what? Consolidation. Therefore, this is a low risk entry because we know for a fact that if it back into this level right here, right, we can have a stop loss pretty close because if it breaks back, then it'll just consolidate." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1345s-1401s])
- **Number two: starting day weak, double bottom retest** "Now number two, the reason number two is also a good as first of all, we start the day week. Okay. So number two actually ends up starting the day week. Okay. Now when we start the day week, we know that, okay, the main objective for today, right, is that we already broke this level of support. We broke this level of support. We came back, but you can of the high that we had, right? So that intraday high, we never broke above. Okay. So where we started or the opening price, we knew. So when we break back down, though we held this higher low, right? Though we held this higher low right here, when we, it confirmed that low of day move or this double bottom, right? So we had a double bottom. Not only did it retest one time, right? So it retested this specific level one time right here, right? But it moved down and we waited for confirmation for that double bottom retest." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1417s-1483s])

## 5. Key Levels

### PDH/PDL, PMH/PML
- **Previous day high and low are primary levels** "I draw the previous day high, they draw the previous day. Okay. Now after the previous day high and previous day low, I'll mark out the key levels that I see." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5572s-5584s])
- **Pre-market high and low offer liquidity** "pre-market high and pre-market low offer a lot of liquidity while you're trading in the day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5195s-5208s])

### Hierarchy
- **Start with higher timeframe (daily, 4hr, 1hr)** "we start off with the higher time frame, the higher time frame consists of the daily, the daily, and the one hour." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3012s-3025s])
- **Daily: pivot levels** "on the daily four hour and one hour, we're looking for what pivot levels, right, whatever, we're looking for pivot levels, right, so, we're looking for pivot levels on the daily, the four hour, and the one hour, this means we're looking for where they had an impulsive move, or consolidation." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3028s-3045s])
- **4hr/1hr: patterns (flag, consolidation)** "on the four hour and on the one hour time frame, we want to look for, okay, or sorry, patterns, not pivot levels, patterns, right, now, these patterns may be, once again, patterns don't have to be this huge, a flag pattern, or it could be, you know, just consolidation." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3116s-3142s])
- **Then 1-minute for execution** "I look at the one minute to actually execute the trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1056s-1063s])

### Zones vs Ticks
- **Draw levels based on context (body vs wick)** "I believe you should draw it based off of context, okay, a lot of traders, right, they only draw it on one, so they would only draw it on the body, or they would only draw it on the wick, both, right, depending on the context of the trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3647s-3669s])

### Liquidity
- **Sellers need to cover at breakouts** "the sellers need to cover. And what that means is when you break this level of resistance. Okay. And you break the stock to the upside. This means that obviously buyers are stepping in. Right. We know that. But in terms of the sellers, right, this means that sellers need to cover their position." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [354s-369s])
- **Stop losses create liquidity** "if we're buyers, right, what you would do as a seller here, right? If your stop loss rate, you have your entry right here, your stop loss is most likely going to be, let's just assume it's right here. Have a stop loss level right here. So if the stock is breaking above this level, you just got stopped out of the trade. Sorry. Right. And trade that means even more aggressive buying is happening. Okay. However, because you're getting stopped out of the trade, there's a lot of liquidity built up here." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [369s-400s])

### Key Levels Should Be Obvious
- **Key levels should be obvious with multiple confluences** "your key levels should be so obvious. Okay. You're obvious meaning they have multiple confluences and timeframe alignment." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2005s-2019s])
- **Keep charts clean - 5-6 levels max** "I like the least amount of levels possible, however, sometimes, you know, even you'll see me having a lot more levels, right, sometimes you'll see me having, like, maybe eight levels, that's a lot for me, that's a lot, a lot, you'll see about, how much is this, one, two, three, four, five, like, around five to six levels at most." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3772s-3790s])
- **Draw levels as day progresses** "you can draw levels as the day's progressing, right, like this is a stock should retest this high and move to the upside, right, so you can draw new levels as the day progresses, you just don't want to go crazy with it, drawing every pivot level." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3248s-3262s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

### News Days
- **Avoid trading before 10am on news days** "we try to not trade before 10 o'clock, especially on news days." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1892s-1897s])
- **News candles can whip** "Sometimes what will happen with our trades, right? After we have some realized profit or whatever, right? It'll come so hard on the one minute and then zoom back to the upside, right? That happens on news primarily." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1901s-1911s])

### Chart Timeframe Rules
- **Timeframe hierarchy: daily, 4hr, 1hr, 1min** "I look at the daily time for four hour timeframe. And I'm going to talk about these more in detail when we look at the examples, four hour timeframe, one hour timeframe, one minute timeframe." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1028s-1041s])
- **Skip 5min/10min/15min - too much noise** "the reason I skip from the daily four hour, one hour to one minute, and I skip everything asking why not the five minute is because then you just add too much noise, right? Because we have to, you have to remember, we have to draw pivot levels, right? And for all of these timeframes, for myself, we're going to talk about this again later when we were talking about key levels later on in this workshop, right? But key levels and the five minute and the 10 minute, it's not going to give you that much more information when you've already looked at the daily, the four hour, the one minute." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1129s-1164s])
- **5min used only to guide in trade, not for entry** "Sometimes what I'll do is I'll look at the five minute to guide me in that trade, right? Because the noise, which that could happen, it's a lower timeframe. And so the five minute, sometimes we'll get rid of that noise for me. Okay. The five minute will get used that only to guide me in the trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1174s-1196s])
- **Extended trading hours always on** "I always have extended trading hours on, right? I always have hours on it. I don't use regular trading hours. I use extended trading hours as well." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5688s-5692s])

## 7. Exits

### Scaling %
- **Three contracts: one at HOD, one at higher timeframe level, one runner** "if you have three contracts, one a high of day, and this is for like, on a high of day, right, pushes, right, higher timeframe consolidation, right, second one at a higher timeframe consolidation, right, and then you let the third one having stop loss trailing stop loss means you don't have a specific profit target for it." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2625s-2650s])

### Breakeven (When)
- **Move stop to breakeven after breaking a key low/high** "when we're looking at our stop loss, right, we're not going to change our stop loss until we break this low, because once we break this low, then our stop simply just becomes breakeven, okay? Because once we break this low, and this is based off of trend two, this doesn't happen every single time, because sometimes it really depends on the chart context, but in this chart context, right, once we break this low, we create a new lower low, and this becomes a new lower high. So, if we break this, the stock will actually just reverse, right? Theoretically, it will reverse, and therefore, we can just break even on this trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4240s-4278s])

### Position Management
- **First scale makes trade breakeven** "the second it comes, I'll scale up some and then I like my, you know, second scale, whatever. But the second I get my first scale is really just a break even trade for me depending on the type of setup." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4848s-4859s])
- **Most profitable trades within 10-15 minutes** "most of my profitable trades are usually within 10 to 15 minutes. Like they're much, much below 15 minutes and that's just most of my profitable trades, right? And that's why I don't like sitting around waiting for trades." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4832s-4844s])

## 8. HTF Application

### Weekly/Daily Levels
- **Daily timeframe for overall trend** "the daily timeframe specifically, the overall trend of the market is, okay. I'm looking at what the market wants to do. How did that daily candle close? Are buyers in control? Are sellers in control from heat to the four hour and one hour timeframe? And I'll create a thesis." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [1067s-1081s])
- **HTF thesis determines direction bias** "from the daily timeframe, what we saw, the four-hour timeframe, the one-hour timeframe, we saw some consolidation, but overall, the trend is still to the downside, right. We're still breaking those lows." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3969s-3979s])

### Multi-Day Holds
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### HTF Overriding Intraday
- **Don't trade if stock in HTF consolidation range** "if a stock looks like this on the four hour time frame, I'm going to mark out the high and the low of this consolidation, and what this shows me intraday, right, time for when the stock opens up, let's say the stock's gonna open up here, if it's in this range, I'm not trading it, not trading it whatsoever." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3052s-3067s])

## 9. Trade Selection

### A/A+/B/C Grading Criteria
- **A+ setup requires strong displacement** "this retest right here is an A plus setup retest. Okay, this is an A plus setup retest. The reason this is, is because look at displacement on this move." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [4007s-4028s])
- **A+ requires strong trend established + HTF alignment** "we have a very strong trend established rate and also on the higher time frame, this trend is the exact same bearish. Okay. So on the higher time frame, we're bearish. And then we have this trend is we're looking for as a retest from here." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [5315s-5328s])

### Confluence
- **Compare individual stocks to QQQ for relative strength/weakness** "I get market context by being able to identify intraday relative strength and weakness by comparing the individual stocks to the indices." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2768s-2775s])
- **QQQ alignment example with Tesla** "if I'm trading Tesla, I'm forever for myself because I know the personality, right, I know the personality of how Tesla likes to move, right, the personality of Tesla, when I see QQQ, and, you know, let's just say, for example, is very strong on the day, right, so this is the QQQ chart the day, right, and I see that Tesla, right, is consolidating, right, it's consolidating right under level support, okay, what does, this shows me that sellers are sitting here and Tesla is weaker on the day, therefore, when Q's becomes weak or breaks another key level here on QQQ, right, we break, you know, change of character, and Tesla starts dropping hard, all of a sudden, I see this on Tesla, right, and therefore, whenever Tesla, right, because Q obviously needs a little bit of a pull back, whenever QQQ starts pulling back up, back up into this consolidation for a retest, I will enter this trade ten times out of ten." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2784s-2860s])
- **For futures: compare ES and NQ** "if you trade futures, or if you trade QQQ, then you do the exact opposite, right, so when you're trading ES, for example, right, you have to look at, right, vice versa, when you're trading NQ, you look at ES." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2872s-2885s])

### Max Trades/Day
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Stop-When-Green
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

### Market Regime
- **Defense mode in choppy markets** "in defense mode, you'll notice I'm in hardcore defense mode. For example, the market we are in right now for the last literally last it started last week, I'd say in this market, I have to be in defense mode. Okay, I have to be very, very selective with entries and I have to be very, very selective because the market is just not giving as many opportunities." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2579s-2599s])
- **Market context is the edge** "my edge comes from experience, right, boring answer, I know you guys were expecting some secrets, awesome, sorry, right, from experience in the markets, the more time you spend, the more you will learn. So for myself, understanding market context structure are the best, that's what I'm good at, right, I'm really good at this part right here, the market context." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [2689s-2710s])

### Stock Selection
- **Basket of 5-10 tech tickers, narrow to 3** "I usually have on my screen, four to five different names on a daily basis. Okay. Now by different names, they're usually the same names. However, there's four to five different individual stocks that I'm looking at. Tesla, it may be NVIDIA, it may be Apple, it may be, what else do I trade? AMD, right." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [787s-804s])
- **Choose stocks with best HTF setup** "out of these 10 tech names that we have, right, which one have the best higher timeframe, right? Let's say for that specific day, we have Tesla, D, right? And when you guys are live trading with us, those three names that have the best higher timeframe." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [849s-863s])
- **Can trade momentum stocks even if not in basket** "if Nvidia did decide to move up based off news or whatever, right, we're still momentum traders. And because we're momentum traders, it is fine for us to still trade Nvidia." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [933s-946s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **Target minimum 1:2 risk-to-reward** "what I'm looking for is at least a one to two risk-to-reward." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3832s-3837s])
- **Average RR ~1:2 to 1:2.2** "on average, if you look at my trades over a long period of time, it'll be a one to two to one, one to two point two-ish around risk-to-reward strategy." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_2_My_Bread_Butter_Strategy_How_To_Draw_Key_Levels.txt [3852s-3858s])

# EXTRACTION FROM mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest

### Valid Break (Bodies vs Wicks, Displacement)
- **Displacement defined as candle not touching previous resistance/support** "All displacement is the move from where you break out... displacement only occurs when you have a candle that does not touch your previous resistance turn support" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2689s-2840s])
- **Displacement is the second move, not the first** "This first candle, right? Though we broke out, we never, this isn't a displacement, right? Displacement is this second move. This second move is displacement." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2789s-2797s])
- **No displacement = no entry (unless HTF thesis)** "This first trade isn't actually a good trade. I was just saying that for the example, there's no displacement on this trade, unless you had a higher timeframe thesis on this trade, this isn't a good trade because there's no displacement." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2556s-2563s])
- **Displacement requires candle to not touch the level** "I don't really want it to touch back into this, okay? So displacement is simply the move that happens after we break out of the stock and then we come back for the retest." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2819s-2833s])

### Valid Retest (Touch/Zone, Max Wait)
- **Wait for high of day or low of day break and retest** "I'm going to wait for a high of day break in retest or a low of day break in retest. I don't want to do anything in between the chop." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1711s-1716s])
- **Everything between the range is noise** "Everything in between this is noise." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1698s-1700s])

### Entry Trigger (Hammer/Shooting Star)
- **Enter on weak candle close at retest** "You get weak candles, right? You would most likely enter into which candle, right? Which candle would I enter into? I would enter into. This candle right here, right? This candle right here. When you see that close of that candle, right?" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1831s-1843s])

### Stop Placement
- **Stop loss = break of the impulsive candle** "Where is our stop loss? Our stop loss now is going to be a break of the OB, right? It's literally just a break of the OB, right? This candle right here, right? So it's just going to be a break of the OB" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1887s-1898s])
- **Stop loss = break of the impulsive candle that broke out** "my stop loss would be in this specific trade is going to be whatever the candle the impulsive candle that broke out, okay, so the impulsive candle that broke out of this, this consolidation, right, it's going to be a break below that candle, right, that's going to be my stop loss in terms of this momentum trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [447s-465s])
- **Stop loss = break back into consolidation** "my stop loss is simply a break back into consolidation, right? So if we break back into this consolidation" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [373s-379s])
- **For momentum trades, stop is break of impulsive candle** "the stop loss would be the break of the impulsive candle. Can someone tell me why it's the break of the impulsive candle compared to why it's not just 118 down here?" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4186s-4194s])

### Targets (HOD/LOD -> Liquidity)
- **Target minimum 2R** "we're looking for that two R multiple, right? So two R multiple right here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4246s-4251s])
- **Use psychological numbers when no immediate levels** "I would just simply look at psychological numbers, right? So 120 would be my psychological number that I would be looking at." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4355s-4361s])

### First-Break vs Late Breaks
- **Wait for break of range, not trades inside range** "A break of the range or a break of structure... Now you have a clear downtrend. You have displacement of the downtrend right here. You get the retest. You get weak candles... This is the A plus entry. Everything else earlier was noise." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1776s-1822s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** (No mention of "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as named concepts)

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

### Conditions
- **Requires A+ entry** "the thing you need to know about the 84% rule is you need an A plus entry. Okay, so let's consider this to be an A plus entry" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [545s-551s])
- **First entry fails, then wait for stock to come back to same area** "when the stock comes back up into the same area that we were previously at, right, so the same area that we took earlier, when the stock comes back up, in theory, it should work 84% of the time." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [580s-589s])

### Psychology
- **First entry may just be timing off** "that entry, it may just be the timing was off, right, the execution or the timing was off for the first specific entry. But the entry was good." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [604s-612s])
- **Second entry has higher probability because more buyers attracted** "the second time it comes up, you have a higher probability chance of the market working out. Why? Because now you also attracted more buyers off this previous level." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [615s-625s])

### Sizing
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE** (No specific sizing rules for 84% re-entries)

## 4. Order Blocks
- **Order blocks referenced but not defined in detail** "J-Dub will be talking about this, right? So if you don't know why I drew this right here, this Saturday, J-Dub will be talking all about order blocks, fair value gaps, all that stuff." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1798s-1806s])

## 5. Key Levels

### HOD/LOD
- **High of day and low of day continuation is key** "I'm looking for high of day or low of day continuation." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2265s-2267s])
- **No HOD/LOD continuation = choppy day** "If there's no high of day or low of day continuation, most likely it's going to be choppy" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2632s-2636s])
- **HOD break then fail proves buyers not strong** "When you get high of day, OK, up here, and you break back below, what does this prove? This proves that buyers were not strong enough to move the stock to the upside." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2294s-2309s])
- **LOD break then fail proves sellers not strong** "When you get a low of day and you still can't move to the upside, what does this or say move to the downside? What does this mean? This means that sellers aren't strong enough" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2315s-2328s])

### Previous Day High/Low
- **Opening inside previous day range = careful day** "If we open up in between previous day high and previous day low, that means we're already in range, right? So that's those are days that I'm going to be careful in anyways." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2052s-2060s])

### Psychological Levels
- **Use psychological numbers as profit targets** "I would just simply look at psychological numbers, right? So 120 would be my psychological number that I would be looking at." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4355s-4361s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

### Trade Window
- **Trade before 11 o'clock** "I don't trade at this time. I want to make this very clear. I don't trade at this time. I trade before 11 o'clock." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1855s-1859s])

### News Days (FOMC/CPI)
- **FOMC days = range-bound until news** "When we have a day of FOMC, for example, like today, right? Buyers and sellers believe stock is at a fair value, right? You see it in the range... And then when you get news, this is when the buyers and sellers really step in because we have news, right? This is going to determine which way we trend on the markets." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2427s-2450s])

### Identifying Choppy Days
- **Can tell in first 30 minutes** "30 minutes into the market, I could have already told you this day it was going to be choppy on Nvidia, right? It's a stock you want to avoid because you can just tell in the first 30 minutes that it's going to chop around." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2599s-2608s])

## 7. Exits

### Scaling %
- **Scale 50-60% when QQQ not showing strength** "you take out around 50 to 60%... Because QQQ isn't showing you the strength that it needs to show you, right? It's consolidating at that previous day close, right? So it's not showing you the strength that it needs to for this trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3526s-3545s])
- **At 4R, could take off even more** "you're already at a 4R, right? So you could even take off a little bit more if you really wanted" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3585s-3588s])

### Position Management
- **When both QQQ and stock break structure, stop out** "now that we know both QQQ broke to the downside and Nvidia broke to the downside as well... we stop out of the trade. We stop out of the full trade still in profit" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3661s-3671s])

## 8. HTF Application

### HTF Overriding Intraday
- **HTF thesis can justify entry without displacement** "This entry was only good if there was something on the higher timeframe that I saw that was good." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2673s-2676s])

## 9. Trade Selection

### A/A+/B/C Grading Criteria
- **A+ requires strong displacement + strong retest candle** "This is an A plus example of displacement. This is not only an A plus example of displacement, this is an A plus example of what? The retest as well, right? Very strong candle, right? Pushes above, retest, very strong price action candle here, had good displacement here as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3913s-3927s])
- **A+ requires QQQ confirmation** "We need Qs to break this high, right? If Qs breaks this high, then I would be much more interested in Nvidia." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3996s-3999s])
- **Trade can be decent without being A+** "Is this an A-plus trade? No, right? This is not an A-plus trade. But comparing it to QQQ, right? This is a decent trade just for the comparison of QQQ" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3446s-3456s])
- **A+ trades may not look textbook technically but are A+ based on buyer/seller understanding** "They may not look A plus in terms of technical analysis, but if you understand the buyers and sellers and what is happening, right? Then you understand why one trade is A plus and one trade may not be A plus" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4146s-4155s])

### QQQ Alignment (Index Confirmation)
- **Need QQQ confirmation before entering stock** "We do not have QQQ confirmation, okay? And that's what we need." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3992s-3993s])
- **QQQ in range = no reason to enter stock** "QQQ is in range, okay? There's no reason for me to be entering Nvidia because if Qs brings me into Nvidia, I'm not entering into Nvidia here, right? Because if Qs breaks to the downside, well guess what? Nvidia is about to tank as well." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3965s-3984s])
- **Both making new highs = buyers in control** "Both of them have strength because QQQ is pushing to the upside and NVIDIA is pushing to the upside." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3285s-3292s])
- **Stock with relative strength before QQQ breakout is better** "Nvidia had relative strength before Qs broke out of this. So before QQQ even broke out, right, of this range, Nvidia was a ticker that already had strength" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4099s-4111s])

### Market Regime
- **Range action = buyers and sellers believe fair price** "We have range action when buyers and sellers believe the stock is in a fair price, right? So when we have range, that means both buyers and sellers believe that we have the prices in fair value" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2405s-2419s])
- **No one strong = don't trade** "If nobody is strong in this market and no one's trying to take the heavy force and move a stock to either the upside or to the downside, right? If no one is trying to take the stock to the upside or to the downside, then obviously, right? Obviously, that is a day we do not want to trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2347s-2362s])
- **Think about why trade didn't work (buyer/seller psychology), not just technicals** "Stop looking at the technical reason why the trade doesn't work and start thinking about why your trade didn't work, right? Start thinking about what actually happened behind the scenes for your trade to not work." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2372s-2385s])

### Stock Selection
- **NVIDIA and QQQ correlation example** "When we're looking at QQQ correlation, we want to see the relative strength and weakness based off of QQQ and NVIDIA." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3243s-3248s])

### Max Trades/Day
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

### Stop-When-Green
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

## 10. Concrete Numbers

### R:R
- **Looking for 2R multiple** "we're looking for that two R multiple, right?" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [4248s-4250s])
- **At 4R, could take more off** "you're already at a 4R, right? So you could even take off a little bit more" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [3585s-3588s])

### Average Daily Range
- **Factor in stock's average daily range** "the stock has an average price that moves per day, right? So Tesla might move $2 a day, right? Whereas Apple may move $1. That's the average range of the stock, right? So the average range that a stock moves intraday, right? That's a big thing you have to factor" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [944s-963s])
- **Don't enter trades requiring more than average daily range** "we don't want to enter into a stock that has an average range of like, let's say five and our profit targets of 15." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [980s-985s])

### Drawdown
- **NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

### Gap Fill Stats

## PSYCHOLOGY / RISK MANAGEMENT (Methodology Only)

### Stop Loss Discipline
- **Always respect your stop loss plan** "there's a reason you have that plan. And when you go against your plan, right? You, bad things happen realistically at the end of the day, bad things happen when you go against your plan." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1446s-1454s])
- **Never double down on losing trades** "I decided to double down at a random key level, right? There's a random ass level. So I decided to double down on this random level. And now the stock is down from our original to our multiple to 0.64" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1255s-1268s])
- **Doubling down on losers leads to 10x loss** "you hold more or you buy more so it comes back up and then even if it comes back up a little bit, you have more confidence and then it drops and you end up losing 10 X the amount you were supposed to originally make." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1370s-1382s])
- **Friday + 0 DTE = theta decay kills** "if you look at the bottom left or bottom screen right here, Friday, right? We're trading on a Friday. So guess what's killing our contracts even more theta. Okay. So not only do you have theta against you, you also are in the wrong trade and most likely on a Friday, you're in what zero DTE." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1419s-1438s])

### Patience
- **Patience of hours to make money in minutes** "you had to have the patience of three hours to make your money in 13 minutes." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [1939s-1948s])

### Understanding "Why"
- **Understand why your strategy works** "really understand why the strategy works. Once you understand the why behind your strategy, everything else will fall into place" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_3_How_To_Read_Trend_Structure_How_To_Use_Indexes_Mast.txt [2545s-2550s])

# Extraction from mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Definition/Simplicity]** "The entry has to be so simple that if you were to sit down with a five-year-old, they would also understand how easy it is to buy the break and retest." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2446s-2452s])
- **[Entry Process]** "We're looking for a stock to break out of a specific key level, right? Pull back into the retest, right? When it pulls back, it needs to show strong price action, meaning buyers are putting, buying into the stock, right? Which means this week right here, right? Buyers are entering into the stock, right? And it needs to be back where the resistance turns support, right? So resistance turns support. We enter into the trade here, right?" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2479s-2502s])
- **[Entry Signal]** "Our entry signals is that strong price action candle." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2507s-2508s])
- **[Exit Signal]** "Our exit signals is if we break back below this consolidation, which would be our stop loss and our profit targets at a two R multiple." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2510s-2515s])
- **[Process Summary]** "Wait for momentum and trend to develop. Look for the stock to break a key level. Once broken, look for the retest. On retrace or look for a retrace. On retrace, look for a hammer candle. Once price action is confirmed by into stock looking for high of day as exit or key VWAP... And once again, VWAP is not really as important, but key level as stop loss." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2520s-2540s])
- **[Breakout vs Break-and-Retest Personality Fit]** "When I try to trade the breakouts... I'm not a breakout trader because the second this stock would come back down, especially with options... this would be down 20%... I was always nervous buying the breakout. Why was I nervous buying the breakout? I was nervous buying the breakout because when I didn't have a clear risk... we had so much more to lose." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1652s-1704s])
- **[Reversal Trading vs Break-and-Retest]** "I didn't like reversal trading... it would just take 10,000 years, in my opinion, right, to actually come back either to your profit target or to your stop loss... it's taken way too long." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [1728s-1758s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[84% Rule Mentioned]** "We always have that 84% rule that we could potentially enter in on again, right? So back up here, we always have the 84% rule, right? But before then, right, we need to manage and limit our risk." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [570s-579s])
- **[84% Rule After First Loss]** "The first trade that I lose, okay, I can maybe look back at it, be like, okay, I see why I lost in real time. And then I could retake maybe the 84% rule on that trade, right? Now, if I lose the 84% rule on this same trade, right, what does that mean? Is that me, or is that the markets?" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3793s-3809s])
- **[84% Rule Loss = Step Away]** "Regardless of if it's me that's taking the bad trade, or if it's a market being choppy, I need to step away, right? Historically, I should not be taking two losing trades in a row." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3846s-3859s])
- **[84% Rule for Re-entry After Stop Out]** "Sometimes the risk to reward is just a little bit too skewed and we can use the 84% rule to get back in, obviously, but we need to respect our rules in terms of that." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5923s-5928s])

## 4. Order Blocks

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Profit Targets - HOD/LOD/Key Levels]** "We take profits at high of day, low of day, key levels, and that's it, right? Those are literally three things that we take profits at. There is nothing else, there is nothing else that we take profits at." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5646s-5660s])
- **[Higher Timeframe Key Levels]** "High of day, right? Low of day and then key levels which are higher time frame, right? Higher time frame. It could even be a previous day level." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5663s-5670s])
- **[Psychological Levels at All-Time Highs]** "If something's at all time highs, where do we take profits if something's at all time highs? We take them on psych levels, right? Which is also gonna be key levels." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5677s-5685s])
- **[Psych Levels Definition]** "In terms of psych levels, all I'm looking for really... is going to be whole numbers." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5703s-5707s])
- **[Why Psych Levels Work]** "When you think about it before you became a trader... where were you buying stocks? ... You were buying at round numbers, right? ... Do you think these big institutions are putting in orders at 105.53? No, right? They're also buying at whole numbers." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5762s-5810s])
- **[Psych Levels = Volume/Liquidity]** "That's why I use psych levels, because there's a lot of volume and liquidity built up there." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5842s-5844s])
- **[Stop Loss - Change of Character]** "We always exit the trade at change of character." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5883s-5885s])
- **[Stop Loss - Back in Range / Impulsive Candle]** "Back in range or the impulsive candle that broke the range? Okay, obviously not every stock's gonna be ranging before it breaks out, so it'll be the impulsive candle or back of range, and obviously change of character, depending on where that is as well." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5908s-5923s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Trade Window]** "I trade from 9:30 a.m., right? To 11 a.m. Eastern every single day. That is a rule I have. I do not trade after 11 a.m. Eastern. Very rarely will you ever see me trade after 11 a.m. Eastern." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3212s-3224s])
- **[Swing Trades Exception]** "The only trades that I take after 11 a.m. Eastern are swing trades, right? So swing trades, obviously, they're a little bit different. So I take those after 11 a.m. Eastern." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3229s-3236s])
- **[95% Rule]** "9:30 a.m. Eastern to 11 a.m. Eastern is where 95% of my trades come. Let's just say swing trades are 5%." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3240s-3248s])
- **[FOMC Day After = Trend Day]** "We talked about how today's going to be the day that we should see some sort of trend, right? So me and JDub kind of came in with that thesis, right? That Tesla should rip... we should just see a trend in the market after FOMC because we've been chopping on the higher timeframe for like the last week." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2913s-2926s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling - First Scale May Not Be 2R]** "When I take my first scale, my first high of day scale may not be two R, okay? This first high of day scale may not be two R, right? And that's fine, okay? Because over the long period of time... it'll balance out." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5964s-6027s])
- **[Scaling Example - Increasing R Multiple]** "If you take one off at 3.18 and then you hold the other one for the 7.09... Now all of a sudden you've increased your R multiple... to around that five R multiple... So a trade that should have been three R is now five R." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6808s-6834s])
- **[Trailers Make More Money]** "Your trailers will end up making way more money than the original position, right? And this is mostly true for me as well. My trailers, if I can hold them, they will make so much more money than my original position usually." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6913s-6927s])
- **[Trailer Stop Loss - Market Structure]** "For trailers, in terms of your stop loss, as the trailer keeps running up, you just kind of look at the market and you kind of determine where that market structure would break and that's where you kind of put your stop loss." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [7048s-7056s])
- **[Trailer Stop Loss - Trailing %]** "Or you can have trailers where you have a trailing stop. This just means if this contract drops, let's say 15% in value, you just exit out because it's already up so high or whatever." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [7059s-7065s])
- **[Profit Taking System - Simple]** "To hold the winner longer, it needs to be simple and repetitive each time. So high day, low day, key levels." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5850s-5855s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Higher Timeframe Trade Quality]** "The trade itself on the higher timeframe was good. That's why it worked out." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6174s-6176s])
- **[Swing Trade Drawdown Expectation]** "Before I entered the swing, I said in terms of swing positions, you have to be comfortable with letting 50 to 60% drawdown happen, right? Now, 87% was way more than I was predicting myself... But 50 to 60% drawdown on a swing position, right, is normal." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6402s-6417s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Quality Over Quantity]** "Stop believing that you have to take a trade a day and focus on quality trades." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2972s-2974s])
- **[One to Two Trades Per Quarter]** "All it takes is one to two trades to make up your whole quarter, right? Your whole quarter will, literally all you need is one to two quality trades." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3015s-3025s])
- **[Three A+ Setups Per Week]** "If you can find three A plus setups a week, you are on the right track, right? Only three, which means for two days, you don't even trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4912s-4926s])
- **[Defense Mode After Losses]** "Last week, two losing trades in the same day, became completely, you know, 100% like, I'm not taking a trade unless it's A plus. Like, there's no way I'm touching the market unless it's A plus." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3885s-3895s])
- **[Offense vs Defense Mode]** "As a trader, you need to know when it's offense, meaning you wanna be putting on risk every single day because it's just like, the market's rewarding you for that... But then also you need to know when it's time to switch up completely and put that wall or that barrier up in front of you where it's like, okay, no, no, no. You can only come through the barrier or I can only walk out of this door if and only if I see the most A plus setup with the rules that are say with the market also complying with my individual ticker." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3908s-3939s])
- **[Quality Trade Example - FOMC Aftermath]** "It was the highest quality trade after FOMC, right? So we had FOMC and this whole week... We've been talking about how the day after FOMC is going to be a good day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2893s-2906s])
- **[Stop Following Other Systems]** "It is your time to stop following every trader... You can listen to them for psychology for sure, right? Like listen to something they say about psychology or, you know, maybe something about technical analysis in terms of like market structure or something, but stop listening to their system." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5074s-5092s])
- **[Lack of Discipline = #1 Reason]** "The number one reason traders don't reach profitability is a lack of discipline and conviction in their own system." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5111s-5117s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Win Rate / R:R Trade-off]** "In the beginning stages, I would never take the high of day skill, right? I would take whatever my main profit target was. And my win rate was pretty low, right? My win rate went down to like 40%. But my risk to reward increased to 2.5 to 3R... However, when I implemented selling at the actual high of day, my win rate increased to 50 to 60%, right? But my risk to reward went down to 2R." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5239s-5276s])
- **[Win Rate]** "50 to 60% win rate, cool." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3771s-3772s])
- **[Max Trades Per Day]** "I'm able to take three trades maximum per day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3708s-3710s])
- **[Actual Trades Per Day]** "75 to 80% of the time, right? I'll only be taking one trade a day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3729s-3733s])
- **[Max Losses in a Row]** "My rule that I have is if I lose two trades in a row, call it quits, right? Call it quits on that day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3758s-3761s])
- **[Max Stop Loss for Day = Average Winning Day]** "Whatever your average winning days are, average that out, right? And that should be your maximum stop loss for the day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3332s-3335s])
- **[Max Stop Loss = ~2R]** "Your winning trade is a two R, right? So technically your maximum stop loss should be basically two R or around that level somewhere." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3463s-3469s])
- **[Position Size Recommendation]** "I personally always recommend one to three contracts. I don't care if you're a multimillionaire... with three contracts, you're able to not only scale, so you're gonna be able to scale those trades as well, but also you lose very small." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3508s-3536s])
- **[Tony's Sizing - 50 Contracts Base]** "Per entry, I enter in with 50 contracts, right? So I enter in with 50 contracts per trade in the beginning." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4428s-4436s])
- **[Tony's Sizing - Increase to 100]** "I will increase to 100 contracts only if I see another good potential entry, right? Only if I see another good potential entry or if the stock comes back and retraces into a level that I've said earlier." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4442s-4456s])
- **[Tony's Sizing - 200 Contracts Rare]** "In some good markets, will I size up to 200 contracts? Yes, I actually will, right? I will size up to 200 contracts, but only in good markets... I honestly haven't taken 200 contracts in a very long time. Probably been around eight months, maybe more." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4464s-4534s])
- **[Tony's Sizing - Choppy Market]** "If the market is choppy, for example, all of last week, I did not take a trade over 50 contracts." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4479s-4482s])
- **[Tony's Sizing - Incremental]** "I'll increase 10 cons at a time, right? I'll increase 10 cons at a time to either get up to 100 or stay between that 50 to 100 mark." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4511s-4517s])
- **[Example Day - $24,000]** "That Tesla trade today, for example, obviously including the Apple trade was like a $24,000 day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [2976s-2980s])
- **[Apple Swing - 87% Drawdown to 123% Top Scale]** "At one point, this thing was down 87%... my absolute top skill that I got on Apple today based off my original entry was 123%." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [6084s-6138s])
- **[Tesla R:R Example]** "My Tesla risk-to-reward by the end of the day today was like six to seven R." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [5986s-5988s])
- **[Backtest Period]** "You can literally back test over a three to six month period, right? Or however long your trading view allowed you to go." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3130s-3134s])
- **[Account Size Reference]** "Right now the account that I trade is 300,000, right? So I traded on a $300,000 account." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4803s-4807s])

## Special Audit Questions

### A/A+ Grading
- **[A+ Only After Losses]** "I'm not taking a trade unless it's A plus. Like, there's no way I'm touching the market unless it's A plus." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3889s-3895s])
- **[A+ Setup Criteria - Defense Mode]** "You can only come through the barrier or I can only walk out of this door if and only if I see the most A plus setup with the rules that are say with the market also complying with my individual ticker." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3928s-3939s])
- **[Three A+ Setups Per Week]** "If you can find three A plus setups a week, you are on the right track." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4912s-4915s])
- **[A+ Setup = Larger Size]** "If it's a really good A plus setup, right? Those 200 contracts might come in." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4520s-4522s])
- **[Focus on A+ First]** "Your only objective and your main goal is to enter A plus trades. That's literally the only thing you have to do." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4268s-4271s])
- **[Master A+ Before Dollar Value]** "Once you master the A plus setup, right? Then you move on to the dollar value. Then you move on to everything else." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [4311s-4315s])

### Stop-After-Win / Max Trades Per Day
- **[Max Trades Per Day = 3]** "I'm able to take three trades maximum per day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3708s-3710s])
- **[Actual = 1 Trade 75-80% of Time]** "75 to 80% of the time, right? I'll only be taking one trade a day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3729s-3733s])
- **[Two Losses = Stop]** "If I lose two trades in a row, call it quits, right? Call it quits on that day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3758s-3761s])
- **[No Daily Win Goals]** "I think a daily goal is one of the most dumbest things you can have as a trader... get rid of those daily goals... Because the daily goals, let's say your daily goal is to make $50 a day, cool. Now, all of a sudden, until you make that $50 a day, right? You're going to keep trading. You're going to keep trading until you make that $50 a day and you're gonna end up losing $500 that day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_4_How_To_Create_A_Winning_Day_Trading_System_How_To_H_transcript.txt [3982s-4012s])
