# EXTRACTED TRADING RULES

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Displacement requirement]** "We didn't have displacement on the entry" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6191s])
- **[Timeframe flexibility]** "The break and retest works on any time frame. I just personally like the one minute time frame. It does work on the five minute time frame. You just have to wait a little bit longer." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2416s-2421s])
- **[Five-minute retest entry]** "We took the five minute retest. That's where we took Tesla today." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [120s-121s])
- **[Order block retest on five-minute]** "We were waiting for the five minute candle retest back into this order block... So now we had an order block and we were waiting for the five minute retest for the candle." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6207s-6219s])
- **[Stop placement on Tesla trade]** "My stop loss for this Tesla trade. It was about 246.57" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6292s-6295s])
- **[Target - HOD scale]** "We take the high of day scale" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6274s-6276s])
- **[Target - 249 scale]** "We take that 249 scale, right? This is where I said in the Discord I was taking that scale" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6346s-6348s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[Same thesis requirement]** "The 84 percent rule has to have all the same thesis, which means this first time it was trending. But now it's consolidating. Therefore, it's not the same thesis." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2775s-2782s])
- **[Re-entry danger on choppy days]** "You ended up taking it the third time on a consolidating or choppy day... Where you should have lost only one hundred dollars, this is literally the max you should have lost because you should have understood it's a consolidating or choppy day. You ended up losing two hundred dollars on that same day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2828s-2840s])

## 4. Order Blocks

- **[Order block retest entry]** "We were waiting for the five minute candle retest back into this order block... So now we had an order block and we were waiting for the five minute retest for the candle." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6207s-6219s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Pre-market high as key level]** "I wanted Tesla above previous day high. Tesla breaks above previous day high, cool." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6183s-6184s])
- **[Inside bar breakout significance]** "On the daily timeframe, we had an inside bar... Usually what that means is whichever way we're going to break out, it's going to have a lot of strength." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6130s-6142s])
- **[Gap fill as confluence]** "We're also in this gap kind of fill on Tesla... So we wanted to fill that gap all the way back on this previous candle... Therefore, Tesla had multiple reasons to be bullish today." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6143s-6153s])
- **[Four-hour break and retest confluence]** "On the four hour timeframe, you can see that we actually had a break and then we did the retest of this previous 12 o'clock or this candle right here on the four hour timeframe... and now it's breaking back to the upside." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6154s-6163s])
- **[Flat top on one-hour]** "Even the one hour timeframe, you can see this flat top, we dropped a little bit and then pre-market, we're trying to break back above 243.97" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6167s-6171s])
- **[QQQ pre-market high as trigger for stock entry]** "I want QQQ to break above the pre-market high for me to be able to take this retest on Apple on that consolidation slash stalling level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5931s-5939s])
- **[QQQ pre-market high break as momentum signal]** "I need QQQ to be above its pre-market high to show momentum." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5951s-5953s])
- **[QQQ flat top as risk management]** "As long as Q's doesn't break above 294.70 and you do see this wedge to the downside, then you can enter into Apple because you see that Apple has relative weakness." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5485s-5493s])
- **[QQQ low of day as invalidation]** "QQQ was breaking the low of day... Because QQQ was breaking that low of day, I did not want to take Tesla there either." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6197s-6203s])
- **[QQQ holding above low of day as reason to stay in trade]** "As long as QQQ is holding above low of day, right? This Tesla trade has relative strength. So if Q's just doesn't make a new low of day, Tesla should continue to the upside" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6305s-6317s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Trade window - 9:30 to 11:00]** "I trade from nine thirty to eleven o'clock. Every single day. It's because this is the time for myself where I think I'm probably the most aware of my decisions." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2264s-2271s])
- **[Why not trade after 11]** "After this, if I kept sitting on the charts. What would happen? I would start overtrading. Why would I start overtrading? I would start overtrading because now I'm just bored." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2274s-2282s])
- **[Most volume in morning]** "Nine a.m. to eleven o'clock. Most volume as well. So that's what I trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2429s-2431s])
- **[Done in one hour example]** "We took a nice test of trading. But I didn't want to sit there all day today. Like I was done the day at ten thirty in an hour." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2288s-2293s])
- **[Choppy day avoidance]** "Such a choppy day. I mean, wow, if you look at QQQ, this is something you do not want to trade today." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6103s-6108s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling - sell partial at key level]** "If you do have like three, maybe sell one at 190, right? To lock in profits if we do come back down." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5301s-5305s])
- **[HOD scale as target]** "We take the high of day scale" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6274s-6276s])
- **[249 scale as target]** "We take that 249 scale, right? This is where I said in the Discord I was taking that scale" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6346s-6348s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Daily inside bar as strength indicator]** "On the daily timeframe, we had an inside bar... Usually what that means is whichever way we're going to break out, it's going to have a lot of strength." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6130s-6142s])
- **[Four-hour break and retest confluence]** "On the four hour timeframe, you can see that we actually had a break and then we did the retest of this previous 12 o'clock or this candle right here on the four hour timeframe... and now it's breaking back to the upside." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6154s-6163s])
- **[One-hour flat top confluence]** "Even the one hour timeframe, you can see this flat top, we dropped a little bit and then pre-market, we're trying to break back above 243.97" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6167s-6171s])
- **[Multiple timeframe alignment]** "So on most timeframes... were very bullish on Tesla going into the day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6166s-6177s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[Max trades per day - 1 to 3 quality setups]** "I always talk about one to three quality setups. One, really. And then two, maybe Max. I do not trade more than that." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2181s-2189s])
- **[Why not more trades]** "The more trades you take in the day, the less mental capacity you have." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2192s-2196s])
- **[Choppy day avoidance]** "Such a choppy day. I mean, wow, if you look at QQQ, this is something you do not want to trade today." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6103s-6108s])
- **[Choppy week example - only 3 trades]** "I took three trades that week. I think one was green, two were red." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3002s-3003s])
- **[Overtrading on choppy days leads to blowing account]** "I saw people blowing their accounts and I'm like, how are you blowing? I literally explained how this is not the time to be sizing up on your account." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3009s-3013s])
- **[Don't deviate from your system]** "Do not deviate away from your system... You're trading the break and retest. And now because you're good at the break and retest, you're like, oh, let me add the breakout as well. Well, that doesn't make sense. You know why the break and retest works. The break and retest works because the breakout doesn't work. But now you want to trade the breakout too, huh." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4239s-4256s])
- **[Don't switch instruments after profitability]** "You got profitable trading options. Now you switch to futures. Or you got profitable trading futures. Now you wanna switch to options. So do not deviate away from your system." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4231s-4239s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[Win rate - high 50s to low 60s]** "High 50 percent, low 60 percent kind of win rate. With the two are multiple and sometimes right in different markets. Sometimes that win rate will be higher. Sometimes it will be lower. But on average, I'd say it's in the high 50 percent." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [1819s-1829s])
- **[Consistency definition - $100 days, $50 losses]** "You're having consistent, you know, $100, $200 days. Let's just say $100 days and you're only losing, for example, all the days you lose $50." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [1325s-1331s])
- **[Sizing up - 2-3 months consistency required]** "My usual recommendation to you is to show at least two to three months, two to three months of consistency. Once you have two to three months of consistency, then we can talk about sizing up because you're gonna be much more comfortable sizing up after two to three months of consistency rather than one month of consistency." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5103s-5118s])
- **[Reasonable sizing jumps - 3 to 5, 5 to 10]** "Three to five contracts... That is a reasonable jump... From five contracts to 10 contracts... Another reasonable jump." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4556s-4577s])
- **[Unreasonable sizing jump - 10 to 50]** "From 10 contracts, can we go from 10 contracts to 50 contracts, is this a reasonable jump? No." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4580s-4591s])
- **[Timeframe multiplier - multiply by 3]** "The time frame, whatever you give yourself, multiply it by three. Always multiply the time frame by three." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [1173s-1180s])

## 11. Relative Strength/Weakness (QQQ Alignment)

- **[QQQ as edge]** "The secret sauce comes from QQQ. You can be a great technical trader, but until the actual markets align with your thesis, you're never going to have that complete edge in the markets." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5348s-5356s])
- **[QQQ break as trigger for stock entry]** "I want QQQ to break above the pre-market high for me to be able to take this retest on Apple" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5931s-5939s])
- **[QQQ break shows momentum]** "I need QQQ to be above its pre-market high to show momentum." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5951s-5953s])
- **[QQQ flat top as risk management for short]** "As long as Q's doesn't break above 294.70 and you do see this wedge to the downside, then you can enter into Apple because you see that Apple has relative weakness." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5485s-5493s])
- **[QQQ low of day break invalidates long]** "QQQ was breaking the low of day... Because QQQ was breaking that low of day, I did not want to take Tesla there either." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6197s-6203s])
- **[QQQ holding above low of day = stay in trade]** "As long as QQQ is holding above low of day, right? This Tesla trade has relative strength. So if Q's just doesn't make a new low of day, Tesla should continue to the upside" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6305s-6317s])
- **[Relative strength - stock goes up while QQQ drops]** "Every single times QQQ dropped, Tesla was going up in price, showing great relative strength today." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6228s-6233s])
- **[Stock not near HOD while QQQ is = potential catch-up]** "This is making new high of day. It's very close to new high of day at that 309 level where Tesla's not even close to high of day... if Qs does push here to new high of day and Tesla breaks this flag, you're going to get a lot of strong momentum." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5317s-5335s])
- **[QQQ consolidation at HOD = probability play]** "Q's is holding right at high of day and we're consolidating at high of day... it is still more high probability than having it underneath this 482.45 level. So we were playing the probability of it being at high of day and then moving back up from high of day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6243s-6267s])
- **[QQQ ranging = don't take trade]** "If Q's is ranging, for example, then we don't want to take this trade... because then this could consolidate, so that's why we want to wait for the breakout of Q's at that 308.86 level to take that trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [5440s-5449s])
- **[Use QQQ for tech names]** "I use QQQ because QQQ is a tech name and Tesla is a tech name and therefore I use QQQ but I also use Spy at the same time." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6417s-6421s])
- **[Apple as biggest QQQ component = follows QQQ]** "What ticker takes up the biggest spot on QQQ if QQQ is pushing? Apple, right? So Apple is going to push with it." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [6047s-6056s])

## 12. Psychology / Trader Journey

- **[Hide P&L to improve executions]** "Hide your P&L... if you don't see how much you're up or down, you literally can only trade one thing and that's the chart... your executions will increase." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4005s-4031s])
- **[Don't show P&L after becoming consistent]** "When you see that consistency, you were hiding your P&L before, but now all of a sudden, because you think you're such a good trader, you want to show your P&L... now all of a sudden your performance starts to decrease because your executions start to decrease." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4114s-4152s])
- **[Psychology only matters after system is learned]** "Psychology is only holding you back when you are trying to be a consistent trader. Psychology was not holding you back when you needed to learn the system. Psychology was not holding you back when you needed to learn market structure." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3871s-3882s])
- **[Revenge trading - hardest phase to escape]** "The just enter phase is basically who cares? You're already down money. What's another one thousand dollar loss going to do? Let's just enter the subpar trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2727s-2732s])
- **[Revenge trading leads to blowing account]** "When you're in this phase, it is extremely, extremely likely you will blow your account." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2723s-2725s])
- **[Start small - most important advice]** "If I was to start back from the beginning and knew nothing. One thing that I would hammer into my head is start with the smallest amount, even if I'm making ten dollars a day, twenty dollars a day." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [783s-793s])
- **[Don't quit your job to trade]** "I wouldn't consider quitting your job and then going all in on trading all in. I would consider that stupid." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [1245s-1248s])
- **[Trading exchanges decisions for money, not time]** "In trading, it's not the time for money that we have. It's the decision for money. We're exchanging our decisions for money." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2379s-2395s])
- **[More screen time = less pay when consistent]** "Once you're consistent, it is the exact opposite. The more you sit in front of the screen in real time and you're not able to control that emotion of not taking trades. The less you get paid." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2359s-2370s])
- **[Don't change what got you there]** "Whatever got you there, keep doing it. If it's not broken, don't fix it." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4222s-4225s])
- **[Sizing up - first look at new risk, not reward]** "Whenever you're sizing up, the first thing that you need to look at is how much is your new size are you going to be willing to risk." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4909s-4916s])
- **[Slow sizing is faster than jumping]** "The slow sizing was actually a faster way to size up to the 80 contract goal than just going right up to the 80 contract goal." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4946s-4950s])
- **[Size back down when performance drops]** "When I got up to this 80 contracts, I actually had to end up sizing back down. Because I realized my performance was basically garbage the second I started sizing up." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [4917s-4925s])
- **[Journaling is important]** "If you don't journal, that is going to be important as well." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3294s-3296s])
- **[Record yourself trading]** "Record yourself and put it in your journal... you can see where I entered, where I exited and what I was thinking about." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3361s-3427s])

## 13. Swing Trading Options

- **[Size smaller on options swings]** "You always, always, always size smaller on options wings because you know that you can lose 50 to 60 percent on that." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2653s-2659s])
- **[Swing with profits only]** "You want to only swing trade with the profits for the week or a very small amount that you've made on that week." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2674s-2676s])
- **[Options swing loss should equal day trading loss]** "Your 50 to 60 percent loss on your options wing should be no greater than your day trading loss of whatever, you know, 10, 15 percent." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [2668s-2674s])

## 14. Discord Alerts / Following Mentors

- **[Don't follow alerts blindly]** "If you think that just entering a trade because your mentor is entering the trade, that's what's going to make you money in the long run... How many people do you know that make money consistently long term over two to three years following discord alerts? Nobody." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3529s-3541s])
- **[Take trades because you understand, not because mentor took it]** "Don't try to take the trade because we take it... take the trade because you know what's going on in the trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [30s-60s])

## 15. Pre-Market Preparation

- **[Watch pre-market sessions]** "How many people even watch Jaydub's pre-market sessions? ... Those are useful as well. Because you're coming into the day well, way more prepared than the average person." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3696s-3705s])
- **[Weekly outlook + daily outlook + real-time]** "When you come into the day with the weekly outlook, you know what's happening in the week based off the news. Then in terms of the daily outlook, you know what Jaydub and I would be most likely be looking at. And then in real time, you're looking for it. There's so much more confidence there." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_5_How_To_Grow_A_Trading_Account_How_To_Spot_Trade_Rel_transcript.txt [3716s-3729s])

# EXTRACTION FROM mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[B+ trade criteria - no displacement]** "The reason this would be a b plus trade right here right is because once again we see right no real displacement on this move right now we are at previous day high which is the green line right which we need to understand but no real displacement on this move and then this order block right we're kind of consolidating near this level right so just based off the actual trade itself it's like a b plus type of trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2372s-2391s])
- **[Consolidation after break = lower grade]** "the reason this is b plus is because the order block right you have the order block here and then you have the previous day high level here right however because of the order block in the previous day high level right you have consolidation this trade is consolidating it didn't come back and retest right away for a move up right it kind of stayed in consolidation and when it did come back for the retest right it kind of broke back below that one two fifty five seventy showing a little bit more consolidation and showing the buyers weren't stepping in aggressively" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2406s-2436s])
- **[Break-and-retest works best in trending markets]** "my strategy is a momentum break and retest strategy... I was doing really really well because the market was obviously in a clear uptrend" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2981s-2997s])
- **[Break-and-retest can fail in choppy/consolidating markets]** "the break and retest was working absolutely fantastic... however i just want to make sure that you guys understand there will be some times where we are trending and the the strategy that you're trading is not working" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1956s-1967s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **[84% rule for re-entry after stop-out]** "if i personally stop out of the trade okay if i personally stop out of the trade i will reassess the trade and potentially re-enter using the 84% rule" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2184s-2196s])
- **[No problem losing a trade - can always re-enter]** "i have no problem losing a trade if i take an L on a trade i have absolutely zero worry in the world... you have to understand with a 50 to 60 win rate you're not expecting to be right every trade right but what we are expecting to do is manage our risk every single time we're in a trade and if we do have for example a losing trade we can always re-enter with the 84% rule if it presents itself" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2196s-2228s])
- **[If 84% re-entry never comes - three reasons]** "if it never presents itself what does that mean it just means that that trade was either not a there's a couple reasons right but number one a right is it was not a good trade right which we would have to go back and that's something that you would want to stress about right if it's not a good trade right then that's something you want to worry about right but then b it's a choppy day right if it's a choppy day then that's something right if you weren't able to identify that before the day was uh starting or as the day was starting you weren't able to identify it was a choppy day that's something that you can work on as well but that's something i wouldn't you know stress as much as not having a good a good trade and then c right it's just the probabilities don't work right everything else aligned right every single thing aligned but the probabilities of the trade did not work" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2228s-2268s])

## 4. Order Blocks
- **[Order block + previous day high = confluence but consolidation lowers grade]** "the reason this is b plus is because the order block right you have the order block here and then you have the previous day high level here right however because of the order block in the previous day high level right you have consolidation" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2406s-2418s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Previous day high as key level]** "we are at previous day high which is the green line right which we need to understand" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2377s-2383s])
- **[Stop loss at break of previous high]** "our stop loss is just a break of this previous high" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2449s-2457s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Trade first hour for learning]** "throughout this week, I want you guys to be trading. Now, that doesn't mean trading with, you know, every single day you have to trade. This means just be at the market and pay attention, right, for the first hour or whenever you can, right? Just pay attention to the markets in real time" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4660s-4684s])
- **[Market choppy before predictable news]** "before news or the predictable news right the market will be extremely extremely choppy" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1662s-1668s])

## 7. Exits
- **[High of day scale - take half off at HOD]** "we're going to take that high of day scale right seven hundred and twenty dollars but we're going to take half off right here right so we're going to take we're going to go here we're going to take half off here now we're going to sell half right there" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2790s-2801s])
- **[HOD scale reduces loss even if not 2R]** "every time i say guys take a high day scale nobody listens to me because they're like oh the risk to reward is off on high day scale tony it's not two r so therefore i can't take high day scale it's not a two r risk reward i know it's not a two r risk reward but here's the thing if that thing comes back into your profit target or so your stop loss right you're about to lose big on that trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2833s-2851s])
- **[HOD scale turns $920 loss into $460 loss]** "we were able to mitigate that nine hundred twenty dollar loss to four hundred sixty dollars right half of this loss right by taking half off a high of day" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2862s-2868s])
- **[HOD scale increases win rate at cost of some profit]** "does this mean we're limiting our potential profit factor here sure we're limiting it a little bit but at the same time we're increasing our win rate" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2868s-2878s])
- **[Profit target needs at least 2R multiple]** "our profit target needs to be at least a two or multiple" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2457s-2462s])
- **[Don't take HOD pops if not your strategy]** "we don't take high of day pops right" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2499s-2504s])
- **[Move stop to body candle close not wick]** "maybe my stop shouldn't have been at that wick right there it probably should have been at the body candle close here which makes a little bit more sense" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2568s-2577s])
- **[Higher timeframe stop = where to actually exit]** "on the higher time frame on the higher time frame this is going to be the actual stop right so based off the one hour chart this is where i should come out to" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2595s-2608s])
- **[10-minute candle low as stop reference]** "once we break this first 10 minute candle low that's really where i would want to stop out of this trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2615s-2622s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[Higher timeframe thesis needed on low volume days]** "Yesterday was one of the lowest volume days. It was a day that you most likely didn't want to trade if you didn't have a higher time frame thesis. We just had a higher time frame thesis on Tesla. That's why I worked out." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [24s-43s])
- **[Swing trade into HTF trend when day trading not working]** "we can swing trade into this higher time frame and still capture that move with swing trades meaning we trade the break and retest on the higher time frame" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1933s-1942s])
- **[HTF trending doesn't guarantee lower timeframe strategy works]** "what you need to understand is even though the higher time frame is trending to the upside that does not mean that the market sentiment based for your specific strategy is working" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1942s-1951s])
- **[One-hour/four-hour timeframes show market cycles]** "you can literally come on the one hour time frame or the four hour time frame and you can see these different kind of cycles happen right uptrend downtrend sideways action" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3148s-3158s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[B+ trade criteria - no displacement + consolidation]** "The reason this would be a b plus trade right here right is because once again we see right no real displacement on this move right now we are at previous day high which is the green line right which we need to understand but no real displacement on this move and then this order block right we're kind of consolidating near this level right so just based off the actual trade itself it's like a b plus type of trade if you were to enter this trade i wouldn't be mad or anything like i think it would be a decent trade but it's just not a high probability a plus trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2372s-2397s])
- **[Market sentiment determines position size]** "market sentiment determines position size this is so so so important... based off each different market i have to size based on that market right that's one thing i'm good at is understanding market sentiment and i feel like that's one thing that has led to my profitability over a long period of time is the fact that i'm able to adapt to each market" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1842s-1886s])
- **[Don't trade in current choppy market with full size]** "in this market that we're currently in do you think i should be trading with 100 contracts... no" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1886s-1911s])
- **[Only takes 2-3 trades to make a quarter]** "it only takes two to three trades to make your whole quarter right two to three trades to make your whole quarter i really do mean this" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3186s-3200s])
- **[Focus on one setup - backtest it extensively]** "now it is your time to find the one setup that aligns with you... the one setup that really aligns with your personality... back test that set up to gain more confidence. So every time you see it in real time, you'll be able to execute on that trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4228s-4295s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[50-60% win rate expectation]** "you have to understand with a 50 to 60 win rate you're not expecting to be right every trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2209s-2219s])
- **[Average green day $500-$700]** "my average green day is probably let's say 500 to 700" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2641s-2647s])
- **[Original loss on example trade was $920]** "the original loss that we were risking right here was nine hundred and twenty dollars" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2751s-2756s])
- **[HOD scale reduced loss to $460]** "we were able to mitigate that nine hundred twenty dollar loss to four hundred sixty dollars" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2862s-2868s])
- **[Win streak of 2 months with 1-2 losses/month in trending market]** "we were having some crazy win streaks... we'd probably lose like one or two trades a month... but it's just because that was a very easy trending market" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [112s-125s])
- **[$100k+ months during trending market]** "my whole win streak in that the crazy months i was happening 100k plus month like two three months in a row they all happened in this time frame" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2962s-2968s])

## 11. Risk Management (NEW TOPIC)
- **[Risk management is #1 most important thing in strategy]** "the number one thing the number one thing that you need to define in your strategy at first is risk right. That's going to be the biggest and most crucial thing that will help you long term. There is nothing else that will help you more than understanding your risk parameters for each trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [483s-507s])
- **[Risk management keeps you in the game longer]** "if you can have proper risk management when you're learning you will stay in the game much longer right. Rather than someone that's trying to go for these big wins" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [538s-556s])
- **[Big red days happen even with good risk management]** "does that mean that you will never have big red days you will still have big red days right. Even with proper risk management I don't care who you are and you will still have big red days." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [556s-573s])
- **[Candle knifing = unavoidable big loss regardless of risk management]** "if the candle knifes on you there's not much we can do about that right like we're going to take that bigger L than expected and that's with any strategy right. Breakouts supply demand you know ICT SMC any strategy if the candles knifing on you you will have a big red day no matter what your risk management is" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [576s-601s])
- **[Stop loss is the only thing differentiating you from blowing account]** "your stop loss is the only thing that differentiates you okay from someone that's going to blow their account it's the only thing... if you don't follow your stop loss you will 100 no matter who you are you will slowly trickle that account all the way down" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2020s-2056s])
- **[Must follow trading plan religiously every day]** "when you have a trading plan you must follow that trading plan every single day religiously right without your trading plan there is no way that you will actually see long-term success in the markets" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2070s-2082s])
- **[Sustainable trading = small consistent hits over time]** "a sustainable way to trade is always having those small consistent hits right small consistent hits time over time over time over time" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2158s-2170s])
- **[Market risk - interest rates, exchange rates, commodity prices]** "market risk right. This refers to the potential for losses due to changes in market conditions such as interest rates exchange rates and commodity prices." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [786s-794s])
- **[News is usually already priced in by institutions]** "most of the time in my opinion the price on on especially the spy for example or the overall market it's already priced in... the bigger institutions and hedge funds they're doing these forecasts for like 10 years... they've already had that kind of priced in into the markets" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [921s-961s])
- **[Market never does exactly what theory says it should]** "I've never seen the market actually do exactly what it was supposed to do right. Never" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [974s-982s])
- **[Credit risk - you are a credit risk to yourself on margin]** "you are a credit risk to yourself when you trade on margin right so thinking like a bank it wouldn't be a responsible idea or a good investment for you to have margin" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1451s-1471s])
- **[Cash account is the way to go for beginners]** "in the beginning cash account is the way to go right a cash account is the way to go because in the cash account you can only lose however much you have in the account" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1433s-1446s])
- **[Don't trade before predictable news]** "in predictable news settings there is no way we should be taking trades before that predictable news why because we know news can either increase or decrease the stock from low of day to high of day" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1593s-1606s])
- **[Random news isn't as random as you think - engineered for HTF needs]** "the random news isn't as random as you think... it's usually when a higher time frame is doing something and the actual company needs it to do something else... this random news is actually based off of it's actually engineered news based off of what they need the stock to do right both fundamentally and technically" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1717s-1826s])
- **[Bad news dropped pre-market so no one can exit]** "why do they drop it pre-market so no one can exit the trade and no one can exit the trade pre-market so they drop bad news... they want to drop it pre-market so then the people that are in this trade the buyers they're going to stay in this trade right and therefore they're going to be forced or the bigger banks right are going to be forced to bring the stock back to the upside just to right exit liquidity just for exit liquidity at a minimum before they keep dropping it below" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1764s-1799s])
- **[Size smaller on swing trades because of engineered news]** "you come in with a lower percentage trade or lower percentage size than you would in a day trade because this random news is actually based off of it's actually engineered news" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [1807s-1822s])
- **[Focus on technicals not P&L numbers]** "i'm not looking at the profit numbers i'm not looking at the loss numbers all i care about is the technicals because that's what tony said right tony said to make sure that the technicals is what i should focus on right never worry about the number here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [2651s-2663s])
- **[College analogy - pay less to learn same thing]** "you're getting the exact same experience as everyone in this mastermind class, right? The exact same experience. However, why would you want to lose more money than everyone else? But when the final result is profitability, and then once you're profitable, you can size up because you're already comfortable with risk and make more money." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4537s-4555s])

## 12. Psychology / Trader Journey
- **[Learn one thing at a time - don't trade 3 strategies at once]** "focus it's crucial for new traders to learn one thing at a time rather than trying to learn everything at once for example do not try to trade three different strategies when you don't even understand how to read price action" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3391s-3401s])
- **[You will never eliminate emotions - only mitigate them]** "you will never, you will never, ever, ever eliminate your emotions. Okay, you will only mitigate your emotions." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4012s-4019s])
- **[Write down goals + internal motivation behind them]** "I want you to write down your goals, right? Your goals for 2025... then I want you to write down what is your motivation, right? What is the motivation for that goal? What is the back end psychological reason that you want to hit that goal?" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4044s-4082s])
- **[Read Trading in the Zone by Mark Douglas]** "I want you to read, you don't even have to read the Mark Douglas Trading in the Zone, but I want you to either watch a summary on YouTube... I personally recommend it's a good book... A lot of the things that I say in my own personal trading is really summarized through that" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3949s-3974s])
- **[This is not a get rich quick scheme - you will lose money learning]** "you need to understand this is not a get rich quick scheme. You will lose money in the process, but that is why you can't quit your job" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4648s-4655s])
- **[New traders want to take trades just to see if they're right]** "you kind of want to take trade just because you want to see if you're right. Just so to see that what you've been learning works which is cool. I mean I was the same way when I first started learning to see the charts in the correlation of QQQ compared to whatever I was trading individual stock and then seeing that relative strength and weakness and then also seeing the set up work in real time. Like I wanted to obviously to trade more and take more trades because I was excited." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [164s-195s])

## 16. Backtesting / Learning Roadmap (NEW TOPIC)
- **[October 6-7: Focus on charts - trend structure, candlesticks, volume]** "for sunday right i think sunday is going to be october 6 on october 6 on the sunday right this is what i would do right... i would come through right and i would go through trend structure candlesticks volume" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3444s-3486s])
- **[October 8-9: Backtest patterns and setups]** "october 8th to 9th patterns and setups... I want you to purposefully look at every other setup right I want you to look at flag patterns right I want you to back test the the uh the flag patterns okay so the higher time frame break and retest not just the break and retest on the one minute but the higher time frame we can retest right I want you to go and back test all this" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3600s-3642s])
- **[October 10: Study option contracts - ITM, OTM, ATM, different expiries]** "I would spend a half a day or one day specifically looking at the option contracts, and I would go and look at how they move... Look at in the money contracts, look at out of the money contracts, and then look at at the money contracts, right? And look at different expires, right? Look at the weekly contracts, look at zero DTEs, right? And then look at maybe even like monthly contracts" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3772s-3810s])
- **[October 11: Psychology - read Trading in the Zone + self-reflection]** "October 11th... I want you to read... the Mark Douglas Trading in the Zone... I want you to sit down piece of paper... and I want you to write down your goals, right? Your goals for 2025." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3943s-4050s])
- **[October 12: Find and deeply backtest ONE setup]** "now it is your time to find the one setup that aligns with you... back test that set up to gain more confidence. So every time you see it in real time, you'll be able to execute on that trade." (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4228s-4295s])
- **[October 13: Throughout week - trade first hour for real-time practice]** "throughout this week, I want you guys to be trading... just be at the market and pay attention, right, for the first hour or whenever you can, right? Just pay attention to the markets in real time, because obviously, all this education in this theoretical education is good, right? But you need to, at one point, make it real education" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [4660s-4692s])
- **[Backtest volume - high volume days vs low volume days for break-and-retest]** "I would go through and I would look for on high volume days on days with more volume were stocks more likely to break and retest and work right compared to break and retest and fail right so on high volume days did the stock work more on low volume days did the stock work less" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_6_Risk_Management_What_To_Focus_on_As_A_New_Trader_transcript.txt [3542s-3569s])

# EXTRACTION: mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **Definition of standard break-and-retest vs. play-the-open** "With the normal break and retest, we are waiting for the trend to confirm... However, playing the open, you enter before a clear trend" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1049s-1054s])

- **Standard break-and-retest: wait for first 5-minute candle** "Let's say you have the first five minute candle, right? This is your first five minute candle. So you mark out the high and you mark out the low. When you get this retest of the first five minute candle on the one minute timeframe, all you literally have to do is enter" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [643s-658s])

- **Standard break-and-retest: wait for trend to confirm** "With break and retest, we are waiting for the trend to confirm... we enter after the trend is clear" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1016s-1049s])

- **Standard break-and-retest: risk level** "your risk level will always be a break of key level or a break of trend or market structure, candle close" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1162s-1167s])

- **Break-and-retest as fallback when open trade is missed** "if you're not able to catch that trade, right, this was the break in retest trade, okay, on Nvidia... we were able to capture the second best entry for Nvidia 4 move to the upside" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3098s-3118s])

- **Break-and-retest entry on Nvidia after missing open** "we were able to capture this retest trade... we know that we missed the playing the open... However, we know that there is still a move left on Nvidia to this previous high... because that's where the most liquidity will be sitting" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3025s-3046s])

- **Break-and-retest entry on Nvidia had better QQQ context** "On top of this, this right here had better context with QQQ, okay? And because of this, this is what we took for the break in retest" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3048s-3055s])

- **Best break-and-retest entry on Nvidia was 954 candle** "I would say break and retest wise, the best entry was over here, the 954 candle" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3196s-3199s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

- **Opening candle retest described as "the opening candle"** "the opening candle, right? We come back, we retest, nice move, right? Showing strong price action from buyers" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2800s-2807s])

- **Opening candle entry on Nvidia** "you would have been able to capture this whole move based off of just the opening candle, right? Literally you enter in at 9.30" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2938s-2943s])

- **Stop loss on opening candle trade** "your stop loss here would be a break of this candle... I would give it a little bit more room than the break of the candle, right? So I would put it probably like a couple of cents or like 10, 15 cents below that just to give it a little bit more room" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2984s-2999s])

- **NOT named "one candle rule" or "opening candle retest" as a formal named strategy** — described descriptively as "the opening candle" retest

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 4. Order Blocks

- **Order block definition: down close candle before continuation** "the way you get the order block, right, is going to be the down close candle. So you can see this, we're moving up, right? And then this down close candle before the continuation move up" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3380s-3387s])

- **Order block drawing: wick-to-body** "we just mark out the top wick to the body. So this is the down... I usually just do body to top wick when I'm doing it" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3389s-3408s])

- **Order block can be whole candle or wick-to-body** "You could also mark out this whole thing right here... You could mark out this whole down close candle as an order block. I usually just do body to top wick when I'm doing it. However, this technically is the whole thing" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3400s-3410s])

- **Order block entry for retest** "And that would be your order block entry for the retest on top of obviously this retest of this previous resistance turn support" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3413s-3418s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **More emphasis on key levels for play-the-open vs. break-and-retest** "the biggest thing for, or the biggest differences, I would say, number one, is going to be more emphasis on key levels... You need to trust your levels" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [779s-786s])

- **Key levels vs. zones: personal preference** "JDubb obviously has his way that he draws key levels. Now notice most of the time ours are very, very aligned. However, he kind of draws zones whereas I draw key levels themselves specifically. I don't really draw zones" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [854s-864s])

- **Both key levels and zones work** "You can either take that JDubb way where you have that general area, or you can take my way where you kind of have the key level itself. That's really up to you. Both of them work in their own, you know, kind of way" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [866s-875s])

- **Trust in key levels is primary edge for play-the-open** "your main kind of edge... Our edge comes from QQQ. With this, you're obviously still looking at QQQ... but really you need to trust your key level... Sometimes QQQ may not look the best in this scenario... But this is where the trust in the key level really comes in" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [824s-846s])

- **Pre-market low as key level for entries** "I would mark out the pre-market low level which is right here... And I would simply wait for the stock to come back into this level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1797s-1805s])

- **Previous day level as profit target** "we can have profit targets here at previous day levels as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2398s-2402s])

- **123.13 level was resistance where stock stalled** "12313 was just where we had resistance, right? It's where the stock stalled out and then moved back down for the retest. It's also where the order block is" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3257s-3264s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **Play-the-open defined as 9:30am to 9:45am Eastern** "When I say play the open, I usually mean anywhere from around 9.30 a.m. Eastern to around, I want to say 9.45-ish, right? That would be like playing the open. And then everything after this would just be like the regular break and retest" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [237s-248s])

- **Most volatility is first 1-5 minutes** "the most volatility in the market is usually the first one to five minutes" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1128s-1130s])

- **Most volatility 9:30-11:00** "The market is most volatile from 9.30, right? To 11 o'clock is usually when we trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1114s-1118s])

- **10am news can affect volatility** "Unless there's obviously news at 10 o'clock, right? Obviously 10 a.m. news sometimes affects this" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1123s-1125s])

- **Jerome Powell speaking — no trades** "I won't most likely take a trade because tomorrow is I believe Jerome Powell speaking. Therefore, I won't be taking a trade when that happens" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3471s-3477s])

## 7. Exits

- **Profit targets: high of day / low of day** "our profit targets are still gonna be high of day slash low of day, right? I always take high of day or low of day scales" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1312s-1317s])

- **Reason for HOD/LOD scaling: realized profits/cashflow** "The reason I do this is to... make sure that we have realized profits or cashflow... And this way we stay in the game much longer" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1319s-1330s])

- **Scaling reduces RRR but protects** "Yes, it may reduce RRR, right? But at the end of the day, if the trade even does decide to reverse back against us, right, we would still exit out of the trade for breakeven or a small loss" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1333s-1342s])

- **A+ trade scaling: 15-20% at first target** "I would take anywhere from 15 to 20%, right? I would only take 15 to 20% on a really, really high probability trade, right? On an A plus trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1412s-1416s])

- **A+ trade scaling: 20% at second level, 50% at final, 10% ride** "I take another, let's say 20%, right? So let's say we're at about 40% here... And then I'd leave the rest... let's say we get to the final profit target. I take about 50 here... And then I'd still let 10% ride" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1454s-1466s])

- **Letting 10% ride: bar by bar on 5-min or 10-min** "The way I would let it ride is bar by bar... Bar by bar simply means you're looking kind of at each candlestick on not the one minute, but you can go over to the five minute chart or the 10 minute chart, and you're basically bar by barring it" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1473s-1486s])

- **Bar-by-bar exit: sell remaining on change of character** "So whenever the trade fails or the thesis, right? So whenever we get the change of character here, right? Intraday, that's where I would sell out the remaining top 10%" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1490s-1496s])

- **Choppy market scaling: take more off at each level** "if it was like a choppier market... I would probably take a little bit more off at each level... So here, it would probably go from 20 to 30%, 30% to 35%... 30 to 35%, this would be, once again, 30%, and then this would be the remaining up here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1514s-1530s])

- **Scaling at key levels helps hold through consolidation** "Notice how we do have to sit through a little bit of consolidation here... and this is exactly why you want to take those scales... It's very, very important that you take those scales so you can sit through this kind of consolidation period" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2477s-2487s])

- **Scaling at psych levels (420, 419)** "Those scales at those key levels, right? 420.74s, 420 key level scale, right? You could have a 419 scale because that's another psych level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2004s-2011s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **Higher timeframe for play-the-open: 4-hour and 1-hour** "the higher timeframe that I personally look at patterns on is going to be the four hour timeframe and the one hour timeframe" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [546s-550s])

- **Reason for 4H/1H: can be filled in a trading day** "the reason I only trade the one hour and the four hour is because this can be filled, right? The higher timeframe objectives on one hour and four hour can be filled in trade day. When you have higher timeframe objectives on one week or daily, right? You're going to have to take a couple of days for those to get filled" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [762s-776s])

- **Weekly timeframe fakes out too much** "the problem with like the one week, for example, is that it, for some reason, it really does... it fakes me out a little bit too much... because it takes too long" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [570s-580s])

- **Weekly to 1-minute execution is impractical** "if you're going from the weekly timeframe, your thesis is on the weekly timeframe and you're trying to execute it on the one minute timeframe, yeah, good luck... You're going to get faked out a lot and the risk to reward that you would have to put on the trade would be obviously based off the weekly... you're going to have to increase this by 10X" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [582s-614s])

- **HTF thesis allows entry before 5-minute confirmation** "The only time I enter in before the five minutes is if I have a higher timeframe thesis" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [183s-185s])

- **HTF break-and-retest: already broke on HTF, retesting on 1-min** "it is retesting because on the higher timeframe, it already broke out... So we're just waiting for the retest on the one minute intraday timeframe" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [224s-233s])

- **HTF pattern examples: flag patterns, flat tops, double tops/bottoms** "I usually just look for flat tops or flags. Those are my favorite. I look for double tops and double bottoms as well, but flat tops and flags are gonna be my favorite pattern that I trade on the higher time frame" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3208s-3217s])

- **HTF pattern on AMD: double top + flag** "not only is AMD making a double top, but at the same time, we're making a flag pattern" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2658s-2665s])

- **HTF pattern on Nvidia: flat top + previous day level retest** "the one hour timeframe not only had a flat top, but it was retesting the previous day level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2887s-2890s])

- **HTF targets from 1-hour timeframe** "these higher timeframe targets, once again, are from the one hour timeframe... And you can see this is the pivot level... that we would gravitate towards... Based off liquidity" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2811s-2824s])

- **HTF patterns don't occur every day** "these higher timeframe patterns happen all the time, not every single day will they occur, but they do happen all the time" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2568s-2571s])

- **HTF thesis: emphasis on pattern but still trade price action** "I will always trade the price action that's presented to me, but I will have a little bit more emphasis on, you know, different tickers if they have a better higher timeframe pattern" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2557s-2565s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **Play-the-open is ~30% of trades, break-and-retest ~70%** "If I was to guess 70% break and retest, right, and then 30% probably open trades" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3143s-3147s])

- **Play-the-open requires more confidence and experience** "you need to have so much more conviction... And confidence, both conviction and confidence in your key levels... And your setup and your edge. Now, until you kind of have the break and retest down itself... you're not going to master this strategy... Because you don't have as many confirmations" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1071s-1093s])

- **Play-the-open requires acting fast** "you need to act fast. This is probably the biggest thing... these examples happen one minute into the market open... So if you don't have that proper thesis on the higher timeframe... you're not going to" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [888s-921s])

- **Starter position for opening plays** "For opening plays, you always start off with a starter position. If it comes back down into your key level, which increases your risk to reward, you can add more into your position" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3221s-3228s])

- **Reason for starter position: more volatility at open** "I personally would start out with a starter because there's more volatility in the open... And therefore, you kind of have to give it a little bit more room for that risk" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3233s-3241s])

- **Big candle wicks at open can hit stop then reverse** "sometimes what will happen is, for example, even if it comes down to your stop loss, it may have a big candle move down below your stop loss, but then come right back up... Because we have so much volume that there were just buyers coming in" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3243s-3253s])

- **AMD trade: hesitant because buying at high of day** "we were basically buying that trade at high of day. So that was kind of why I was like a little bit more hesitant on it" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2793s-2795s])

- **Nvidia open trade: missed because didn't feel comfortable** "this was a bigger candle to be honest, and I just didn't feel comfortable executing this" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2912s-2914s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **AMD trade: first scale ~1.19 R:R, full target 4.74 R:R** "That was about 1.9, 1.19 on these scales... However, right, if you look all the way up here, this would have been a 4.74 trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2781s-2789s])

- **Nvidia open trade: 3.2 R:R** "you can see this is a 3.2 hour trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3004s])

- **SPY bear flag example: 8.23 R:R** "this would be an 8.23 risk to reward trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1892s])

- **Tesla wedge example: 4.35 R:R** "this would be a 4.35 risk to reward trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2261s])

- **Nvidia open trade: 2-3 point move on underlying** "you would have caught this basically what? Two to almost three point move on Nvidia" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2927s-2930s])

- **Nvidia open trade: done by 11:30, majority out by 10:20** "you would have been done the day at 11.30... But the majority of your trade would have already been out near this level right here around 10.20 or 10.06" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2950s-2956s])

- **SPY bear flag: entered 5 minutes into open, 70% done by 10:30** "We entered into this trade about one, two, three, four, five minutes into the open. Most of the trade, I'd say 70% of the trade worked out right before 1030" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2026s-2031s])

- **Stop loss placement: 5-10 cents below low** "I'd put it like five, 10 cents a little bit below that" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2768s-2769s])

- **Stop loss on opening candle: 10-15 cents below** "I would put it probably like a couple of cents or like 10, 15 cents below that just to give it a little bit more room" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2993s-2996s])

## Heiken Ashi Candles (Supplementary)

- **Purpose: filter noise, identify patterns on HTF** "HA candles take the average price of the stock candle to create candles, to create the candlestick and filter out market noise" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [416s-422s])

- **Do NOT enter on Heiken Ashi candles** "We are not entering in on Heiken Ashi candles because they don't give you enough data to actually enter the stock in off of... I do not recommend entering off HA candles either" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [464s-476s])

- **Use HA on 4H/1H to identify patterns, then switch to regular chart** "Go to the four hour, go to the one hour and simply just look at the AJ candles to see any patterns that form... And you can do this first and then mark out your key levels... But I like to just go on the AJ candles just to identify any patterns that might be forming that I simply cannot see within the actual bar chart" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1704s-1722s])

- **HA shows trend strength via consecutive candles** "seven red candles, right, on Heiken Ashi in total compared to all of the rest of the green candles... So this, once again, shows us how strong of a trend we have to the upside" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [514s-526s])

## QQQ Alignment

- **Edge comes from QQQ** "Our edge comes from QQQ" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [829s])

- **Still look at QQQ for play-the-open** "With this, you're obviously still looking at QQQ. You're still looking at the correlation of QQQ" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [831s-835s])

- **QQQ may not look best — trust key level instead** "Sometimes QQQ may not look the best in this scenario... But this is where the trust in the key level really comes in" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [840s-844s])

- **Break-and-retest entry on Nvidia had better QQQ context** "On top of this, this right here had better context with QQQ, okay? And because of this, this is what we took for the break in retest" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3048s-3055s])

- **Market context important even in examples** "it's really important to have that market context in mind as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2521s-2523s])

## Risk Management (Play-the-Open Specific)

- **Risk level for play-the-open: break of another key level or HTF pattern** "for higher timeframe or playing the open, your risk will be a break of another key level or higher timeframe pattern" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1169s-1174s])

- **Reason: break of structure not yet identified intraday** "This is because the chart has informed me that because break of structure hasn't been identified in today, therefore, you need to give the trade room to breathe" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1176s-1182s])

- **Stop loss sources: previous day levels, HTF key levels, HTF pattern** "The key level we can have from either previous day... or, right, we can have the higher timeframe key levels... Or we have the higher timeframe pattern" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1253s-1265s])

- **Stop loss on SPY bear flag: close above pre-market low** "your stop loss here would be a close simply above this level right here now... above the previous candle that broke... And this is because if we do break back above this level of pre-market low... we could see some consolidation before we see another move down" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [1863s-1888s])

- **Stop loss on Tesla wedge: break above previous high** "our stop loss in this specific scenario would simply be just a break above... this previous 209.02 level or just a break a little bit above this previous high that we made right here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2247s-2258s])

- **Stop loss on Nvidia open: break of opening candle** "your stop loss here would be a break of this candle... I would give it a little bit more room than the break of the candle" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [2984s-2992s])

- **If 1-minute breaks HTF structure, don't enter** "If the one minute breaks the structure on the higher timeframe line, then that means you don't enter... if it breaks on the first one minute candle, then you don't enter, you wait" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_7_How_To_Play_The_Open_Multi_Time_Frame_Analysis.txt [3353s-3361s])

# EXTRACTION: mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **A+ entry definition: candle breaks level, tries to reclaim, fails** "an A plus entry... is when the candle actually breaks your level, tries to see [reclaim]... if a candle actually breaks the level and still can't break and comes back down it shows that there's too many sellers for the buyers to actually push it up" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3468s-3484s])
- **A+ entry vs consolidation entry** "rather than when it's just consolidating underneath that level because then you know those breakout buyers will come and they'll push up the candle" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3484s-3491s])
- **Entry trigger: inverted hammer / hammer candle at retest** "with the very very very nice inverted hammer candle... you can enter into Tesla here looking for low of day" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3568s-3576s])
- **Entry trigger: strong hammer candle at retest** "we come back and retest that level which we do right here on this candle... it's also a strong hammer candle so we can enter into Tesla here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3766s-3775s])
- **Break-and-retest on Nvidia example: break previous day high, retest, short** "we have to break previous day high... when we broke previous day high we actually came out for the retest... if you were to enter into this short position here... your risk was literally just a candle close above this level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4390s-4411s])
- **Break-and-retest on SPY: break opening candle level, retest with good candle** "we got a retest on SPY... and this is a really good candle as well for that retest entry" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4106s-4117s])
- **Break of market structure on QQQ defines entry timing** "the break of market structure on QQQ... would essentially occur on QQQ if we break below this level right here... we come back and retest... this is a really low risk entry" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3504s-3608s])
- **Target: low of day** "you can enter into Tesla here looking for low of day" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3576s-3580s])
- **Target: previous day low** "for Nvidia it came down to previous day low" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4461s-4465s])
- **A+ setups don't always hit full target** "sometimes even A plus setups don't work out in full... the full technically profit target... it doesn't work out like that sometimes but this is still a really good trade for the risk to reward" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3673s-3687s])
