## 5. Key Levels
- **Daily timeframe: mark daily high (green), daily low (red)** "mark out the daily high... we mark that green... mark out the daily low... we mark that red" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4533s-4546s])
- **Daily timeframe: also mark previous pivot highs and lows** "mark out the next day low... mark out the next high... the previous pivot highs... the pivot low down here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4549s-4585s])
- **No HTF levels inside daily range except daily high/low** "the only higher time frame levels in the daily range should be the daily high and the daily low... there should not be a higher time frame level in between your daily high and your daily low" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4616s-4631s])
- **One-minute pivot points marked as white lines** "the white lines are just going to be previous pivot points" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4794s-4799s])
- **Green lines = HTF resistance, Red lines = HTF support** "the green lines are higher time frame resistance the red lines are higher time frame support" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4789s-4794s])
- **White lines = previous one-minute candle pivot points** "the white lines are the previous one minute candles... previous day one minute pivot points" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4499s-4509s])
- **Levels are zones, not exact ticks** "it's more of a zone-ish area... you don't have to get it to the exact set... as long as you have the key area in that zone and understand where the key points are" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5045s-5086s])
- **JDub draws levels as zones** "JDub kind of draws out... he'd have this zone right here... and then he'd have like a zone like this" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5049s-5065s])
- **Level selection: how much it sticks out, not minimum touch count** "it's not how many touch points it's how much it stands out to you on the chart... I personally just look at how much it sticks out... it comes with experience" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4863s-4881s])
- **Start fresh chart every day** "you remove all the drawings right in the beginning of the day... every single day brand new... you want to start off with a brand new chart every single day" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4708s-4718s])
- **Pre-market high/low: draw when gap up/down, otherwise confluence** "I usually don't draw pre-market high and low because they usually just kind of come in with... whatever I'm trading... I'll draw it when we gap up or gap down specifically" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4906s-4927s])
- **Pre-market high/low used with confluence** "I do trade pre-market high and low retest they just 90 percent of the time... they'll just be in conjunction with the level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5318s-5329s])
- **Drawing levels: body touches, wick touches, average of touches** "the body touched the body touched the wick touched... I kind of do it more on the bodies... if you had to make this line even more perfect it would be like right here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5024s-5045s])
- **Futures: same method, mark previous day high/low, pivot points, use 9:30 session** "for futures... mark out the high and low of previous day... mark out the previous pivot points... from the 9:30 session I would mark out where the pivot levels are" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5116s-5222s])
- **Don't draw unclear levels, keep mental note instead** "I personally don't see a level here too much... I would just have like a mental note... I wouldn't draw it out going into the day because this isn't as clear of a level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5266s-5295s])
- **Drawing levels: recommended in the morning** "for you guys I would recommend in the morning... because you have pre-market right in the beginning" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [5001s-5011s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **FOMC day: not an A+ trade because of context** "it was FOMC it wasn't an A plus trade because of the context" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4429s-4435s])

## 7. Exits
- **Scale when QQQ hits target level** "the way I would scale on this is basically when QQQ hits a low of day that's where I would scale on Tesla as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3628s-3636s])
- **Scale at key levels, not just low of day** "we do want to still have some key levels up where we want to scale out on" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3647s-3650s])
- **Stop loss: break above VWAP/previous level** "our stop... is basically a break above that VWAP slash 202 level... if we broke above that we would have to cut the trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3687s-3698s])
- **Stop loss: break of market structure on QQQ** "on QQQ essentially you would... need to make sure that QQQ holds below 296.85 the whole market structure... our stop loss doesn't change... I only change stop loss when we change market structure" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4126s-4184s])
- **Cut remaining position when QQQ breaks market structure** "you'd cut it when it broke below that... for the rest of the trailers at about 10:35... that's where the market structure broke on QQQ and that's where you can cut the rest of your trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3935s-3947s])
- **Stop loss: candle close above level** "your risk was literally just a candle close above this level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4408s-4411s])
- **Stop loss: psychological level** "that 204 psych level could be used as our stop loss" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3878s-3881s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **HTF levels: mark daily high/low, previous pivot highs/lows** (covered in Key Levels section)
- **4H/1H: look for patterns, not pivot levels inside daily range** "on the four hour time frame... what we're really looking for is if we can see a pattern... on the one hour time frame... is there any specific level... no... because in the daily range we don't want to draw out any higher time frame levels" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4585s-4626s])
- **HTF profit targets as magnet levels** "the higher time frame profit targets that can be used as magnet levels" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3993s-4000s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **A+ setup criteria: strong momentum/relative weakness** "this stock you're trading has really strong momentum because as you know we're momentum traders and we need that strong momentum... we don't want to be stuck in chop" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3369s-3386s])
- **A+ setup: relative weakness into open** "we have relative weakness right into the open... this is very important for an A plus setup" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3365s-3373s])
- **A+ entry: candle breaks level, tries to reclaim, fails** (covered in Break-and-Retest)
- **A+ setup: QQQ forming pattern + stock with relative strength/weakness** "this was an A plus trade with the candlesticks with the relative strength with QQQ forming a pattern as well... above VWAP on the chart that we're actually trading" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3985s-3996s])
- **A+ setup: QQQ holding key level with strong hammer candle** "at the same time right there on QQQ we were holding that 294.57 with a strong hammer candle as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3863s-3871s])
- **A+ setup: break below VWAP + market structure break + candle/price action confluence** "great example of how to use patterns market context market structure break below VWAP and candles and price action to essentially help you enter trades much more effectively and identify A plus setups" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4212s-4231s])
- **Only take A+ trades in live sessions** "we try to take only A plus setups... because if I take low quality trades then a bunch of people will follow me into the low quality trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [2867s-2901s])
- **Don't trade if not ready - just watch** "for those people don't trade just watch... don't even open the broker... there's no point of you opening the broker to lose money because you're going to enter the trade only because off of the signal" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3157s-3196s])
- **Paper trading: 1-2 weeks max to learn platform** "if you want to learn the platform... for the first week or two... you can paper trade... after the one or two weeks... you're really doing too much" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3219s-3243s])
- **Paper trading too long is a crutch** "the longer you paper trade it's like a crutch... it's like basically riding a bike with training wheels... you have to at one point let go of the training wheels" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3246s-3260s])
- **Start with small real account instead** "even if you start off with a hundred dollar account or like a five hundred dollar account... something that really really doesn't affect you too much... I would do that" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3260s-3270s])
- **Goal: enter trades before Scarface/JDub** "the goal is to make sure that you are in the trade... you enter into trades before me and him... there's mastermind students... they execute before me and Jack even get into the trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3056s-3078s])
- **Confidence comes from backtesting** "the fastest way to gain confidence and conviction is back testing your setup" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3007s-3013s])
- **Fear of losing = lack of confidence in system** "the biggest reason that you're not executing is because you're just scared of losing the trade... and you're scared of losing the trade because you don't fully have the confidence within the system itself" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3115s-3125s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Tesla A+ trade: entry to low of day target** "you can enter into Tesla here looking for low of day... that body candle so 198.84 to 198.52... it actually technically didn't hit low of day" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3576s-3593s])
- **Tesla A+ trade: entry 204.38, target 206, then 207-208** "we're looking for a move up on Tesla to 206... and then of course the higher 207 and then 208 which it did hit here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3779s-3800s])
- **SPY bear flag: target 400 psych level** "looking for... those lower levels at 400 psych... that level has been used as resistance and support on SPY so much intraday" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4141s-4161s])
- **Nvidia A+ trade: entry at previous day high retest, target 124.11** "your profit target right the first one itself was right here at this 124.11 level" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4414s-4424s])
- **Nvidia A+ trade: QQQ target 487.44 and 486.09** "Q's has this previous 487.44 it can get to and then also 486.09 down there" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4452s-4457s])

## Discipline Framework (NEW TOPIC)
- **Three types of discipline: Preventive, Supportive, Corrective** "there's actually three types of discipline... preventive discipline... supportive discipline... corrective discipline" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [505s-798s])
- **Preventive discipline: establishing rules** "preventive simply means that you're just establishing the rules of trading... establish your rules" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [535s-547s])
- **Preventive discipline alone is insufficient** "if you break your rules what does preventive discipline say nothing... all you have is hope" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [560s-585s])
- **Supportive discipline: warning after first rule break** "supportive discipline happens when the first time you kind of break your rule you don't really do too much except give yourself a warning... like a yellow card" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [678s-700s])
- **Corrective discipline: consequences after repeated rule breaks** "corrective discipline is if you fail to change behavior after supportive discipline... this has consequences" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [798s-805s])
- **Corrective discipline example: run 5km for each rule break** "corrective discipline for myself... whenever I broke a rule I had to run five kilometers... no matter what the weather was... every single time I broke my rule I did it" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1009s-1050s])
- **Corrective discipline should be something beneficial you dislike** "I usually recommend to do something that's going to be beneficial for you that you don't like... something that would benefit you in the real world... but you don't want to do" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1380s-1409s])
- **Corrective discipline must be done same day** "you have to use corrective discipline the same day for it to be as effective as possible" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [2729s-2736s])
- **Physical consequences bridge screen to real world** "when you add the physical aspect to it... something in the real world you have to do that you don't like... all of a sudden it gets much more real" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1082s-1100s])
- **Journaling as discipline: tedious but effective** "I hated journaling... it was taking me at least a minimum of 15 to 20 minutes... imagine you were taking two to three trades per day and you had to do this every single day and you completely hated it" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1111s-1210s])
- **Journaling changes trade selection mindset** "all of a sudden I wasn't looking at the trade to think oh how much money can I make I was thinking what's the probability of the trade winning how much money could I lose and is it worth taking this trade" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1249s-1260s])
- **Negative reinforcement works better than positive in trading** "I've seen that negative reinforcement works better than positive reinforcement" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [778s-784s])
- **Focus on weaknesses for easy wins** "the easy wins is to focus on the weakness... if you focus on the weakness... the strengths are already there" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1903s-1936s])
- **Start small, incrementally increase** "start with small size... the reason new year's resolutions don't work is because you're trying to do a whole 360 on life" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [1996s-2110s])
- **Quarterly goals with reflection** "I divide it up per quarter... every quarter I give myself kind of deadlines to hit... it's a reflection on what you've done in that one quarter" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [2470s-2538s])
- **Discipline in life = discipline in trading** "if you don't understand how the discipline works in your life... you just don't have discipline in your life you won't have discipline in trading that is a guarantee" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [2598s-2604s])
- **Once system is profitable, only psychology stops consistency** "once you have a profitable system once you have the edge proven... the only thing stopping you from the consistency is your own mind" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [2751s-2765s])

## QQQ Alignment
- **QQQ context for A+ setups: break of market structure on QQQ** "the break of market structure on QQQ... would essentially occur on QQQ if we break below this level right here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3504s-3516s])
- **QQQ as stop loss reference** "what we can use for QQQ essentially... as a stop loss is this break of this level right here... looking for low of day on QQQ" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3611s-3625s])
- **QQQ forming pattern confirms A+ setup** "this was an A plus trade with... QQQ forming a pattern as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3985s-3996s])
- **QQQ holding key level with hammer candle = strong context** "at the same time right there on QQQ we were holding that 294.57 with a strong hammer candle as well" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [3863s-3871s])
- **QQQ and SPY both showing weakness = confirmation** "we have weakness in both names here" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4037s-4041s])
- **Can use SPY for relative strength/weakness on QQQ trades** "if you're playing QQQ you can use SPY to see relative strength and weakness vice versa" (mastermind-5-0_Lesson_8_A_Setups_How_To_Create_Discipline_transcript.txt [4053s-4061s])

## Performance Coaching Framework (NEW TOPIC - methodology only)
- **Performance coaching vs psychology coaching: framework vs root cause** "the difference between the psychology coaching, and maybe the performance coaching... people that they understand their psychology... they understand the root of that issue... what we are going to address on this performance coaching will be how to create a work frame to avoid these mistakes, how to create a framework that even with my triggers, I can stay inside of my rules" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [962s-1078s])
- **Performance coaching: clear rules, entry criteria, goals** "Do I have clear rules? Do I have a clear entry criteria? Do I have something that I'm following as a goal as a trader or as an individual?" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1078s-1094s])
- **Framework creation for emotional decisions** "we will try to create a framework for you. So you can leave all of your emotional decisions on side and start taking... decisions based on your data based on your experience" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [386s-423s])
- **Plan when calm, not under pressure** "it's always easier to plan on a calm head and a slow environment for when the real struggle shows... it's going to be easier to set the rules when we are calm, instead of trying to guess what to do when a trade is going against us" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1196s-1219s])
- **What-if scenario preparation** "we will try to have these emotional regulation techniques so you can prepare for any scenario in your trade" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1560s-1569s])
- **Money as goal leads to P&L-based decisions** "money should be... one of the goals, but that can't be the main goal because you will be taking decisions based on your P&L and we need you to take the P&L from your side" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1492s-1508s])
- **Journaling should include emotions and triggers** "journaling your emotion, journaling your trigger, journaling if you set the right stop loss, journaling if your plan actually worked or if you didn't plan" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1611s-1628s])
- **Most issues resolved by applying what you already know** "most of the issues on the performance... won't be resolved with new stuff. We will try to put together everything you already know... most of you already have the answers guys" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1420s-1449s])
- **Sizing up/down depends on individual** "there's no good and wrong about sizing up and down. It all depends on you and what you want to do, what you want to accomplish and what you are facing now" (performance-coaching_Neto_s_Introduction_transcript.txt [1324s-1335s])

## NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE
All 10 extraction topics (Break-and-retest, One-candle-rule, 84% rule, Order blocks, Key levels, Time-of-day, Exits, HTF application, Trade selection, Concrete numbers)

# EXTRACTION: performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Strong retest / price action confirmation]** "When I have, when I wait for strong price action or strong retest on my key levels, instead of just a dodge or instead of almost a retest, when I have this strong retest, I have my best winners. Why? Because it's confirming the market structure. It's actually confirming the direction of the movement. Usually I don't have any drawdown on those trades." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1016s-1045s])

- **[Weak retest = worst winners]** "On my worst trades... my winners? Well, I have a weak retest. So maybe just a brief touch or almost a touch. And then, I mean, sometimes I feel like it was kind of luck." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1139s-1156s])

- **[Put-side break-and-retest: less confirmation needed]** "Now that we are on a down trending market... when taking puts, we are not expecting like a perfect textbook, you know, like inverse hammer candle, because... when you are looking to the downside, it's usually elevator down, escalator up... So you are not looking for 10 out of 10 confirmation criteria." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1896s-1936s])

- **[Hammer/shooting star as retest confirmation]** "Usually they take a hammer candle or an inverted hammer candle or how they call them, these shooting star candle as a retest for the key level." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [998s-1016s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 4. Order Blocks

- **[Order blocks only with confluence]** "I don't like to take order blocks too much. I mean, if there's an order block in a key level, that's something that I like, but I don't like to take an order block on an off trend where the order block is in the middle of nowhere... not even close to pre-market levels, not even close to previous day levels, not even close to the daily chart key levels. So it was just an order block. Well, I don't like to take trades out of just order blocks." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1448s-1497s])

- **[Order blocks can work for others with data]** "There's people that they like and there's people that they have their data backing up their decisions. It's not only that I don't like them, it's that I don't have enough data to decide if it's working for me or if it has been working on my tickers." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1497s-1520s])

- **[Cam's counter-example: order blocks work for him]** "I actually think that, you know, until recently, I wasn't identifying order blocks correctly. But, you know, after going through so many of Jack's lessons and, you know, just kind of reviewing my statistics, I started back testing it. And I've seen a huge improvement. And that for me is the number one playbook that's been working for me, at least in these markets is our order blocks place." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [2326s-2354s])

## 5. Key Levels

- **[Multiple levels watched, not all traded]** "People ask me all the time like, okay, so what is the key level that I should be paying the most attention? And I was like, dude, I mean, I'm looking at all of them. That's why you listen to me mentioning them, right? However, I don't take all of them." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1435s-1448s])

- **[Levels mentioned in trade reviews]** "I'm talking about the one minute opening range, the five minute opening range, the daily gap, the pre-market levels, the previous day levels. And also I'm talking about the key levels on the daily chart." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1410s-1425s])

- **[HOD/LOD as scaling targets]** "Every time or 90 something of the times that I was taking partials on this 10, 15% level actually went to hit my high of the day or my low of the day. So what was the change that I did? Well, I started instead of taking like 20, 25% of my trade, I started taking a bit more, but once I hit the high of the day or low of the day." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1734s-1769s])

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Personal entry window: 9:40-10:30]** "My time, my trading time, well, my entry time, it's from 940 to 1030. I only trade, I only take entries on this 15 minutes window." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [538s-548s])

- **[Why narrow window]** "I'm not good reading the market on the lunchtime. I'm not, I'm not good reading the market on the power hour. So I'm good reading the market on that time." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [548s-561s])

- **[Not good holding past noon]** "Even when my entry criteria tells me that I'm, I can take trades from 940 to 1030, I'm not super good holding positions beyond noon." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [588s-604s])

- **[Adapting to volatility: waiting longer]** "Tony and Jack... they have been saying. Okay, well, let's wait 10, 15 minutes for the market to settle. Let's wait to see if the indices show us a nice and clear trend or direction. Let's wait for the IV crush... They are waiting a bit more. They are not taking opening plays." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1964s-2002s])

- **[Opening plays to downside are different]** "If you are taking an opening play, it's to the downside. And there are a few things that happen with the downside opening plays. They are super fast. You need to be prepared prior to the market open and you need to have a light speed execution." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [2017s-2041s])

- **[Best days: Tuesday-Thursday]** "My journal says that I'm best at it's Tuesday to Thursday. I mean, Mondays and Fridays are, you know, like regular days for me. My best days are Tuesdays and Thursdays." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3530s-3543s])

- **[SPY/QQQ before 10am = gambling]** "The only traders that I know that trade SPY or QQQ before 10 a.m. Eastern time, they are not traders. They are gamblers." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [4449s-4466s])

- **[Best traders trade SPY/QQQ mostly before 10am]** "The best traders that I know, they only trade the SPY or QQQ. And it's mostly before 10 a.m. Why? Because all of these, because they identify a trend. They can start looking at volume. They look to price action. I mean, they add more and more and more stuff to their own strategies." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [4469s-4496s])

- **[Tito's best entry time: 9:40-9:50]** "Yeah, it's between, um, nine 40 and, uh, nine 50." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3035s-3041s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Scaling out: changed from early partials to HOD/LOD]** "I actually made a twist to my execution a few months ago. I was taking a look because I was taking some contracts out of my trade when I was hitting like 10 or 15% of my contracts. But then I started, I started looking at my journal, I started looking at my data and ended realizing that I was just leaving money on the table because every time or 90 something of the times that I was taking partials on this 10, 15% level actually went to hit my high of the day or my low of the day. So what was the change that I did? Well, I started instead of taking like 20, 25% of my trade, I started taking a bit more, but once I hit the high of the day or low of the day." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1704s-1769s])

- **[Best losers: early exit before stop loss hit]** "My best losers are when I take early exits instead of, you know, like forcing my trade to break my invalidation or my stop loss level." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1098s-1111s])

- **[Early exit based on context]** "Sometimes I don't wait for the candle closure if I'm looking at the context, right? If I'm looking at, I don't know, like the indices were already failing or whatever. So I just take an early exit." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1084s-1098s])

- **[Worst breakevens: forcing trades in choppy markets]** "My worst trades that ended as a break even? Well, it's when I just forced my trade and I entered when it was a choppy market. Instead of like a trending or waiting for the best setup, I decided just to enter in a trade. And suddenly I'm stuck into a choppy market or choppy ticker." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1244s-1269s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[Daily chart key levels mentioned]** "I'm talking about the key levels on the daily chart." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1425s])

- **[Know your ticker's daily/weekly/monthly levels]** "It's super important that you get to know your ticker. You identify your key levels on the daily chart, on the weekly chart, on the monthly chart, and you start planning on it." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3656s-3669s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[A+ setups timing on SPY/QQQ]** "Most of my A plus setups happen after nine o'clock or 10 o'clock for you or Eastern time." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [4418s-4425s])

- **[Cutting noise: trade your own plan]** "Start with two days a week where you have your plan, you have your tickers and you just cut the noise." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3477s-3492s])

- **[Don't follow coaches' trades blindly]** "If you are not very into Tesla, probably you don't know the local people, the key levels or the digital numbers. So you can miss one of these key levels... Tony knows Tesla like the back of his hand, right? So he knows what are the important key levels." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3615s-3642s])

- **[Market condition tracking]** "If you are recording or if you are tagging your trades, you should be tagging. So how was the market condition? It was choppy, it was trending, if it was up trending or if it was down trending." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1344s-1358s])

- **[Don't buy calls in downtrend]** "I don't buy calls when the market's trending down." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3817s-3821s])

- **[Focus on fewer tickers]** "I pulled back just to like four or five tickers that I watch." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [3845s-3849s])

- **[If setup doesn't happen in first hour, walk away]** "If my setup doesn't happen in the first hour, I just walk away for the day." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [4360s-4366s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers

- **[90%+ of partials at 10-15% went to HOD/LOD]** "Every time or 90 something of the times that I was taking partials on this 10, 15% level actually went to hit my high of the day or my low of the day." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1734s-1744s])

- **[1:2 risk-reward ratio mentioned]** "Taking trades that are at least one to two risk reward ratio." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1643s-1652s])

- **[Scaling: 20-25% changed to more at HOD/LOD]** "I started instead of taking like 20, 25% of my trade, I started taking a bit more, but once I hit the high of the day or low of the day." (performance-coaching_Session_10_Adaptability_in_Trading.txt [1751s-1769s])

# EXTRACTION FROM performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **[Strategy = break and retest; setups = different key levels]** "The strategy for this community, the strategy that we trade is the break and retest, right? That's the strategy. And the different setups that we take is the five minute opening range, the pre-market high and low, the previous day high and low, the order blocks or the break and retest of the high of the day or low of the day." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1692s-1718s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 4. Order Blocks
- **[Order blocks listed as one of the setups within break-and-retest strategy]** "The different setups that we take is the five minute opening range, the pre-market high and low, the previous day high and low, the order blocks or the break and retest of the high of the day or low of the day." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1699s-1718s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Pre-market levels refined night before]** "Every night I go back to my chart, refine my levels, and I found everything, right?" (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1125s-1130s])
- **[Wait for clear trend on indices before watching tech names]** "I wait for a clear trend or direction on the indices. First, before I'm watching any tech name, I'm looking at the indices." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1212s-1222s])
- **[Look for names moving same direction as indices first, then relative strength/weakness]** "I start looking at the tech names that are moving on the same direction, right? I am not looking at the relative strength or relative weakness at the market open. I'm looking at the names that are moving on the same direction than the indices, right? And once I identify the ones that are moving to the same direction, I start looking now for the one with relative strength or relative weakness against the indices." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1239s-1272s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **[Wait 10-15 minutes after market open for market to settle]** "I wait at least 10 to 15 minutes for the market to settle, right? The market to chill a little bit and, you know, avoid the IV crotch and all that stuff." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1193s-1203s])
- **[Fixed trading hours to avoid overtrading]** "Having a fixed training hours so you don't overtrade... you need to define what your schedule looks like. I mean, when is the time when you perform better, when you have mental clarity, when you are actually available to trade." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1505s-1566s])
- **[Personal example: stops trading at 11:30 AM]** "In my case, after 1130 in the morning is not a good time for me. I lost clarity, I'm tired of trading, I'm looking at charts and my concentration is not on my, it's not my A game. So usually what I do, it doesn't matter the outcome of my trade, I try to close my trades at 1130." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1608s-1636s])
- **[Trade only when in right emotional state]** "Trade only when you are in the right emotional state... if you are not on your A game, just stop trading." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2274s-2370s])
- **[Personal example: skipped trading because sick kid]** "Last week I didn't trade one day because my kid was sick... I was super tired in the morning and I just decided to not take a trade, right? Why? Because I wasn't on my A game." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2289s-2316s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Scale out at planned key levels/targets]** "You need to scale out on your key levels or your taking profit levels, right? Why? Because you were planning for that... if you were planning to take your trade off the table at certain level, do it. I mean, there should not be any FOMO on planned actions." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2107s-2136s])

## 8. HTF Application
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[Focus on one repeatable strategy]** "Focus on one repeatable strategy. Get really good at it. You don't need five strategies to be consistent. You just need one that you understand inside and out." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [875s-890s])
- **[Strategy must fit personality, style, and trading hours]** "Needs to be a strategy that fits your personality. Needs to be a strategy that fits your style. Needs to be a strategy that fits your trading hours because that will be the base to make it repeatable." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [891s-915s])
- **[Stop switching strategies after losses]** "If you have a loss one day and it's because your mistakes or... the setup didn't work and you are switching between strategies every time that you don't get a win, that's going to be super hard to find consistency." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [355s-374s])
- **[Trade with the trend: puts in downtrend, calls in uptrend]** "When I'm taking these trades, I'm taking the trades with the trend. So if it's down trending, I'm taking puts. If it's up trending, I'm taking calls." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1272s-1285s])
- **[Don't seek perfection, seek execution]** "Don't seek perfection, seek execution... don't try to look for the very bottom or the very top of the movements. Don't try to go for every penny of your trades." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2076s-2097s])
- **[One trade per day rule from student example]** "One of my rules on my whiteboard now, since I actually looked at all the data is one trade per day, win or lose." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2837s-2847s]) — *Note: This is a student sharing their personal rule, not the coach teaching it*

## ADDITIONAL METHODOLOGY RULES

### Pre-Market Routine
- **[Be at computer at least one hour before market open]** "I need to be at least one hour before the market opens in front of my computer because even when I do my levels the previous night." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1107s-1118s])
- **[Prepare levels night before]** "Every night I go back to my chart, refine my levels." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1125s-1130s])

### Post-Market Journaling
- **[Journal every trade, not just winners]** "Journal every trade, not only the winners, every trade for the next 10 trading days." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [3670s-3679s])
- **[Wait before journaling to clear emotions]** "You need to wait a few minutes or you need to go take a walk or get away of your desk so you can clear your emotions and you don't have your journal with a lot of heart, of emotions. You need to have your journal with technicals, with cold heart, cold brain thought." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1439s-1469s])
- **[More data in journal = more precise adjustments]** "The more data you add to your journal entries, the more precise data your system will have and the more or the easiest will be to make adjustments to perform better." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [1489s-1505s])

### Mindset
- **[Accept losses as part of the system, not mistakes]** "We need to accept losses, quote unquote, into our system, into our system, not just any loss. I mean, not a mistake loss, not an ego loss... we need to accept losses inside of our system." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2007s-2036s])
- **[Detach identity from outcomes]** "Detach your identity from outcomes. If you take a loss, you are not stupid. You are not failure. You are not a loser... The outcome of a trade doesn't have anything to you. It's not related to you." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2170s-2217s])
- **[P&L is a lagging indicator]** "The P&L or yeah, our P&L, it's a lagging indicator... you will first, you need to be the trader you want it to be, right? The discipline, consistent, et cetera. And then you will start having the best trades possible... So first you need to be, then you need to do, and at the end, you will have." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [3500s-3588s])

### Common Mistakes That Kill Consistency
- **[Revenge trading, oversizing, overtrading, hopping between strategies, trading when emotional]** "Revenge trading... oversizing positions... overtrading... hopping between the strategies... trading when emotional." (performance-coaching_Session_11_Consistency_in_Trading.txt [2937s-3088s])

# EXTRACTION FROM NEW TRANSCRIPTS

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **[Valid break requires displacement before retest]** "I learned to be patient for a real displacement and then retest. And that gave me better entries" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1922s-1948s])
- **[Break-and-retest vs dip-and-rip distinction]** "A dip and rip, which is an instant entry almost on the same candle, as opposed to a break and retest where you have to be patient enough to wait for the displacement and then the retest either on the one or the five" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2356s-2375s])
- **[Retest can occur on 1-minute or 5-minute timeframe]** "wait for the displacement and then the retest either on the one or the five" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2371s-2375s])
- **[Example of waiting for 5-minute retest when 1-minute didn't give one]** "with Nvidia, I took that trade with the thought of a one minute retest, but it never gave that to you, to us and just kept on going. So I waited for the five minute and that's where I took my entry" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2384s-2394s])

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest

**NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE**

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries

- **[84% rule mentioned in context of failed A+ setups]** "when I'm wrong, the 80% you know, the 84% rule comes in 10 minutes later and then the trade works out" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2234s-2246s])
- **[84% rule applies after A+ setups with higher timeframe alignment]** "if you can align your higher timeframes... that's an A plus set up... if it didn't work at first, it will work later" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2116s-2198s])
- **[Personal experience: rare to have two red trades back-to-back]** "it's super unusual that I took two red trades back to back. So that built me the confidence to take my trades" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1843s-1865s])

## 5. Key Levels

## 6. Time-of-Day

- **[Volume decreases at noon Eastern time]** "At noon Eastern time, volume decreases like heavily... most of the traders at New York, they go for lunch" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2915s-2937s])
- **[Noon to 1pm is autopilot period]** "from noon to 1 p.m. all the traders at New York Stock Exchange, they are at lunch... sometimes it's moving in automatic, like autopilot" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2962s-2973s])

## 7. Exits

- **[Using a 5-minute timer after entry to check validity]** "I use a timer on my computer every time I enter a trade... it's a five minute timer... every time it rings in my computer says, is this still a valid trade?" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1295s-1313s])
- **[Moving invalidation level after taking first/second profit targets]** "after I take my first profit point or my first or second take profit level, I start moving my invalidation level" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1341s-1355s])
- **[Different approaches: moving stop vs trusting original level]** "There's people like Jack that he says, you know what, I trust so much on my levels that I am going to wait for my level. And if it doesn't take my level and it goes back to my stop loss, I mean, I'll take the loss" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1357s-1374s])
- **[No single right answer on stop placement - depends on personality]** "it's about knowing your personality... if you feel better with a higher win rate or you feel better with a higher risk reward ratio... there's no right and wrong answer about it" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1388s-1401s])

## 8. HTF Application

- **[A+ setup defined by higher timeframe alignment]** "if you can align your higher timeframes, say, weekly, daily, four hour chart or the daily four hour and one hour chart to your smaller timeframes? I mean, that's an A plus set up" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2116s-2136s])
- **[HTF alignment makes failures easier to identify]** "if they for any reason fail, it's super easy to identify where they fail and how and cutting them quick... because if it fails now, I mean, the higher time frame is backing you" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2144s-2162s])
- **[HTF alignment gives confidence for re-entry after loss]** "all the trades that I'm taking now are with some higher time frame bias... so if it didn't work at first, it will work later" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2185s-2198s])

## 9. Trade Selection

- **[A+ setup = higher timeframe alignment]** "if you can align your higher timeframes, say, weekly, daily, four hour chart or the daily four hour and one hour chart to your smaller timeframes? I mean, that's an A plus set up" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2116s-2136s])
- **[Only taking A+ setups now]** "that's the only kind of set up that I'm taking now" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2136s-2139s])
- **[Coming into day with plan, only looking for A+ setups]** "coming into the day with a plan and trading their plan only looking for the A-plus setups... you don't chase price and you let it come and set up and happen" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [142s-162s])
- **[A+ setups give higher win percentage]** "only taking the A-plus setup, so then that gives you a higher win percentage" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [162s-170s])
- **[Strategy should match personality and risk tolerance]** "your strategy should match your personality and your risk tolerance" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [277s-295s])

## ADDITIONAL METHODOLOGY RULES

### Risk Management
- **[Define risk based on dollar amount, not contracts]** "I focus on dollar amount because that's how I'm used to do it... you need to define your size and your risk based on a dollar amount instead of contracts" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2648s-2731s])
- **[Earn the right to size up through experience]** "you have to earn the right to size up. And that means experience and being comfortable with your risk" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2736s-2743s])
- **[Eureka moment came from defining risk tolerance]** "my moment was very related to risk management... once I started mastering when to stop of my losses, everything made sense and everything clicked" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1782s-1803s])
- **[Invalidation level is key question]** "most of my questions are, okay, what is your invalidation level? I'm not asking for next levels. I'm not asking for price action. It's like, okay, how to identify my risk" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1726s-1742s])
- **[Going beyond max loss for day triggers loss of control]** "if I go beyond my max loss, allow it for the day, I feel like I'm not in control of my decision" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [1742s-1760s])

### Focus on Single Ticker
- **[Recommended to focus on one ticker at beginning]** "I recommended at the very beginning... focusing on only one ticker or one asset... if you are getting to know the ticker, that's great... you will understand how it reacts to news, how it reacts to fan speakers, how it reacts to the ETFs" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2831s-2879s])
- **[Add more tickers once trading becomes second nature]** "once you start, you know, like improving or getting used to be second nature, you're trading on that ticker. You can start adding more and more" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2879s-2882s])

### Journaling
- **[Journal missed trades too]** "if you miss a trade, journal it anyway and be, you know, acknowledge what happened" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2772s-2796s])
- **[Journal poor execution too]** "if you weren't good at your execution because you were using limit orders or you just felt that you wasn't paying attention to your entry trigger and you were late to the party... journal them" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2781s-2802s])

### Psychology / Stages of Trader
- **[Stage 4 = conscious competence - applying strategy deliberately with full attention]** "stage four is the conscious competence. Now you know your strategy and apply it deliberately, but it still requires full attention" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [387s-398s])
- **[Stage 5 = unconscious competence - trading becomes automatic]** "unconscious competence... trading becomes almost automatic and you react appropriately without much conscious effort" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [775s-831s])
- **[Stage 5 traders are robotic - not excited about wins, not sad about losses]** "Jack looks like robotic on his decisions because he's now unconsciously competent... he's not super excited about the good trades. He's not super sad about the lost trades because everything is inside of his system" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [783s-823s])
- **[Stage 2 traders: optimistic on losses, pessimistic on winners]** "traders on stage two, they are super optimistic on their losses and very pessimistic on their winners... on your winners, you are pessimistic, but on your losers, you are very optimistic" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [880s-929s])
- **[Stage 5 = data-driven, not emotion-driven]** "this is the stage where you become automatic on your decisions because everything is data driven, is not anymore driven by emotions" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [943s-954s])
- **[Accept uncertainty of markets]** "we need to accept the uncertainty of markets... in trading, nothing works all the time and everything works sometimes" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2260s-2277s])
- **[Don't try to outsmart the market]** "most of you think that market is something that you can decipher... something that you can outsmart... I wouldn't accept that my ideas or my thesis were wrong. And it's not about being right or wrong. It's the market uncertainty" (performance-coaching_Session_1_Stages_of_a_Successful_Trader.txt [2292s-2335s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest

## 2. One-Candle-Rule / Opening-Candle-Retest
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 3. 84% Rule / Re-entries
- **[After a loss on a ticker, avoid same ticker unless 84% rule]** "if I had a loss on a ticker, my second trade usually is not the same ticker. I try to step away of that name for the day, unless it's an 84 percent rule" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [2883s-2889s])

## 4. Order Blocks
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key Levels
- **[Overlooking higher timeframe levels causes leaving money on the table, not necessarily losses]** "I have left a lot of money on the table because I'm overlooking them... I'm having a good trade, and I'm holding runners, or I'm holding part of my position, and I don't see, or I wasn't prepared for a level to have like a huge reaction, and sometimes I say like, okay, this is not a strong level, and suddenly, boom, it reverses right there" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4659s-4691s])
- **[Higher timeframe planning is main reason losses are minimized]** "I'm planning on the higher timeframe, and I am executing on the smaller timeframe" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4795s-4800s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 7. Exits
- **[Scaling out based on emotions vs. data-driven scaling]** "the way I was scaling out before, it was based on my emotions, right? Because I wanted to have a higher win rate instead of a higher risk-reward ratio... I was scaling out my first portion of my trade when I hit the 10 or 15% of my trade" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5032s-5063s])
- **[Switch to scaling at HOD/LOD instead of small profit targets]** "I started cutting just as Jack said, high of the day or low of the day, right?" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5086s-5089s])
- **[Reduced position sizing 15% but maintained same profits through better scaling]** "I reduced my position sizing since January, like 15% and I'm still getting kind of the same profits on my trades... my risk is going smaller, but my profits are being consistent" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5103s-5125s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **[Plan on higher timeframe, execute on smaller timeframe]** "I'm planning on the higher timeframe, and I am executing on the smaller timeframe" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4795s-4800s])
- **[Preparation goes from daily to 4-hour to 1-hour chart]** "when I'm preparing my trading plan, I go from the daily to the four hour to the one hour chart, and then I'm expecting on the smaller timeframe to get on that same direction" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4703s-4716s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **[C and B- trades with poor results should be identified and reduced]** "one of my smart goals is to reduce the C and B trades with poor results... focus on identifying and minimizing trades categorized as C and B that resulted in poor results" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3608s-3671s])
- **[Track trade grade, system type, scaling, entry/exit, anxiety levels]** "I put together a spreadsheet identifying system. Either it's a break retest, open drive, or no system at all. Underline the grade, A, B, or C. How many times your net return on investment? Did you scale? Entry? Your exit? Entry, full time, anxiety levels one through five" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3626s-3656s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **[Reduced position sizing 15% but maintained same profits]** "I reduced my position sizing since January, like 15% and I'm still getting kind of the same profits on my trades" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5103s-5113s])

## ADDITIONAL METHODOLOGY RULES

### Growth vs. Fixed Mindset
- **[Growth mindset = emphasis on continuous learning, challenges as opportunities, setbacks as part of journey]** "emphasis on continuous learning... seeing challenges as growth opportunities... understanding that setbacks are part of the journey" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [352s-375s])
- **[Fixed mindset = avoidance of risk, blaming external factors, stagnation]** "on the fixed mindset, it says drawbacks, avoidance of risk, blaming external factors, stagnation. Example, I'm not good at trading. The market is against me" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1768s-1783s])
- **[Fixed mindset goals focus on money outcomes; growth mindset goals focus on process improvement]** "if you set your goal with all that background, sounds amazing... the thing is, what happens if it's a sideways market? If you never hit your outcome goal, you are failing?" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1235s-1251s])
- **[Process-oriented goals vs. outcome-oriented goals]** "why instead of having a goal of making $500 a trade, why don't we say, I will have better entries? Or, I will scale out according to my plan. Or, I'm only taking trades that make sense on the risk reward ratio" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1086s-1099s])
- **[Money goals can drive decisions that backfire]** "fixing our goals to money not only can increase our emotions, but can drive our decisions on the money. And this can backfire in both sides... it can hold us from runners, from good winners because we are afraid of losing unrealized profit" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1669s-1698s])
- **[Self-reflection: how many of your trading rules are growth vs. fixed mindset]** "how many of your rules are growth mindset and how many of your rules are fixed mindset?" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1744s-1754s])
- **[Example of fixed mindset rule: stopping after loss out of fear vs. sticking to system]** "how many of you have a rule of I'm not going to make a second trade if I lost my first one?... And then you see that your setup actually shows it continues nicely. And you miss out out of fear, out of hesitation, out of a fixed mindset" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1806s-1859s])
- **[Growth mindset alternative for red folder news: stick to system with awareness]** "why don't say, on red folder news days, I'm going to stick one hundred percent to my system and I will be very aware of my risk and reactions" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [1870s-1875s])

### Visualization / Preparation
- **[Visualize the trading process, not just the outcome]** "when you start your session, you will need to visualize your trading process, not just the outcome, not just the money, not just the numbers" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4270s-4283s])
- **[Visualize multiple scenarios: what if it goes my way, against me, takes forever]** "on your visualization process, you should have, like, okay, well, what happens if this goes my way? What happens if this goes against me? What happens if this takes forever?" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4303s-4320s])
- **[Emotional state matters - don't trade when rushed or distracted]** "if you are on a rush and you have a doctor's appointment in one hour, are you taking a trade now? Of course not" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4344s-4352s])
- **[Create your trading zone - environment matters]** "when I'm trading in my zone, I prepared my zone. I stepped into my zone and I made all of this environment to be my zone... to be prepared, to be calm and to be ready to take action" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [4528s-4542s])
- **[Daily visualization homework: visualize one part of trading process before market opens]** "before market opens, visualize how I'm waiting for my trade, how I'm managing my trade, how I'm gonna scale out on my trade, how I'm gonna define my risk before taking my trade" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5267s-5297s])

### Risk Management
- **[Cut trade and ask questions later - Tony's rule]** "cut your trade and ask questions later" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [2570s-2574s])
- **[Step away from desk after loss - 5 minute break]** "what I do is just I take a walk. I step away from my desk. I go pour some coffee, drink some water, try to detach from the trading session for at least five minutes" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [2630s-2644s])
- **[After a loss on a ticker, avoid same ticker unless 84% rule]** "if I had a loss on a ticker, my second trade usually is not the same ticker. I try to step away of that name for the day, unless it's an 84 percent rule" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [2883s-2889s])
- **[Second trade after loss is separate - not trying to recover]** "I'm not taking a second trade trying to recover the first loss. It's a completely different trade for me. It's separate" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [2863s-2869s])

### Journaling
- **[Journaling reveals patterns for improvement - scaling example]** "I switch after reviewing hundreds of trades, literally hundreds of trades. I started being confident of, instead of cutting my trade on the first 15% profit, I started cutting just as Jack said, high of the day or low of the day" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5070s-5089s])
- **[Journal data over time gives big picture perspective]** "I'm not thinking on what I need to type into my journal entry today, but I'm looking at what my data, one year of trades is gonna tell me" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [5169s-5179s])

### Psychology / Mindset
- **[Acceptance of losses and uncertainty is hardest thing in trading]** "acceptance of losses and acceptance of uncertainty... the uncertainty of market" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3183s-3201s])
- **[Do you want to be right or do you want to make money?]** "do you want to be right or do you want to make money?" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3201s-3212s])
- **[Cannot judge strategy on single trade]** "you can't judge your strategy. You can't judge your performance. You can't judge if you're doing good or wrong on a single trade" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3163s-3171s])
- **[Stick to every aspect of system, not just winners]** "when I say stick to it, it's stick to every aspect of it, not only to the winners, because everyone can hold the winner like really good... but what happens when a loser trade just kicks in?" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3238s-3262s])
- **[The fastest way to profitability is going slow]** "the fastest way to profitability, it's going slow" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3765s-3771s])
- **[Set goals with 1-3 month timeline, not 1 week]** "we can set a goal like that if we are expecting to have results in one week, right? Because maybe in one week you didn't find any of your setups... having a month or two or three months as our timeline sounds solid" (performance-coaching_Session_2_Performance_Mindset.txt [3722s-3745s])

## 5. Key levels
- **[PDH/PDL as simplest setup]** "if you want to make something easy easy easy piece that's just I mean just look for previous day high and low or pre-market high and low right but the look for anything else I mean that don't look for up any granges don't look for order blogs don't look for I mean let's focus on one set of or two set of stops" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3410s-3447s])

## 6. Time-of-day
- **[Tony's trading window based on data]** "why do I trade from night 30 to 11? Okay, well, this is because my data from my journal is saying my edge is working better is performing better" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2190s-2208s])
- **[No universal rule - data-driven]** "a fixed mindset will be just just stopping at the 11 because someone told you. But if you tell me like a net of you know what? I mean I found a lot of setups between 11 and 1 p.m. or you know power hour the last hour of the market I can find a lot of my setups. I will say go for them go for them. I mean if you already have data backing your decision go for them" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2228s-2263s])
- **[Tony/Jack limited hours due to other jobs]** "Johnny and Jack they don't trade too much... too many hours on the day that's because they have another job or another job they have their own businesses to run" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2391s-2405s])
- **[Data determines best trading time]** "my data is telling me that my best training time is this I will focus on this and I'm not gonna be actively looking for something else out of my edge" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2412s-2427s])
- **[Accelerator traders - daily rejections around noon]** "people who's in the accelerator... rejections on the daily time frame... and what time happens at and they say that it happens about noon" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2546s-2566s])

## 7. Exits
- **[Exiting early due to emotional control issues]** "the death of a musk control would be you exit early... exit late just don't fall the plan like you supposed to" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [696s-710s])
- **[Concrete example - 3x more money by following plan]** "I would have made three times as much money if I had just exited where the stop loss was supposed to where I'll notice that it and in my targets would have been where the chart said to set it" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [720s-735s])

## 8. HTF application
- **[Pre-market HTF analysis]** "trying to understand market context in higher time frames identifying levels of confluence" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1052s-1061s])

## 9. Trade selection
- **[A+ setup mentioned - position sizing]** "even if you want to use multiple different positions has this for like if it's an A plus setup or whatever, you know, that's that's great, but if it's just like this is the one I'm going big I mean that feels a little risky" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [540s-546s])
- **[Focus on one setup to master]** "focus on one thing focus on one thing master that one thing and then move on... laser focus to one setup" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3498s-3579s])
- **[Tony takes one trade a day]** "Tony says that he only takes one trade a day" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2077s-2079s])
- **[Growth mindset - take setups as they appear]** "you should be actively looking for your setups and if you'll find out that your setup is you know showing in some air some hour of the day you just you don't take it or or paper trade it" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2298s-2315s])
- **[No fixed rule on number of trades - data driven]** "if I'm taking my first trade a lot I'm not taking another trade just because that's that's a rule of me and I'm I'm always saying like why I mean just just because you don't want to take a second trade like a rule" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2264s-2283s])

## 10. Concrete numbers
- **[Personal loss figures - Oscar]** "I have lost $2,500 in year one and last year $2,800... a little over five grand on the negative" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1277s-1304s])

## 11. Discipline / Routines / Accountability
- **[Discipline definition]** "discipline means sticking to your trading plan, managing your emotions and consistently following through with your strategies" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [171s-183s])
- **[Pillars of trading discipline]** "Adherence to trading plans, emotional control and consistent on track on strategy execution" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [184s-196s])
- **[Example of non-adherence - changing plan when market opens]** "you are planning for calls... but suddenly market opens and you see that your plan is not... going up. You decide to change a trade and you say okay, well, this is not going up then it's going down... just chasing a trade out of your plan" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [397s-438s])
- **[Example - no plan for downside]** "people who only planned for the upside and they didn't have a plan for the downside but they wanted to trade and that's even if it's out of the system even if it's out of the plan" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [444s-462s])
- **[Revenge trading as worst emotion]** "revenge trading. I think that's the most important on the motion control... revenge was one of the worst emotions on my system because... anger like entitlement like you know market on me something because I've been here sitting looking at charge for so long that I should be receiving some money" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [604s-669s])
- **[Overconfidence after big win]** "you have an amazing trade... double what you use to do on your winners and then you are feeling over confident like okay... the key for the markets now... and you start doing something crazy like okay, well, let's take another trade because I'm on the straight... let's take another trade with double the size" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [761s-801s])
- **[Friday 0DTE - half size after loss]** "you are trading on a Friday zero DTE same day expiration and you just have a trade that was supposed to be green but suddenly to red... you want to take another trade... half size because I don't want to risk everything I just won" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [833s-884s])
- **[Daily routines - pre-market analysis and post-trade review]** "On the daily routines we have our pre-market analysis and also our post-trade or after trading review" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1009s-1020s])
- **[Pre-market routine - HTF context, levels, compare to Jack's prep]** "Before I watch the pre-market prep I'll kind of look at the market and evaluate what it did overnight and then I'll kind of mark out my levels for the day and then compare it to jacks pre-market prep" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1328s-1346s])
- **[Journaling immediately after trades]** "after the trading day I'll journal my trades directly after so I can like I can know the emotions I had right after the trades because you kind of forget them" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1346s-1356s])
- **[Night review and next day planning]** "at night I'll review what I did that day and I'll kind of plan for tomorrow just a little bit" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1361s-1365s])
- **[Weekend review]** "on the weekends I'll look over my trades for the week and plan for the next week pretty much" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1367s-1371s])
- **[Monthly routine - tracking metrics across categories]** "on a monthly time scale what I do is I just kind of add up all of my scores across all of the categories I'm tracking so I look at the pre-market preparation I look at my trade execution I look at my risk management and I look at my mental game" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1448s-1472s])
- **[Visualization as part of routine]** "prepare myself better emotionally for imagining visualizing what how will I react when I lose when my setup... is not and that I have to exit" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1090s-1115s])
- **[Not trading if pre-market routine not thorough]** "if I don't do it I simply don't trade that's also if I don't if I don't if I feel that I I wasn't thorough in my pre my pre market routine I she not trade" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1182s-1195s])
- **[Paper trade as alternative to forcing trades]** "I still do the urge to do paper trade instead to force myself to just paper trade" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1202s-1207s])
- **[Journaling as mind dump after trading]** "my best time for journaling is just after I closed my trip I usually take a walk... and then I go back to my desk and I journal right away why because for me it's kind of a mind dumb... once I journal I'm done with charts. I'm not thinking about taking another trade and I'm also detaching from the outcome" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [1939s-1980s])
- **[Accountability body concept]** "it's like going to the gym with a friend... if you know that he's gonna be there at six a.m. in the morning to go to the gym together... there's someone expecting you at the front door... someone making the same effort than you to improve" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2818s-2858s])
- **[Consequences board for breaking rules]** "I have at my trading rules but I also have like a reminder of the consequences of not following my rules... loss of money, regret of mistakes, waste of time, mess up stats, adding stress on pressure, disappointment" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [2694s-3181s])
- **[Punishment for breaking rules - 5km run]** "if I break my rules I take a five kilometer run besides my exercise routine" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3205s-3218s])
- **[P&L is a lagging indicator of discipline]** "the PML it's a lagging indicator... once you start being very discipline on your trading you should be focusing on different things" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3708s-3736s])
- **[Signs of discipline progress - reduced friction, enjoying process, shorter emotional recovery]** "reduce friction... you are starting to enjoy the process of trading... shorter emotional recovery times" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3752s-3975s])
- **[Discipline leads to not caring about losing trades]** "if you are very disciplined what happens if you are taking your losses on your stop loss... you don't care about the losing trade. Why? Because you are following your system because you are following your rules" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3922s-3947s])
- **[Discipline reduces over-excitement on wins]** "when you are having like a big win I mean the over excitement... starts slowing down why? Because you now have a better emotional control" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [3983s-4006s])
- **[Homework - commit to one action for discipline]** "commit to one action that will enhance your discipline... it doesn't need to be 10 different action it doesn't need to be like a huge plan just one action" (performance-coaching_Session_3_Discipline_Fundamentals.txt [4133s-4207s])

## 4. Order blocks
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key levels
- **[Key levels as basis for trade management]** "To stay on a trade, I just go off the key levels. I mean, I always go off the next key level. If it hits the key level, sometimes usually I take my gains and I stop it." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2483s-2493s])
- **[Key levels as trade plan basis - no order blocks]** "I don't do a small block orders. I don't do a small supply demands. I wait for my key levels to be mitigated or retested. That's it. And then whether it's up or down." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3714s-3727s])

## 6. Time-of-day
- **[Trading window 9:30-11:00 AM EST]** "I am a momentum trader. So I also only trade from nine thirty to eleven in the morning Eastern Standard Time." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3907s-3915s])
- **[Red folder news awareness]** "I also look at the red folder news for the day so I know which time I should be trading, which time maybe I should wait." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1501s-1506s])

## 7. Exits
- **[No fixed breakeven rule - routine to reassess invalidation level]** "I don't have a rule for moving to break even. I have a routine of updating my validation level once I start taking profits. If it makes sense, if it makes sense, because I mean, if it goes back to my entry level, but you know, the trade is still intact on my trading idea or my trading thesis. I mean, I'm not moving everything, but sometimes when it starts going my way and I started taking profit, I try to update my validation level, but it's not like a rule. It's part of my routine to reassess if I need to move it." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3389s-3438s])
- **[Moving invalidation level to change of character, not break of structure]** "In validation level, I'm just moving my validation level to a change of character. Right. I can tolerate break of structure, but I cut my trades on a change of." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2948s-2962s])
- **[Scaling - moving invalidation after taking profits]** "once I start taking profits, I started moving my validation level" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2935s-2941s])
- **[Risk-reward target at least 1:2]** "you draw the risk-reward all the way to at least one to two, right?" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2636s-2644s])

## 8. HTF application
- **[HTF chart switching during trade]** "I started going back and forth between my 50 minute chart or my one hour chart. I'm starting identifying next levels." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2990s-3005s])
