## 11. Discipline / Routines / Accountability (NEW material)
- **[Routines as backbone of discipline]** "Routines are the backbone of discipline trading. They help us manage our time, reduce decision fatigue and ensure we are consistently applying our strategies." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [825s-835s])
- **[Mechanical decision-making reduces emotions]** "the more mechanical it takes your decision taking process, the easiest and the simplest and the best for your trading" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [835s-853s])
- **[Consistency across trade lifecycle]** "you want to be disciplined enough like to not hesitate on making the same decisions every time. And this is for starting a trade to manage a trade and to end a trade. So the less room we leave for our emotions is going to be the better." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [882s-909s])
- **[Day trading is not trading every day or full day]** "remember that day trading is not trading every day and for sure is not trading the full day" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [932s-940s])
- **[Routines must fit your personality, not copy others]** "the discipline and the routines they need to fit your goals, not the standard goals, not the Twitter influencer trading influencer goals" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1190s-1206s])
- **[Jack and Tony have different routines despite similar strategy]** "Jack and Tony, they are super different. Even when they have like a similar style or they are trading the same strategy. They both are super different. So, yeah, that's something Luke has a point. You need to find your own routine." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1292s-1311s])
- **[Pre-market routine - previous night preparation]** "I start the night before. I just mark the previous day high, low." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1459s-1465s])
- **[Pre-market routine - morning levels and news]** "Then in the morning, I take my dogs to the park. I come back. Then I watch what happened during the pre-market, like what movement the market did. And I start marking my levels. My intraday levels. While I'm having breakfast, I'm also looking at the news like Bloomberg and MarketWatch to see if there's anything relevant." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1470s-1495s])
- **[Pre-market routine - red folder news]** "I also look at the red folder news for the day so I know which time I should be trading, which time maybe I should wait." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1501s-1506s])
- **[Pre-market routine - prayer and stretching]** "I pray before I trade. So I do a little prayer and I stretch." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1513s-1517s])
- **[Pre-market routine - reading rules and consequences board]** "part of my pre-market routine, it's reading them both. I have like an everyday reminder, not only about my rules, but about the consequences of breaking my rules." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1841s-1863s])
- **[Pre-market routine - visualization exercise]** "Part of my pre-market routine, I do this exercise that we actually shared a few lessons ago about visualizing what happens if I go to this scenario. What happens if the market takes this path? What happens if news drops right at this time? What happens if the economic event creates a trend?" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1882s-1908s])
- **[Pre-market routine - big why reminder]** "I also have here as a part of my pre-market routine, my big why. It's a reminder of why I am doing this, why I am having this challenge with me, right? Why I'm pushing harder every day, why I'm not stopping to run, right?" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [1996s-2020s])
- **[Pre-market routine - tech checkup]** "something on my pre-market prep is I always reboot my... I close my Weeble, and I open it again while I'm in the pre-market prep with Jack. So I have enough time. If it needs an update or something is not working or whatever, I can prepare my laptop or my iPad or whatever just to make sure that I'm not going to be in the middle of an upgrade or update when the market opens." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [6250s-6278s])
- **[Pre-market routine - internet speed test]** "I use this Speedtest app. So every day in the morning, I'm just taking a look if my Wi-Fi is working properly. So yes, a tech checkup every time in the morning before going to the market." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [6290s-6307s])
- **[Two chart layouts - fishing and sniper]** "I have my fishing screen and my sniper screen. So I kind of prepare my charts in the morning, like having my different views or my different layouts so I'm ready for the journey, right? Like I have my fishing layout for choosing whatever I want to trade. And once I am in a trade, I switch to the sniper chart and, you know, it's a different layout." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2191s-2217s])
- **[Fishing chart - 8 tickers on 1-minute]** "It's eight different charts. Everyone is on the same time frame, usually the one minute time frame. I have QQQ, Spy, Tesla, Nvidia, Apple, AMD, Amazon and one other. That will be Google, Microsoft, Meta, ABTO." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3469s-3497s])
- **[Sniper chart - 3 timeframes]** "the second chart, it's once I'm on a trade, I use the what I call the sniper chart. And it's a split in three and it has the one minute, the five minute and a long one with the 15 minute or one hour chart." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3497s-3510s])
- **[In-trade routine - journal before entry]** "For managing a trade, well, for starters, when I always journal every time I'm going to place a trade, I journal what ticker I'm going to play. For example, Apple, I journal it. I write it down first, and then I look at the ticker, and then I look at the strike price, obviously. And then before I get in, I already know what I'm going to get in and where I'm going to get out if it doesn't go the way that I want it to go." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2359s-2381s])
- **[In-trade routine - timer for emotional/technical check]** "I do have this timer. Once I enter a trade, I do have a timer. The name of the timer, what it says is, is this still a valid trade? And I set it to five minutes. Every five minutes, it starts ringing on my screen. Is this still a valid trade? And this timer helps me with two things, right? First, to actually re-evaluate the technicals of my trade every five minutes... And also, it's an emotions check, right? Like, OK, well, how do I feel? I am anxious. I am fearful. I am being greedy." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2700s-2816s])
- **[Timer progression as emotions improve]** "when I started doing this, it was every three minutes. So I've been pushing this timer a bit because I'm more in control of my emotions. I'm not reacting to my emotions now. So now that I control a little better my emotions, the technicals of my trades are something easier to evaluate. So I push my timer to every five minutes." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2827s-2857s])
- **[In-trade routine - keep drawing on chart]** "another thing that I like to do while I am in a trade, it's keep drawing on my chart. So I am trying to identify patterns. I am trying to identify if I can move my invalidation level." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2896s-2907s])
- **[In-trade routine - emotion notes on chart]** "While I am on the trade, sometimes I take notes of how I am feeling on my chart. I mean, I use my trading view chart. So I just text there like, oh, well, I'm feeling very anxious here because it's not breaking or I'm feeling really here because it dropped hard, but it bounces. So I'm also adding my emotions to my chart so I can remember later when I felt those emotions and what were there." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3206s-3241s])
- **[In-trade routine - orders ready to react]** "I have my order ready to sell. And sometimes I mean, I use market order. So if the trade is not going my way, it's like I like to have my orders ready to react." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3143s-3155s])
- **[Post-trade routine - step away before journaling]** "I try to one side, I'm done with my trading. I step away from my desk before journaling. So usually what I do is I go for some coffee at the kitchen. And most of the days I just go walk my dog and I usually it takes like forty five to sixty minutes. Then I go back to my desk and start journaling. And one of the reasons I step away of my trading desk is because when I'm journaling, I don't want to be like this emotional and intense on the journaling of my trade. So I want I take this time, you know, to cool down my emotions, to cool down my thoughts." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [3940s-4001s])
- **[Post-trade routine - journal fresh but not emotional]** "I want to have a fresh memory about what I was doing for my trade and also for my emotions, but not that emotional like when you just close your trade." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4031s-4043s])
- **[Post-trade routine - mind dump for closure]** "when I'm journaling, I do this like a mind dump. Right. It's like I take everything from my trading journey of today and I dump it on my journal... Once I journal, it's over for me. And the good thing about this is, I mean, if I took a win, it's not like I'm overexcited the whole day... But on the opposite, right, if I took a loss this day, it's not like I'm super sad the whole day... One of the main reasons I mind dump my journal is to get away of emotions and also to give a closure to my trading journey." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4058s-4159s])
- **[Post-trade routine - gratitude practice]** "after my trading journey, I like to give thanks to my God about the opportunities that I receive, not only as my day trading job, but also all of the learning. And usually I say thanks for the community that I have because I found not only great peers and members, but also friends here." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4333s-4365s])
- **[Weekly routine - scorecard/grading system]** "I keep a pretty detailed scorecard throughout the week. You know, it's sort of a daily report card where I grade myself according to different categories... And what I try to do is, you know, basically compare the previous week to this week to the current week." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4488s-4506s])
- **[Weekly routine - Sunday planning based on previous week]** "a weekly plan is something that I make on every Sunday, you know, and it's based on what I did well or what I didn't do so well in the previous week." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4513s-4523s])
- **[Weekly routine - review rules followed/broken]** "I'll first review my rules that I set for the, well, not my rules, my things I kind of needed to work on from the past week. And I'll review if I followed those in all my trades. So as I'm reviewing my trades I took that week in my journal, I'll see which rules I followed and which ones I broke for that week. And then I'll set a new list of rules for the next week." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4820s-4841s])
- **[Weekly routine - educational video time]** "usually on Saturdays, Saturday nights, usually I'll watch videos and I'll do my weekly outlook on Sunday, most of the stuff on Sunday, but Saturday I like to watch a lot of videos." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [4881s-4890s])
- **[Habit stacking concept]** "the concept of stacking habits... if you want to create or to stick to a new habit in a ready built routine, is that you start doing things together... creating stacking habits on a single routine is the easiest way to build more habits." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5089s-5219s])
- **[Example of habit stacking - weekly recap + journal + chart refinement]** "I'm attending the weekly recap, but also I'm creating my weekly journal entry and also I'm refining my charts on the higher timeframes. So instead of having three different times for these three different habits, I just squeeze them on the same routine." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5271s-5298s])
- **[Steps to implement winning routines - define clear objectives]** "you need to have clear objectives for your routine. Is it to be more prepared? Is it to be more aware of emotions? Is it to be more sharp on your decision-taking process?... So you need to have clear objectives on your routines so they become valuable for you. Because if it's just a repetitive task, but with no value, it's going to be super hard to stick to it." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5477s-5546s])
- **[Steps to implement winning routines - allocate specific time]** "You need to allocate specific time for each routine element. You cannot do pre-market prep once the market is already open. You cannot journal your past trade while you are on another trade. You cannot refine your charts if the market hasn't closed, right?" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5549s-5577s])
- **[Steps to implement winning routines - use consistent tools]** "try to just be consistent, right? I mean, you don't want to write it down on a text and the next day you actually write it down in a notebook, but the next day you use something electronic. So try to be consistent with your tools." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5660s-5674s])
- **[Steps to implement winning routines - review and adapt]** "regularly assess if your routine is working... if you are doing something that is not adding value to your day, adding value to your journey, is it worth it to keep it just because someone else is doing it?" (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5686s-5710s])
- **[Goal of routines is consistency, not perfection]** "the goal is not perfection, but consistency. That's the goal for our routines. It's not about having perfect steps or accomplish everything every time, but to be consistent with our routines." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [5758s-5775s])
- **[Not trading when sick]** "for me, a rule or part of my discipline is that if I'm not feeling like completely well, I don't trade. I don't trade at all. I mean, I go see charts and everything, but I don't even open my broker because I won't be focused. I won't be like taking the best decisions. My reasoning will be slow. I will be lacking on decisions." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [733s-751s])
- **[Paper trading after max loss to satisfy urge]** "I would reach my max loss for the day and I want to keep trading. And I'm like, but I realized that I want to keep trading because I wanted to keep like trying, not so much like a little bit was revenge, but some of it was just I wanted to be in the market. So I switched now to paper trading. So like today, you know, I took a loss and I'm like, I want to keep trading. I know I got to follow my plan. All right, close my broker, open paper trading." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [2097s-2124s])
- **[Physiological preparation - restroom before market]** "Part of my pre-market prep is actually going to the restroom. I mean, I prepare. I don't want to be while I'm on a trade, right?... I even have my time for going to the restroom. So usually, like, 99% of the trading days, it's like I'm done with that." (performance-coaching_Session_4_Creating_Winning_Routines_for_Trading_Success.txt [6137s-6206s])

- **[Need system before sizing up]** "Do you need to be able to have a system in place? Because if you don't, I mean, there's no reason for you to exercise again. Why? - **[Different tickers have different edges]** "I have an edge with AMD. I have a different edge with Amazon. I have a different edge with Nvidia. I have a different edge for for QQQ." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1441s-1449s])

## ADDITIONAL METHODOLOGY RULES

### Sizing decisions
- **[Sizing based on system consistency, not win rate]** "this sizing for your trade friends should not be related to your winning rate. But it should be related to your triggers." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1032s-1041s])
- **[Downsize until you find your edge]** "if you don't [have a system], I mean, you should be downsizing to the less, like, let's say three contracts or whatever you want to take, until you find your edge, until you find that you are consistent with your entries." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1085s-1098s])
- **[When to size up - comfortable with consistency]** "when is the best time to increase your size again... when you feel comfortable with your consistency? And when you actually are consistent with a single thing, right? I mean, when you found your edge." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1268s-1285s])
- **[Don't double size based on feeling good]** "just don't do the mistake of, okay, well, I'm, I'm, I'm training five contracts and because I'm feeling good, I'm training 10 contracts. Okay, I double the slice can be dangerous." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1711s-1725s])
- **[Larger size = faster emotional reaction to losses]** "if you were training with one thousand dollars and now when you are training with two thousand dollars, it's going to be faster, to worry... if the price goes up in June, it's going to be quicker to reach that one hundred dollars of risk." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1742s-1770s])

### Trading psychology / body awareness
- **[Different body reactions for system trades vs mistakes]** "When I'm taking a trading side of my system, I'm super calm... But if I make a mistake, it takes a lot for me to recover... even if I win something... I feel, I feel there. I don't feel like I earn it." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [2743s-2807s])
- **[Mistake punishment pattern]** "when they make a mistake, they kind of on their subconscious, they punish themselves. Like, okay, so this should be my exit on a regular trip. But because I made a mistake, let me see how long I can fall in." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [2833s-2850s])
- **[Plan for mistake recovery - suffocate fire early]** "I have developed a plan for me to stop to, I mean, to stop me to make a mistake. Just because I'm a mistake at the very beginning. So I don't sabotage me, I don't punish me. Okay, well, let's try this on some business. I made a mistake and let me try to suffocate the fire before it consumes the whole house." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [2896s-2918s])

### Trade management tools
- **[Separate charting from execution]** "I used to use soft pieces of software to execute my trades where I placed the trade on the chart. And so that I think was causing me to make some emotional decisions. And so I kind of rearranged myself so that I'm now separate. I've just used creating the view from my charting and I'm using IBKR for my execution." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3161s-3181s])
- **[Drawing stop level + profit targets on chart]** "drawing the stop level on the underlying and trading view... Drawing the the profit targets oftentimes I have them but then once I enter like draw it again, right, it gives me sure that I know that that's the levels that I'm aiming for." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3215s-3233s])
- **[3-minute timer for trade check]** "I'm going to start with a with a three minute timer, you know, just ripping off your idea." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3233s-3239s])

### Pre-market planning
- **[Draw scenarios on chart before market open]** "I capture like the scenarios on the 15 minute chart before the market opens. And then at the end of the day, I look back and I see how many of these did I get and then how many presented a trade able opportunity." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3464s-3476s])
- **[Visual reference of plan - draw long/short positions]** "I actually draw the call just like, just like, that doesn't on his chart and on the library, I just draw my long or short position. And now I have a visual reference of what I was expecting." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3389s-3406s])

### Journaling / review
- **[AI analysis of journal comments]** "I took all my comments from Pacella and just paced them into a text file and then I uploaded them into Grock and had them do an AI analysis for me... it was very insightful like areas that I can specifically focus on based on the words that I used." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3753s-3789s])
- **[Monthly reports focusing on emotions and mistakes]** "I run with monthly reports and I focus on the emotions and mistakes on my trading and I run it on Grock." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [3798s-3814s])

### Trading to recover mindset
- **[Trading to recover = already lost]** "if you are trading to recover, you already lost. You are having fake mindset, right? Because you will be focusing on the wrong things. And that's going to start making you take the wrong decision." (performance-coaching_Session_5_Insight_Ignition_Q_A.txt [1001s-1019s])

## ADDITIONAL METHODOLOGY EXTRACTED

### Journaling / KPI Tracking
- **Why KPIs are non-negotiable** "the key performance indicators gives you clarity on your performance... it helps to have a goal setting precision... it gives us an early warning system for a trading flow" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [408s-495s])
- **KPI objectivity requirement** "KPI needs to be very objective and they need to be like kind of square right" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [1119s-1128s])
- **Areas to track** "General statistics set ups entries position sizing trade management exit mistakes emotions and traders" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [1188s-1210s])
- **Long vs short tracking importance** "when you are trying to figure out your style your edge is super important to know what are you better act right... if my data says that I'm actually better at boots I mean don't be afraid to this if my data in such a way better or I have better I to catch long setups. I mean come on just follow the data right" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [1476s-1522s])
- **Dollar amount vs contract count** "How many contracts we are taking it's kind of not relevant however the dollar amount is and why it's important because we can keep or risk and we can train our ice and breaking for the ups and downs on a similar amount every time" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [1570s-1594s])
- **Entry time tracking for strategy refinement** "I was looking at my stats certainly I realize but with my journal data that my best time to trade it's between nine 45 and 10 15... What I did was they've got to dive into my journal data and just reshave my strategy I refine my strategy" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [1927s-2016s])
- **Trade management tracking** "Did I have a correct treatment management, or did I have a full-treat management?" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [2822s-2828s])
- **Mistakes to track** "I took a trade because I was bored. I was chasing I was chasing the price. I was chasing my stop loss... revenge trading if you didn't cut the losses quickly if you traded with formal if you're invalidation level was not respected. If you were selling quick if you took an invalid trade or no setup and you just made a mistake on this. If your trade was not in your plan if you were over trading if you were if you panic panic selling if you solved by mistake" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [3385s-3489s])
- **Emotions tracking** "what are the emotions you are having during your trade what are the emotions you are having why do you are actually taking a set up what are the emotions you are having while writing while writing your trader" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [3943s-3960s])
- **Emotion intensity scale** "I have three different scales low medium and high and once you identify how intensity how intense was your emotion you can actually start like identifying your triggers" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [5217s-5226s])
- **Learning takeaway section** "what work today... how satisfied I'm with my performance... what was your biggest challenge... how did I handle this challenge... intensity of your emotions... one focus for tomorrow... how will I materialize it" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [5004s-5272s])
- **Journaling with intention** "journal with intention journal as you are not trading but gathering pieces of data so you can trade better like so every time you take a trade think that you are actually collecting data for improving and this is going to change the way to journal this is going to change the way you prefer" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [6376s-6406s])
- **Different journaling at different skill stages** "Tony he's on the on the unconscious competence because everything he does now it's kind of a memory Moscow like like very intuitive right like he's not thinking too much anymore because everything is super automatic... he's journaling it's a bit different than we are doing now that we are let's say conscious competence" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [6189s-6266s])
- **Why mentors journal differently** "he's not anymore I don't know like journaling his trade management because he's not making mistakes anymore and he's a way beyond that or he's not feeling the same emotions than us because why because he's not getting greedy like us or he's not having fear on his trades like like we do because we have not the same confidence" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [6277s-6310s])

### Trade Review Judgment
- **Emotion tracking led to fixing early exits** "I started looking at my journal to what my data was telling me about my early my early selves and suddenly I identified that I was living a lot of money on the table because instead of falling to high today or longer today I was cutting my positions when I hit a certain percentage... completely out of grip more than out of fear" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [4246s-4295s])
- **Data-driven behavior change** "I fix my first my first partial my first taking profit level so now I'm waiting for high the day or a lot of the day" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [4403s-4410s])

### Turbo Trader Avoidance
- **Personal rule against Turbo Trader** "I don't use the Turbo Trader. And the reason it's because when I was gathering data from my journal, I realized that I was having a lot of fall to trade management on those trade" (performance-coaching_Session_6_Key_Performance_Indicators_KPIs_transcript.txt [3057s-3072s])

- **Second target at 1:2 or 1:2.5** "Then I take 50 or 60% on my first on my first target that is usually the one to two or the one to 2.5 risk rewards" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3960s-3975s])
- **Runners to next levels** "And I let the other runners to my next levels or to my validation level" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3975s-3975s])
- **HOD/LOD vs risk-reward** "sometimes high of the day or low of the day is not my one to two risk reward level. So I take some realized profits there" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3976s-3992s])

## Trade Selection
- **Max 2 trades/day** "I tried to not take more than two trades on a day" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1458s-1468s])
- **Index trend confirmation** "I started waiting 10 and now 15 minutes to take my first trade to try to have a clear vision of what the trend is with the indices" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1063s-1079s])
- **Following Jack's trades for learning** "at the very beginning following jack trades it's a nice thing to do because I mean you will be understanding what he's looking for what is the reasoning behind his trades" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [4111s-4128s])
- **Don't blindly follow** "just don't blind following him. I mean if you don't understand what he's doing don't take the trade" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [4152s-4177s])

## Concrete Numbers
- **Win rate after waiting** "now this past month I have 80 80 80 something percent on my win rate" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1079s-1096s])
- **Risk-reward target** "we are aiming to the one to two Richard wire greater" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3498s-3512s])
- **Win rate needed with 1:2 R:R** "we only need like 40% of a Wimpray with a strong one to two Richard wire rating" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3529s-3540s])
- **Consistency before scaling** "I started the mastermind for on May and around October. I decided to scale up after five months in a row being consistent with my performance and having profitable months" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3758s-3769s])

## ADDITIONAL METHODOLOGY EXTRACTED

### Risk Management / Position Sizing
- **Same dollar amount per trade** "I tried to take the same... dollar amount trades on my everything right so it's not like okay well I I love my first trade then the second one is going to be bigger or it's going to be smaller because I don't want to raise... I try I try to be on on the same dollar amount" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1583s-1607s])
- **Avoid doubling down** "there was a time where I say like oh this is so clear that I'm actually double down on this trade and I'm not going to lie most of the times it was that clear that it never worked" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1627s-1643s])
- **Find your comfortable dollar amount** "if you start looking at your data and you start identifying what is the dollar amount that you are comfortable with... then you can actually go to that size and have a state or emotion right" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1670s-1685s])
- **1% rule not universal** "I never been risking one percent of my account. I'm always risking more... because that's my personal situation. Because I have other incomes because I have other trading accounts because I'm very confident with my system" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1966s-1991s])
- **Risk based on what doesn't trigger emotions** "what is the dollar amount that doesn't make you take emotional driven decisions? I think that's one of the most important things" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [2034s-2043s])
- **Drawdown reduces risk** "if you are in a drawdown on your account value, you will be guided to risk less... I started with $10,000, but now I don't know, like I'm not $8,000 because I already lost $2,000. So I'm not risking the same amount at the very beginning" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [2125s-2146s])

### Stop Loss Discipline
- **Stop loss caps losses** "stop loss is simply, why it is important because it caps losses... They protect your capital" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [2310s-2315s])
- **Personal failure with stop loss** "if I will cut my, all of my losses in the point that I should cut them, now I will be millionaire. The thing is that my first year or my first 18 months, I wasn't too disciplined with my stop losses" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [2353s-2360s])
- **Consequence of no stop loss** "if my standard risk was... risking $500, sometimes I got losses of $2,000... because I wasn't respecting my stop loss" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [2371s-2382s])
- **Wait for candle close beyond stop** "I wait for the candle to close beyond my stop loss level because most of the times when a candle touches my stop loss, it's only a week. So on the long run this system of waiting for a candle close beyond my stop loss level has been working great for me" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3846s-3867s])
- **Stop placement relative to retest candle** "I usually have my stop a little bit beyond both or under the candle that actually read test my level or broke my level" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3811s-3831s])

### Psychology / Emotional Control
- **Optimistic on losses, pessimistic on wins** "on our losses, we are very optimistic waiting for the price to re wear... but on our winners, we are very pessimistic and we are looking at green and say, okay, well, let me take profit here" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [2772s-2785s])
- **Emotions cannot be avoided** "trading should be emotional less... humans are made of emotions. Humans cannot avoid emotions. You will always have your emotions" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3662s-3684s])
- **Identify triggers** "what we can do is to identify these triggers is to identify how we react to these emotions and take decisions based on data and not on their emotions" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3684s-3691s])
- **Early exit due to lack of conviction** "when you are not confident about your system... you are not having your stop loss work... you are not letting your trade go to the taking profit levels" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1761s-1786s])
- **Fix through backtesting** "the more you test your system and your plan, and you see how it works, the more convinced you will be to take the trades" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1824s-1832s])

### Journaling / KPI Tracking
- **Monthly performance review** "I usually have a monthly performance review. What I do is I start looking at my journal and I tried to identify how this is I was like... how many of my trades were an actual break and read this how many of my trades were actually waiting these 10 15 minutes after the market open... how many of my trades are actually following the trend of the index" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1386s-1420s])
- **Track entry timing relative to market open** "I started looking up my data and looking at the charts and I started identifying that the kind of trees that I take where usually better when the trend from the in this is was clear" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1035s-1053s])
- **Track price action type of entry** "what if you start journaling your trades and you say okay I enter my trade on the read test or I enter my trade on the confirmation candle or I enter my trade once price I don't like when beyond the top or the bottom of the of the candle that I actually that actually read that" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1342s-1355s])
- **Use numbers not words** "try to do your know with numbers instead of words" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1528s-1530s])
- **Track MFE (Maximum Favorable Excursion)** "what I do is capture the mic cell spreadsheet and I just see what point did I enter the contracts... what was the highest point that went to... my target is to try to get at least 50% of the move" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3166s-3208s])
- **Track plan vs actual risk** "I just added the plan versus actual risk to more journal about 20 minutes ago" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [3923s-3931s])

### Trade Review Judgment
- **Data-driven strategy adjustment** "I started looking up my of my data. I realized that 90% of the times when I was taking profit when I hit the some percentage on on on my P and L it actually went all the way to the high of the day or to the low of the day and that was actually my first taking profit" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [930s-946s])
- **Index trend addition improved win rate** "I started waiting 10 and now 15 minutes to take my first trade to try to have a clear vision of what the trend is with the indices and now I haven't increased my win rate like... now this past month I have 80 80 80 something percent" (performance-coaching_Session_7_KPI_Extension_Risk_Management_for_Performance_transcript.txt [1063s-1096s])

## NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE
This transcript is primarily a coaching session on KPI tracking, journaling, and pattern identification. It does NOT contain substantive trading methodology rules for the following topics:

1. **Break-and-retest** - NOT COVERED (only mentioned as a strategy being learned, no rules)
2. **One-candle-rule / opening-candle-retest** - NOT COVERED
3. **84% rule / re-entries** - NOT COVERED
4. **Order blocks** - NOT COVERED
5. **Key levels** - NOT COVERED (only mentioned in passing as part of student's plan)
6. **Time-of-day** - PARTIALLY COVERED (see below - student observations, not taught rules)
7. **Exits** - NOT COVERED
8. **HTF application** - NOT COVERED
9. **Trade selection** - NOT COVERED (no grading criteria taught)
10. **Concrete numbers** - NOT COVERED

---

### Time-of-Day (Student Observations, Not Taught Rules)

- **Student pattern: trading before 9:45 AM leads to losses** "If I trade before 945, I tend to lose... I get excited and I enter too early. I don't wait for confirmation. So, when I wait for my best trades, 10th come after 950 AM." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [491s-510s])
- **Student pattern: best days are one-trade days** "And my best days are by far when I wait for my entry. And I take one trade." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [516s-520s])
- **Student pattern: revenge trading triggered by missing wins, not losses** "I get really angry and I start revenge trading when I miss a win... when a setup goes by without me, when my trading friends get wins... not getting the win sets me off on a path and I start revenge trading" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [618s-637s])
- **Student pattern: ES preferred over NQ for stability** "I tend to do a little bit better on ES, because it's not quite as volatile... I have gotten whipped out of too many trays on NQ, whereas that doesn't happen on ES quite as much. It's a bit more stable." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [685s-746s])
- **Student pattern: flexibility across timeframes works better than forcing one** "If I allow myself from some flexibility on either the pre-market or five minute high-end low-braken retest, as opposed to getting stuck on only one level... If I limit myself to, it's got to be the one minute or it's got to be the five minute or it's got to be the pre-market. That's when I tend to mess up" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [695s-724s])
- **Student pattern: fewer screens = better performance** "When I keep one screen, if I'm trading futures, if I keep one screen, and honestly, if I turn off the sound on the live session, I'd do better." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [799s-804s])
- **Student observation: Fridays are not great trading days** "Fridays are usually not a really great day to trade." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1042s-1047s])

---

### Trade Review / KPI Tracking (Methodology)

- **Focus on what works, not just what's wrong** "there's people that they focus a lot on what they are doing wrong... But then sometimes they forget to also focus on what actually works" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [172s-201s])
- **Identify patterns in best days AND worst days** "we will try to identify the patterns on your best days, but also in your worst days, and once we identify those patterns, then we can start fixing them" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [321s-330s])
- **Shift mindset: you're generating data for your system, not just trading** "The moment you realize that you are not actually trading the trading front of you, but you are actually generating data for your system, everything changes... your commitment to create the best quality piece of data for your system" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1148s-1175s])
- **Execute for your system, not to make money** "You start executing or performing for your system instead for trying to win to make money on the trading front of you. You start just now trading a system like a machine... he knows exactly how his system behaves. He knows exactly what happens if he breaks the rules" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1182s-1226s])
- **Without following rules, data is unreliable** "if you don't follow your playbook, the data, it's not going to be reliable... you will never be able to say, okay, well, this strategy can hold a 15% drawdown. Why? Because you always are moving your stop loss, or because you are not respecting your stop loss." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1268s-1296s])
- **Your work is to create data, not make money on the next trade** "Your work now won't be, make money on the next trade. It's going to be create enough data for your system. So in the time, it will give you what you are looking, but the one requisite that is going to take from you to actually make money is that you actually follow the system" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1322s-1348s])
- **Measure trade quality on execution, not outcome** "we don't measure the quality of our trades on the outcome... we trade the quality of our trades on your performance on your execution... There could be a super nice trade that ended red why because you closed it right in your stop loss... that's a win in my books because you did everything right." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [3112s-3167s])
- **Focus on performance, P&L follows** "if you start focusing on your performance and your execution, the outcome will follow... the moment you decide to be consistent with your execution... you will see the outcome will follow you" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [3183s-3207s])

---

### KPI Tracking Categories (Framework)

- **Track technicals: setup type, market conditions** "the set up or the technical focuses on the execution of your strategy... what kind of setup you were trying to find... the market environment during my trading session. It was trending, it was range bound, it had volatility, it had the reaction on some levels. Did we have news? Did we have events?" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2148s-2203s])
- **Track mental/emotional state** "this is about your mindset and your emotional state including your focus, confidence, stress and emotional management" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2217s-2231s])
- **Track environment: physical and digital** "environment wise this is the impact of your physical and digital environment such as noise levels, workspace, but then shelter distractions" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2384s-2394s])
- **Track physical factors: sleep, nutrition, hydration, exercise** "physical well being such as if you sleep good or if you sleep enough hours, if you about your nutrition, about your hydration, if you are exercising, something affecting your energy" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2471s-2502s])
- **Track on-chart activities: what you do on your charts** "On my chart for trading I am drawing my levels, I am drawing my daily key levels, I am drawing my intraday levels, I am drawing my pre-market level" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2647s-2667s])
- **Track off-chart activities: preparation, journaling, review** "off charts activities... includes everything that you prepare and your reflected task like the pre-market analysis, journaling, review your trades, planning but also any other routine task that help your well-being" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2685s-2705s])
- **Track time management: your trading window** "what's the most efficient time management considering your schedule... you need to be clear with your trading window" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [2734s-2965s])
- **Track recovery: how you recover energy** "how do you recover, what are the activities to help you recover energy? Because trading is super exhausting" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [3001s-3038s])

---

### Trade Review Judgment (Specific)

- **Student: best days = one trade, worst days = revenge trading after missing a win** "On my worst days, I usually took two losses and the second trade is a revenge trading wanting to make back the last money... On my best days, I realized it usually one trade" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1539s-1548s])
- **Student: preparation routine improves performance** "I find that I do better when I wake up early, do meditation, pray, yoga, slow down the mind. Do the pre-market with yoga after I have done my own charting." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [1588s-1619s])
- **Student: sticking to plan + pre-market verification = success** "pattern that I've recognized that has been giving me a fair amount of success lately is really just sticking to a plan. Doing my own pre-market and trying to find the lines and levels in the direction that I will be... and then when the market verifies what I see and gives me the break and retest off one of the levels that I think are going to be key" (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [3494s-3526s])
- **Student: watching fewer tickers improves focus** "I'm also watching less tickers, I'm focusing just on my three main tickers: Tesla and Nvidia and Amazon." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [3551s-3558s])
- **Student: most positions within first half hour** "Most of my positions are within the first half an hour pretty much for sure... anything after that and I feel like there's no momentum in the market and I just want to get out, just stressful." (performance-coaching_Session_8_KPI_Playbook_transcript.txt [3615s-3642s])

## 1. Break-and-Retest
- **A+ setup criteria (5-minute opening range)** "the five minute opening range was more probable so I kind of just focused on that over the last month... - **Student confirms: specific entry criteria = good displacement, retest, hammer candle** "I'm doing the five minute range and it has to be a good displacement come back to see a hammer candle and it's very specific one" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [567s-579s])

## 5. Key Levels
- **Student: watching for reaction at key levels** "waiting for reactions of your key levels hammer or inverted hammer candle on key level" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [760s-771s])
- **Student: liquidity sweep at previous day high** "it went to the previous day you know high okay and then I didn't enter the tent because there is a liquidity sweep it went to you know a very little beyond the you know previous day high I see a rejection with the long wicks and the real general resistance" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [2684s-2702s])

## 6. Time-of-Day
- **Student: trades first two hours** "for us in our group we trade the first two hours" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [619s-625s])
- **Student: first two hours had no good setups today** "the first two hours of the day there were really no good setups" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [593s-606s])
- **Student: KRE/Q after lunch (12-12:30)** "I like Kiki-Q after lunch time with like 12 after 12, around 12 to 30 if they're any setup" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [4177s-4184s])

## 7. Exits
- **Hide P&L to focus on process, not outcome** "one thing that I started doing when I was super worried and attached to the outcome of every trade was actually hiding my P&L... I wasn't looking at how much I made or lost on the day I was focusing only on my chart" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3440s-3465s])
- **Hiding P&L makes decisions easier** "if you start hiding your P&L you will notice that your decisions will be easier because they are going to be based entirely on your chart" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3491s-3499s])
- **Personal example: downsized position, held longer, made more money** "I used to be trading like 10,000 dollar trades... making 1500 or 2000 dollars per trade... now with this kind of improvements I actually downsize and I'm trading with seven or eight thousand dollars positions and I'm making more money out of them" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [1351s-1388s])
- **Data showed 90%+ of times scaling out early, trade reached HOD/LOD** "90 something percent of the times that I was cutting my trade or scaling out on percentage happen to actually reach the high of the day or the low of the day so I was leaving a lot of money on the table" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [1255s-1272s])
- **Downsizing reduces emotional attachment** "I reduced my positions so the emotions about making money or the risking money are less I can manage my position I can be more patient because the numbers when I have a drawdown the numbers are smaller" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [1426s-1451s])
- **Student: cutting at breakeven when trade reverses but doesn't hit stop** "once I hit my T.P. once it hits break even I end up cutting my trade but then it reverses back and runs more... it hits kind of break even or slightly in the red but it doesn't cross the stop limit and then the trade works out" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3376s-3404s])
- **Coach response: that's fear-based decision making** "it's decisions based on fear and fear to keep whatever you already made on the day and fear to lose it" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3419s-3430s])

## 8. HTF Application
- **A+ setup requires HTF thesis** "the only thing that I have to your A plus setup criteria will be if there's any thesis or idea on the higher time frame" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [740s-751s])
- **Student confirms: uses HTF for planning** "I'm using the 15 minute chart to plan my trades and I'm using the one minute chart for my entries and exit" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [4832s-4840s])

## 9. Trade Selection
- **A+ setup = 5-min opening range + good displacement + hammer candle + HTF thesis** "A plus five minute range with a good displacement come back give me a good signal with a hammer candle... the only thing that I have to your A plus setup criteria will be if there's any thesis or idea on the higher time frame" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [726s-751s])
- **Student: discipline to trade only A+ not B+** "do we have the discipline to just trade that perfect A plus setup when it shows itself and not all the ones that are just B plus and all of the B plus where I can't break any of them or losing money" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [707s-718s])
- **Student: 9 trades, 6 winners, 3 losers in 2 weeks after getting specific** "I've had nine trades six winners three losers just in the last two weeks" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [579s-585s])
- **Student: learned not to trade on choppy days** "I've also had to learn not to trade and today was a day not to trade" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [586s-592s])
- **Staying cash is a position** "staying cash is is is a position right" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [807s-815s])
- **Better to be patient than brave** "better to be patient than brave right" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3181s-3187s])
- **Ranging market = work on risk management, not force trades** "this is the best time to work on your risk management right so you should be defining your max losses how much of a drawdown you are willing to take" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3223s-3240s])
- **Trading is not trading every day** "trading is not trading everyday so you're doing right" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [3277s-3287s])

## 10. Concrete Numbers
- **Student: 9 trades, 6 winners, 3 losers in 2 weeks** "I've had nine trades six winners three losers just in the last two weeks" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [579s-585s])
- **Student: 90%+ of times scaling out early, trade reached HOD/LOD** "90 something percent of the times that I was cutting my trade or scaling out on percentage happen to actually reach the high of the day or the low of the day" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [1255s-1272s])
- **Personal example: downsized from $10k to $7-8k positions, made more money** "I used to be trading like 10,000 dollar trades... making 1500 or 2000 dollars per trade... now I actually downsize and I'm trading with seven or eight thousand dollars positions and I'm making more money out of them" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [1351s-1388s])

## SPECIAL AUDIT QUESTIONS

### A/A+ Grading
- **A+ = 5-min opening range + good displacement + retest + hammer candle + HTF thesis** "A plus five minute range with a good displacement come back give me a good signal with a hammer candle... the only thing that I have to your A plus setup criteria will be if there's any thesis or idea on the higher time frame" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [726s-751s])
- **B+ = setups that are not A+ and lose money** "all the ones that are just B plus and all of the B plus where I can't break any of them or losing money" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [713s-718s])

### QQQ Alignment
- **Student: trades with QQQ and SPY direction** "part of my rules is trading with whatever QQQ and Spy are doing I wanted to do the same thing they're doing" (performance-coaching_Session_9_Open_Mic_transcript.txt [606s-615s])

## EXTRACTION NOTE
This transcript is primarily about defining performance coaching vs. psychology coaching vs. technical analysis. It contains NO specific trading methodology rules (break-and-retest, one-candle-rule, 84% rule, order blocks, etc.). It focuses on journaling, goal-setting (SMART goals), and performance tracking frameworks.

## SUPPLEMENTARY: Performance Coaching Framework (for context only)

### Performance Coaching Definition
- **Performance vs. Psychology vs. Technical** "The technical side of trading... it's basically what Tony and Jack are teaching us... And then we have this psychology side of trading... when our emotions drive our decision... And the performance coaching, it's about not avoiding these emotions. It's about having systems to perform, even if we have these emotions" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [617s-630s])
- **Performance focus** "Performances, a focus on metrics and outcomes... And also it's about developing discipline and routines... And also, it's about mindset... building a performance oriented mindset" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [769s-839s])

### Journaling Framework
- **Tags for trades** "I have tags for my sizing. I have tags for my race. I have that for my trade execution. I have tags for my entry, for my scaling out... I have tags for my emotions on the during the trade" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [2505s-2536s])
- **Detachment through journaling** "once I journal my trades for today, I completely detached from them. So even if it's a win or a loss, it's like, okay, I already drained my brain about that, and I'm ready for this day" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [3935s-3951s])

### SMART Goals
- **SMART framework** "smart goal it is. This is smart, it stands for specific, measurable, attainable, relevant and time-based" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [3114s-3124s])
- **Example goal** "to improve my win rate by 5% within the first three months of the year" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [3151s-3162s])
- **Example goal** "to reduce emotional trades by half over the next two months" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [3464s-3484s])

### Ticker Focus
- **Limit tickers** "I actually just trade AMD, Apple Amazon, Nvidia Tesla, and that's it... there's a lot of people that they just started trading a few months ago and they have like 10 different tickers on their charts. It took me years to have five on my screen... you should be respecting the process, right? Like going one ticker a time" (performance-coaching_What_Is_Performance_Coaching.txt [4557s-4616s])

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 5. Key levels
- **Preparation of levels the night before** "I need to come to my desk in the afternoon on the boss market and prepare my levels for next day. So I can't, you know, I can't go to bed without knowing that I went to refine my levels and I just marked the high-allow for these days and now I'm moving my daily charts." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [1893s-1929s])
- **Too many lines = fear** "How many times do you compare your charts with dozens of lines and levels on every candle that looks like you know worth it? And then you go watch Jack or Tonys or Lawrence or my chart just to see that why this guy only has four lines. Why do you only have seven lines? Why do you know the previous day high and low and one or two other levels? So having too many lines, it's just a sign of fear of missing or fear of loss." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3989s-4029s])
- **Key levels = PDH/PDL + one or two others** "Why do you only have seven lines? Why do you know the previous day high and low and one or two other levels?" (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4019s-4029s])

## 6. Time-of-day
- **Sitting out first 15 minutes** "For me, recently it's been getting my trades in the stats and seeing that I had it just like absolutely terrible when I went to the first 15 minutes of market. And so the last like two weeks, it's been kind of nice sitting for the first 15 minutes knowing I'm not going to do anything at all and just wait for stuff to play out." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [1976s-2005s]) — *Note: This is a student sharing their personal rule, not the coach teaching it as a system rule.*

## 7. Exits
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 8. HTF application
NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE

## 9. Trade selection
- **Reducing account to limit trades** "I reduced my account down, so I could only take maybe three, four trades or so, max." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4380s-4388s]) — *Note: Student sharing personal strategy*
- **Two to three trades max** "I try to keep my account for only taking two or three trades. Usually two trades, right? That's one way to keep me on the control of not over trading or revenge trading." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4412s-4427s]) — *Note: Coach sharing personal practice*
- **A, B, C trade categorization** "measuring my A B and C type trades, and I'm reducing my C type trades, by categorizing or by identifying." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4509s-4524s]) — *Note: Student sharing, not coach defining criteria*

## SUPPLEMENTARY: Performance Coaching Framework

### Preparation Philosophy
- **Preparation vs. goals** "We don't rise to the level of our goals. We fall to the level of our preparation." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [615s-627s])
- **Set up like a pro, execute with confidence** "Set up like a prop, execute with confidence." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [666s-669s])
- **Mistakes come from lack of readiness** "Most mistakes come from lack of readiness and not lack of skill." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [1112s-1121s])
- **P&L is a lagging indicator** "Your P&L, it's a lagging indicator of your progress." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [2676s-2688s])

### Three Levels of Preparation
- **Mental prep** "We have our mental prep. That is our mindset and energy." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [1323s-1334s])
- **Technical prep** "Having our key levels marked, having our high conviction setups or zones, planning for different scenarios." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3015s-3030s])
- **Environmental prep** "Treat your trading space like a cockpit, nothing extra, nothing messy. Every second of your attention counts." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3122s-3129s])

### Preparation vs. Prediction
- **Not predicting, reacting** "It's not about guessing. It's about reacting. You will always be better positioned to react to a scenario that you create, bear." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3052s-3070s])
- **Plan for scenarios, not certainty** "Focus on different scenarios, not certain. You need to be okay with market uncertainty." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4068s-4078s])
- **Preparation ≠ control** "Do not confuse preparation with control. Remember, market. I mean, you need to be fine with market uncertainty, right? So you are not trying to guess what will happen. You are preparing for how to respond when it does." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3942s-3967s])

### Pre-Market Routine
- **Routine signals game time** "Every time you see your routine or just start your routine, you know, it's game time. And that's how you reduce randomness." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3372s-3383s])
- **Journaling is part of the session** "Your journaling is part of your session. It's not a different thing, it's not something optional, it's not something that you do in your spare time. It's part of your trading session." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3236s-3249s])

### Distraction Management
- **Phone is main distraction** "What was our main distraction? Or what we agreed? What was the the most frequent distraction for us? ... Phone." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [1216s-1255s])
- **Live trading as noise** "Listening to the live trade was noise for me. So I just decided to stop joining the live trade." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [2315s-2332s])
- **No caffeine during session** "I recommend during trading session, just water, no coffee... I have seen a huge improvement on my focus when I switch from coffee to water, only only on my trading session." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3178s-3205s])

### Trade Mindset
- **Treat every trade like a championship fight** "Treat every trade, like a championship fight, right? Because nothing before matters, this is the last match. This is the last fight. Nothing before matters. And nothing on the next, on the next session will matter either." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3272s-3303s])
- **Don't carry baggage** "Never carry baggages from the, from the last one or from the next one. Focus on that." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [3310s-3320s])

### Homework Assignment
- **Create pre-market checklist** "Create your own pre-market checklist, create your own pre-market checklist." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4283s-4292s])
- **Practice routine 3 days in a row** "Practice your routine three days in a row." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4733s-4741s])
- **Set daily intention in journal** "What is your intention today? Today, I will be more selective with my trades. Today, I will be more intentional with my validation level." (performance-coaching_session-14-performance-through-preparation.txt [4758s-4779s])

# EXTRACTION FROM: performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review_transcript.txt

### Edge / Playbook (NEW - Detailed Definition)
- **Edge = Playbook = Personal Checklist** "Your playbook it's the physical checklist of your edge" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [3224s-3237s])
- **Edge is your optimal setup + optimal state of mind** "The edge is your optimal setup along with your optimal state of mind and the emotion" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2983s-2989s])
- **Edge = checking boxes of your trading plan** "The edge is really just you checking the box of your playbook your trading plan" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2656s-2663s])
- **Edge includes recognizing which setups work for YOU** "It is part of your edge it's recognizing which are the setups that work really well for you" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2669s-2676s])
- **Playbook must be super personal and unique** "Your playbook it's... there's no right or wrong way to build it but it needs to be super personal and unique to you because it needs to have your own process your own setups your questions the situations that you face when you are trading" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [3313s-3329s])
- **Playbook includes: context, entry signal, position management** "You need to define the context on your set up so what is triggering your set up what is your actual entry signal what needs to be happening at the same time... also how to manage your position" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2180s-2211s])
- **Playbook includes personal factors (sleep, breakfast, kids)** "If I don't take breakfast or if I don't take my meds in the morning if I can't trade... if my kids are on vacation I don't take any trade because it's gonna be a mess in my house" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2538s-2560s])
- **Playbook includes time-of-day rules** "I am taking a trade right at the open or I'm waiting 10 minutes or I'm waiting 30 minutes or I'm just trading between these hours" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2567s-2583s])

### Trade Selection / Edge Personalization (NEW - Personal Examples)
- **Personal edge: Tesla break-and-retest of PDH/PDL only** "For me my edge it's taking Tesla trades on the break and retest of the previous day high or low nothing else" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2702s-2709s])
- **Personal edge: No trades inside PDH/PDL range on Tesla** "I will never take a trade inside of the previous day high or low on Tesla because Tesla when it flies but when it holds inside of the range it can take weeks inside of the range" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2676s-2692s])
- **Personal edge: Does NOT take 84% rule trades** "In my edge I don't take 84 percent for trades I don't take them I don't feel good at them because I feel that when I'm taking them it's my mind tricking me like oh well this is kind of like a revenge trade because I lost the first time I need to make it back" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2727s-2760s])
- **Personal edge: Order block + key level only, not random OBs** "An order block plus a key level that's part of my edge but just an order block randomly in a chart it's not part of my edge" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2794s-2809s])
- **Personal edge: One-candle-rule not taken in last 5 minutes** "The one candle rule I don't take it if it's like in the last five minutes" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2769s-2775s])

### Over-Trading Prevention (NEW - Account Size Method)
- **Reduce account to limit bullets** "I just reduced my account value so... once I complete my third trade I'm done I don't have buying power all my funds are on sale and I can't take any other trade" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [966s-992s])
- **3 bullets max per day** "I only have three bullets for the day so this stops me to over trade but also encourages me to take only quality trades because I only have three bullets" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1032s-1048s])
- **Account sized for 2-3 trades** "I try to keep my account for only taking two or three trades. Usually two trades" (CONFIRMED from previous extraction - performance-coaching_session-15-handling-drawdowns.txt)
- **Natural mechanical stop** "It has worked wonders because that's a natural mechanical stop for me to over trade" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [995s-1002s])

### Exits / Trimming (NEW - Data-Driven Approach)
- **Review data to find optimal trim point** "When I went back to review my journal to take a look to my data I realized that if I never took profits on a fixed percentage it actually went to the high of the day or low of the day" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1597s-1614s])
- **Trim at first 15-minute chart key level** "What I'm doing now is instead of waiting to high of the day or low of the day I'm waiting for my first 15 minute chart key level so now I trust a lot on my 15 minute chart key levels and now I'm taking profits there" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1644s-1660s])
- **Minimum 1:1 to 1.5 R:R to key level** "If I don't see an entry point where I can get at least a 1:1 to 1.5 risk reward to this key level I don't take a trade" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1669s-1677s])
- **Win rate vs R:R is personal choice** "Do you want a better win rate or do you want a better risk reward and there's not a wrong or right answer for this it's whatever you feel comfortable" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1700s-1715s])

### Journaling (CONFIRMED + NEW)
- **Journal as if explaining to spouse** "Try to journal like you are explaining to her or to him... this will change dramatically how you think about going crazy on entries or managing your position" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1451s-1468s])
- **Deeper journaling reduces trades** "Extensive journaling is helping me to reduce number of trades... because you are giving a good think before jumping into a trade" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1366s-1396s])

### Sleep / Rest (NEW - Performance Factor)
- **Sleep tracking as part of playbook** "For some people tracking the amount of rest time or sleep it's something so if they don't get enough sleep they don't trade" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2523s-2530s])
- **7 hours minimum** "I'm gonna stop trading with less than seven hours of sleep I see a big difference" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [2948s-2958s])

### Advice for Beginners (NEW)
- **Only two things: find an edge + only trade that edge** "Find an edge and only trade that edge... nothing else. So don't try to jump from one setup to the others" (performance-coaching_session-30-august-q-a-community-review.txt [1903s-1925s])

- One-candle-rule / opening-candle-retest (exact spec)

- **Trade window (930-11AM)** "That's why we only trade between those windows, because that's when we make the most money. And then after the fact, right, we end up losing less money or the opportunities less." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1178s-1186s])
- **Trade window (930-11AM)** "I should be trading between 930 to 11AM every single day." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1425s-1431s])

## 9. Trade selection
- **A+ setups** "what setups are those A plus setups that you can hit on a day-to-day basis." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [154s-158s])
- **A game = A+ setups** "This is where you want to your A plus setups. This is where you want to push the the floor to the this is where you want to push on size, right? This is where you want to trade the best. When you're in your A game." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1915s-1931s])
- **A game characteristics** "your very relaxed, your decisive, your patient on your setups, your confident and your trusting your gut." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1905s-1910s])
- **A game tactical skills** "clear understanding of a context of a market as whole, clear understanding of correlation with the NASDAQ, ES and Q, clear understanding of location price and where we are on a chart, letting price come to me, seeing trap traders." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [2012s-2028s])
- **B game = scalp mode** "When you're in your B game, you're going to want to step a little bit back. You want to be making, you want to be taking more scalps, right? You don't want to be over pushing it." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1931s-1937s])
- **B game characteristics** "maybe a little bit overthinking, tension to wrong market, right? Losing focus, missing obvious trades. Look at P&L reacting slower, going against gut." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1883s-1894s])
- **B game tactical skills** "scalping for a two's takes, but not big trades, not aware enough to sit out or pick my spots, cutting trades too soon, and not giving enough room." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [2034s-2044s])
- **C game = step back** "when obviously you're in the C game, right? You want to step back completely. If you know you're distracted, right? You know your forcing trades? Right? There's no reason to be trading where you're in the C game." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1937s-1949s])
- **C game characteristics** "distracted, risk adverse, hesitating our entries, forcing trades to overcome hesitance, no patience, negative self-talk, self-doubt, trading P&L." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1824s-1841s])
- **C game tactical skills** "keep chasing price, keep fading directional moves, cutting trades too soon, because I wanted to get paid, didn't follow risk management rules, making impulsive trades because I'm overreacting to one element, nothing without a plan for going price against me." (the-accelerator-course_how-to-journal-your-key-statistics.txt [1982s-1997s])

- **Stop-when-green** NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE
- **Market regime** NOT COVERED IN THIS SOURCE
