## SPECIAL AUDIT QUESTIONS

### Platform/Execution (NEW material - platform setup)
- **TWS as preferred broker** "This is my personal broker, right interactive brokers, this is Tony's personal broker" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [4s-9s])
- **IBKR for non-US traders** "This is what I feel like everyone should be using if you're, let's say, outside of the US" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [9s-14s])
- **IBKR for US traders** "If I was in the US, right, it has the best executions, right, best fills" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [18s-22s])
- **Regulated broker importance** "This is a regulated broker. This is one of the best brokers on the market" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [65s-70s])
- **KYC requirements** "You have to put in your KYC where you send them your social insurance number" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [86s-91s])
- **Options/futures application** "if you want to be trading options and features, you have to apply for that and then you have to take some sort of exam on your trading experience" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [167s-177s])
- **TWS layout grouping** "whenever you see this green thing right here, if the same colors are aligned, that's what's called the group. And then whenever you click it, it'll change the chart" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [316s-324s])
- **Quote monitor for contracts** "I like to have what's called the quote monitor... I put all my contracts and everything. If I'm flipping through contracts" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [414s-437s])
- **Dragging contracts to quote monitor** "I'll drag it onto the quote monitor, and then I can see a bunch of more information" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [463s-469s])
- **Book trader vs order entry** "The book trader and order entry are both the same thing... I like to use the book trader... I have the buttons, and it's a lot quicker" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [484s-503s])
- **Trade summary for P&L** "Once you have your trade summary, it shows the P&L that you have for the day" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [508s-521s])
- **Pre-loading contracts for speed** "I just drag all the contracts that I need for the day onto the cool monitor. And then I just quickly flip through the cool monitor and then be able to enter" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [766s-776s])
- **Five-second entry execution** "It takes like five seconds. Very quick" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [787s-792s])
- **Book trader one-click entry** "All you have to do is just click market buy... it just takes one button and then it instantly routes into the market" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1578s-1594s])
- **Instantaneous order transmission** "I always have the transmit order instantaneously" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1657s-1661s])
- **Hotkeys alternative** "If you don't use the order entry or the book trader. A lot of people like to use hotkeys" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1762s-1770s])
- **Hotkey configuration** "click control H... this brings up two hotkeys on the chart trader" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1774s-1785s])
- **Hotkey for fixed contract size** "if I want to buy that default size is let's say if I want to play 10 contracts I can do absolute" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1807s-1818s])
- **Data subscription fees waived** "the US equity options and add on streaming bundle... waived if monthly commission reaches $30 USD" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1970s-1989s])
- **30 contracts for fee waiver** "if you trade 30 contracts each way right 15 15 contracts like 15 buy and 15 sales rate you're most likely going to get a waived" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1990s-2002s])
- **TradingView integration not recommended** "I don't like it like I've had bad experiences with the trading view integration especially when trading futures... I've gotten a slip a couple of times" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [2082s-2095s])
- **TradingView only supports futures/stocks** "it currently only supports futures and stocks right it does not support options" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [2057s-2062s])

### Indicators used
- **VWAP** "volume, weighted average price... intradivolium weighted average price is VWAP" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [914s-925s])
- **90 MA** "Personally, I like to use the the 90 MA" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [948s-954s])
- **21 MA optional** "you can also add the 21 MA if you really like" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [977s-984s])

### Option chain preferences
- **Implied volatility check** "if it's over 100. Make sure to stay away because those contracts are probably super juiced up" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1376s-1380s])
- **Volume priority** "You want to always look for the contracts with the most volume" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1380s-1384s])
- **Open interest for swing trading** "if you're swing trading, you want to see the open interest that means how many contracts are being held overnight" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1408s-1418s])
- **Option chain columns** "I like to look at the bid ask and apply volatility, volume and open interest" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1361s-1370s])

### Time and sales / Level 2
- **Not used for mega caps** "I don't use it personally. I used to use it when I was training penny stocks, but it's not as useful when you're trading mega caps" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1103s-1114s])
- **Level 2 not needed** "I personally don't use that level 2... When I'm trading mega caps because I already know where the liquidity is resting" (the-accelerator-course_setup-interactive-brokers.txt [1118s-1135s])

# EXTRACTION FROM: the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview_transcript.txt

## Indicators used
- **VWAP, 9 EMA, 21 EMA** "Me personally, I solely focus on price action, but I also do usually have VWAP in a couple EMAs on my chart just to have it for aesthetic reasons that's just about it" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [255s-268s])
- **9 and 21 EMA only** "The only indicators I use are the 9 and 21 EMA" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [294s-298s])
- **VWAP bands removed** "for VWAP, why is you have to take off the bands" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [326s-333s])
- **Candle color disassociation** "A lot of people don't like green and red, right? Because it associates with green being positive and red being negative emotions, right? So you kind of want to disassociate those colors when you're trading right" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [191s-202s])

## Chart layout
- **QQQ on left, stock on right** "I have the QQQ on the left hand side and I have Tesla on the right hand side" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [386s-391s])
- **QQQ importance for tech stocks** "that QQQ is one of the most important things, especially when you're trading the tech stocks" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [413s-420s])
- **Relative strength/weakness via QQQ** "You want to flip through the charts to see if you have any relative strength or weakness. And then you want to keep the QQQ on the left hand side, which is the NASDAQ" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [420s-428s])
- **Extended trading hours** "if you guys do want to have extended trading hours on... you can go down onto the regular trading hours here and click extended trading hours" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [350s-360s])
- **Timeframes used** "I like the one minute, the five minute, 15, one hour, daily, weekly charts. These are the main charts that I like to look at personally" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [448s-457s])
- **Multiple layouts for different instruments** "I have my Nasdaq layout, which is my NQ. I have my ES on this one... I have multiple layouts" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1242s-1255s])
- **Weekly outlook layout** "This is my weekly outlook layout where I draw my thesis coming into the week" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1302s-1309s])

## Drawing tools
- **Fibonacci retracement levels** "I like to have is a 0.382, the 0.5 level, the 6.1 level, the 7.1, the 6.18 level, and I also like to have the negative 6.18 level as well" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [584s-601s])
- **Boxes for supply/demand** "Boxes can be used for supply and demand areas, order block, fair value gaps" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [526s-533s])
- **Risk/reward projection tool** "the projection tools, which are the forecasts, which will tell you the risk to reward ratios on each trade. I like this a lot when I'm calculating the risk reward ratio" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [617s-627s])

## Backtesting / Replay mode
- **Replay mode for backtesting** "trading view has a feature, which is called replay mode, and you guys can go back in time and look at historical data" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [956s-963s])
- **Start on higher timeframes, go to lower** "It's good when you're looking at the replay modes to go back and go on the higher timeframes... and then go into the lower timeframes to see how you had traded this in real time" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1048s-1057s])
- **Replay with QQQ alongside** "you can have the replay mode where you can show how the QQQ up beside, which will give you even more extra confluence when back testing" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1156s-1165s])
- **Best experience = real time, second best = past price action** "the best way to gain experience is through real time price action. That is the best, the second best is to go look past price action" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1204s-1212s])

## Trade example (A+ setup)
- **Break and retest entry** "This is the clear break and retest level... Comes back close to this retest level, you can see that price ends up tapping it perfectly right here. This is where I actually personally entered, right? Off this break and retest level" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [997s-1024s])
- **Target = pre-market high (liquidity)** "my targets, my targets is the all-time high level, which is this pre-market high" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1035s-1040s])
- **A+ setup grading** "This was an A plus setup for me, right?" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1043s-1048s])
- **Scale majority, leave runners** "I end up scaling majority of my position here, and then end up leaving trailers, right?" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1079s-1085s])
- **Order block retest for re-entry** "price comes back down into this one minute order block, and then it's up gravitating back towards the highs" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1091s-1098s])
- **Best trade of the year** "I remember this trade very clearly, it was actually my best trade of the year" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [1087s-1091s])

## Alerts
- **Price alerts on key levels** "you can set price targets, and potential price alerts on the chart" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [844s-850s])
- **Alert on horizontal ray** "we can right click on this, and you can click add a alert on the horizontal ray" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [863s-866s])
- **Alert for swing trading** "especially if you're swing trading and you're not staring at the screen all the time, right? You want to have price alerts set, which is very important" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [942s-951s])

## Watchlist
- **Separate watchlists per instrument** "if you're trading not just tech names, you're trading penny stocks, you're trading mega caps, you're trading crypto, you're trading futures... you want to have a watch list for every different instrument you trade" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [777s-791s])
- **Sort by % change for relative strength/weakness** "you can click this change button, which sorts it from the highest to least highest... if you're looking for relative weakness on the day" (the-accelerator-course_setup-tradingview.txt [798s-812s])

# EXTRACTION: the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends_transcript.txt

## 1. Break-and-Retest

- **Core definition - resistance turns to support** "previous resistance should now act as support and then it's the same as the other side... previous resistance should now turn into support and then support should turn into resistance" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [438s-443s])
- **Entry trigger** "the entries will be that break in retest" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [528s-530s])
- **Stop placement** "your stops will be a break below market structure" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [531s-533s])
- **Target** "the target will be that new resistance and above" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [533s-535s])
- **Breakout identification** "we have our break out and then what we want to see is... this resistance now turn back into support" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [463s-479s])
- **Buy location** "where we would look to buy is around this new formed support level and then we look for the targets being that new resistance level" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [498s-508s])
- **This is how Tony trades** "This is how I trade this is how Tony trades right we feel like is the best way" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [508s-517s])
- **Break-and-retest in order blocks** "break and retests in order blocks so support turn resistance and resistance turn into support... that's how we like to enter that's how we like to use support and resistance every single day" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [767s-783s])

## 2. Support and Resistance - Areas vs Lines

- **Support/resistance is an AREA, not just a line** "a lot of people what they get wrong is that support and resistance is not just a key level on a chart it also can be represented by a key area of support and resistance" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [245s-258s])
- **Draw boxes for areas** "That's why you see some people draw boxes... where there's multiple touches right of resistance or support" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [258s-269s])
- **Wicks vs bodies - draw the full area** "sometimes you'll see that people draw the wicks or sometimes you'll see people draw the bodies... what they don't understand is you can draw the full area of the support and resistance levels" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [271s-284s])
- **Areas prevent fakeouts** "What you can do to not get fake out a lot of the times right is you can draw these areas of support resistance instead of just these key levels so you can give extra confirmation when trading these zones" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [403s-414s])
- **Example of area respect** "if you drew out the area you can see that it respects it a lot better right this box... you can see this is an area of resistance right not just the key level" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [345s-384s])

## 3. Key Levels - Drawing and Hierarchy

- **Draw around every key pivot high and low** "we're gonna look to draw them around every single key pivot high and low" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [999s-1004s])
- **Key pivot highs/lows on higher timeframes** "The key pivot highs and lows happen on the higher timeframes" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1004s-1008s])
- **Start weekly, then daily, then hourly, then 5-min** "start on the higher timeframes weekly chart... move your way down to the daily chart mark out those key highs and lows right then move on to your one-hour chart... and then move all the way down into the five minute time frame" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1226s-1241s])
- **Higher timeframe levels have most significance** "the key levels are always based off of the higher timeframes right those are the ones that have the most significant price action those are the ones that have the most liquidity and those are the ones that are gonna have those multiple touches" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1241s-1257s])
- **Key levels = old highs and old lows** "key levels should not be hard to draw right there just old highs and old lows right all you have to do is look left" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1213s-1220s])
- **"When in doubt zoom out"** "when in doubt zoom out look left and draw the key pivot highs and lows on those higher time frames" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1220s-1226s])
- **Levels act as zones/magnets** "these levels act as zones right magnets... prices are always gonna go from support to resistance to support to resistance" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1279s-1294s])

## 4. Pre-Market Levels

- **Draw pre-market highs and lows** "you can also draw more levels which will be your pre market highs and then your pre market lows" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1412s-1419s])
- **Pre-market levels are key liquidity levels** "pre market highs and lows are these key liquidity levels as I mentioned before" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1543s-1548s])
- **Trade pre-market levels in trending markets** "how we want to trade those pre market highs and pre market lows is when we're in a trending market" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1464s-1472s])
- **Need strong displacement above pre-market high** "if you can get above the level with strong displacement we're gonna be looking for a retest" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1495s-1499s])
- **Strong candle closure confirms break** "very strong five minute candle closure right creates a displacement... then we have a nice little push back into where right pre this resistance is now should act as support" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1505s-1526s])
- **Higher timeframe levels above for confluence** "we have higher time frame levels above you can use this pre market high as a retest level where we can enter" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1549s-1559s])

## 5. Order Blocks - Intraday Levels

- **Order blocks are the best intraday levels** "the only new levels right when you're trading intraday are things that are called order blocks" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1617s-1623s])
- **Order blocks work best in trending markets** "when you're drawing support and resistance levels intraday wise, what you want to be drawn is those things called order blocks... these levels are very key in the market right especially as I mentioned in trending markets" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1639s-1655s])
- **Best order blocks have multiple confluence** "the best order blocks happen when there's multiple touches when you have the displacement you have the break and retest when you have a fair value gap... all those things aligning" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1661s-1676s])
- **Order blocks coincide with key levels** "you'll often see that the best order block coincides with key levels" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1655s-1661s])
- **Drawing order blocks - wick to body** "this one minute order block from the wick you can draw to the body" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1735s-1738s])
- **Bullish confirmation - close above order block** "you know this is very bullish when it closes above the top of the order block" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1746s-1752s])
- **Down close candles in uptrends** "down close candles... once price closes above these down close candles you can use it as support" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1805s-1820s])
- **Drawing from top of wick to top/bottom of body** "from the top of the wick you can draw it to the top of the body or the bottom of the body right it's all this whole down close candle" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1860s-1865s])
- **Avoid order blocks in consolidating markets** "when you're in consolidating markets that's when you can easily get chopped up right so that's why you always want to be drawing these levels on trending markets" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1959s-1976s])

## 6. Market Trends - Identification

- **Three types of trends** "there's three types of trends in the markets... up trend... down trend... consolidation or a sideways trend" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2088s-2144s])
- **Uptrend = higher highs, higher lows** "up trend... higher highs higher lows higher highs higher lows" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2088s-2101s])
- **Downtrend = lower highs, lower lows** "downtrend when prices making a lower high lower high lower low lower high lower low" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2101s-2114s])
- **Consolidation = range between highs and lows** "consolidation right when price is bouncing in between highs and lows right in between a range" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2125s-2138s])
- **Majority of time = consolidation** "your consolidation is what happens majority of the time... price is majority of the time in a range" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2144s-2156s])
- **Make money in trends, not consolidation** "price moves the best when we're in up trends or downtrends and that's where we want to capture money... it's very hard to make money in consolidating phases" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2165s-2181s])
- **Stay out during consolidation on higher timeframes** "when price looks like this on the higher timeframes right you really want to look for either size down or you want to wait for price to trend in one clear direction" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2306s-2312s])

## 7. Pullback vs Correction

- **Pullback/retracement = still forming higher highs/lows** "retracement are when we still form our higher highs and higher lows... we're reforming higher highs higher lows so we know we're in bullish structure" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2434s-2459s])
- **Correction = break of structure / change of character** "a correction right instead of a retracement or a pullback... it's when we finally break structure or we have a what's called a change of character" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2472s-2480s])
- **Correction invalidates higher low** "this push down invalidated our higher low and then you can see right now we start forming what's called lower highs and lower lows" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2484s-2498s])

## 8. Break of Structure vs Change of Character

- **Break of structure = continuation in trend direction** "a break of structure forms in the direction of the trend creating continuation patterns... breaks of recent lower lows when bearish or breaks of recent higher highs when bullish" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2523s-2543s])
- **Change of character = end of trend / market structure shift** "a change of character forms at the end of a trend... this is what's called your market structure shift" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2556s-2563s])
- **Change of character invalidates the trend structure** "when a new high is formed... what we have is a pulse of move towards the downside which is your change of character which is also could be classified as a market structure shift... that's when this higher low is invalidated" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2569s-2591s])
- **Breaks of structure used in trending markets** "breaks of structures are used in trending markets" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2605s-2608s])
- **Change of character = moving in opposite direction** "change of characters can be used when we end up breaking structure and moving in the opposite direction" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2608s-2610s])

## 9. Liquidity Levels

- **Liquidity = old highs and old lows** "liquidity levels essentially means old highs and old lows" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [711s-717s])
- **Key pivot highs/lows on daily, weekly, hourly** "key pivot highs and pivot lows on the daily chart... previous day highs previous day lows key levels on the weekly chart on the daily chart on the one hour chart" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [723s-729s])
- **What rests above old highs = liquidity** "what's resting above those old highs and old lows is what's called liquidity" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [729s-735s])
- **Equal highs/equal lows = multiple touches = liquidity** "equal highs and equal lows means there's multiple touches means there's at least two to three touches and that's where liquidity is resting" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [744s-751s])
- **Liquidity = support/resistance levels coinciding** "liquidity right liquidity is where all support and resistance levels coincided with one another" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [760s-767s])

## 10. Strong Support/Resistance - Multiple Touches

- **Best areas = multiple touches (2-3+)** "the best areas of support and resistance right happen around key levels... multiple touches right multiple touches really means two to three touches right the more touches the better" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [631s-652s])
- **Key psychological levels = whole numbers** "key psychological levels right these can be whole numbers... the hundreds... the two hundred levels right these whole psychological levels right a lot of people have orders resting above and below those levels" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [662s-690s])
- **All-time highs = key point of interest** "the all time high... those all time high levels will be used as a key point of interest because everyone's looking at those all time highs levels because what's resting above that level is what's called liquidity" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [690s-704s])

## 11. Price Movement Pattern

- **Price moves: trending → consolidation → expansion** "price always moves from trending to consolidation right and vice versa right when prices consolidate right the next thing that's gonna happen is an expansion and when prices expanding we either have a consolidation or we have a retracement" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2380s-2399s])
- **Full model: downtrend → consolidation → break of structure → uptrend → retracement → higher low → push to highs** "we have our downtrend and then we have consolidation we have a break of structure change of character and then you start going to uptrend have a retracement we create a higher low and then we end up pushing back to where the highs... which is what is resting there is liquidity" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [2399s-2416s])

## 12. Opening Range / First Candle

- **Opening range five-minute candle as down close candle** "market opens right here this is your opening range five minute candle right this is also a down close candle so if you can get above it, we use it as support" (the-accelerator-course_support-resistance-key-levels-market-trends.txt [1983s-1993s])

# Complete Trading Rules Extraction
## From Day 5 & Day 6 Boot Camp Recordings

## DAY 5: EVERY SETUP

### OPENING PLAYS OVERVIEW

**Context & Timing:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Plays Definition
- Rule: "Opening plays are within the first 15 to 30 minutes" of market open
- Context: Not every single day has opening plays; usually only when higher timeframe thesis is clear
- Timestamp: [86s-105s]
- File: Day 5

**Context & Timing:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Play Frequency
- Rule: "Usually if the higher time frame is more clear while some more opening plays towards the upside"
- Context: Opening plays are conditional on higher timeframe structure
- Timestamp: [110s-132s]
- File: Day 5

**Context & Timing:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Patience Rule for Opening Plays
- Rule: "If there's no opening plays then we usually want to be patient so sometimes you'll hear us say Right let's wait for the first 15 to 30 minutes to play out. We don't want to be taking any trading opportunities"
- Context: When market is stuck in middle of range with no clear setup, wait
- Timestamp: [125s-138s]

### GAP AND GO / BREAKAWAY GAP SETUP

**Definition:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Gap and Go / Breakaway Gap
- Rule: "Gap above your key levels. In this case primarily our previous day highs and previous day lows. Old highs roll lows and what we would now want to see is a hold above our key level in Continuation primarily away from the gap"
- Context: When market gaps above key levels and continues away, this is bullish continuation
- Timestamp: [499s-516s]
- File: Day 5

**Gap Statistics:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Gap Fill Probability
- Rule: "Gaps actually fill 70% of the time in a 48 hour period"
- Context: Key statistic for understanding gap behavior; used to favor gap fills in ranging markets

- File: Day 5

**Gap Identification:**
- Setup/Setup Name: New York Session Gap
- Rule: "There's a New York session gap and majority of the times these gaps actually tend to fill... whenever there's a gap in the chart... we call this the New York session gap"
- Context: Gap between 4pm Eastern close and 9:30am EST open; most common gap type
- Timestamp: [420s-440s]
- File: Day 5

**Gap Mechanics:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Gap Fill Definition
- Rule: "Gaps that consider filled when the wicks... Just basically has to fill the imbalance so in this case let's say for Tesla What is the gap fill you just want to mark out this The wick and then once it touches it I consider that the full gap fill"
- Context: Gap is filled when price touches the wick of the gap
- Timestamp: [1056s-1078s]

**Gap Mechanics:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Partial vs Full Gap Fill
- Rule: "Does a gap have to be completely filled or it can be partially exact that's a good question as well it can be partially filled. If there's a big gap in the chart and say something like Oracle It doesn't have to be filled the whole way because this would be a very big Felt which we don't expect you to are usually it fills Half the gap right that's a key level especially if it's a very big one Whereas if you have a small gap These ones you can expect to be filled just because they're not that big"
- Context: Big gaps may only fill 50%; small gaps typically fill completely

**Entry Models:**

### OPENING DRIVE PLAY

**Definition:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive Play Definition
- Rule: "An opening drive play is where traders capitalize on the initial Often strong movement of a stock right after the opening bell looking for a potential continuation or break us Capturing volatile price movements that market open Or if prices in a hurry to take out the liquidity slash fill in balance"
- Context: Capitalize on initial strong move at market open; can be continuation or liquidation
- Timestamp: [1703s-1726s]
- File: Day 5

**Criteria:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive Play Criteria
- Rule: "The criteria for an open drive play Clear targets slash draw on the quiddy Really the higher time friends were good to be looking for Previous day highs previous day lows And then really main thing when it comes to opening drive is being able to Identify relative strength and weakness if you're able to do this consistently Right this will give you a clear edge in the market"
- Context: Key levels, higher timeframe thesis, and relative strength identification are critical
- Timestamp: [1810s-1847s]
- File: Day 5

**Relative Strength Requirement:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive Relative Strength Rule
- Rule: "Right you want to focus on names with that are leading the markets towards upside Or that have relative strength in our leading the markets lower"
- Context: Must identify which names have relative strength; leads overall market direction
- Timestamp: [1836s-1851s]
- File: Day 5

**News Context:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive News Catalyst Rule
- Rule: "News Right understand news is fairly key for an open drive play or something that's in continuation Or what I call is in play so a lot of times the open drive plays happen after a large news So if you have a news and then maybe day two day three This usually happens on a day two move or day three move after strong news catalyst"
- Context: Opening drive plays often occur day 2-3 after major news catalyst
- Timestamp: [1891s-1924s]
- File: Day 5

**No News Scenario:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive with No News
- Rule: "And then no news Right so the open drive play can actually be on both sides of the market We usually like looking for continuation but you can also have an opening drive play where there's maybe like a gap in the chart and then Right there's no news so the gap occurred for no reason you can maybe expect it to Fill the imbalance fairly quickly"
- Context: Opening drive without news can mean gap fills imbalance quickly
- Timestamp: [1943s-1963s]
- File: Day 5

**Day Number Theory:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive Day Number Theory
- Rule: "So this is I consider day number one All right this can be any single day day number one specifically refers to a strong trending day any day that has news or just as a nice trend higher And then you can see in this case we have your after hours in pre-market All we do is consolidate So we just consolidate in the half-barrows in pre-market and then date number two Is one we can be looking for an open and drive so after a strong trending day and we consolidate what's after consolidation Is usually expansion or a potential reversal"
- Context: Day 1=strong trend/news; Day 2=opening drive play setup
- Timestamp: [1979s-2024s]
- File: Day 5

**Caution Warning:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive Difficulty Warning
- Rule: "I'd say the opening drive plays one of the harder ones to master just because there's a lot more discretion Involved whereas the other set as you talk about there's more criteria the opening drive play really more based off of red scalping Reading the tape and you have to be very quick"
- Context: Opening drive requires tape reading and speed; most discretionary setup
- Timestamp: [1787s-1801s]
- File: Day 5

**Volatility Risk:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Opening Drive Risk Warning
- Rule: "But you have to be very cautious right because when the market opens within the first kind of five to 15 minutes Very volatile you can make a lot of money if you're correct You can lose a lot of money if you're wrong or because price can all of a sudden flip on you"
- Context: First 5-15 minutes are most volatile; high risk/reward
- Timestamp: [1751s-1772s]
- File: Day 5

### DIP & RIP SETUP

- Rule: "So criteria for a different route clear trend towards upside consolidation slash retracement dip into a key level Preferably previous say highs hold highs slash lows Really just want to see a bounce up a key level in group"

- Context: Dip and Rip has dip first; Opening Drive just pushes up immediately

I usually like looking at this on the five and see this basically confirms that reversal"

## INTRADAY SETUPS

### ORDER BLOCK SETUP

**Definition:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block Definition
- Rule: "Order block is essentially one candle Right it can be on the one five 15 minute, but all it is is right up close candles And down close candles on a chair"

- File: Day 5

**Core Principle:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block Market Structure
- Rule: "An order block is is an area where large market participants have placed significant by and cell orders Order blocks are key level such areas where there's lots of liquidity on a chart And essentially institutions and market makers will place their areas In their place their orders in these areas of significance"
- Context: Order blocks are institutional liquidity zones
- Timestamp: [5462s-5486s]
- File: Day 5

**Uptrend Rule:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block in Uptrend (Down Close Candle)
- Rule: "We're gonna be looking for down close candles in an uptrend Talk about high probability versus low probability order blocks in a second Basically we're just gonna be marking out All right down close candles. We're gonna talk about if you're gonna mark up the work to the body You're gonna mark with a full candle"
- Context: Down close candles in uptrends act as support; mark from wick to body
- Timestamp: [5491s-5527s]
- File: Day 5

**Downtrend Rule:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block in Downtrend (Up Close Candle)
- Rule: "Up close candles in a downturn and down close candles in an up turn"
- Context: Up close candles in downtrends act as resistance
- Timestamp: [5893s-5898s]
- File: Day 5

**Probability Hierarchy - Uptrend:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block High Probability (Uptrend)
- Rule: "Respects the width to the body Even closes above it could work underneath sorry can we get to it but if it closes above That's your highest probability order block"
- Context: Wick to body = highest probability for uptrend order block
- Timestamp: [5591s-5601s]
- File: Day 5

**Probability Hierarchy - Uptrend (2nd):**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block Second Probability (Uptrend)
- Rule: "Your second highest probability you're gonna draw from essentially wicks and respects the top of the wick to the bottom of the body"
- Context: Top of wick to bottom of body = second highest probability
- Timestamp: [5609s-5617s]
- File: Day 5

**Probability Hierarchy - Uptrend (3rd):**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block Low Probability (Uptrend)
- Rule: "And then your third order block or technically I want to consider this your lowest probability because it's all based off context, but you're gonna draw at the fourth In Basically she's areas in price"
- Context: Full candle = lowest probability (most room for price to bounce)
- Timestamp: [5617s-5624s]
- File: Day 5

**Probability Hierarchy - Downtrend:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block High Probability (Downtrend)
- Rule: "The most bearish price action is gonna respect this It could come up all the way here and still reject But as I mentioned the wick to the body is the most important part for the most bearish price action"
- Context: Bottom of wick to bottom of body = highest probability for downtrend
- Timestamp: [5832s-5856s]
- File: Day 5

**Validation Rule:**
- Setup/Setup Name: Order Block Validation
- Rule: "When is an order block valid in order block is valid when price closes underneath They don't without close candle so this right here it validates the order block If we have no close underneath it then it's not valid"

## DAY 6: MARKET STRUCTURE & INDICES

### FIRST PULLBACK RULE

**Definition:**
- Setup/Setup Name: First Pullback Rule
- Rule: "Essentially all the first pullback rule is it's very simple right? It's if we see strong relative strength in the morning or relative weakness within the first let's just say 5 10 15 minutes of the day Right, and we're seeing a very big uptrend or we're seeing a very big downtrend the first pullback in the upside Okay, so the first pullback for the upside Usually gets bought up for that continuation move to the upside or the first pullback to the downside Usually gets bought up for a move to the downside, right? It's a little of the that simple"
- Context: In strong moves (up or down), first pullback usually gets bought/sold for continuation
- Timestamp: [275s-304s]
- File: Day 6

**Upside Application:**
- Setup/Setup Name: First Pullback Upside
- Rule: "So the probability of buyers stepping up for the first pullback is a lot higher because once again After this huge move up you have to understand the different you have to understand the Correlation of buyers and sellers in the market right so in this market We can see that if buyers are aggressive here right there is definitely unfilled by orders Right because there's limit orders that buyers have placed around these areas that may have not gone filled"
- Context: In uptrends, first pullback has unfilled buy orders; high probability to get bought
- Timestamp: [335s-373s]
- File: Day 6

**Best Entry Point:**
- Setup/Setup Name: First Pullback Entry
- Rule: "Now however right this is the first kind of pullback and as you can see the first pullback got bought up right back up into high of day"
- Context: Enter on first pullback for best risk/reward
- Timestamp: [378s-383s]
- File: Day 6

**Bearish Version (Downside):**
- Setup/Setup Name: First Pullback Downside
- Rule: "So for example if we obviously this is what I was trying to play on Nvidia today was the first pullback plus the 5 and a high plus previous day high Didn't end up working today. However if we look at something like AMD right? This is exactly what the first pullback rule is right there's a higher probability chance at the first pullback that's bought up"
- Context: First pullback in downtrends gets sold; can apply same principle to shorts
- Timestamp: [311s-326s]
- File: Day 6
